question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Use laryngoscope to expose vocal cords through mouth of 25-30g female Yorkshire pig
2. Spray vocal cords with two puffs of 2% lidocaine topical solution
3. Intubate with 6.5 millimeter tube
4. Use syringe to inflate tube cuff with 3-5 milliliters of air
5. Ensure tracheal tube is in proper position
6. Apply ointment to animal's eyes
7. Protect eyes with cover patch
8. Disinfect neck and abdominal wall with 1% iodine-based scrub solution
9. Make vertical midline anterior neck incision to sternum using number 23 blade
10. Dissect strap muscles
11. Retract muscles laterally using Kelly tissue scissors and Lahey retractor to expose trachea from first ring to thoracic inlet
12. Expose common carotid artery and internal jugular vein for anastomosis
13. Create 1 cm window in second or third tracheal cartilage using number 11 blade
14. Verify endotracheal tube placement through tracheal defect and confirm continuous aeration
15. Design flap on upper abdomen using surgical marking pen
16. Create 4x3 cm elliptical skin incision through anterior sheath using #23 scalpel
17. Position incision on medial side of flap
18. Hold fascia with Allis clamp
19. Elevate flap from rectus abdominis muscle sheath
20. Locate perforators going to skin flap
21. Perform blunt dissection of exposed muscle tissue
22. Follow perforators to superior epigastric vessels
23. Make lateral skin incision in flap using number 23 scalpel
24. Clamp superior epigastric vessels and venae comitantes with haemostat
25. Cut above haemostat using Kelly tissue scissors
26. Ligate vessel below haemostat with three-oh suture to allow haemostat removal
27. Clamp carotid artery with two haemostats placed 1-2 cm apart
28. Cut between haemostats using microscissors
29. Tie superior part of vessel with double suture to prevent leakage
30. Place first two stay sutures 120 degrees apart around vessel circumference using 10-0 simple full thickness interrupted suture
31. Place 2-3 stitches between stay sutures
32. Release vascular clamp
33. Check anastomosis for leakage
34. Create anastomosis between internal jugular vein and venae comitantes
35. Prick skin flap with 18-gauge needle syringe to test viability
36. Observe for blood droplet appearance at puncture site
37. Close tracheal window using 3-0 suture with muscle fascia of superior epigastric artery perforator flap
38. Exteriorize skin panel of flap
39. Suture flap skin panel to cervical midline skin incision
40. Close abdominal skin incision

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Obtain informed consent from subject
2. Provide instructions for pain scoring
3. Introduce subject to electronic Visual Analogue Scale slider
4. Instruct subject to indicate pain intensity (0-100%) by moving slider left to right
5. Inform subject that moving slider fully right terminates painful stimulus
6. Provide standardized pain scale definitions and instructions during training
7. Shave skin if necessary
8. Clean area overlying tibia bone using skin preparation gel
9. Place two silver-silver chloride electrodes on prepared skin
10. Position anode with middle 100 mm distal to caudal end of patella
11. Position cathode directly underneath anode
12. Instruct subject to sit comfortably with foot flat on floor
13. Record resistance of electrodes and verify <2 kiloohms
14. Remove electrodes and re-cleanse skin with preparation gel if necessary
15. Connect electrodes to constant current stimulator
16. Apply tetanic pulse (0 mA start, 0.5 mA/s increment, 10 Hz frequency, 0.2 ms duration)
17. Use Visual Analogue Scale to record pain detection threshold (rating >0)
18. Record pain tolerance level (when stimulus becomes intolerable)
19. Terminate painful stimulus administration
20. Record area under stimulus-response curve
21. Place 11 cm wide tourniquet cuff over gastrocnemius muscle
22. Instruct subject to sit comfortably with feet flat on floor
23. Inflate cuff at 0.5 kPa/s constant rate up to 100 kPa using electro-pneumatic regulator
24. Instruct subject to rate pain continuously using electronic Visual Analogue Scale during inflation
25. Cease stimulus by deflating cuff upon intolerable pain indication or reaching 100 kPa pressure
26. Prepare two thermostat-controlled circulating water baths set at 35°C and 1°C
27. Measure subject's resting diastolic blood pressure
28. Place 35 cm tourniquet on subject's non-dominant upper arm
29. Instruct subject to sit with palm flat, fingers spread wide, without touching bath edges, and rate pain intensity
30. Subject rates pain intensity
31. Place non-dominant hand in 35°C warm water bath for 2 minutes
32. Inflate blood pressure cuff on upper arm to 20 mmHg below resting diastolic pressure
33. Transfer hand from warm water bath to cold water bath at similar depth
34. Subject rates pain response during cold immersion using electronic Visual Analogue Scale
35. Remove arm from cold water bath after reaching tolerance or 120-second limit
36. Deflate blood pressure cuff
37. Dry forearm with towel
38. Repeat electrical stimulation task within 5 minutes after cold pressor test
39. Turn on UVB lamp and allow warm-up for at least ten minutes
40. Instruct subject to stand with left hand holding right shoulder
41. Place UVB lamp in direct contact with skin on right upper back/shoulder area
42. Apply UVB exposure based on subject's Fitzpatrick skin type to healthy skin
43. Apply ascending UVB doses to six 1x1 cm areas on subject's back to determine minimal erythema dose
44. Apply 3x3 cm UVB exposure equivalent to three-fold individual minimal erythema dose to subject's back
45. Ensure UVB exposure produces homogeneous, well-demarcated skin erythema to induce hyperalgesia
46. Prepare 3x3 cm thermode for thermal detection threshold assessment
47. Measure thermal pain detection threshold on normal skin contralateral to UVB-irradiated site
48. Set thermode initial temperature to 34°C
49. Ramp thermode temperature at 0.5°C per second
50. Repeat thermal pain threshold measurement on UVB-irradiated skin
51. Record average pain detection threshold from three consecutive stimuli

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Record weight of each mouse
2. Label each mouse tail with marking pen
3. Place mice in experimental room for ≥30 minutes acclimation
4. Set up 25x25x40 cm hanging box with rotatable mesh lid on lab bench
5. Place one mouse onto center of mesh lid
6. Turn mesh lid upside-down carefully
7. Measure fall latency of mouse from mesh lid
8. Return mouse to home cage
9. Place 9.9 by 42 cm white paper onto lab bench
10. Place dark goal box at distal end of paper
11. Place barrier boxes along both sides of runway to prevent escape
12. Add black and red ink to separate 35 mL Petri dishes
13. Place mouse at proximal end of paper facing goal box
14. Allow mouse to walk from proximal end to goal box
15. Gently scruff mouse upon reaching goal box
16. Tuck back and tail between thumb and fingers to limit hind limb movement
17. Immerse bottoms of forelimbs in red ink
18. Immerse bottoms of hind limbs in black ink
19. Place mouse back at proximal end of paper facing goal box
20. Allow mouse to walk from proximal end to goal box
21. Measure stride length three times using ruler by determining distances between same parts of paw prints
22. Draw line between consecutive same-side front footprints for front base-width measurement
23. Measure vertical line length from front footprint pad to drawn line for front base-width
24. Draw line between consecutive right hind footprints for hind base-width measurement
25. Measure vertical line length from left hind footprint pad to drawn line for hind base-width
26. Measure distance between pads of front and hind footprints for overlap
27. Use square drill to make 16 two-millimeter holes in 50-milliliter tube along scale marks
28. Make holes on backside of each scale mark
29. Cut off tube tip to create five-millimeter breathing hole
30. Make four-millimeter hole in tube cap for tail
31. Scruff mouse
32. Place mouse head-first into restraining tube with forelimbs confined
33. Pass tail through hole in cap
34. Cap the tube
35. Place mouse on bench top for two hours at room temperature
36. Remove mouse from tube after stress-loading period
37. Return mouse to home cage
38. Place mouse onto paper immediately after inking limb bottoms to prevent ink drying

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Add sodium citrate to chloroauric acid solution
2. Boil mixture to synthesize colloidal gold with appropriate particle size
3. Dissolve centrifuged colloidal gold particles in appropriate solution
4. Resuspend solution to obtain colloidal gold antibody conjugate
5. Submerge glass fiber membrane into conjugation mixture
6. Drop artificial antigen on NC membrane to form test line (T line)
7. Drop secondary antibodies on NC membrane to form control line (C line)
8. Attach NC membrane, absorbent pad, sample pad, and glass fiber membrane onto PVC board
9. Cut assembled paper board into 3.5 millimeters wide strips
10. Prepare full percent gold chloride acid solution and 1% sodium citrate solution
11. Turn on magnetic stirrer and place round-bottom flask on mixer
12. Add 120 milliliters distilled water into round-bottom flask
13. Heat flask contents to boiling on thermostatic magnetic stirrer
14. Quickly add 0.5 milliliters full percent chloroauric acid to boiling mixture
15. Add 5 milliliters of 1% sodium citrate to boiling mixture
16. Continue heating mixture for 5 to 10 minutes
17. Add five milliliters of 1% sodium citrate
18. Continue heating mixture for five to ten minutes
19. Observe solution color changing from pale yellow to wine red
20. Turn off power of thermostatic magnetic mixer
21. Cool mixture to room temperature
22. Transfer mixture to clean bottle
23. Store at 4°C
24. Add 100 microliters sodium chloride solution to eight tubes
25. Adjust colloidal gold solutions to pH 5-12 using 0.1 mol/L potassium carbonate
26. Add 100 microliters colloidal gold solution to eight tubes containing sodium chloride
27. Stand tubes for several minutes after blending
28. Observe color change of each tube solution
29. Record tube that remains red
30. Choose pH value with lowest amount
31. Record test tube that remains red
32. Select pH value with lowest potassium carbonate addition and stable solution color
33. Observe color change in test tubes
34. Record test tube that remains red
35. Select antibody amount with lowest monoclonal antibody concentration and stable solution color
36. Take 10 milliliters of colloidal gold solution
37. Use 0.1 mol/L potassium carbonate to adjust solution pH to optimal value
38. Slowly add SSd mABs at appropriate concentration
39. Shake mixture at room temperature for 30 minutes
40. Centrifuge mixture at 2000 RPM for 20 minutes at 4°C
41. Remove precipitate containing impurities of colloidal gold
42. Centrifuge mixture at 10,000 RPM for 40 minutes
43. Discard supernatant
44. Add Tris hydrochloric acid, BSA, Tween 20, and PEG 20,000
45. Blend mixture well to prepare resuspension buffer
46. Add resuspension buffer
47. Dissociate precipitate
48. Place NC membrane on PVC board positioned 2 cm from edge of section end
49. Dispense 2 mg/mL SSd-BSA on NC membrane at 2 cm from upper edge as test line
50. Dispense 1.5 mg/mL gold anti-mouse IgG on NC membrane at 2 cm from lower edge as control line
51. Attach absorbent pad to PVC sheet above NC membrane with 2mm overlap
52. Trim glass fiber membrane to 5cm length and 2cm width for use as conjugate pad
53. Submerge glass fiber membrane in gold nanoparticles mAB conjugate solution
54. Soak membrane to facilitate gold nanoparticles mAB absorption
55. Dry membrane in incubator at 37°C
56. Paste pretreated conjugate pad under NC membrane with 0.1 cm overlap
57. Trim Fusion3 membrane to 1.8 cm length and 3.5 cm width for sample pad
58. Place sample pad on PVC board overlapping conjugate pad by 2 mm
59. Cut assembled paper board into 3.5 mm wide strips using cutting machine
60. Compact strips using batch lamination system
61. Place test strips into the shell
62. Seal test strips in aluminum foil bag containing desiccant
63. Store sealed test strips away from light
64. Drop 50 microliters of sample solution onto sample hole
65. Observe chromatography process

16. Cap flask

18. Remove cap from flask

20. Add 1.25 mL of PVP solution to flask using micropipette
21. Place cap back on flask

23. Remove cap from flask

25. Place cap back on flask

27. Reduce room light to minimum
28. Turn heater off
29. Maintain stirring to prevent fluid burning
30. Use clamp to raise flask above heating bath
31. Remove flask cap
32. Remove flask from heating bath
33. Allow flask to cool for approximately 20 minutes

38. Centrifuge tubes at 5,150 G for 10 minutes
39. Remove top supernatant using micropipette
40. Remove supernatant using micropipette while leaving 100 microliters residue at tube bottom

43. Sonicate tubes

49. Place clean cover slip on top of solution drop
50. Rinse sample with deionized water after 10 minutes incubation
51. Dry sample using clean nitrogen gas

53. Place 20 microliters of diluted nanocube solution onto clean cover slip using micropipette

55. Rinse sample with deionized water
56. Dry sample using clean nitrogen gas

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Add 200 nanograms of high molecular weight DNA to a 0.2 milliliter thin-walled tube
2. Top off volume with distilled water to 7.5 microliters
3. Add 2.5 microliters of fragmentation mix from kit
4. Mix tube gently using inversions
5. Centrifuge tube briefly to spin down contents
6. Transfer tube to thermocycler for one-minute cycle at 30°C followed by one-minute cycle at 75°C
7. Pulse-spin the tube
8. Add 1 microliter of rapid adapter
9. Add 0.2 microliters of blunt/TA ligase master mix
10. Mix the tube by inversion
11. Pulse-spin the tube
12. Store the tube on ice
13. Remove Flow Cell from packaging
14. Attach Flow Cell to portable real-time sequencer
15. Attach sequencer to computer using USB cable
16. Start sequencing software
17. Select start device command and QC platform option in software
18. Press execute to run quality control
19. Navigate to dialogue box in software
20. Enter sample name in Sample ID field
21. Enter Flow Cell ID code from sticker
22. Open sequencer lid
23. Slide priming port cover left to access port
24. Remove few microliters of buffer including any bubble
25. Prepare sufficient buffer to fill sensor array
26. Prepare 1 ml of freshly made thoroughly mixed priming buffer
27. Add 800 microliters of priming buffer
28. Wait 5 minutes
29. Add remaining 200 microliters of priming buffer
30. Chill RBF1, LLB, and adapted tethered library on ice
31. Add 25.5 microliters of RBF1 to new 0.2 mL centrifuge tube
32. Add 12 microliters of room-temperature nucleus-free water to tube
33. Mix LLB reagent using pipette trituration
34. Add 26.5 microliter aliquot of LLB to tube with diluted RBF1
35. Mix combined reagents using pipette trituration to prevent bead settling
36. Add 11 microliters of adapted tethered library to mixture
37. Mix final sequencing mixture by gentle tube inversion
38. Perform pulse spin centrifugation to pool sample
39. Aspirate 75 microliters of sample while avoiding beads
40. Add sample to flow cell port dropwise
41. Replace sample port cover ensuring bung insertion
42. Replace device lid
43. Initiate 48-hour sequencing run through software
44. Subject 50 sequences of at least 350 nucleotides to BLASTN or BLASTX analysis

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Fast mouse for 8 to 12 hours prior to surgery
2. Prepare 5% sodium taurocholate solution
3. Filter sodium taurocholate solution through 0.22 microfilter
4. Autoclave surgical instruments and materials (forceps, scissors, blade holder, needle holder, hemostatic clip, vascular clamps, sterile drapes)
5. Sterilize micro-syringe through irradiation
6. Induce general anesthesia via intraperitoneal injection of 1% pentobarbital sodium solution at 50 mg/kg dosage
7. Fix mouse in supine position on irradiated foam flat plate using tape
8. Remove fur from mouse abdomen using depilation cream within boundaries: xiphoid process to bilateral anterior superior iliac spine line, parallel to front axillary lines
9. Wipe skin 2-3 times with 0.5% iodophor
10. Let skin dry for 1-2 minutes after each iodophor application
11. Cover mouse with sterile drapes
12. Cut open skin layer and subcutaneous layer
13. Make 2-3 cm median abdominal incision
14. Locate duodenum from left to right along stomach
15. Gently pull duodenum to left side using surgical forceps and disinfectant-soaked cotton swab
16. Expose pancreas, biliopancreatic duct, and duodenal papilla
17. Use 8-0 sutures to pass through duodenum-pancreas connection around papilla
18. Pull suture to left side of mouse body to fix duodenum
19. Gently pull skin and lower edge of liver aside
20. Clamp common hepatic duct
21. Pierce common hepatic duct with microinjector
22. Clamp the common hepatic duct
23. Pierce microinjector into distal biliopancreatic duct retrogradely
24. Fix needle tip near duodenal papilla with clamp
25. Infuse 10 microliters of 5% sodium taurocholate into biliopancreatic duct at 5 microliters/minute
26. Maintain biliopancreatic duct closure for 5 minutes
27. Remove clamps
28. Remove the clamps
29. Pull out the microinjector
30. Remove the other clamp
31. Fix the suture
32. Restore the duodenum to original anatomical position
33. Check for bleeding
34. Close abdominal incision with 6-0 sutures
35. Remove sterile drape
36. Disinfect incision with 0.5% iodophor
37. Place animals on 37°C thermal pad for anesthesia recovery
38. Administer 0.8 mL of 0.9% saline subcutaneously for fluid supplementation
39. Anesthetize mouse with pentobarbital sodium using same dose and injection route
40. Collect whole blood from abdominal aorta
41. Centrifuge blood at 1,500 x g for 15 minutes
42. Collect pancreas and lung tissues for histology evaluation
43. Immerse pancreas in 4% paraformaldehyde solution for 24 hours
44. Dehydrate pancreas in series of alcohol concentrations
45. Embed pancreas in paraffin
46. Section pancreas into 5-micron thick slices using microtome
47. Perform H&E staining on pancreas sections
48. Perform retrograde injection of sodium taurocholate into biliopancreatic duct
49. Ensure well-exposed biliopancreatic duct for successful puncture
50. Maintain appropriate angle and force during puncture to avoid duct damage

Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Apply quick-drying water-resistant glue to fix 2mm diameter steel sphere to end of thin non-metallic string/rod
2. Thread string through center hole of PTFE cuvette cap
3. Tape string in place with 22mm length hanging below cap
4. Equip water-filled syringe with 0.22 micrometer filter
5. Heat aqueous gel until viscosity is sufficiently reduced for pouring
6. Half-fill 2mm optical path rectangular glass cell with heated gel using syringe
7. Allow gel in cell to cool
8. Place 0.5mm diameter steel sphere at center of gel surface
9. Fill remainder of cell with heated aqueous gel using same method
10. Allow filled cell to cool
11. Equip halogen lamp with fiber light guide
12. Place narrow bandpass filter (transmittance peak at 1150 nm or 1412 nm) between fiber light guide and cell
13. Place wider bandpass filter around chosen wavelength between halogen lamp fiber light guide and narrow bandpass filter
14. Mount iris diaphragm between narrow bandpass filter and cell holder
15. Set up Near-IR camera in line with sample cell
16. Fix object-space telecentric lens between cell and camera
17. Turn on Near-IR camera
18. Turn on Near-IR camera
19. Ensure camera is connected to computer
20. Open image acquisition software
21. Turn on halogen lamp
22. Check image displayed on monitor
23. Adjust lamp output power as needed
24. Adjust axis, position, and focus of telecentric lens

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Combine chloroform solutions of desired lipids in clean glass test tube
2. Evaporate solvent with dry nitrogen gas while rotating tube to form thin lipid film
3. Remove residual solvent using centrifugal evaporator for one hour at 37°C
4. Add pre-heated buffer A to achieve final lipid concentration of 1-2 mM
5. Incubate for 30 minutes at 37°C with occasional vortexing to resuspend lipid mixture
6. Transfer incubated lipid mixture to plastic test tube
7. Place test tube in liquid nitrogen for 30 seconds until completely frozen
8. Transfer test tube to 37°C waterbath for 1-2 minutes until fully thawed
9. Soak four filter supports and polycarbonate filter in buffer A
10. Assemble lipid extruder according to manufacturer instructions
11. Extrude lipid solution 21 times through filter using gentle constant pressure
12. Store extruded liposomes at 4°C
13. Incubate His-tagged Mff with scaffold liposomes in buffer A and BME for at least 15 minutes at room temperature
14. Incubate liposomes with His-tagged GFP control protein to shield exposed Nickel-NTA
15. Add Drp1 to the liposome mixture
16. Incubate Drp1-liposome mixture for one hour at room temperature
17. Transfer 5 microliters of sample to laboratory film
18. Place carbon-coated copper-rhodium grid onto sample
19. Incubate sample for 1 minute
20. Remove excess liquid using filter paper
21. Add drop of 2% uranyl acetate stain
22. Incubate sample for 1 minute
23. Remove excess stain using filter paper
24. Transfer sample to grid box
25. Incubate His-tagged Mff and Fis1 with scaffold liposomes for 15 minutes at room temperature in buffer A and BME
26. Add Drp1 to mixture
27. Incubate mixture with Drp1 for 15 minutes at room temperature
28. Transfer tubes to thermocycler
29. Set thermocycler to 37 degrees Celsius
30. Initiate reactions by adding GTP and magnesium chloride
31. Transfer 20 microliters of reaction mixture to microtiter plate wells
32. Add five microliters of 0.5 molar EDTA to wells to chelate magnesium and stop reaction
33. Prepare phosphate standards by diluting potassium phosphate in buffer A and BME
34. Add 20 microliters of each phosphate standard to wells containing 5 microliters of 0.5 M EDTA
35. Add 150 microliters of malachite green reagent to each well
36. Read OD 650 after 5 minutes incubation (reagent must be added within 10 minutes of measurement)

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Filter water sample through sterile muslin cloth to remove particulate matter
2. Prepare serial dilutions of filtered water sample
3. Plate 100 microliters of appropriate dilutions on solidified R2A agar plates in duplicates
4. Spread samples evenly on R2A agar plates
5. Incubate sample spread plates at 35-37°C for 48 hours
6. Count colonies on plates after incubation
7. Calculate total bacterial load in CFU/mL
8. Add antibiotics separately into tubes containing 20 milliliters of sterile molten R2A agar modified at 40 degrees Celsius
9. Swirl tubes to mix evenly and pour mixture onto sterile Petri plates before agar solidifies
10. Spread 100 microliters of bacterial suspension onto respective antibiotic-containing R2A agar modified plates
11. Incubate plates at 35 to 37 degrees Celsius for 48 hours
12. Use sterile toothpick to take single isolated pure colony from Petri plate
13. Suspend bacterial colony in 100 microliters of sterile double distilled water in microcentrifuge tube
14. Boil suspension in boiling water bath or dry bath for 10 minutes at 100°C
15. Centrifuge suspension at 10,000 g for two minutes to pellet debris
16. Transfer supernatant to fresh sterile microcentrifuge tube
17. Prepare 40 microliters of PCR reaction mixture in a PCR tube
18. Place PCR tube in thermal block
19. Run PCR program in thermal cycler
20. Mix 10 microliters of amplified PCR product with 2 microliters of 6x gel loading buffer and load mixture into wells of 1.5% agarose gel alongside 100 bp DNA ladder
21. Perform electrophoresis in TAE buffer at 80-100 volts until tracking dye migrates 3/4 of gel length
22. Visualize amplicon bands under UV transilluminator
23. Aseptically inoculate a single isolated purified antibiotic resistance colony using sterile loop into 2 ml of non-antibiotic LB broth
24. Incubate broth culture at 37°C with 80 rpm shaking overnight
25. Add 100-150 microliters of overnight culture to 2 ml fresh non-selective LB broth
26. Incubate subculture for 2-4 hours until OD₆₀₀ reaches 0.4-0.5
27. Dilute freshly grown culture suspension using sterile 0.85% saline solution
28. Mix gently to distribute cells evenly until reaching 0.5 McFarland standard density
29. Dip sterile cotton swab into inoculum
30. Remove excess suspension from swab to avoid over inoculation
31. Spread culture evenly on Mueller Hinton agar plate in back-and-forth motion from edge to edge
32. Rotate plate by 60 degrees
33. Swab plate again after rotation
34. Use flame-sterilized forceps to aseptically transfer antibiotic discs onto inoculated agar plate
35. Press discs gently to ensure complete contact with agar
36. Place appropriate number of antibiotic discs considering organism, antibiotic, and plate size to avoid zone overlap
37. Invert plates within 15 minutes of disc application
38. Incubate inverted plates at 37°C overnight

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Measure 150 milliliters of acetone using graduated cylinder
2. Transfer measured acetone to 250 milliliter Erlenmeyer flask
3. Add 30 grams of commercial gliadin powder to flask while stirring
4. Seal flask opening with aluminum foil
5. Stir solution continuously at room temperature overnight
6. Filter solution through filter paper
7. Wash retentate with approximately 50 milliliters of fresh acetone
8. Transfer retentate and filter paper to square cell culture dish
9. Cover dish with large filter paper to slow acetone evaporation
10. Transfer dried defatted gliadin to airtight container
11. Store container on lab bench
12. Measure 150 milliliters of deionized water using a graduated cylinder
13. Transfer measured deionized water to a 1,000 milliliter Erlenmeyer flask
14. Measure 350 milliliters of absolute ethanol using a mess cylinder
15. Add measured absolute ethanol to the Erlenmeyer flask containing deionized water
16. Stir solution vigorously until air bubbles are no longer observed
17. Add 20 grams of defatted gliadin powder to the stirring solution
18. Transfer entire solution to a 1,000 milliliter mess cylinder
19. Allow solution to sit for two days
20. Transfer supernatant to round-bottomed flask using flexible tubing
21. Remove ethanol from supernatant via rotary evaporation
22. Freeze solution containing gliadin aggregates by immersing flask in methanol dry ice mixture
23. Freeze-dry solution at -70 degrees Celsius under vacuum
24. Crush freeze-dried gliadin with mortar and pestle
25. Grind crushed gliadin with coffee grinder to obtain fine powder
26. Transfer powder into airtight container
27. Store powder at room temperature
28. Add 3.2 grams of distilled water and 6.8 grams of absolute ethanol to a tared scintillation vial
29. Stir solution vigorously until air bubbles are no longer observed
30. Add 40 microliters of four normal hydrochloric acid to vial while stirring
31. Add 20 milligrams of purified gliadin powder
32. Continue stirring until powder is fully dissolved
33. Slowly add 80-100 microliters of ethyl cyanoacrylate monomer while stirring
34. Reduce stirring speed to 500 rpm
35. Continue stirring solution at 500 rpm for one hour
36. Transfer solution into centrifuge tube
37. Balance weight of centrifuge tube
38. Centrifuge sample at 10,000 × g for 20 minutes
39. Transfer nanoparticle suspension to scintillation vial using pipette
40. Store nanoparticle suspension at room temperature
41. Prepare 68 weight percent aqueous ethanol solution in 20 milliliter scintillation vial
42. Stir solution until no air bubbles are observed
43. Add 40 microliters of 4N hydrochloric acid to solution while stirring
44. Add 50 microliters of nanoparticle suspension to solution while stirring
45. Measure nanoparticle size using dynamic light scattering following manufacturer's instructions
46. Wash glass plate surface with soap water
47. Spray nanoparticle suspension on half of glass plate
48. Rinse surface with water
49. Spray water on entire glass plate surface
50. Observe wettability difference between coated and uncoated surfaces

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Inoculate S. cerevisiae cells
2. Grow S. cerevisiae overnight at 30°C with 150 RPM agitation
3. Measure optical density at 600 nm (OD600)
4. Dilute S. cerevisiae culture to OD600 0.1 in 100 ml YPD medium
5. Grow S. cerevisiae until OD600 reaches 0.8
6. Measure OD600 of S. pombe overnight culture
7. Dilute S. pombe culture to OD600 0.1 in 500 ml YAS medium and grow until OD600 reaches 0.8
8. Prepare fresh solution of 2M four-thiouracil
9. Keep prepared solution at room temperature

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Place Nematostella vectensis anemones in temperature- and light-controlled incubator
2. Program incubator for 8 hours of light at 25°C
3. Remove animals from incubator
4. Transfer animals to bench top at room temperature with light on for spawning
5. Use modified transfer pipette to place egg packages from female container into sperm-containing male container
6. Leave egg packages in male container for at least 15 minutes to allow fertilization
7. Place fertilized egg packages in seawater containing 3% cysteine in glass Petri dish
8. Gently agitate dish on shaker for 12 minutes
9. Break up clumps with plastic pipette
10. Continue agitation for 2-3 minutes until completely dejellied
11. Store fertilized eggs in dish at 16°C or room temperature
12. Prepare DNA extraction buffer according to manuscript
13. Vortex DNA extraction buffer to mix well
14. Aliquot 20 microliters of DNA extraction buffer into each PCR tube
15. Dissolve 1% agarose in seawater
16. Pour dissolved agarose into Petri dish to cover bottom
17. Cool agarose on bench top to solidify gel bed
18. Cover solidified gel bed with fresh seawater
19. Transfer 24-hour post-fertilization embryos using pipette into agarose gel bed-containing Petri dish
20. Insert tungsten needle into needle holder
21. Sterilize needle tip by dipping in alcohol
22. Place needle in flame to burn off alcohol
23. Make depression in agarose using tungsten needle under dissecting microscope
24. Remove piece of surface agarose approximately embryo-sized
25. Place embryo into depression using tungsten needle
26. Position embryo with lateral side facing down to restrict movement
27. Surgically remove piece of aboral tissue along oral-aboral axis using tungsten needle
28. Transfer isolated aboral tissue to PCR tube containing 20 microliters of DNA extraction buffer using P20 pipette
29. Transfer post-surgery embryo into well of 24-well plate containing at least 500 microliters of fresh seawater
30. Place plate containing post-surgery embryos in incubator at 16 degrees Celsius
31. Centrifuge PCR tubes containing DNA extraction buffer and embryonic tissues using mini centrifuge
32. Incubate tubes at 55°C for three hours with periodic vortexing (30 seconds every 30 minutes)
33. Inactivate proteinase K by heating at 95°C for four minutes
34. Set up PCR reaction using extracted genomic DNA
35. Set up PCR reaction using extracted genomic DNA as template to amplify locus of interest
36. Set up 20 microliter PCR reaction
37. Run agarose gel electrophoresis to determine size and presence/absence of PCR products
38. Adjust conditions of agarose gel electrophoresis based on expected PCR product size
39. Assign genotype to each post-surgical embryo based on PCR product size and presence/absence
40. Sort embryos according to assigned genotype
41. Perform control PCR using reverse primer binding to wild type sequence

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Collect 500 milliliters of water sample in five sterile glass bottles from different water body sites
2. Measure pH and temperature of each water sample
3. Filter sample in 100-milliliter batches through 0.22-micron pore size filter sheets under aseptic conditions
4. Place filter sheets over nutrient media plates (one sheet per plate)
5. Peel filter sheets off plates using sterile forceps after two-hour incubation
6. Add 100 microliters of unfiltered water sample to 900 microliters of sterile double distilled water
7. Repeat tenfold serial dilution process
8. Continue dilutions until reaching 1:1,000,000 dilution ratio
9. Mix dilution by pipetting
10. Verify final volume of each dilution is 1 milliliter
11. Spread 100 microliters of diluted samples on growth media plates in triplicates
12. Incubate plates at 30°C for 24-48 hours
13. Pick a colony using sterile toothpick or pipette tip
14. Perform quadrant streaking
15. Incubate plate overnight
16. Screen colonies based on morphological features (color, texture, shape, size, margin, elevation)
17. Re-streak colonies to obtain pure cultures
18. Perform gram staining of each pure culture
19. Inoculate single colony in three milliliters of appropriate growth media
20. Incubate culture at 30 degrees Celsius
21. Add 700 microliters of overnight culture and 300 microliters of 100% glycerol to cryo vials
22. Freeze vials at minus 80 degrees Celsius for long-term storage
23. Pick a colony from freshly-streaked plate
24. Inoculate colony in 5 ml of tryptic soy broth or nutrient broth
25. Grow culture overnight at 30°C with shaking at 200 RPM until absorbance reaches ~2
26. Pellet cells by centrifugation
27. Discard supernatant and wash pellet twice with 2 ml autoclaved saline
28. Spin cells again by centrifugation
29. Resuspend pellet in 2 ml liquid carbon-free basal medium (LCFBM)
30. Measure absorbance of resuspended cells
31. Label two sterile 150-mL Erlenmeyer flasks as A (control) and B (experimental)
32. Add 40 mL LCFBM and styrene at 5 mM final concentration to flask A
33. Add 35 mL LCFBM and styrene to flask B
34. Add cell suspension to flask B to achieve ~0.1 absorbance
35. Adjust volume in flask B to 40 mL with LCFBM
36. Incubate both flasks at 30°C with 200 RPM shaking for 30 days
37. Pick a single bacterial colony
38. Inoculate colony in fresh growth medium within sterile test tube
39. Pellet 1.5 milliliters of grown culture via centrifugation
40. Remove supernatant from pelleted culture
41. Resuspend pellet in 200 microliters of lysis buffer
42. Add 66 microliters of five-molar sodium chloride solution
43. Mix solution thoroughly
44. Centrifuge resulting mixture
45. Pipette clear supernatant into fresh microcentrifuge tube
46. Add equal volume of chloroform to supernatant
47. Mix solution by inverting tube multiple times
48. Observe mixture until milky solution forms
49. Centrifuge tube again
50. Transfer supernatant to clean vial
51. Add 1 mL of ice cold 100% ethanol
52. Mix by inversion until white DNA strands precipitate
53. Centrifuge precipitated DNA
54. Discard supernatant
55. Wash pellet with 1 mL of 70% ethanol
56. Discard wash supernatant
57. Dry DNA pellet for 5 minutes at room temperature
58. Resuspend pellet in 100 μL of Tris-EDTA buffer
59. Measure DNA concentration using spectrophotometer
60. Run DNA on 1% agarose gel to assess quality
61. Prepare 25 microliters of PCR mix on ice
62. Add one microliter of DNA template to PCR mix
63. Set PCR cycling conditions for gene amplification
64. Mix five microliters of PCR sample with one microliter of 5X DNA loading dye
65. Run sample on 1% agarose gel to verify amplification
66. Set up 16S rRNA gene sequencing reaction in higher volume
67. Mix entire sample with DNA loading dye
68. Load sample on agarose gel
69. Perform gel extraction method to purify amplicons
70. Convert sequencing results file into FASTA format
71. Check sequence similarity using BLAST tool on NCBI
72. Visualize small DNA sample on agarose gel to confirm genomic DNA integrity
73. Amplify 16S ribosomal rRNA gene using universal primers

Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Add 3 grams of sediment sample to pre-weighed sterile 50 milliliter tubes using ethanol-flamed metal scoops under UV-sterilized biosafety cabinet
2. Add 15 milliliters of sterile filtered 1% sodium hexametaphosphate (SHMP) solution to sample using sterile graduated burette
3. Homogenize sample at 17,500 RPM for one minute using liquid disperser/homogenizer with sterile dispersion rotor
4. Let sample rest for two minutes
5. Repeat homogenization process
6. Allow sample to rest for 10 minutes for particle sedimentation
7. Transfer supernatant containing cell biomass to clean 50 ml tube without disturbing pellet
8. Add 15 ml aliquot of SHMP to sedimented pellet
9. Repeat homogenization of pellet with SHMP
10. Collect supernatant from homogenized sample
11. Combine collected supernatant with first supernatant aliquot
12. Centrifuge combined sample at 20 × g for 1 minute
13. Transfer supernatant to fresh 50 ml tube
14. Discard pellet after supernatant transfer
15. Sterilize forceps using ethanol flame
16. Add sterile nitrocellulose membrane to filtration unit using sterilized forceps
17. Add half of supernatant sample onto membrane in filtration unit
18. Apply vacuum pump to filtration unit to collect cells on membrane
19. Stop vacuum pump after liquid fully passes through filter
20. Remove membrane from filtration unit using sterilized forceps
21. Place membrane into sterile petri dish
22. Repeat filtration process with fresh sterile membrane and remaining half of solution
23. Thaw membrane if frozen
24. Cut membrane into smaller pieces using ethanol-flamed sterilized scissors
25. Transfer membrane pieces into sterile 2 ml tube
26. Add 900 microliters of 1X TE buffer to tube
27. Vortex tube thoroughly to release biomass from membrane
28. Incubate tube at 65°C and 80 RPM for 10 minutes
29. Remove tube from incubator and cool for 15 minutes
30. Add 100 microliters of freshly prepared lysozyme (to final 2 mg/ml concentration)
31. Incubate sample at 37°C and 80 RPM for 60 minutes to lyse vegetative cells
32. Add 250 microliters of 3 normal sodium hydroxide and 250 microliters of 6% SDS solution to sample
33. Incubate sample at room temperature and 80 RPM for 60 minutes
34. Prepare sterile filtration unit holding 25 millimeter diameter membranes by autoclaving
35. Place 25 millimeter diameter 0.2 micrometer nitrocellulose membrane onto cooled filtration unit
36. Add sample onto membrane
37. Apply vacuum pump to filter liquid and capture endospores on membrane
38. Add 2 milliliters of sterile physiological solution to membrane
39. Apply vacuum pump to filter wash solution
40. Turn off vacuum pump
41. Leave membrane on filtration unit
42. Add 450 microliters of sterile water onto membrane
43. Add 50 microliters of 1X DNase reaction buffer onto membrane
44. Add 0.5 microliters of DNase enzyme onto membrane
45. Incubate membrane at 25°C for 15 minutes
46. Add 1 milliliter of physiological solution
47. Turn on pump to wash residual enzyme from sample
48. Transfer membrane containing endospores into sterile petri dish using sterile forceps

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Prepare incubation chamber
2. Place incubation chamber on microscope stage
3. Replace rich medium with starvation medium
4. Induce autophagy response
5. Select appropriate cells for imaging
6. Set video microscopy parameters
7. Seed low-passage HEC293T cells (stably expressing GFP-DFCP1) on 22 mm round cover slips in DMEM medium
8. Incubate seeded cover slips overnight at 37°C with 5% CO₂ to achieve 30-40% confluency
9. Prepare transfection complex mix containing Opti-MEM I, reduced serum medium, DNA, transfection reagent, and PECFP-LC3 plasmid DNA
10. Mix transfection complex gently by pipetting up and down
11. Incubate transfection complex for 15 minutes at room temperature
12. Aspirate medium from cell plates
13. Add fresh DMEM prewarmed to 37°C to cell plates
14. Add transfection complex to cells
15. Incubate cells at 37°C with 5% CO₂ for 24 hours
16. Remove two milliliter aliquot of lyso tracker containing medium
17. Warm aliquot to 37 degrees Celsius
18. Select red lyso tracker for lysosome marking
19. Aspirate medium from transfected cells
20. Replace with lyso tracker containing medium
21. Incubate cells for 30 to 60 minutes
22. Clean metal and plastic O-rings with 75% ethanol
23. Apply silicone grease on the rim of the metal O-ring
24. Remove cover slip from plate using forceps and dry excess medium from bottom side
25. Place cover slip to rest on ledge of plastic O-ring
26. Fit metal O-ring on top of plastic O-ring with cover slip sandwiched between
27. Top up chamber with medium from plate to create closed chamber
28. Select cells exhibiting increased omega zone production
29. Use high magnification lens for photobleaching prevention
30. Adjust excitation light intensity to 10-20% of maximum
31. Set camera to 100-500ms exposure, 2x2 spinning, and 100 gain
32. Set image acquisition rate to 1 frame per 10 seconds
33. Initiate video capture after 30 minutes or during established autophagy response

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Prepare 15 milliliters of basic culture medium
2. Supplement basic culture medium according to manuscript directions
3. Prepare three milliliters of holding medium
4. Add five micromolar cilostamide to holding medium
5. Pour holding medium into 35 millimeter Petri dish
6. Prepare long IVCO medium by supplementing basic culture medium according to manuscript directions
7. Add 200 microliters of long IVCO medium to each well of 96-well coated plate
8. Fill four edges of plate with sterile water to compensate for evaporation
9. Incubate 96-well plate and holding medium at 38.5°C and 5% CO₂ with maximum humidity
10. Wash ovaries four times in sterile saline maintained at 26 degrees Celsius
11. Aspirate follicles >2mm diameter using 18-gauge needle connected to aspiration pump
12. Place aspirated ovaries in beaker with sterile saline maintained at 26 degrees Celsius
13. Place one ovary on sterile PTFE cutting board and cut 1.5-2mm thick slices of ovarian cortex parallel to major axis using surgical #22 blade
14. Place slices of ovarian cortex in sterile glass Petri dish with dissecting medium
15. Position dish on warm plate at 38.5°C
16. Transfer one ovarian cortex slice to 60mm glass Petri dish
17. Add 2-3 mL of M199D medium to dish
18. Position dish under dissection microscope
19. Select follicles measuring 0.5-2 mm in diameter
20. Identify healthy non-atretic follicles using morphological parameters (translucent appearance, vascularization, regular granulosa layer)
21. Discard atretic follicles
22. Use surgical blade to remove ovarian tissue surrounding follicle
23. Continue tissue removal until follicle is exposed
24. Use 26 gauge needle to make slit in exposed follicle wall
25. Release follicular content containing COC through slit
26. Identify COC and examine for cumulus integrity
27. Examine COC for zona pellucida integrity and cytoplasm homogeneity
28. Aspirate COC with P20 pipette if integrity criteria are fulfilled
29. Place isolated COC in M199D cilostamide medium
30. Continue isolation procedure for 30 minutes
31. Prepare 16 drops of 20 microliters M199D cilostamide in a 60 millimeter Petri dish
32. Place one healthy COC into each prepared drop
33. Use inverted microscope with camera to measure oocyte diameter (excluding zona pellucida)
34. Make two perpendicular measurements of oocyte diameter under clear visualization
35. Verify mean of two measurements is within 100-110 micrometers range
36. Transfer selected COCs to 35 millimeter dish containing M199H medium
37. Transfer one COC into center of each well of pre-prepared 96-well plate
38. Incubate plate for five days at 38.5°C and 5% CO₂ with maximum humidity
39. Replace 100 microliters of old medium with 100 microliters of fresh long IVCO medium
40. Perform medium replacement under stereo microscope to avoid disturbing COCs

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Grow HTT11619 cells in 25 ml of prepared medium in T-175 flask
2. Aspirate culture medium from T-175 flask
3. Wash cells with PBS without calcium and magnesium
4. Aspirate PBS from T-175 flask
5. Add trypsin drop-wise to T-175 flask using 5 ml pipette
6. Incubate flask to allow cell detachment
7. Tap flask to dislodge cells
8. Quench cells with 8 ml complete medium over flask surface
9. Pipette mixture up and down to break cell clumps
10. Transfer cell mixture to 15 ml conical tube
11. Take 10 microliters from 15 milliliter conical tube
12. Combine sample with 10 microliters of trypan blue
13. Transfer 10 microliters of cell-trypan blue mixture to hemocytometer
14. Count cells in four outer grids of hemocytometer
15. Add 5 milliliters of complete medium and 6 micromolar aphidicolin to each 10-centimeter plate of cells
16. Transfer 100 microliters of re-suspended cell pellet to each centimeter plate
17. Swirl plate gently to mix
18. Incubate plates to synchronize cells at G1/S border
19. Mix RNA components to 45 micromolar concentration in equal molar concentrations
20. Add 6.75 microliters of 200 micromolar CR RNA stock to 1.5 mL centrifuge tube
21. Add 6.75 microliters of 200 micromolar tracer RNA stock to centrifuge tube
22. Add 16.50 microliters of IDT buffer to centrifuge tube
23. Heat mixture at 95°C for 5 minutes in PCR machine
24. Cool samples to room temperature
25. Dilute 2.22 microliters of CR RNA complex and 2.78 microliters of IDT buffer to final volume of 5 microliters
26. Dilute 1.67 microliters of Cas9 protein (60 micromolar stock) in 3.33 microliters of low sera medium to final volume of 5 microliters
27. Mix 5 microliters of Cas9 protein with 5 microliters of complex RNA
28. Aspirate medium from plate
29. Wash plate with 5 ml of PBS
30. Aspirate PBS from plate
31. Add 1 ml pre-warmed trypsin to each 10 cm plate
32. Place plates in incubator
33. Tap 10 cm plate to dislodge cells
34. Quench cells with 4 ml complete medium
35. Pipette mixture up/down to break cell clumps
36. Transfer cells to 15 ml conical tube
37. Transfer 100 microliters of cell suspension to each 4mm gap electroporation cuvette
38. Add 10 microliters of RNP complex to each cuvette containing cells
39. Add 2 micromolar ODN to each sample
40. Transport sample rack to electroporation machine
41. Flick each sample gently to mix
42. Place samples in electroporation chamber
43. Return rack to biosafety hood
44. Transfer each electroporated sample to well containing 2ml complete medium in 6-well plate
45. Incubate plate
46. Electroporate synchronized HTT11619 cells (5 × 10⁵ cells/100μL) with RNP complex (100 pmol) and 72 NTODN (2.0 μM)
47. Transfer electroporated cells to six-well plate
48. Allow cells to recover for 72 hours
49. Sort individual cells into 96-well plates using FACS with 488 nm laser (EGFP+/- selection)
50. Isolate cellular gDNA from wells showing growth using commercial DNA isolation kit
51. Amplify target base regions via PCR
52. Perform DNA sequencing analysis on PCR-amplified samples

18. Immerse slides in 2.5 M hydrochloric acid for 80 minutes
19. Wash slides three times in PBS for five minutes with dabbing
20. Collect excess PBS onto paper towel
21. Orient slides horizontally
22. Pipette 5% BSA in PBS solution onto each slide
23. Place cover slip on each slide
24. Incubate slides for 20 minutes
25. Gently move cover slip down glass slide
26. Remove excess BSA using paper towel
27. Pipette 50 microliters anti-BRDU primary antibody solution onto each slide
28. Place cover slip on each slide
29. Incubate slides in humidified chamber for 2 hours
30. Remove cover slips from slides
31. Wash slides three times in PBS (5 minutes per wash)
32. Apply 50 microliters of secondary antibody solution to slides
33. Position cover slips on slides
34. Protect slides from light
35. Incubate slides for 1 hour
36. Remove cover slips from slides
37. Wash slides three times in PBS (5 minutes per wash)
38. Add drop of VectorShield mounting medium to each slide
39. Gently press cover slip onto slide
40. Remove excess fluid around cover slip
41. Seal cover slips with transparent nail polish
42. Air dry sealed slides
43. Place drop of immersion oil onto slide near pencil mark
44. Start locating DNA fibers using immersion oil
45. Move microscope away from main fiber bundle to find areas with clearly separated fibers
46. Take approximately 10 pictures of each sample using one color channel to avoid bias
47. Move along slide to capture images from different areas for representative sampling

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Lay surgical drape
2. Arrange instruments for gross dissection
3. Set up bio safety cabinet for eye visualization on dissecting microscope
4. Set up bio safety cabinet for dish assembly
5. Set up open globe injury induction workspace inside bio safety cabinet
6. Position cross tracking vice on lab jack in front of puncture platform
7. Wash eyes with antibiotic antimycotic PBS
8. Place eye on antibiotic antimycotic PBS-soaked gauze
9. Create incision near eye equator using sterile razorblade or scalpel
10. Hemisect eye using curved surgical scissors to isolate anterior segment
11. Scoop vitreous humor from anterior segment using micro scissors as shovel
12. Remove lens from anterior segment using micro scissors
13. Cut iris radially to iris root until trabecular meshwork is visible
14. Extend cut 360 degrees around iris to expose entire trabecular meshwork region
15. Clean residual iris covering trabecular meshwork if necessary
16. Trim ciliary body remnants posterior to trabecular meshwork region
17. Leave one millimeter thin band of tissue
18. Wet cotton swab in media and gently dab center of cornea to remove pigment
19. Hold eyes with forceps and wipe extra pigment around sclera
20. Place inverted anterior segment over elevated region of bottom dish with cornea centered
21. Position clamping ring on top of anterior segment
22. Tighten screws gently with L-key
23. Attach both fluidic connectors with O-rings to threaded ports on dish bottom

25. Attach length of tubing to first fluidic connector
26. Attach 18 gauge needle hub to first fluidic connector
27. Attach nylon syringe filter to first fluidic connector
28. Attach three-way valve to first fluidic connector
29. Attach 20 millimeter media-filled syringe to first fluidic connector

31. Keep three-way valves open to syringes
32. Gently push media through system via first fluidics connector port
33. Remove bubbles by gently pushing media into dish
34. Invert dish to push out bubbles
35. Place anterior segment organ culture dish into cell culture incubator
36. Direct tubing lines out through bottom of incubator door
37. Position tubing lines with reservoir at pressure transducer instrument
38. Connect side three-way valve to pressure transducer setup while PBS flows through line
39. Set pressure regulator on puncture platform to 50 PSI
40. Position cross-tracking vice on lab jack in front of puncture platform
41. Extend piston arm to maximal distance
42. Position corneal apex within 1 mm region of puncturing object
43. Retract piston arm
44. Bring anterior segment 1 cm closer to puncturing object
45. Turn on puncture device
46. Open solenoid valve with second switch
47. Press second switch to retract device from eye
48. Verify injury induction via visual inspection and media leakage from injury site
49. Assess corneal epithelium integrity using optical coherence tomography
50. Image injured anterior segment tissue immediately after injury
51. Image injured anterior segment tissue 72 hours post-injury

4. Mince tumor into small pieces in Petri dish
5. Transfer dissected tumor to 1.5 ml tube with 300 microliters NSC medium
6. Gently homogenize tumor using disposable syringe
7. Add 1 milliliter of cell detachment medium
8. Incubate sample for 5 minutes at 37°C
9. Pass cell suspension through 70 micron cell strainer into 50 milliliter centrifuge tube
10. Wash strainer with 25 milliliters of NSC medium
11. Centrifuge suspension at 300 × g for 5 minutes at room temperature
12. Resuspend cell pellet in 6 milliliters of NSC medium
13. Transfer tumor cell suspension into T25 culture flask
14. Culture cells at 37°C in tissue culture incubator with 95% air and 5% CO₂ atmosphere until neurospheres form
15. Centrifuge 1×10⁶ neurosphere cells at 300×g for 5 minutes
16. Decant supernatant after centrifugation
17. Resuspend neurosphere pellet in 1 ml balanced salt solution
18. Transfer suspension to low protein binding 1.5 ml microcentrifuge tubes
19. Centrifuge suspension as before (300×g for 5 minutes)
20. Decant supernatant from cell pellet
21. Re-suspend cell pellet in 95 microliters of digestion buffer
22. Supplement digestion buffer with protease inhibitor cocktail (1:100 ratio per 1×10⁶ cells)
23. Pipette cell suspension up and down to prevent clumping and avoid bubble formation
24. Prepare diluted micrococcal nuclease by mixing 5 microliters of 0.1X nuclease with 145 microliters of digestion buffer
25. Add 5 microliters of diluted micrococcal nuclease per 1×10⁶ cells
26. Flick tube to mix contents
27. Incubate tube in 37°C heat block for exactly 12 minutes
28. Add 10 microliters of 10X micrococcal nuclease stop buffer per 1×10⁶ cells
29. Place samples on ice for subsequent steps
30. Add 110 microliters of 2X RIPA buffer supplemented 1:100 with protease inhibitor cocktail per 1×10⁶ cells
31. Flick tube to mix samples
32. Centrifuge samples for 15 minutes at 4°C and 1700×g
33. Transfer chromatin-containing supernatant to 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tube
34. Place transferred supernatant on ice
35. Dilute chromatin with RIPA buffer supplemented with protease inhibitors at 1:1000 ratio
36. Adjust diluted chromatin to 100-200 microliters per IP reaction
37. Remove aliquot equivalent to 10% of ChIP volume as input sample
38. Add 100 microliters of TE buffer supplemented with proteinase K to input sample
39. Incubate input sample at 55 degrees Celsius for one hour
40. Purify input sample using PCR purification kit
41. Measure concentration of purified input using micro volume spectrophotometer
42. Record concentration results in notebook
43. Add 10 microliters of washed protein A/G magnetic beads per IP sample
44. Incubate samples for one hour at 4°C with rotation at 20 RPM
45. Place samples on magnetic holder for bead separation
46. Transfer required volume to new 1.5 milliliter tubes
47. Add desired antibody to each tube
48. Wrap tube caps with plastic paraffin film to prevent evaporation
49. Incubate samples overnight at 4°C with rotation at 20 RPM
50. Spin tubes at 2000×g for 10 seconds
51. Add 10 microliters of beads to each IP
52. Incubate samples for 3 hours at 4°C with rotation at 20 RPM
53. Place samples on magnetic stand for bead separation
54. Remove supernatant using pipette
55. Add 150 microliters of RIPA buffer with protease inhibitors to each IP
56. Incubate samples at 4°C at 20 RPM for 5 minutes
57. Place samples on magnetic stand
58. Allow magnetic beads to separate
59. Remove supernatant using pipette
60. Add 150 microliters of lithium chloride buffer with low-concentration protease inhibitors to each IP
61. Incubate at 4°C with rotation at 20 RPM for five minutes
62. Place samples on magnetic stand
63. Allow magnetic beads to separate
64. Remove supernatant using pipette
65. Add 150 microliters of cold TE without protease inhibitors to beads
66. Incubate at 4 degrees Celsius with rotation at 20 RPM for five minutes
67. Re-suspend sample in 100 microliters of TE buffer supplemented with 0.5 milligrams per milliliter proteinase K
68. Incubate at 55 degrees Celsius for one hour
69. Purify amino precipitated DNA using PCR purification kit
70. Perform qPCR with primers targeted to positive and negative control regions

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Make incision in abdominal skin using surgical scissors and tweezers
2. Cut muscle and peritoneal membrane from bladder to left lower rib edge
3. Moisten two cotton swabs with PBS
4. Pull intestines aside using moistened cotton swabs
5. Place moistened swabs to identify left kidney and ureter
6. Close peritoneal membrane with 4-0 nylon suture
7. Close abdominal incision with 4-0 nylon suture
8. Make abdominal incision and cut muscle/peritoneal membrane for UUO
9. Place 2.5-ml syringe underneath mouse
10. Moisten two cotton swabs with PBS for UUO
11. Pull intestines aside using tweezers and moistened swabs
12. Place swabs to identify left ureter
13. Lift peritoneal membrane with tweezers and make sideways incision at upper edge using surgical scissors
14. Continue incision along lowest edge of ribs using surgical scissors
15. Identify left kidney
16. Reflux kidney with PBS until it turns yellow-white
17. Cut left renal artery and vein with surgical scissors to remove kidney
18. Place kidney in Petri dish
19. Wash kidney carefully with PBS
20. Cut kidney into approximately 10-mg samples using surgical scissors and tweezers
21. Transfer each kidney sample to individual 1.5-ml microcentrifuge tube
22. Close microcentrifuge tube cap
23. Place kidney sample in silicone homogenizer
24. Add 700 microliters of phenyl/guanidine-based lysis reagent to homogenizer
25. Homogenize sample by slowly pressing and twisting pestle against kidney tissue
26. Transfer homogenized lysate to biopolymer spin column
27. Centrifuge spin column at 14,000 × g for 3 minutes
28. Transfer precipitated lysate to unused 1.5-ml microcentrifuge tube
29. Add 140 microliters of chloroform to lysate and cap tube tightly
30. Invert tube 15 times to mix lysate and chloroform
31. Incubate mixture at room temperature for 2-3 minutes
32. Centrifuge samples at 12,000 × g for 15 minutes at 4°C
33. Transfer supernatant to new 1.5-milliliter microcentrifuge tube without disturbing precipitate
34. Add 1.5 times volume of 100% ethanol to supernatant
35. Vortex mixture for five seconds
36. Load 700 microliters of sample onto membrane-anchored spin column in two-milliliter collection tube
37. Close column cap and spin at 15,000 × g for 15 seconds
38. Discard precipitated lysate in collection tube
39. Add 700 microliters of wash buffer one to membrane-anchored spin column in two-milliliter collection tube
40. Centrifuge sample
41. Discard used collection tube
42. Repeat wash twice with 500 microliters of wash buffer two per wash
43. Centrifuge membrane-anchored spin column in two-milliliter collection tube at 15,000 × g for one minute
44. Discard precipitated lysate
45. Transfer membrane-anchored spin column to new 1.5-milliliter tube
46. Add 30 microliters of RNase-free water to column to dissolve total RNA
47. Close column cap
48. Incubate column for five minutes at room temperature
49. Centrifuge column at 15,000 × g for one minute
50. Transfer total RNA sample to new microcentrifuge tube on ice
51. Measure concentration of total RNA by spectrophotometry
52. Prepare master mix solution according to manuscript directions
53. Add 8 microliters of master mix to each tube of eight-well tube strip
54. Place 12-microliter aliquot of adjusted total RNA into each tube and close caps
55. Centrifuge tubes for 15 seconds
56. Place tubes in thermocycler and incubate for 60 minutes at 37°C
57. Incubate tubes for 5 minutes at 95°C for cDNA synthesis
58. Transfer cDNA to new 1.5-milliliter microcentrifuge tube and dilute 10 times
59. Create unilateral urethral obstruction mouse model via left ureteral ligation
60. Completely obstruct ureters using double ligation with 4-0 silk sutures
61. Collect kidneys at eight days post-surgery

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Thoroughly wash two LCMs with deionized water
2. Clean LCMs in ultrasound bath with deionized water
3. Dry LCMs in oven at 80 degrees Celsius for several minutes
4. Clean LCM holder, O-rings, and sample chamber with acetone and compressed air
5. Place both clean, dry LCMs on clean, dry LCM holder
6. Designate one LCM as reference position and one as sample position
7. Connect LCM holder to oscillator using high temperature resistant electric cables
8. Turn on oscillator
9. Check for presence of resonance frequencies for both LCMs using oscillator software
10. Close and insulate the sample chamber
11. Evacuate the sample chamber
12. Purge chamber with nitrogen gas multiple times
13. Set chamber pressure to lowest test pressure
14. Set chamber temperature to lowest test temperature
15. Measure resonance frequencies of both LCMs using Butterworth Van Dyke equivalent circuit model
16. Determine and record F-0 value
17. Slowly add nitrogen gas to pressurize sample chamber to next test pressure
18. Wait for pressure to stabilize
19. Obtain delta F-0 value
20. Pipette several drops of Zeolite mixture onto center gold electrode of sample LCM
21. Avoid contact between mixture and oscillator connection points on outer gold electrodes
22. Dry loaded LCM in oven at 80 degrees Celsius for 2 hours to form viscous gel
23. Place 10 milliliters of deionized water in PTFE-lined autoclave reactor
24. Place PTFE holder in autoclave reactor
25. Place loaded LCM on holder ensuring no contact with liquid water
26. Close autoclave reactor
27. Transfer autoclave reactor to oven
28. Heat reactor at 150 degrees Celsius for 48 hours for steam-assisted crystallization
29. Wash loaded LCM with deionized water
30. Dry washed LCM in oven
31. Cool LCM to room temperature
32. Place LCM in oven
33. Heat LCM at 3 Kelvin/minute to 450 degrees Celsius for calcination
34. Hold LCM at 450 degrees Celsius for 4 hours
35. Cool LCM at 3 Kelvin/minute to room temperature
36. Soak LCM in 1 millimolar ammonium chloride for ion exchange
37. Repeat ammonium chloride soaking for ion exchange
38. Calcine LCM using identical heating/holding/cooling protocol
39. Place reference and sample LCMs in holder
40. Ensure resonance frequencies are detectable
41. Close and evacuate sample chamber
42. Heat LCMs overnight under vacuum to activate Zeolite
43. Set sample chamber temperature to 50°C for absorption measurements
44. Wait for temperature stabilization
45. Connect sample LCM to oscillator
46. Measure resonance frequency of loaded sample LCM
47. Connect oscillator to reference LCM
48. Measure resonance frequency of reference LCM
49. Determine mass of H-ZSM-5 deposited on sample LCM using Sauerbrey equation and frequency differences
50. Slowly fill sample chamber with selected gas to first desired pressure
51. Wait for temperature stabilization
52. Measure and record resonance frequencies of reference and sample LCMs
53. Repeat frequency measurements at several different pressures
54. Calculate combined mass of H-ZSM-5 and absorbed gas using Sauerbrey equation
55. Subtract deposited mass of H-ZSM-5 to determine absorbed gas mass
56. Determine gas absorption isotherm at temperature using calculated absorbed gas masses

9. Aspirate gelatin fibronectin solution from wells of culture plate

13. Aspirate PBS from flask

31. Incubate plates at 37 degrees Celsius
32. Change medium daily until cultures are confluent

34. Observe 12-well culture plates under microscope to determine HL-1 cell confluency

37. Put Petri dish into 37°C water bath

40. Transfer cover slip to 3 milliliters of oxygenated RH 237 solution
41. Incubate cover slip in RH 237 solution for 15 minutes
42. Place cover slip with double-stained cell monolayer in circulation chamber on inverted fluorescence microscope

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Weigh 10 grams of soil sample into each of two 50-milliliter methanol/chloroform-compatible PTFE tubes
2. Add approximately 10 milliliters of chloroform-washed stainless steel and garnet beads to each tube
3. Add 4 milliliters of cold ultrapure water to each tube while on ice
4. Transfer samples in ice bucket to fume hood
5. Use a 25-milliliter glass serological pipette
6. Add 20 milliliters of ice-cold 2:1 chloroform to methanol (v/v) to tubes
7. Tighten tube lids
8. Vortex soil mixture into solution
9. Attach tubes to 50-milliliter tube vortex attachments
10. Horizontally vortex samples for 10 minutes at 4°C (preferably inside fridge)
11. Place samples in -80°C freezer for 15 minutes to cool completely
12. Set up probe sonicator inside fume hood
13. Sonicate each sample using six millimeter (1/4-inch) probe
14. Maintain sonication at 60% amplitude for 30 seconds with samples on ice
15. Transfer samples to -80°C freezer immediately after sonication
16. Centrifuge samples at 4,000 × g and 4°C for 5 minutes
17. Place samples on ice
18. Transfer upper metabolite layers from two 50-milliliter tubes into one large glass vial using 10-milliliter glass serological pipette
19. Slightly tilt 50-milliliter tube to release protein interlayer above lower lipid layer
20. Scoop both protein interlayers using clean stainless steel flat-head lab spatula into one new 50-milliliter tube
21. Transfer lower lipid layers into one large glass vial using 25-milliliter glass serological pipette
22. Add 20 milliliters of ice-cold methanol to two tubes containing debris
23. Add 20 milliliters of ice-cold methanol to one tube containing protein interlayer
24. Briefly vortex all tubes
25. Centrifuge tubes at 4,000 × g and 4°C for 5 minutes
26. Decant methanol supernatant into hazardous waste container inside fume hood
27. Freeze protein and debris pellets in liquid nitrogen
28. Lyophilize pellets for approximately 2 hours
29. Add 20 ml of protein solubilization buffer (50 mM tris, pH 8.0) to each debris pellet
30. Add 10 ml of protein solubilization buffer to protein interlayer tube
31. Probe sonicate samples at 20% amplitude for 30 seconds in fume hood
32. Vortex all samples for 2 minutes
33. Place protein interlayer sample in lab tube rotator at 50°C for 30 minutes to solubilize protein
34. Horizontally vortex debris samples for 10 minutes to lyse remaining intact cells
35. Rotate debris samples with protein interlayer samples for remaining 20 minutes
36. Centrifuge samples at 4,500 × g at room temperature for 10 minutes
37. Collect supernatant from each tube into two 50-milliliter tubes
38. Add 10 milliliters of solubilization buffer to protein interlayer pellet
39. Sonicate pellet to resuspend in solution
40. Vortex pellet to resuspend in solution
41. Combine supernatants equally into two 50-milliliter tubes containing debris pellet supernatants
42. Centrifuge tubes in fixed-angle bucket rotor at 8,000 × g at 4°C for 10 minutes to pellet remaining debris
43. Divide supernatants into two 50-milliliter tubes
44. Add 7.5 milliliters of TCA to each tube using 10-milliliter glass serological pipette
45. Vortex samples into solution
46. Place samples in -20°C freezer for 2 hours to overnight
47. Centrifuge sample at 4,500 × g at 4°C for 10 minutes to pellet precipitated protein
48. Decant supernatant into waste
49. Add 10 milliliters of 100% ice-cold acetone to each protein pellet
50. Vortex tubes
51. Combine like pellets into one tube
52. Centrifuge samples
53. Decant acetone
54. Wash pellet twice with smaller volume of acetone
55. Transfer suspension to 1.5 or 2-milliliter tube for final spin
56. Decant supernatant into waste
57. Allow pellet to dry inverted on paper wipe in fume hood for 20 minutes
58. Add 100-200 microliters of protein solubilization buffer (volume dependent on pellet size)
59. Sonicate and vortex pellet to resuspend into solution
60. Shake sample at 300 rpm and 40°C for 30 minutes in incubator/shafter to solubilize protein

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Remove culture media from 10 cm tissue culture dish containing HeLa cells
2. Rinse cells twice with 6 ml ice-cold PBS
3. Add 1 ml ice-cold PBS to 10 cm tissue culture dish
4. Harvest cells using cell scraper
5. Transfer harvested cells to 2 ml siliconized plastic tubes
6. Remove supernatant after centrifugation
7. Add 1 ml methanol to each cell pellet
8. Vortex tubes briefly
9. Place samples into bath-type sonicator
10. Sonicate samples at 200 watts for five minutes
11. Transfer whole cell homogenate
12. Transfer homogenate into Teflon-lined screw cap test tubes
13. Add reagents to each test tube: 1 ml methanol, 1 ml chloroform, 0.8 ml double distilled water, 50 μl of 10 μM internal standard
14. Cap sample tubes
15. Vortex tubes at 2,500 rpm for 5 minutes at room temperature
16. Add 1 milliliter of chloroform to each tube
17. Add 1 milliliter of double distilled water to each tube
18. Vortex tubes at 2,500 rpm for 5 minutes at room temperature
19. Centrifuge samples to separate aqueous and organic phases
20. Transfer lower phase into disposable glass tubes
21. Add 2 milliliters chloroform to test tubes
22. Vortex tubes at 2,500 rpm for 5 minutes at room temperature
23. Transfer modified lower phase into glass tubes with initial lower phase
24. Place glass tubes under nitrogen stream
25. Completely remove organic solvents from collected lower phase
26. Reconstitute samples with 500 microliters of methanol or ethanol
27. Filter resulting mixture with 0.02 micron filter into glass vials
28. Perform serial dilutions of standard compound and add 50 microliters of each concentration into separate test tubes
29. Prepare three quality control samples: first with concentration within three times lower boundary of standard curve, second near center of curve, third near upper boundary of curve
30. Add one milliliter of methanol containing cell homogenate to each test tube
31. Extract total lipid fraction from each sample using previously demonstrated method
32. Prepare aqueous mobile phase by mixing acetonitrile, methanol, and double distilled water at 2:2:1 ratio
33. Prepare organic mobile phase using isopropanol in glass bottles with Teflon lined screw caps
34. Sonicate both mobile phases for 5 minutes in bath-type sonicator
35. Add formic acid to final concentration of 26.4 mM and ammonium hydroxide to 14.9 mM to each mobile phase
36. Activate high performance liquid chromatography system
37. Place inlet tubes into mobile phase glass bottles
38. Purge HPLC lines
39. Connect C18 HPLC column to HPLC system
40. Set column oven temperature to 50°C
41. Condition column with aqueous mobile phase at 100 microliters per minute

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Combine 16 ml mucin stock solution, 2 ml potassium chloride stock solution, 2 ml sodium chloride stock solution, 200 µl egg yolk emulsion, 5.6 ml DNA stock solution, 120 µl ferritin stock solution, 5.78 ml essential amino acid solution, 5.78 ml non-essential amino acid solution, and 2.44 ml sterile water
2. Gently shake mixture to combine components
3. Pipette 5 milliliters of medium into eight sterile 15 milliliter centrifuge tubes
4. Add 900 microliters of medium to each of eight sterile 1.5 milliliter microcentrifuge tubes under sterile bio-hood
5. Homogenize sputum samples using a 3 ml syringe through repeated withdrawal and ejection until smooth consistency is achieved
6. Add 100 microliters of homogenized sputum to each of eight microcentrifuge tubes
7. Vortex microcentrifuge tubes to sufficiently mix contents
8. Label eight sterile 15 milliliter centrifuge tubes according to prepared microcentrifuge tubes of medium
9. Sterilize paper towel with 70% ethanol solution
10. Allow sterilized paper towel to dry
11. Tear dried paper towel into pieces approximately four square inches each

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Remove germ-free mice from isolators
2. House mice in conventional husbandry room cages equipped with filters
3. Mix half of a three-day-old litter from control cage with germ-free animals' litter
4. Position 1.5 milliliter micro tube under mouse for urine collection
5. Gently massage mouse bowel to stimulate urination
6. Snap-freeze urine in liquid nitrogen
7. Store urine at ≤ -40°C until NMR analysis
8. Prepare 0.2 molar sodium phosphate buffer solution in heavy water at pH 7.4 containing one millimolar TSP
9. Mix 30 microliters of urine with 30 microliters of sodium phosphate buffer
10. Transfer 50 microliters of mixed solution into 1.7 millimeter NMR capillary tube using 50 microliter glass syringe with metal needle
11. Fit capillary adapter on capillary containing urine sample
12. Place capillary assembly into 2.5 millimeter NMR micro tube for five millimeter NMR probe
13. Acquire one-dimensional proton NMR spectra using protocol parameters
14. Remove capillary from 2.5 mm NMR tube using extraction rod and capillary adapter
15. Prepare for liver biopsy
16. Avoid using alcohol-containing products to prevent contamination
17. Wash tools using water or saline solution
18. Collect liver biopsies from center of left lobe avoiding peripheral areas
19. Avoid gallbladder perforation to prevent bile leak
20. Wash tissue immediately with water or saline solution
21. Snap freeze biopsies in liquid nitrogen immediately
22. Store biopsies at -80°C until NMR analysis
23. Insert third biopsy into zirconium rotor
24. Fill remaining rotor volume with pure heavy water for NMR lock
25. Insert 50 microliter Teflon spacer using cylindrical screw
26. Unscrew cylindrical screw
27. Calibrate spacer using depth gauge on short side
28. Place thread pin on spacer and screw gently with screwdriver
29. Dry residual water with tissue
30. Place cap at top of rotor
31. Insert rotor into rotor packer
32. Press firmly until cap is seated without space
33. Mark half of rotor bottom with black marker pen for spin detection
34. Place sufficiently packed rotor inside NMR spectrometer
35. Start spinning rotor at 5 kilohertz
36. Acquire proton NMR spectrum using CP-MG pulse sequence
37. Calibrate NMR spectra using glucose doublet peak at 5.22 parts per million

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Crush dry ice block into fine flakes using hammer and hard flat object in 5L plastic container
2. Sieve fine dry ice flakes from non-crushed material using large strainer into another 5L plastic container
3. Pour 200 cubic centimeters of dry ice flakes into upright 1L glass beaker
4. Add 10g frozen cannabis sativa inflorescences onto dry ice layer and cover with additional 200cc finely crushed dry ice
5. Cover beaker opening with 2-3 layers of 1mm screen door mosquito net secured with rubber bands
6. Pour liquid nitrogen into large round-bottom stainless steel container
7. Insert 350 micrometer mesh in flour sifter to cover mesh from below
8. Place flour sifter above stainless steel container filled with liquid nitrogen
9. Shake one liter glass beaker with opening downward toward flour sifter to separate trichomes
10. Set aside beaker every 2-3 minutes to allow sifting of accumulated dry ice and plant material on flour sieve
11. Sift flour sieve horizontally to pass plant material into liquid nitrogen in stainless steel container below
12. Add liquid nitrogen to stainless steel container when level runs low
13. Add crushed dry ice to one liter glass beaker when level runs low
14. Refill glass beaker containing used plant material with 10 grams of fresh plant material
15. Repeat sifting process until sufficient enriched trichomes accumulate in container
16. Verify presence of white powder-like substance at bottom of stainless steel container submerged in liquid nitrogen
17. Add small amount of liquid nitrogen
18. Add liquid nitrogen to clean small round-bottom stainless steel container
19. Fold 40x40 cm microsieve (150 μm mesh) twice to obtain 20x20 cm fold
20. Open folded microsieve into cone-like shape
21. Secure mesh cone to container edge with clothespins, positioning cone upright with pointed end submerged in liquid nitrogen
22. Pour liquid nitrogen containing white powder-like substance into microsieve cone
23. Gather and transfer residual plant material from first container into mesh cone using wide brush
24. Detach clothespin from container lid
25. Open microsieve cone
26. Locate trichomes in middle of opened microsieve
27. Hold all four corners of microsieve together
28. Submerge middle part containing trichomes in liquid nitrogen
29. Gently dip and shake microsieve in liquid nitrogen for one minute
30. Puncture cap of 50 milliliter test tube with sterile needle to create vent holes
31. Transfer non-sifted plant debris, dry ice, and larger trichomes from microsieve center into 50 milliliter test tube
32. Store filled test tube at -80 degrees Celsius
33. Transfer sifted trichomes submerged in liquid nitrogen sequentially through 105μm, 80μm, and 65μm microsieves
34. Transfer sample through 50μm microsieve
35. Collect retained trichomes from each microsieve separately
36. Label each collected trichome size fraction
37. Make tiny holes in cap of tube using sterile needle
38. Transfer trichomes (submerged in liquid nitrogen and wrapped in 50 micrometer microsieve) to clean plate using pre-chilled spoon
39. Transfer powder-like trichomes into labeled pre-chilled 1.5 milliliter tube using pre-chilled spatula
40. Immediately store tubes at -80°C
41. Carefully perform T bag-like infusion motion of 150 to 50 micro microsieved isolation
