Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Calibrate electric goniometer according to manufacturer's instructions
2. Calibrate EMG sensors according to manufacturer's instructions
3. Turn on continuous passive motion machine
4. Activate Extension Flexion mode on CPM machine
5. Program CPM machine for 90-130 degrees elbow extension at 0.23 degrees per second
6. Seat participant in standard-height chair with straight back and feet flat on floor
7. Verbally prepare participant for EMG sensor and electro goniometer placement using standardized script
8. Manually resist elbow flexion to locate biceps brachii muscle belly
9. Mark central point of biceps brachii muscle belly with washable marker for EMG sensor placement
10. Manually resist elbow extension to locate lateral head of triceps muscle belly
11. Mark central point of triceps brachii muscle belly for EMG sensor placement
12. Determine midpoint of dorsal aspect of wrist
13. Mark wrist midpoint with washable marker
14. Palpate most prominent aspect of lateral epicondyle
15. Mark lateral epicondyle with washable marker
16. Palpate greater tubercle of humerus
17. Mark humerus tubercle with washable marker
18. Verify tubercle location by passively moving testing arm through internal/external rotation
19. Attach string end to lateral epicondyle mark using paper tape
20. Pull string taut to dorsal wrist midpoint mark
21. Trace line along proximal forearm aligned with string using washable marker
22. Move free end of string to greater tubercle mark
23. Pull string taut
24. Trace line along distal humerus
25. Align traced line with string using washable marker
26. Remove string
27. Measure 1.5 inches from lateral epicondyle mark along proximal forearm line
28. Mark measured location with washable marker dot for distal paddle placement
29. Measure 1.5 inches from lateral epicondyle mark along distal humerus line
30. Mark measured location with washable marker dot for proximal paddle placement
31. Secure remaining electric goniometer components to skin using paper tape
32. Position participant's upper extremity in CPM machine
33. Adjust CPM machine height and orientation to achieve 90° sagittal plane shoulder flexion, 90° elbow flexion, and neutral forearm
34. Align participant's lateral epicondyle with rotational axis of CPM machine
35. Adjust CPM hand support to fit palm comfortably and secure forearm via wrist strap
36. Inform participant of testing procedure using standardized verbal information
37. Hand participant trigger switch and test switch functionality
38. Inform participant of additional testing procedure aspects
39. Place noise-canceling headphones on participant to diminish auditory input
40. Occlude visual input of CPM machine and test arm using visual screen
41. Loudly state 'Begin'
42. Wait specified trial duration
43. Initiate movement of CPM machine
44. Monitor EMG sensor feedback readings to detect active muscle assistance
45. Remove participant's test arm from CPM machine
46. Passively move participant's elbow through full extension
47. Passively flex elbow back to 90 degrees
48. Reposition arm in CPM machine
49. Complete eight trials including two catch trials without arm movement

Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Remove skin from decapitated mouse head to expose skull and nose using paper towel
2. Wipe away remaining blood and tissue
3. Dissect away lower jaw and front teeth
4. Separate septum from maxilla by cutting dorsal bone bilaterally 1-2 mm parallel to suture line
5. Split nose with single cut without touching septum
6. Carefully remove remnant bones to expose septum
7. Remove nasal bone by inserting curved forceps tip between epithelium and bone, applying pressure, and lifting bone
8. Remove remaining maxilla to access septum from either cavity
9. Remove remaining maxilla
10. Cut along border between olfactory and respiratory epithelium using fine spring scissors
11. Extend cut to separate septum from ventral connection to vomer bone
12. Cut along border between septum and lamina propria of the seth
13. Place adhesive glass slide onto edge of Petri dish filled with Ringer's solution
14. Submerge half of slide using tines of forceps
15. Transfer ethmoid bone with olfactory epithelium to dish
16. Grab perpendicular plate of ethmoid bone with first forceps
17. Use second forceps to peel epithelium off septum by sliding tip between plate and epithelium
18. Position epithelium with ciliary side facing upward
19. Grab epithelium border without touching ciliary side
20. Tug epithelium onto glass slide
21. Rotate the septum
22. Peel epithelium off the other side of septum
23. Remove slide from dish
24. Wick away solution surrounding tissue using paper towel
25. Draw circle around tissue with liquid blocker pen
26. Apply 150 microliters of fixative indirectly to tissue
27. Wait 10 minutes after fixative application
28. Aspirate fixative solution without disturbing tissue
29. Wash tissue twice with PBS
30. Apply blocking buffer to tissue
31. Incubate tissue with blocking buffer for 1 hour at room temperature
32. Dissolve primary antibody in blocking buffer at appropriate concentration
33. Centrifuge primary antibody solution for 5 minutes to remove precipitates
34. Apply primary antibody dilution to epithelium
35. Place slide in humidified box
36. Transfer humidified box to 4°C
37. Perform three 5-minute washes with PBS to remove excess primary antibody
38. Apply secondary antibody prepared similarly to primary antibody
39. Incubate tissue for one hour at room temperature
40. Perform three washes with PBS to remove excess secondary antibodies
41. Remove circle of liquid blocker using razor blade or paper towel
42. Wash epithelium for five seconds using distilled water
43. Mount tissue in anti-fade mounting reagent and visualize immediately

Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Pour molten agarose into reagent trough
2. Dispense 80 microliters of molten agarose into each well of 96-well microplate using multi-channel pipette
3. Refrigerate leftover agarose in sealed bag for up to one week
4. Coil barrier strips around finger to add curvature for secure channel placement
5. Insert barrier strips into barrier strip channels
6. Affix coupler to starvation plate without using coupler to manipulate plate
7. Align angled corner of coupler with angled corner of microplate for correct orientation
8. Sort three- to five-day-old flies under CO₂ anesthesia
9. Load individual flies by column into starvation plate
10. Close barrier strip per column as filled
11. Record sample layout within microplate
12. Remove CO₂ from flies
13. Allow flies to recover spontaneously
14. Initiate six-hour starvation period from anesthesia time
15. Dissolve 0.4 grams of sucrose and 0.1 gram of yeast extract in 10 milliliters of distilled water in a 15 milliliter conical tube
16. Vortex the tube until solids fully dissolve
17. Add 40 microliters of dye stock solution to the liquid food
18. Transfer liquid food into a 10 milliliter syringe tipped with 0.45 micrometer filter
19. Filter approximately 1.5 milliliters of solution into a 1.7 milliliter microcentrifuge tube
20. Set syringe containing solution aside for later filtration during feeder plate preparation
21. Seal bottom of 1536-well microplate with sealing film
22. Adhere film thoroughly using sealing paddle
23. Trim excess film from left and right edges with razor blade
24. Dispense 10 microliters of filtered liquid food column-wise into upper left-hand well of each 4-well cluster
25. Apply sealing film to top of plate
26. Adhere top film using same sealing steps as bottom
27. Centrifuge plates at 200×g for 10 seconds to settle fluid
28. Perforate wells on top plate surface using 0.25mm needle probe in solution dispensing order
29. Wipe needle probe between solutions to prevent cross-contamination
30. Flip plate and perforate wells on bottom surface
31. Read plate absorbance at 630 nm without lid
32. Place internal lid on top sealing film to position condensation rings around perforated wells
33. Place external lid on plate
34. Position feeder plate face up on coupler, aligning guide holes with starvation plate
35. Adjust barrier strips on coupler to open wells for plates
36. Place coupler and plate assemblies in secondary container
37. Place lower half of pipette box containing soaked paper towels into each secondary container for humidity
38. Close lid of secondary container
39. Transfer secondary container to controlled environment
40. Allow flies to consume for 22 hours
41. Check each plate for dead flies
42. Update plate layout according to mortality observations
43. Anesthetize flies en masse by pumping CO₂ inside secondary container
44. Verify all flies are immobilized after 60 seconds of CO₂ exposure
45. Gently tap anesthetized flies into starvation plate
46. Replace plastic barrier strips
47. Remove feeder plates for reading
48. Reread plate absorbance at 630 nm
49. Repeat absorbance reading process for all plates
50. Quantify evaporation for every well
51. Calculate Pearson correlation coefficients for evaporation versus rows and columns

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Dissolve 2.6 grams of P nipam in 2 milliliters of 60% toluene and 40% hexane solution
2. Heat mixture to 60 degrees Celsius for 10 minutes
3. Stir mixture until P nipam is dissolved
4. Cut filter paper into 60 millimeter diameter circle
5. Place filter paper in Buchner funnel
6. Filter solution through Buchner funnel into pre-weighed glass beaker
7. Keep beaker and contents in bell vacuum overnight
8. Weigh beaker with P nipam
9. Add isopropyl alcohol to P nipam
10. Create 50:50 weight/weight solution
11. Place two milliliters of solution on tissue culture plate surface
12. Coat plate for five minutes under UV light
13. Wash cells with two milliliters of warm PBS
14. Add three milliliters of trypsin or cleaving dissociating solution to cells
15. Incubate for five minutes
16. Add 3 mL of culture media or PBS containing 10% FBS to cells
17. Collect cells in conical tube
18. Count cells
19. Centrifuge cells at 1000 RPM for 5 minutes
20. Aspirate supernatant
21. Resuspend cells in growth media
22. Plate cell suspension on 35mm thermosensitive plate at concentration for 100% confluence
23. Incubate plate at 37°C overnight
24. Collect endothelial cells
25. Disperse cells into single-cell solution
26. Deactivate trypsin enzyme with equal amount of soybean trypsin inhibitor or 10% FBS in PBS
27. Collect cells in 15 milliliter conical tube
28. Count cells with hemocytometer and calculate volume needed for patch dimensions
29. Extract 2 million endothelial cells
30. Place extracted cells in new 15 milliliter conical tube
31. Centrifuge cells for five minutes
32. Aspirate supernatant, leaving cell pellet in conical tube
33. Mix high aurin and liquid gelatin in 1:1 ratio
34. Transfer 80% of final hydrogel-gelatin volume to conical tube containing endothelial cell pellet and resuspend cells
35. Transfer suspended cells into fibrous matrix
36. Add 20% of total final volume of crosslinker
37. Incubate at 37°C for 1 hour
38. Remove 35 millimeter P nypa treated plate containing cells from incubator
39. Place plate in cell culture hood
40. Aspirate media from cells
41. Add two milliliters of 37°C 6% normal gelatin to plate
42. Place metal lattice into gelatin
43. Submerge metal lattice below surface of normal gelatin
44. Place entire plate on ice for 5-7 minutes to harden gelatin
45. Use spatula to carefully separate gelatin edges from plate side
46. Lift metal lattice from plate with forceps
47. Transfer cell sheet to dish and place on fibrous matrix hydrogel combination
48. Add 2 milliliters of 37°C media to tissue culture plate
49. Incubate overnight to allow cell sheet adherence to hydrogel surface

Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Euthanize timed pregnant mouse or rat
2. Isolate embryos
3. Separate embryos from amniotic sack and cut umbilical cord
4. Transfer embryos into 50ml tube containing ice-cold Hanks' Balanced Salt Solution (HBSS)
5. Keep embryos in HBSS on ice
6. Place embryo into 100mm Petri dish
7. Use scissors to bisect embryo at level of bolus
8. Remove stomach and small intestine
9. Locate ovaries (female) or testes (male) near kidneys/bladder
10. Position tissue under dissection scope
11. Use Vannas scissors to cut abdominal wall
12. Cut abdominal wall using Venice scissors to expose bladder and posterior abdominal wall
13. Free upper genital tract from attachments
14. Free umbilical cord
15. Cut pubic bone using Dumont fine tip forceps
16. Bluntly separate pubic bone from anterior wall of urogenital sinus
17. Separate posterior wall of urogenital sinus from rectum
18. Cut urogenital sinus with bladder at lower end
19. Separate bladder from urogenital sinus
20. Store urogenital sinus block in ice-cold HBSS
21. Transfer urogenital sinus to 50 mL tube containing 1% trypsin in HBSS without calcium and magnesium
22. Incubate at 4°C for 30 minutes
23. Remove trypsin solution
24. Add 10% FBS in DMEM/F12 medium to neutralize digestion
25. Use needle or fine tip forceps to carefully tease mesenchymal sleeve from epithelial tube
26. Transfer purified urogenital sinus mesenchyme (UGSM) into new 50 ml tube containing DMM F12 medium with 10% FBS, 1% pen strep, 1% non-essential amino acids, and 1 nM R1881
27. Incubate tube at 37°C with 5% CO2 overnight
28. Centrifuge tissue at 200 G
29. Centrifuge tissue at 200 G
30. Discard supernatant
31. Wash pellet with PBS
32. Resuspend tissue in 0.2% collagenase in DMEM-F12 medium
33. Incubate at 37°C with gentle rocking
34. Vigorously vortex digestion mixture three times until homogeneous single-cell suspension forms
35. Neutralize collagenase by adding DMEM-F12 medium
36. Neutralize collagenase by adding DMEM/F12 medium with 10% FBS, 1% pen-strep, 1% non-essential amino acids, and 1 nM R1881
37. Wash cells three times by pelleting at 200 G
38. Resuspend pelleted cells in HBSS
39. Resuspend mesenchymal cells in DMEM/F12
40. Count cells using hemocytometer
41. Prepare sterile surgical area
42. Anesthetize male athymic nude mouse via IP injection of ketamine xylazine
43. Perform toe pinch to confirm sedation level
44. Shave surgical site if necessary
45. Cleanse surgical site with three alternating wipes of 70% alcohol and Betadine solution
46. Apply eye ointment
47. Make 1 cm longitudinal incision on the back
48. Cut abdominal wall
49. Make 1 cm longitudinal incision on the back
50. Cut abdominal wall with 0.5 cm longitudinal incision
51. Gently squeeze kidney out of abdomen
52. Apply drops of sterile saline to keep kidney moist
53. Prepare 27-gauge syringe needle
54. Gently puncture renal capsule to create injection site
55. Fill 28-gauge syringe needle with 10 microliters of cell mixture
56. Slowly insert needle into puncture site through kidney parenchyma to reach area underneath renal capsule
57. Inject 10 microliters of cellular mixture to form bolus underneath renal capsule
58. Withdraw needle from injection site
59. Return kidney to abdominal cavity
60. Secure muscular layer of peritoneum with four aught nylon sutures
61. Close skin incision with sutures or staples
62. Clean blood from skin using sterile saline

1. Remove contaminants and dust from 70 mm × 70 mm × 70 mm mold using brush

6. Mix at 130 rpm for 60 seconds
7. Remove mixing pot from cement mixer
8. Pour cement paste into mold
9. Shovel remaining paste into mold using scraper knife
10. Place filled mold on vibrating table
11. Vibrate mold for 10 seconds to compact paste
12. Seal mold with cling film to prevent water evaporation
13. Allow cement to cure at room temperature for 24 hours
14. Remove hardened cement specimen from mold
15. Cure specimen at 23°C and 95% relative humidity for 60 days
16. Place specimen on 1 cm spacers in plastic box with exposure surface facing down
17. Add 3.5% by mass sodium chloride solution in deionized water to box until solution level is 1 cm above exposure surface
18. Note time of sodium chloride solution addition
19. Seal box with 0.25 mm thick plastic film to prevent evaporation
20. Store box at 23°C and 65% relative humidity for 24 hours from solution addition time
21. Remove specimen from box
22. Remove specimen from box
23. Wipe solution off specimen gently with paper towel
24. Leave specimen at 23°C and 65% relative humidity for six days to dry
25. Fix specimen on base of high precision CNC grinding machine
26. Place powder collecting paper around specimen
27. Set up automated grinding protocol for layer grinding with 60-second pauses between layers
28. Start grinding process
29. Recover powder during each 60-second break
30. Replace collecting paper for next layer during breaks
31. Grind each powder sample with mortar and pestle
32. Sift ground sample through 80 micron sieve
33. Dry sieved samples at 105°C for 2 hours
34. Place 2 grams of dry sample in 100 ml plastic bottle
35. Add 50 ml deionized water to bottle
36. Cap the bottle
37. Shake bottle vigorously to thoroughly mix sample and water
38. Filter solution through filter paper
39. Place two 10-milliliter portions of filtrate in conical flasks
40. Add two drops of phenolphthalein solution to each conical flask
41. Add dilute sulfuric acid to each solution until solutions become colorless
42. Add 10 drops of potassium chromate indicator to one conical flask
43. Titrate with silver nitrate solution
44. Manually shake conical flask during titration to ensure reaction
45. Stop titration when solution appears reddish and color persists
46. Record volume of consumed silver nitrate solution

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Obtain frozen postmortem brain tissue from healthy control and pathologically confirmed AD
2. Use forceps and razor blade to excise approximately 250-milligram portions of gray matter from each tissue sample
3. Allow brain segment to thaw for one minute
4. Dice tissue into roughly two-millimeter cubed pieces during thawing
5. Transfer diced tissue to two-milliliter prechilled Dounce homogenizer tube on ice
6. Place tissue on TARE disposable weigh boats
7. Place weighing boat with brain tissue into polystyrene containers with dry ice to prevent thawing
8. Remove weigh boat containing tissue piece from dry ice after dissection and weighing
9. Add five milliliters of ice cold low-salt buffer
10. Add 5 ml of ice-cold low-salt buffer with protease and phosphatase inhibitor cocktail per gram of tissue
11. Homogenize tissue on ice using 10 strokes of high-clearance pestle A followed by 15 strokes of low-clearance pestle B
12. Transfer homogenate to labeled 2-ml polypropylene tube using nine-inch glass Pasteur pipette
13. Aliquot remaining homogenate into labeled 1.5-ml tubes for storage at -80°C
14. Add 100 microliters of 5M sodium chloride and 100 microliters of 10% sarkosyl to each 0.8-milliliter aliquot
15. Mix aliquots well by inversion
16. Incubate aliquots on ice for 15 minutes
17. Sonicate aliquots using microtip probe: three 5-second pulses at 30% amplitude
18. Add ice-cold sark buffer with protease and phosphatase inhibitors to each sample
19. Adjust samples to final concentration of 10 milligrams per milliliter
20. Transfer 500 microliters of each sample into 500-microliter polycarbonate ultracentrifuge tubes
21. Load tubes into prechilled rotor

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Synthesize DNA fragment containing 5' BglII cutting site, secretion signal sequence, and multi-cloning site
2. Digest 4 micrograms of synthesized DNA with BglII and X1 restriction enzymes
3. Digest 4 micrograms of expression vector DNA with BamH1 and X1 restriction enzymes
4. Mix 10 units of each restriction enzyme with DNA in 1X reaction buffer
5. Incubate mixture at 37°C for four hours
6. Add 10μL ligation mixture (1X T4 DNA Ligase buffer + 5 units T4 DNA Ligase) to new 1.5mL tube
7. Add 100ng linearized vector DNA and 400ng digested DNA fragment to tube
8. Incubate at 16°C for 16 hours
9. Transform 5μL ligation mixture into 100μL DH5 alpha competent cells
10. Plate transformed cells on Ampicillin LB agar plate for colony selection
11. Transform 40 microliters of DH10Bac competent cells with 100 nanograms of construct plasmid
12. Incubate transformation plates at 37°C for 48 hours
13. Pick two white colonies from each transformation plate
14. Inoculate each colony in 2 ml LB medium containing kanamycin, gentamycin, and tetracycline
15. Grow monolayer of Sf9 cells in six-well plate using culture media with 10% fetal bovine serum and 100 μg/ml Penicillin-Streptomycin at 27°C to 80% confluence
16. Add 100 μl unsupplemented antibiotic-free Sf9 medium to 1.5 ml centrifuge tube
17. Dilute 20 μl bacmid DNA into tube and gently flick to mix
18. Add 100 μl unsupplemented antibiotic-free Sf9 medium to separate 1.5 ml tube
19. Dilute 8 μl lipid transfection reagent into second tube and mix by pipetting up/down six times
20. Combine DNA solution and lipid solution, mix by slow pipetting up/down three times
21. Incubate mixture for 20-40 minutes at room temperature
22. Wash each culture well twice with 3 ml unsupplemented antibiotic-free Sf9 medium per wash
23. Add 0.8 ml unsupplemented antibiotic-free Sf9 medium to lipid-DNA mixture tube
24. Mix supplemented lipid-DNA complex by slow pipetting up/down three times
25. Aspirate wash media from culture plates
26. Pipet mixture up and down slowly three times to mix
27. Aspirate wash media from plates
28. Overlay samples with transfection mixture
29. Incubate transfected plate at 27°C for 5 hours
30. Replace transfection media with complete Sf9 culture medium (containing 10% FBS and 100 μg/mL Penicillin-Streptomycin)
31. Incubate at 27°C for 4 days
32. Collect supernatant in sterile 15 mL conical tube
33. Centrifuge at 1,010 × g for 5 minutes to remove cell debris
34. Discard pellet and decant supernatant to clean tube
35. Store supernatant at 4°C for up to one year
36. Grow monolayer of Sf9 cells on 150 mm plate to 80% confluence
37. Aspirate medium from plate
38. Add 2 ml of P0 virus to each well
39. Incubate plate for 1 hour at 27°C
40. Rock plate every 15 minutes during incubation
41. Add 25 ml complete Grace's Media containing 10% FBS and 100 μg/ml Penicillin-Streptomycin
42. Incubate plate for 3 days at 27°C to obtain P1 passage virus
43. Collect supernatant in sterile 50 ml conical tube
44. Centrifuge at 1,010 × g for 5 minutes to remove cell debris
45. Decant supernatant to clean tube
46. Store supernatant at 4°C for up to one year
47. Grow monolayer of Sf9 cells on 150 millimeter plate to 90% confluence
48. Aspirate medium from plate
49. Add two milliliters of P1 virus to plate
50. Incubate plate for one hour at 27 degrees Celsius

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Prepare a 22-gauge syringe needle
2. Blunt needle tip using honing stone
3. Plicate needle at right angle using pliers
4. Prepare required surgical materials including recovery cage
5. Autoclave surgical instruments
6. Place rat in supine position on surgery platform with heating pad
7. Completely shave surgical area
8. Scrub cleanly shaven abdomen with betadine or cleansing reagent
9. Make 2 cm midline abdominal incision using scalpel
10. Moisten abdominal organs with normal saline and maintain moisture during surgery
11. Displace digestive organs to the side using cotton balls
12. Expose inferior vena cava and posterior peritoneal region
13. Identify abdominal aorta juxtaposed to left of inferior vena cava
14. Pierce peritoneum with forceps to uncover vessels
15. Gently isolate abdominal aorta adjacent to renal arteries
16. Pass 8cm 4/0 silk suture underneath abdominal aorta between renal artery origins
17. Make loose double knot with suture and leave 3 mm diameter loop
18. Place blunted/bent 22-gauge needle inside suture loop
19. Tighten suture knot around aorta and needle
20. Remove needle to create 0.7 mm diameter aortic constriction
21. Close abdominal cavity with 6/0 sutures
22. Close abdominal cavity with 6/0 sutures
23. Close muscle and skin incisions using simple interrupted sutures
24. Scrub surgical site with iodine tincture
25. Treat animal with acetaminophen for postsurgical pain
26. Observe animal until regaining consciousness indicated by free movement, food intake, and sternal recumbency
27. Weigh rat at 10 weeks postsurgery
28. Anesthetize rat and confirm anesthesia depth
29. Place anesthetized rat on metal tray
30. Make 5 cm thoracic incision at midline of xiphoid process
31. Pierce diaphragm using sharp forceps
32. Cut and remove rib cage along midclavicular lines on both sides to expose heart
33. Excise heart along cardiac and vascular borders without grabbing tissue
34. Mount heart on modified Langendorff perfusion apparatus
35. Tie aortic trunk to perfusion needle
36. Perfuse heart with Krebs buffer to wash out blood
37. Weigh heart and calculate heart weight to body weight ratio
38. Place organ in 4% paraformaldehyde while wearing mask
39. Fix heart on ice overnight
40. Place fixed heart tissue on tissue sectioning device
41. Cut tissue into two millimeter thick slices
42. Place tissue sections in embedding cassette
43. Deparaffinize slides in xylene tank for 30 minutes
44. Rehydrate tissue in sequentially diluted alcohol series followed by distilled water
45. Apply picrosirius red solution to completely cover tissue sections
46. Incubate sections in picrosirius red solution for one hour
47. Rinse slides two times with 0.5% acetic acid solution
48. Rinse samples twice in absolute alcohol
49. Air-dry samples
50. Mount samples in synthetic resin with cover slip
51. Image slides under microscope using visible light at 200X magnification
52. Calculate percentage of picrosirius red positive zone relative to total area

Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Plate single-cell suspension of Platinum Eco-packaging cells in 10 cm petri dish
2. Add 15 ml DMEM supplemented with 10% FBS to plated cells
3. Cultivate cells until 50-60% confluent
4. Add 20 μg retroviral construct and 10 μg PCL eco-packaging vector to 1 ml serum-free DMEM
5. Add 75 μl PEI to 1 ml serum/antibiotic-free DMEM in separate tube
6. Incubate PEI mixture for 5 minutes at room temperature
7. Combine contents of both DNA and PEI tubes
8. Incubate combined mixture for 10 minutes at room temperature
9. Replace medium on Platinum Eco cells with 8 ml of fresh 37°C DMEM supplemented with 2% FBS
10. Add transfection mixture dropwise to plate
11. Incubate plate at 37°C for 4 hours
12. Replace supernatant with 10 ml of 37°C DMEM supplemented with 10% FBS
13. Incubate at 37°C for 24 hours
14. Transfer ecotropic retroviral particle-containing supernatant to 15 ml centrifuge tube using 5 ml pipette
15. Remove debris and cells by centrifugation
16. Add 10 ml of 37°C DMEM plus 10% FBS to culture dish
17. Incubate culture dish at 37°C for 24 hours
18. Collect second round of viral particles using previously demonstrated method
19. Use tweezers and scissors in tissue culture hood
20. Remove skin and part of muscles from hind limbs
21. Carefully dislodge acetabulum from hip joint without breaking femur
22. Cut paws at ankle joints
23. Spray bones with 70% ethanol
24. Remove remaining muscle with paper towel
25. Remove residual muscle tissue using a paper towel
26. Place bones in 5 cm petri dish containing PBS supplemented with 2% FBS
27. Bend knee joint of each bone set and separate bones using scissors
28. Remove 1-2 mm of each epiphysis using scissors
29. Flush bone marrow from both ends using 2-5 ml syringe with 30-gauge needle loaded with PBS plus FBS into 15 ml centrifuge tube
30. Pellet bone cells by centrifugation
31. Re-suspend pellet in 2.5 ml red blood cell lysis buffer for 2-3 minutes at room temperature
32. Filter lysed cells through 100 micrometer cell strainer into new 15 milliliter centrifuge tube
33. Restore tonicity with 12 milliliters of PBS plus FBS
34. Re-suspend pellet in DMEM supplemented with 10% FBS and antibiotics
35. Plate 5-10 × 10⁶ bone marrow cells in 10-centimeter non-tissue culture treated petri dish with 10 milliliters DMEM supplemented with serum and macrophage colony-stimulating factor (MCSF)
36. Tilt cell culture dish
37. Remove medium leaving approximately 5 milliliters near dish edge
38. Add 20 milliliters of fresh 37°C medium supplemented with FBS and MCSF
39. Return culture dish to cell culture incubator
40. Remove all medium and floating cells
41. Wash culture with 37°C PBS
42. Add 5 milliliters of 0.02% EDTA in PBS
43. Incubate with EDTA for 3-5 minutes at 37°C

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Create complementary sequences for five-prime and three-prime arms
2. Flank DNAzyme catalytic sequence with complementary sequences on three-prime and five-prime ends
3. Order DNAzyme as normal DNA oligonucleotide
4. Pellet cells by centrifuging at 1,000 × g for 3 minutes at 4°C
5. Discard supernatant medium after centrifugation
6. Resuspend RNA pellet in 30 microliters of RNase and DNase-free water
7. Place RNA sample tube on ice
8. Measure RNA concentration using microspectrophotometer
9. Prepare incubation mix containing 5 μg RNA, 200 pmol 10-23 DNAzyme, and 1X incubation buffer in 10 μL total volume
10. Incubate tubes on dry heat block at 95°C for 3 minutes
11. Transfer tubes to ice and incubate for 5 minutes
12. Briefly centrifuge tubes and return to ice
13. Add 20 units of RNase inhibitor to tubes
14. Incubate tubes on dry heat block at 25°C for 10 minutes
15. Combine 5 microliters of 4X 10-23 reaction buffer with 4 microliters of 300-molar magnesium chloride
16. Add 1 microliter of water to reaction mixture
17. Preheat reaction mixture on 37°C dry heat block
18. Place tube containing incubation mix on 37°C dry heat block
19. Add 10 microliters of prewarmed reaction mix to incubation mix
20. Incubate mixture at 37°C for 1 hour
21. Transfer reaction tube to ice
22. Prepare 1.5-milliliter microtube with 5 micrograms RNA in 6 microliters total volume
23. Prepare separate tube with 400 picomoles of 8-17 DNAzyme
24. Keep both tubes on ice
25. Transfer both tubes to 95°C dry heat block and incubate for 2 minutes
26. Transfer RNA sample back to ice
27. Centrifuge DNAzyme tube for 5 seconds
28. Incubate DNAzyme at 25°C for 10 minutes
29. Incubate DNAzyme at 25°C for 10 minutes
30. Prepare 10 microliters of 2X 8-17 reaction buffer
31. Prewarm reaction buffer to 25°C
32. Add prewarmed buffer to DNAzyme tube
33. Transfer 14 microliters of reaction mix to RNA tube
34. Add 20 units of RNase inhibitor to RNA tube
35. Incubate reaction at 25°C for 2 hours
36. Place reaction tube on ice
37. Add 350 microliters of water to reaction tube
38. Add 400 microliters of chloroform to reaction tube
39. Vortex mixture for 30 seconds
40. Centrifuge at 20,000 × g for 5 minutes
41. Transfer upper aqueous phase to new tube containing 1 mL ethanol, 40 μL 7.5M ammonium acetate, and 1 μL glycogen
42. Flip tube several times to mix contents
43. Incubate at -80°C for 2 hours or -20°C overnight
44. Resuspend RNA pellet in 10 microliters RNase/DNase-free water
45. Dissolve 1.5 grams of agarose in 127.5 milliliters of double distilled water
46. Heat mixture in microwave to dissolve agarose
47. Add 15 milliliters of 10X MOPS to agarose solution
48. Add 7.5 milliliters of 37% formaldehyde to agarose solution
49. Add appropriate amount of gel stain to agarose solution
50. Pour agarose solution into tray
51. Let agarose cool for 45 minutes to solidify
52. Combine 10 microliters of digested and purified RNA with 5 microliters of sample denaturing buffer
53. Add 0.5 microliters of six-X loading dye to RNA mixture
54. Incubate RNA samples at 70 degrees Celsius for 5 minutes
55. Incubate RNA samples on ice for 5 minutes
56. Place prepared gel into electrophoresis tank
57. Fill electrophoresis tank with one-X MOPS buffer
58. Load 15 microliters of RNA sample onto gel
59. Run gel electrophoresis at 80 volts until bromothymol blue dye migrates to 2/3 gel length
60. Analyze gel using appropriate imager

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Clean stainless steel tweezers and containers
2. Gather gloves, zip bags, identity beach form, and icebox
3. Investigate area to find plastic pellets
4. Collect plastic pellets from beach using tweezers while wearing gloves
5. Sample 50 to 100 pellets per location
6. Record collected pellet quantity in identity beach form if unable to reach target number
7. Wrap collected pellets in aluminum foil
8. Fill in identity beach form
9. Gently wipe off particles from pellets in laboratory
10. Dry pellets if necessary prior to storage
11. Store pellets in dark at low temperature
12. Sort plastic pellets by color using tweezers
13. Gather 10 pellets of similar color to form one replicate
14. Weigh sample on analytical balance
15. Switch on the pressurized fluid extractor
16. Download the extraction method
17. Warm up instrument to 60 degrees centigrade
18. Place bottom filter and frit in extraction cell
19. Close extraction cell
20. Fill approximately half the cell with ultra-clean quartz sand using funnel
21. Add 10 weighed pellets to extraction cell
22. Add quartz sand up to one centimeter to top of cell
23. Insert top filter into cell
24. Place cell in instrument
25. Place collecting vessels in instrument
26. Start extraction method
27. Empty extraction cell into cleaned glass Petri dish
28. Retrieve 10 pellets from sand
29. Store pellets for plastic identification analysis
30. Transfer extract from collecting vessel to glass tube
31. Concentrate extract in rotating concentrator at 35°C for 20 minutes
32. Place waste tube in rack and Florisil cartridge on manifold in closed-valve position
33. Turn on vacuum source
34. Add 4 mL hexane to cartridge to activate sorbent
35. Open valve to allow solvent to pass through entire sorbent bed
36. Close valve
37. Allow sorbent to soak in hexane for five minutes
38. Open valve to let sorbent pass through
39. Close valve before sorbent dries off
40. Transfer concentrated sample to cartridge using glass Pasteur pipette
41. Gently open valve to allow sample to pass through at 1-2 drops per second
42. Rinse glass tube with 0.5 mL hexane
43. Add hexane rinse to cartridge after extract passage
44. Close valve and turn off vacuum after solvent passage
45. Replace waste tube with collecting tube
46. Close the valve
47. Turn off the vacuum
48. Replace waste tube with collecting tube
49. Use clean solvent guide needle
50. Add 9 mL of solvent (10% acetone, 90% hexane)
51. Turn on vacuum at source
52. Allow sorbent to soak in solvent for 1 minute
53. Open the valve
54. Collect entire eluate in collecting tube
55. Place collecting tube in concentrator
56. Evaporate solvent for 9 minutes at 35°C to reach 1 mL volume
57. Transfer concentrated eluate to amber vial
58. Download analytical method on gas chromatograph control software
59. Place vial containing cleaned and concentrated extract in autosampler rack
60. Run analytical method
61. Inject two microliters of sample
62. Transfer pellets to a glass Petri dish
63. Place the glass Petri dish in a plastic bag
64. Hold one pellet
65. Cut one pellet with a scalpel
66. Place polymer fragment on attenuated total reflectance crystal
67. Screw sample holder to secure fragment
68. Scan sample and record infrared spectrum using FTIR spectrometer
69. Identify plastic type by comparing sample spectrum to spectral library
70. Identify compounds on chromatogram by retention times
71. Record peak areas of identified compounds
72. Determine calibration equations using standard solutions
73. Determine recoveries from solid-phase extraction and concentration steps using standard solutions
74. Detect lindane at retention time 9.915 minutes
75. Record peak area of lindane (3,270 arbitrary units)
76. Recalculate peak area using recovery adjustment
77. Calculate concentration of 1 mL extract using calibration equation
78. Calculate sample concentration (ng/g pellets) using 10-pellet mass
79. Determine median concentrations across replicates
80. Plot results by compound categories (HCHs, DDTs, endosulfans)

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Suspend 20 mg of high surface area carbon black particles in 1 mL ultrapure water
2. Sonicate suspension at lower power for at least 15 minutes
3. Load 15 μL of ~7 mg/mL E. coli hydrogenase one solution into 50 kDa centrifugal filter unit
4. Dilute solution with 450 μL low ionic strength exchange buffer (pH near hydrogenase isoelectric point)
5. Concentrate mixture to 50 μL via centrifugation at 27,000 × g
6. Reconcentrate mixture four times to complete buffer exchange
7. Combine 5 microliters of 20 mg/mL carbon black dispersion with buffer-exchanged hydrogenase
8. Store mixture at 0 degrees Celsius overnight for hydrogenase absorption to carbon black particles
9. Centrifuge modified particles at 27,000 × g
10. Verify supernatant is nearly colorless to confirm hydrogenase absorption
11. Wash particles with 3-5 cycles of centrifugation and resuspension in fresh exchange buffer
12. Concentrate particle mixture to approximately 5 microliters for 20 mg/mL particle loading
13. Clean silicone internal reflection element by low power sonication in sulfuric acid for 15 minutes
14. Continue cleaning by sonication in nitric acid for one hour
15. Rinse element in ultrapure water
16. Dry element under stream of dry nitrogen gas
17. Apply electrical grade silicone sealant to fix IRE into baseplate of five reflection ATR accessory, keeping sealant to edges
18. Allow sealant to dry completely
19. Transfer baseplate into dry anaerobic glove box
20. Mount baseplate on ATR accessory
21. Acquire background infrared spectrum in rapid scan mode
22. Detach ATR accessory baseplate
23. Transfer baseplate to wet anaerobic glove box
24. Dropcast 1 microliter of enzyme-modified carbon black particles evenly over IRE surface without allowing particles to dry
25. Gently place carbon paper soaked in ultrapure water onto IRE surface to cover particle film without contacting silicone sealant
26. Mount custom spectroelectrochemical cell over IRE
27. Add 200 microliters of experiment buffer via solution inlet
28. Connect solution inlet and outlet to experiment buffer vial via peristaltic pump tubing
29. Transfer assembled cell to dry glove box
30. Mount cell assembly on ATR accessory
31. Connect tubing to peristaltic pump
32. Acquire absorption spectrum using previously acquired spectrum as background

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Seed HEK293T cells in poly-L-lysine coated culture dish
2. Culture seeded HEK293T cells at 37°C in tissue culture incubator for one day
3. Transfect HEK293T cells with mt-Keima lentiviral DNA and packaging DNase using transfection reagent according to manufacturer's instructions
4. Remove transfection media
5. Add 8 milliliters of fresh media
6. Collect media containing viral particles
7. Remove cellular debris by centrifugation
8. Filter virus-containing media using 0.45 micrometer syringe filter
9. Plate HeLa-Parkin cells
10. Plate HeLa-Parkin cells in 60 millimeter culture dish
11. Culture cells with 4 milliliters of growth media at 37°C in incubator for one day
12. Remove growth medium
13. Add 1 milliliter of mt-Keima virus media
14. Add 2 milliliters of growth medium containing 3 microliters polybrene stock solution
15. Incubate cells for one day in CO₂ incubator
16. Remove virus-containing media
17. Wash cells twice with PBS
18. Add four milliliters of growth media
19. Treat cells with two micrograms per microliter puromycin for two days
20. Prepare fresh growth media containing 10 micromolar CCCP
21. Remove cultured media from cells
22. Add 4 milliliters of fresh CCCP-containing growth media to cells
23. Incubate cells at 37°C in CO₂ incubator for 6 hours
24. Remove growth medium
25. Wash cells once with PBS
26. Detach cells
27. Treat cells with trypsin EDTA solution
28. Collect cells by centrifugation
29. Re-suspend cells in 1 milliliter of PBS
30. Transfer cells to FACS tube
31. Place FACS tube on ice
32. Turn on flow cytometer
33. Start flow cytometry analysis software
34. Generate new experiment in software
35. Configure parameter window: select BV605 for violet laser and PE-CF594 for yellow-green laser
36. Run control sample of uninfected mt-Keima lentivirus cells
37. Draw polygon gate around live cells in forward scatter versus side scatter plot to exclude dead cells and debris
38. Run HeLa-Parkin cells infected with mt-Keima lentivirus
39. In BV605 vs side scatter dot plot, adjust voltage to distinguish mt-Keima-expressing cells from control cells
40. In PE-CF594 vs side scatter dot plot, adjust voltage to distinguish mt-Keima-expressing cells from control cells
41. Select mt-Keima-positive cell population in BV605 vs PE-CF594 dot plot using polygon gate tool
42. Create new BV605 vs PE-CF594 dot plot showing mt-Keima-gated cells
43. Draw gate around untreated mt-Keima-positive cells in new dot plot (designated as low gate)
44. Draw gate above low gate region (designated as high gate for high mitophagy activity cells)
45. Select Show Population Hierarchy menu
46. Run each sample
47. Record at least 10,000 events in mt-Keima stopping gate

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Grow a single Agrobacterium colony overnight in 5 ml of YEB medium supplemented with antibiotics in a 50 ml centrifuge tube at 28°C and 200 rpm
2. Measure optical density at 600 nm (OD600) of the culture the next morning
3. Centrifuge 1 ml of Agrobacterium culture when OD600 reaches 0.6-1.0
4. Resuspend pellet in 1 ml fresh YEB medium without antibiotics
5. Resuspend pellet in fresh antibiotic-free YEB medium to achieve final OD600 of 0.8 after second wash
6. Place bacterial cells on ice
7. Transfer donor plant from 2MS medium culture to 120x120 mm square plate
8. Inject 3 microliters of A.rhizogenes culture into multiple stem internodes using surgical needle
9. Transfer entire plant to new square plate containing solid MS medium supplemented with 0.1 mM acetosyringone
10. Place second transformed plant onto same plate
11. Seal plate with surgical tape
12. Place sealed plate vertically in growth cabinet for 4 days
13. Transfer plant to square plate with MS medium supplemented with cefotaxime sodium
14. Place sealed plate vertically in growth cabinet for 4 days
15. Excise native roots of each plant
16. Transfer plants to new square plate with MS medium supplemented with cefotaxime sodium
17. Grow transgenic hairy roots in MS medium with cefotaxime sodium for 3-4 weeks
18. Cut leaf from three to four week old plant and place in petri dish, excluding petiole
19. Make 1-3 transverse cuts from leaf center to edges using scalpel without detaching pieces
20. Transfer leaf to petri dish containing 10 ml fresh 2MS liquid medium with abaxial side up
21. Cover petri dish
22. Add 80 microliters of A.tumefaciens culture to liquid medium
23. Gently stir medium for 1 minute to homogenously distribute bacterial solution
24. Seal and cover plate with aluminum foil
25. Incubate plate at 24°C for two days
26. Transfer leaves abaxial-side up to callus-inducing medium
27. Scrape medium with tweezers
28. Incubate in growth cabinet for one week
29. Transfer leaves abaxial-side up to tweezer-scraped shoot-inducing medium
30. Incubate in growth cabinet
31. Cut three 2-cm tall emerged chutes from each callus
32. Place up to five chute transformation events into MG medium flasks supplemented with cefotaxime sodium
33. Label flasks appropriately
34. Incubate in growth cabinet for 3-4 weeks until chutes are vigorous
35. Select most vigorous plant of each event after incubation
36. Cut apical segments with 3-4 internodes from each selected plant
37. Place cut segments into culture flask containing 2MS medium supplemented with cefotaxime sodium
38. Grow plants in culture flask until development of vigorous shoots and roots
39. Fix roots from 2-3 week in-vitro culture in 90% chilled acetone for 20 minutes on ice
40. Wash roots two times in distilled water
41. Treat roots with fresh GUS-staining solution under vacuum for 20 minutes
42. Incubate roots at 37°C in dark for approximately 4 hours
43. Wash roots two times with 70% ethanol after blue color appears
44. Visualize staining under bright field microscope

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Add 1 mL of 10 mM HEPES in DMEM to tumor fragments
2. Mince tissue with sterilized razor blade
3. Add 9 mL of DMEM with HEPES, collagenase type 1/2, and thermolysin to tumor pieces
4. Incubate mixture for 1.5-2 hours at 37°C and 5% CO₂ on orbital shaker
5. Transfer cell suspension to 50 milliliter tube
6. Centrifuge tube at 400 × g for 5 minutes at 4°C
7. Aspirate supernatant
8. Resuspend pellet in 5 milliliters of ACK lysing buffer
9. Incubate tube at room temperature for 3-5 minutes to lyse red blood cells
10. Coat one well of 24-well plate with 150 microliters of ice-cold growth factor reduced basement membrane matrix
11. Place coated plate in incubator for 30 minutes
12. Add 20 milliliters of DMEM to cell-containing tube
13. Centrifuge cell suspension
14. Aspirate supernatant from centrifuged cells
15. Resuspend cell pellet in 1 milliliter of 0.25% trypsin EDTA and 10 micromolar Y-27632 dihydrochloride
16. Incubate tube for 5 minutes in 37°C water bath
17. Neutralize trypsin by adding 10 milliliters of DMEM with 10% FBS
18. Filter cell suspension through 100 micrometer cell strainer into new 50 milliliter tube
19. Centrifuge tube at 400 g for 5 minutes at 4°C
20. Aspirate supernatant
21. Resuspend pellet with 1 milliliter of DMEM
22. Count cells using hemocytometer
23. Transfer 3-4 × 10⁴ tumor cells to 1.5 ml microtube on ice
24. Centrifuge microtube at 400 g for 3 minutes at 4°C
25. Carefully remove supernatant
26. Resuspend cells in 500 microliters of prewarmed organoid medium with 10 µM Y-27632
27. Transfer cell suspension into membrane matrix coated well
28. Place 24-well plate into incubator
29. Replace medium every two days with 500 microliters of prewarmed organoid medium
30. Split tumor organoids
31. Thaw virus-containing aliquot in 37°C water bath
32. Add thawed virus to 250 microliters organoid medium containing 10 μM Y-27632 and 8 μg/mL hexadimethrine bromide
33. Replace organoid medium in 24-well plate with 500 microliters virus-containing medium
34. Incubate plate at 37°C and 5% CO₂ for 12-16 hours
35. Add 500 microliters of collagenase dispase to organoid medium in 24-well plate
36. Pipette basement membrane matrix and medium up and down to mix
37. Incubate plate for 20 minutes at 37 degrees Celsius
38. Collect cells into 15 milliliter tube
39. Add five milliliters of prewarmed DMEM
40. Centrifuge tube at 400 g for three minutes at 4 degrees Celsius
41. Aspirate supernatant
42. Resuspend pellet with 1 milliliter of DMEM
43. Transfer solution to 90 millimeter Petri dish
44. Pick up 10-100 tumor organoids under microscope using P200 pipette
45. Collect organoids into microtube placed on ice
46. Centrifuge microtube
47. Carefully remove supernatant from microtube
48. Maintain pellet on ice until mice are ready for surgery
49. Keep syringes, pipette tips, and basement membrane matrix on ice
50. Position anesthetized mouse in supine position
51. Maintain anesthesia via mask inhalation of 2% vaporized isoflurane
52. Apply povidone iodine with sterile gauze
53. Wipe down with 70% ethanol
54. Make transverse incision in skin and muscular wall of lower midline abdomen using sterile surgical scissors
55. Expose bladder from abdominal cavity
56. Support bladder with saline-soaked cotton swab
57. Resuspend organoid pellets in 80 microliters of organoid medium containing 50% high concentration basement membrane matrix
58. Inject suspension into anterior aspect of bladder dome using insulin syringe
59. Close incision with 4-0 nylon suture
60. Disinfect surgical site with povidone iodine and 70% ethanol
61. Allow mouse to recover under infrared irradiator for 10 to 15 minutes
62. Monitor mouse until it regains consciousness

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Place euthanized mouse torso in 35 mm Petri dish
2. Keep Petri dish with torso on ice
3. Open skin of torso using scissors
4. Remove vertebra using small scissors
5. Extract spinal cord
6. Place spinal cord in dissection buffer on ice
7. Position sample under stereotactic dissection microscope at 2x magnification
8. Remove meninges from spinal cord using forceps
9. Transfer dissected and cleaned spinal cord tissue to C tube
10. Add 50 microliters of enzyme P and 30 microliters of previously prepared enzyme mix to each C tube
11. Invert C tubes and place on heated dissociator, ensuring tissue collects in tube lid
12. Elute cells by removing column from separator, adding 800 microliters of astrocyte culture medium, and pushing cells out with plunger
13. Plate cells in prepared culture flasks by adding 4.2 milliliters of astrocyte culture medium supplemented with 10 ng/mL EGF and bFGF
14. Culture cells at 37 degrees Celsius and 5% carbon dioxide
15. Perform astrocyte seating under biological safety cabinet (safety level 1)
16. Prepare 24-well plates with Poly-D-Lysine coated glass cover slips
17. Isolate six spinal cords from P2 mice (yield ~1 million cells)
18. Aspirate media from T-12.5 culture flasks containing cultured astrocytes
19. Wash astrocytes once with 1x PBS
20. Add 0.5 milliliters of 0.05% trypsin-EDTA to culture flask
21. Incubate flask at 37 degrees Celsius for five minutes
22. Gently tap side of flask to release cells
23. Verify cell detachment under brightfield microscope at 10× magnification
24. Stop trypsinization by adding 2.5 milliliters of astrocyte culture medium
25. Collect cell suspension in 15 milliliter tubes
26. Centrifuge suspension at 300 RCF for five minutes at 4°C
27. Aspirate supernatant
28. Resuspend cell pellet in 1 milliliter of astrocyte culture medium
29. Calculate cell concentration using hemocytometer or automated cell counting system
30. Dilute cell suspension with fresh astrocyte medium supplemented with EGF and bFGF (10 ng/mL per factor)
31. Add 500 microliters of cell suspension to each well of 24-well plates
32. Culture cells at 37°C and 5% CO₂
33. Prepare neuronal differentiation medium by adding 1 mL B27 supplement to 49 mL basic culture medium
34. Replace astrocyte medium with 1 mL neuronal differentiation medium per well after 24-48 hours
35. Culture cells at 37°C with 9% CO₂
36. Supplement neuronal differentiation medium with forskolin (30 µM) and dorsomorphin (1 µM) during medium replacement
37. Add second dose of dorsomorphin directly to culture medium 2 days after initial treatment

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Rear adult leafhoppers on rice seedlings in 40×35×20 cm cage
2. Cover one cage side with insect-proof net for ventilation
3. Place cages in incubator with humidity controller
4. Set incubator temperature to 26°C
5. Maintain relative humidity at 60-75%
6. Set photoperiod to 16 hours light : 8 hours dark
7. Weekly transfer adults using aspirator to new cage with fresh rice seedlings
8. Allow >200 adults to mate and lay eggs in rice seedlings
9. Retain old rice seedlings for nymph emergence
10. Rear non-viruliferous nymphs to second instar stage
11. Transfer second instar non-viruliferous nymphs to glass culture tube using aspirator for starvation
12. Release nymphs into cage containing rice dwarf virus-infected rice plant for feeding
13. Transfer nymphs to new cage containing virus-free rice seedlings for feeding
14. Prepare five small pipe-like feeding cages
15. Cover one end of feeding cages with insect-proof netting
16. Confine 15-20 leafhoppers in each feeding cage
17. Cover other end of cage with thin foam mat
18. Fix one rice seedling between cage end and foam mat using tapes
19. Immerse seedling roots in water
20. Allow leafhoppers to feed on rice seedlings for two days
21. Remove leafhoppers from feeding cages
22. Collect rice seedlings fed upon by leafhoppers
23. Cut seedlings outside cage
24. Recover feeding regions of seedlings
25. Dilute 200 milliliters of 5x Tris-Glycine buffer with 800 milliliters of sterile water to prepare 1x Tris-Glycine buffer
26. Prepare 10x TBS buffer and autoclave at 121 degrees Celsius for 15 minutes
27. Prepare 4x protein sample buffer
28. Mix 800 milliliters of Tris-Glycine buffer with 200 milliliters of methanol to prepare transfer buffer
29. Add 100 milliliters of 10x TBS and 3 milliliters of Tween 20 to 900 milliliters of sterile water to prepare TBST solution
30. Grind 0.1 grams of rice samples with liquid nitrogen
31. Add 200 microliters of 4x protein sample buffer to ground sample
32. Boil sample-buffer mixture for 10 minutes
33. Centrifuge boiled samples
34. Transfer supernatant to new vial after centrifugation
35. Load 10 microliters of supernatant sample onto SDS-PAGE gel
36. Run SDS-PAGE gel in Tris-Glycine buffer at 150 volts for 45-60 minutes
37. Place 0.45 micron nitrocellulose membrane and sandwich supplies in transfer buffer for 30 minutes
38. Assemble gel and membrane transfer sandwich
39. Transfer proteins at 100 volts in transfer buffer for 90 to 120 minutes
40. Place membrane in 7% non-fat dry milk blocking solution in TBST
41. Incubate membrane in blocking solution for 20 minutes
42. Add primary antibody against rice dwarf virus P8 or vitellogenin
43. Incubate membrane with primary antibody for two hours or overnight
44. Wash membrane with TBST three times with 5-minute incubations between washes
45. Add Goat anti-Rabbit IgG secondary antibody
46. Incubate membrane with secondary antibody for 60 to 90 minutes
47. Mix ECL Western kit detection reagents one and two at 1:1 ratio
48. Place membrane onto mixed detection reagent
49. Incubate blot for 5 minutes
50. Drain excess reagent from membrane
51. Capture chemiluminescent and colorimetric image of membrane
52. Combine images to visualize protein bands

Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Thread 20 G needle into one end of polyethylene tubing
2. Place tubing in self-heal pouch for gas sterilization
3. Place collagen into 15 milliliter conical tube
4. Keep collagen-containing tube on ice
5. Add 128 microliters of 10x alpha MEM per milliliter of collagen to tube
6. Mix solution by repeatedly pipetting while minimizing bubble formation
7. Mix solution by repeatedly pipetting while minimizing bubble formation
8. Add 0.8 M sodium bicarbonate to neutralize collagen
9. Continue adding sodium bicarbonate until solution turns light pink (target pH ~7.4), using approximately 30-60 μL per mL of collagen
10. Store neutralized collagen on ice to prevent gelling until ready for use
11. Transfer bag containing sterilized polyethylene tubing to biological safety cabinet
12. Cut off sealed end of bag while maintaining tubing inside bag
13. Use sterilized tweezers to attach 3ml syringe to 20-gauge needle pre-inserted in tubing end
14. Insert free end of tubing into neutralized collagen in conical tube until tip reaches bottom (conical tube maintained on ice)
15. Slowly retract syringe plunger to draw collagen into polyethylene tubing (avoiding air bubble introduction)
16. Remove 20-gauge needle and place both tubing ends back into bag
17. Seal bag with tape
18. Incubate sealed assembly at 37°C for 60 minutes to gel collagen
19. Place collagen-filled tubing in sterile tray in front of cutting device

27. Allow modules to settle to bottom of tube for 5 minutes
28. Transfer settled modules to 15 milliliter conical tube using wide mouth 10 milliliter serological pipette
29. Vortex 15 milliliter conical tube
30. Allow modules to settle after first vortex
31. Transfer settled modules from second vortex to 15 milliliter tube

1. Fit peristaltic pump with two tubes

4. Spray and wipe abdomen of euthanized 250-350g Sprague-Dawley rat with 70% ethanol
5. Perform XO pubic laparotomy incision using autoclave-sterilized surgical instruments
6. Eviscerate small intestine to right side onto ethanol-sprayed paper towel
7. Continuously wet intestine with PBS AA
8. Place suture around SMA and prepare knot
9. Insert 27-gauge cannula into SMA from aorta
10. Withdraw needle from cannula
11. Advance plastic cannula tubing into SMA
12. Secure cannula tubing with 5-0 Vicryl sutures
13. Inject 10 mL PBS to confirm cannulation
14. Incise aorta proximal to SMA using scissors
15. Incise aorta distal to SMA to release cannula
16. Place 5-0 silk suture knot on proximal ileocecal junction
17. Place 5-0 silk suture knot on distal sigmoid colon
18. Cut terminal ileum and colon between suture knots
19. Remove large intestine
20. Cut small intestine at duodenojejunal junction
21. Cut small intestine at duodenal junction
22. Free intestine from abdominal connective tissue
23. Transfer small intestine to Petri dish containing PBS
24. Cannulate proximal intestine section with plastic pipette and secure with sutures
25. Cut pipette to fit luer lock of 60 milliliter syringe
26. Flush intestinal lumen with two 60-milliliter washes of PBS to remove feces and debris
27. Ensure no bubbles are present within intestinal lumen and vascular cannulas
28. Connect three-way stop cock to vascular cannula

30. Start running deionized water through master flex LS variable speed roller pump at 0.6 milliliters per hour
31. Handle tissue carefully to ensure bubbles exit from distal end
32. Transfer apparatus to cold room after bubble removal
33. Start decellularization process

35. Set perfusion to 0.6 milliliters per hour flow rate during the first hour
36. Regularly check apparatus connections for security, minimal leakage, and absence of bubbles within intestine
37. Move decellularization apparatus to room temperature after 24 hours
38. Weigh sodium deoxycholate for 4% solution in 300 milliliters deionized water
39. Vortex-mix solution until clear
40. Replace deionized solution with sodium deoxycholate solution and check connections
41. Remove bubbles and perfuse small intestine at 0.6 milliliters per hour for 4 hours
42. Wash intestine with PBS AA for 30 minutes to prevent chemical interactions
43. Perfuse tissue with freshly prepared DNase I at room temperature (0.6 mL/hour flow rate)
44. Transfer scaffold into tissue culture hood
45. Change plate using aseptic technique
46. Re-sterilize peristaltic pump tubing by running ethanol through
47. Wash intestine with PBS for 30 minutes to remove decellularization solution remnants
48. Transfer scaffold to UV crosslinker
49. Run two UV sterilization cycles
50. Store scaffold in PBS
51. Change PBS storage solution every three days
52. Inject dye through SMA cannula
53. Assess scaffold using DNA quantification kit
54. Perform hematoxylin and eosin staining

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Cover 2/3 of conductive side of FTO coated glass slide with semi-transparent acrylic adhesive tape
2. Coat uncovered areas of slide with zinc powder
3. Pour 2M hydrochloric acid solution in distilled water over slide to etch substrate
4. Wipe residue from exposed slide sections using cotton swab
5. Rinse etched FTO substrate with distilled water
6. Remove tape from substrate
7. Wash etched surface in 2% w/v alkaline liquid detergent concentrate solution
8. Sonicate FTO substrate for 10 minutes in acetone bath
9. Sonicate FTO substrate for 10 minutes in isopropanol bath
10. Treat FTO substrate in oxygen plasma cleaner for 15 minutes
11. Place etched and cleaned FTO substrate on hot plate at 450°C
12. Cover contact area with precut glass slide
13. Allow substrate to heat to 450°C
14. Mix 0.6 mL TAA with 7 mL isopropanol
15. Apply TAA solution to substrate via spray pyrolysis using air carrier gas at 450°C
16. Maintain sample at 450°C for 30 minutes
17. Cool sample to room temperature
18. Remove glass cover after substrate cooling
19. Dilute 30 nanomolar titanium oxide paste with ethanol to 2:7 weight ratio
20. Sonicate suspension for 30 minutes
21. Spin coat sample with suspension for 30 seconds at 5,000 RPM with 2,000 RPM/s ramp rate
22. Anneal titanium film at 500°C for 30 minutes to form mesoporous titanium oxide film
23. Immerse sample in 40 millimolar titanium chloride solution in distilled water
24. Treat sample at 70°C for 20 minutes
25. Anneal titanium chloride-treated sample at 450°C for 30 minutes
26. Transfer sample to nitrogen-filled glove box with <1% humidity
27. Add 1 milliliter of DMF to 553 milligrams of lead iodide
28. Heat mixture to 80 degrees Celsius while stirring continuously
29. Continue heating until lead iodide dissolves
30. Prepare 0.02 molar solution of monovalent cation halide in lead iodide solution
31. Apply 80 microliters of monovalent cation halide in lead iodide solution to substrate
32. Spin coat substrate for 30 seconds at 6,500 RPM with 4,000 RPM/s ramp rate
33. Bake thin film at 80 degrees Celsius for 30 minutes
34. Dissolve 40 milligrams of methylammonium iodide in 5 milliliters of isopropanol
35. Place lead iodide coated substrate in spin coater
36. Apply 120 microliters of MAI solution to substrate
37. Allow MAI solution to sit on substrate for 45 seconds
38. Spin coat substrate with MAI for 20 seconds at 4,000 RPM
39. Anneal perovskite film at 100 degrees Celsius for 45 minutes
40. Add 1 milliliter of chlorobenzene to 72.3 milligrams of spiro-methoxyTAD
41. Shake mixture until solution appears transparent
42. Prepare stock solution of 520 milligrams of lithium TFSI in 1 milliliter of acetonitrile
43. Mix 17.5 microliters of lithium TFSI solution with 28.8 microliters of 4-tert-butylpyridine
44. Combine mixture with spiro-methoxyTAD solution
45. Spin coat sample with mixture for 30 seconds at 4,000 RPM with 2,000 RPM/s ramp rate
46. Cover sample with mask pattern for solar cell top contacts
47. Deposit 80 nanometer gold layer onto sample at 0.01 nm/s rate

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Flame worm pick over Bunsen burner
2. Scoop fresh E. coli from edge of bacterial lawn using pick
3. Transfer scooped E. coli onto large-scale culture plate
4. Pick single gravid adult from fourth chunk plate
5. Add 5 microliters of freshly prepared alkaline hypochlorite solution to corner of plate away from E. coli lawn
6. Place gravid adult into alkaline hypochlorite solution
7. Tap nematode to disrupt cuticle and release eggs
8. Place total of five adults evenly around E. coli lawn using same method
9. Place lid back onto plate
10. Pour 50 milliliters of M9 solution onto large-scale culture plate surface
11. Swirl plate to ensure M9 covers entire nematode growth medium agarose surface
12. Prime sterile serological pipette with M9
13. Tilt plate to gather M9 and worm population in one corner
14. Aspirate worm suspension using primed pipette and add to 50 milliliter conical tube
15. Place tube on rocker to disrupt bacterial clumps and debris
16. Transfer 15 milliliters of worms from each collection tube into three 15 milliliter conical tubes
17. Sediment worms in tubes by centrifugation
18. Carefully aspirate supernatants without disturbing worm pellets
19. Add 13 milliliters of worm suspension to each tube
20. Invert tubes to resuspend pellets and wash off bacteria/debris
21. Centrifuge tubes again
22. Wash worm pellets three times with 10 milliliters of fresh M9 solution per wash
23. Dilute three 100 microliter aliquots of worm sample in 900 microliters of M9 solution per sample
24. Make serial dilutions using the aliquots
25. Place stock worm sample suspensions on a rocker for continuous movement
26. Mix worm dilutions until homogeneous
27. Add five microliters of solution from first dilution to glass microscope slide
28. Count worms by light microscopy
29. Average dilution counts after counting each aliquot replicate three times
30. Split worm samples into experimental aliquots
31. Flash freeze samples in liquid nitrogen for -80°C storage
32. Dilute approximately 5×10⁴ mixed stage worm aliquot to 10 ml M9 solution
33. Add 200 µl of 1 mg/ml E. coli and 1:50 dilution of 0.5 µM red fluorescent microsphere solution to worm suspension
34. Incubate at room temperature with rocking for 20 minutes
35. Collect worms and microspheres by centrifugation
36. Wash worms twice with fresh M9 solution to remove excess bacteria and uninternalized microspheres
37. Resuspend pellet in 5 ml M9 solution after second wash
38. Add 5 ml M9 solution supplemented with 50 mM sodium azide to worms
39. Place tube on rocker to straighten and euthanize worms
40. Open calibrated 384-well plate template
41. Set template to dispense 20 gated objects into four wells
42. Load 40 milliliter sample of worms onto large particle flow cytometer
43. Begin automatic sorting of sample
44. Continuously stir sample to prevent settling
45. Dispense objects from sample into calibrated 384-well plate
46. Remove sample from cytometer
47. Clean instrument

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Prepare 1 mL of 3% w/v low-melting temperature agarose in 1X TE buffer
2. Maintain agarose solution on 90°C heat block until syringe loading
3. Resuspend cells in 1 mL PBS
4. Centrifuge cell suspension
5. Aspirate supernatant from centrifuged cells
6. Resuspend cell pellet in 1 mL of 17% v/v density gradient medium in PBS
7. Maintain cell suspension on ice until syringe loading
8. Load 3ml syringe with fluorinated oil (HFE) containing 2% w/w PFPE-PEG surfactant
9. Fit 27-gauge needle to 3ml syringe
10. Place 3ml syringe into syringe pump
11. Load cell suspension into 1ml syringe
12. Load molten agarose into 1ml syringe
13. Fit 27-gauge needles to both 1ml syringes
14. Place both 1ml syringes into syringe pumps
15. Set space heater to high setting
16. Position space heater 10cm away from syringe
17. Verify syringe temperature reaches 80°C
18. Prime pumps to remove air from the line
19. Connect syringe needles to microfluidic device inlets using polyethylene (PE) tubing
20. Connect tubing to the outlet
21. Place free end of outlet tubing in 15-milliliter collection tube
22. Place collection tube at four degrees Celsius for 30 minutes to ensure complete gelation of agarose
23. Remove lower layer of oil from collection tube using 3-milliliter syringe with 20-gauge needle, avoiding disturbance of top agarose droplet layer
24. Add 1 milliliter of 10% v/v PFO in HFE to break agarose drops from surfactant layer
25. Pipette solution up and down for 1 minute to achieve homogeneous emulsion coating without clumps
26. Spin conical tube at 2,000 × g for 1 minute to collect agarose microgels
27. Aspirate PFO/HFE supernatant
28. Verify cell encapsulation in microgels under microscope at 400× magnification using nucleic acid stain
29. Plug cells inlet of co-flow dropmaker device with small piece of lead solder
30. Prime pumps to remove air from line before inserting tubes
31. Connect piece of tubing to outlet
32. Place free end of tubing in 0.2-milliliter PCR tube
33. Collect approximately 50 microliters of drops into PCR tubes
34. Prime pumps to remove air from lines
35. Connect syringes containing HFE, tagmentation mix, and microgels to microfluidic device inlets using PE tubing
36. Connect tubing to outlet and place free end in empty 1-mL syringe with plunger drawn to 1-mL line
37. Verify microgel encapsulation rate under light microscope at 400X magnification
38. Fit syringe containing tagmentation emulsions with needle
39. Incubate syringe upright in heat block/oven at 55°C for 1 hour to fragment genomic DNA
40. Replace FC-40 oil fraction with HFE 2% weight per weight PFPE-PEG in barcode droplets
41. Transfer barcode droplets into 1-mL syringe
42. Fit syringe with needle and place into syringe pump
43. Load incubated and tagmented microgel droplet syringe into syringe pump
44. Connect three sodium chloride syringes to two electrode inlets and single moat inlet using PE tubing
45. Prime pumps to remove air from line before tube insertion
46. Connect three HFE syringes to two spacer oil inlets and dropmaking oil inlet using PE tubing
47. Treat all droplet reinjection tubing with antistatic gun
48. Connect treated tubing to syringe needles
49. Connect PCR mix syringe, microgel drop syringe, and barcode drop syringe to respective inlets with PE tubing
50. Connect electrode syringe needle to cold cathode fluorescent inverter using alligator clip
51. Set inverter's DC power supply to two volts
52. Run double merger device with recommended flow rates
53. Collect drops into PCR tubes with approximately 50 microliters of emulsion per tube
54. Remove lower layer of HFE oil from emulsions using gel-loading pipette tips
55. Replace removed oil with FC-40 fluorinated oil containing 5% w/w PFPE-PEG surfactant
56. Program thermal cycle
57. Pool droplets into microcentrifuge tube
58. Add 20 microliters of PFO to break emulsions
59. Vortex tubes for 10 seconds
60. Centrifuge tube
61. Pipette upper aqueous layer from tube
62. Transfer aqueous layer to new microcentrifuge tube
63. Discard oil phase

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Cut small agar pieces from C. elegans plate using sterile spatula
2. Transfer agar pieces to individual NA22 E. coli plates
3. Incubate plates at 20°C for 3-4 days
4. Visually confirm presence of gravid adults under stereo microscope
5. Squirt water onto plate containing gravid adult worms
6. Gently swirl plate to dislodge worms
7. Transfer dislodged worms to 15ml polystyrene conical tube using disposable pipette
8. Transfer worms from second plate into the same conical tube
9. Centrifuge worms and wash three times with 15ml autoclaved water per wash
10. Verify water clarity after final wash
11. Add 5 mL of freshly prepared sodium hypochlorite sodium hydroxide solution to worms
12. Mix solution rapidly with vortex
13. Incubate tube on rocker
14. Place drop of worm solution on glass microscope slide and check lysis under stereoscope every 2 minutes for 4-8 minutes
15. Observe when approximately 70% of worms are lysed and eggs released
16. Immediately stop lysis by adding egg buffer
17. Centrifuge embryos and worm carcasses
18. Perform four wash cycles: resuspend pellet in fresh egg buffer and centrifuge between washes to remove residual bleach
19. Verify pellet appears white after final wash
20. Resuspend pellet in 5 mL of autoclaved water
21. Wash L1 larvae three times with autoclaved water
22. Resuspend larval pellet in 1 milliliter of water
23. Dilute worm aliquot to 1:10 ratio
24. Add 10 microliter worm sample drop onto glass slide
25. Count worms under stereo microscope
26. Repeat counting to obtain average worm count
27. Incubate plate covered and upside down at 20 degrees Celsius for 44 to 48 hours or until worms reach late L4 stage
28. Confirm late L4 stage visually under stereo microscope
29. Add 40 microliters of control solution (with or without 0.5 mM amphetamine) into glass spot plate
30. Pick 8-10 late L4 stage worms using eyelash pick under stereoscope and submerge pick in solution well until worms detach and swim into solution
31. Start timer
32. Record number of worms released into well
33. Start timer
34. Record number of worms released into well
35. Observe and record number of worms exhibiting SWIP at each minute mark for 10 minutes
36. Copy raw paralysis data into spreadsheet
37. Calculate percent paralysis: (number paralyzed per minute / total worms tested) × 100
38. Set up camera, worm tracker software, and analysis script
39. Place single late-stage L4 worm into glass spot plate using eyelash pick
40. Record swimming video of one worm
41. Calculate body bend frequency using worm tracker software and provided script

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Check surgical plane of anesthesia via absence of reaction to toe pinch
2. Shave dorsal area of rat from lower back to neck
3. Position rat dorsal side up
4. Place rat on 40°C heating pad to maintain body temperature
5. Sterilize shaved area with iodine-soaked cotton balls followed by 75% alcohol wipes
6. Make skin incision over laminectomy site
7. Make skin incision over laminectomy site covering thoracic vertebrae T7 to T11
8. Cut attached muscles on both sides from T8 to T10 to expose spinous processes, laminae, and facet joints
9. Use scalpel to make incisions disconnecting T10-T11 junction
10. Dissect muscle layer away from T10-T11 junction to expose bone
11. Use scissors to clear muscle from lamina and around pedicle with small snips
12. Insert hemostatic forceps into T10-T11 gap and break pedicle
13. Repeat pedicle breaking procedure on contralateral side
14. Lift and break off T10 lamina to expose spinal cord without leaving bone fragments
15. Repeat lamina removal process for T9 and T8 vertebrae
16. Prepare to use impactor for concussion injury induction
17. Place rat dorsal side up on black non-reflective background
18. Click Measure button to enter measurement graphical user interface
19. Click Scanner Setup button to open Scanner Setup interface
20. Click Scan Size and Display options and select High Resolution mode
21. Click Image Scan option to check scanning parameters
22. Click Video and Distance option to check live video image
23. Position scanner 10-13 cm above surgical window
24. Move background with animal to center exposed spinal cord in scanning window
25. Select Auto Distance function to fine adjust scanning height
26. Apply non-reflective cover with window to expose surgical area and mark animal's direction
27. Attach probe perpendicular to stereotaxic instrument
28. Place rat on stereotaxic apparatus dorsal side up
29. Underlay rat with small Styrofoam piece to level exposed spinal cord
30. Remove excessive liquid or blood from incision using cotton pad
31. Position probe 2mm rostral to exposed spinal cord/lesion point using x- and y-axes
32. Avoid central vein during probe positioning
33. Lower probe to spinal cord surface level using z-axis
34. Position probe to touch spinal cord surface without pressure or light leakage
35. Click 'New Experiment' button in software
36. Check system configuration under General option
37. Click 'Next' after configuration check
38. Select blood flow and oxygen saturation channels in Display setup
39. Input file information
40. Click 'Next' to enter recording interface
41. Click green triangle button to start probe data recording
42. Record data for 8 minutes after signal stabilization
43. Lift probe from subject
44. Remove animal from stereotaxic apparatus
45. Place rat on its side in clean cage on heating pad, avoiding contact between surgery site and cage bottom
46. Monitor animal until anesthesia recovery to ensure no post-surgery bleeding and suture integrity

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Dissolve 0.4 millimoles of tetrabutylammonium tetrafluoroborate and 4×10⁻³ millimoles of organic compound in 4 ml dichloromethane
2. Pipette 2 ml of working solution into 3 ml electrochemical cell (glass V vial)
3. Close electrochemical cell with gasket cap
4. Store remaining working solution for later measurements
5. Mount polishing cloth on immobile support
6. Moisten cloth with 0.05 micrometer alumina slurry
7. Polish 1mm diameter platinum disk working electrode for 30 seconds under moderate pressure
8. Rinse polished working electrode with DCM three times to remove residual alumina
9. Insert polished electrode
10. Obtain platinum wire counterelectrode
11. Ignite butane torch
12. Anneal platinum wire counterelectrode in flame until reddening
13. Anneal silver wire reference electrode in flame
14. Allow both electrodes to cool
15. Mount wire electrodes
16. Mount wire electrodes in electrochemical cell through gasket cap
17. Ensure electrodes do not touch each other
18. Connect three electrodes to potentiostat
19. Equip electrochemical cell with argon gas line
20. Bubble argon through working solution for 20 minutes
21. Close argon flow before measurements
22. Open cyclic voltammetry program in potentiostat software
23. Set initial potential to zero volts
24. Set minimum potential to negative two volts
25. Set maximal scanning potential to two volts
26. Set scanning rate to 100 millivolts per second
27. Acquire voltammogram of working solution
28. Note potential values at maxima of anodic and cathodic peaks
29. Calculate average of peak potentials
30. Add approximately 10 milligrams of ferrocene to working solution in electrochemical cell using spatula
31. Bubble argon through solution for 5 minutes to dissolve ferrocene
32. Adjust cyclic voltammetry program parameters: set minimal scanning potential to -1 V and maximal scanning potential to +1 V
33. Acquire voltammogram to record ferrocene redox trace
34. Average anodic and cathodic peak potentials of ferrocene to estimate reversible oxidation potential
35. Determine redox potential of organic compound relative to ferrocene
36. Clean electrochemical cell by filling with DCM and emptying five times
37. Place 2 ml of working solution in clean 3 ml electrochemical cell
38. Clean electrodes as previously described
39. Insert cleaned electrodes into electrochemical cell
40. Reconnect electrodes to potentiostat
41. Deaerate working solution by bubbling argon for 20 minutes
42. Open staircase EIS program in potentiostat software
43. Set potential range to 0.2 volts (0.1 volts on either side of redox potential)
44. Set potential increment to 0.01 volts
45. Set frequency range to 10 kilohertz to 100 hertz
46. Set number of frequencies in logarithmic scale to 20
47. Set wait time to 5 seconds
48. Set A/C voltage amplitude to 10 millivolts
49. Set measures per frequency to 2
50. Run experiment and wait for set of spectra
51. Import automatically registered EIS spectrum
52. Construct simple equivalent electrical circuit for spectrum
53. Set capacitor initial limits to 1×10⁻⁷ (upper) and 1×10⁻⁸ (lower)
54. Set resistor one initial limits to 2000 (upper) and 100 (lower)
55. Set resistor two initial limits to 1000 (upper) and 100 (lower)
56. Fit the model
57. Repeat fitting process until calculated values stabilize
58. Test alternative EEC if R-squared parametric and amplitude values exceed 1×10⁻²
59. Set initial Warburg element limits to upper=50,000 and lower=10,000 for complex EECs
60. Remove parameters with >100% error values and test alternative EEC
