1. Seed 350,000 human adipose tissue stromal cells onto treated T75 flask with 15 milliliters of low glucose TMEM
2. Remove culture medium when cells reach 80% confluency
3. Rinse cells with 5 milliliters of DPBS without calcium and magnesium
4. Incubate cells in 5 milliliters of trypsin and DPBS for 2 minutes at 37°C
5. Stop enzymatic reaction with 3 milliliters of cell culture medium after cell detachment
6. Transfer cells into 50 milliliter conical tube
7. Centrifuge the cells
8. Resuspend pellet in 2 milliliters of cell culture medium
9. Count the cells
10. Transfer 1.3 × 10⁶ cell aliquot into 15 milliliter conical tube
11. Centrifuge cells again
12. Suspend pellet in 1 milliliter of freshly prepared bioink with homogeneous distribution throughout aqueous alginate solution
13. Load cells into sterile 3 milliliter syringe
14. Screw on sterile 22 gauge plastic tip
15. Turn on bioprint dispenser computers
16. Turn on recirculating water bath
17. Manually set water bath temperature to 4 degrees Celsius
18. Configure printing parameters on correlating dispenser computers
19. Set dispense volume to 230 nanoliters and back steps to zero
20. Set dispensary rate to 10 microliters per second
21. Open design software and USB camera viewing program
22. Manually enter coordinates for 5x5 dot array with 2.4 mm spacing
23. Save program and send to robot
24. Place gelatin titanium dioxide Petri dish on 4°C printer stage
25. Close and lock chamber door
26. Initialize ultraviolet light sources using programmable logic controller to sterilize chamber
27. Open chamber after 90 seconds sterilization
28. Load syringe containing human adipose tissue stromal cell suspension into gun one
29. Close and lock chamber door
30. Turn on fan system using programmable logic controller
31. Wait 30 seconds for internal pressure equilibration
32. Run program containing geometrical pathway and printing parameters
33. Monitor USB camera display on computer to confirm printing accuracy and uniformity
34. Immerse bioprinted constructs in freshly prepared stain solution
35. Place constructs in refrigerator for 15 minutes in the dark
36. Image stained constructs using confocal microscope

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Fill PDMS negative with 400 microliters of MY-133-V-2000 resin
2. Remove MY-133-V-2000 from vacuum chamber
3. Press glass slide against top of overfilled negative to create flat surface
4. Insert MY-133-V-2000 into 400 watt UV oven
5. Set UV radiation to 50% maximum intensity for 300 seconds to cure microchannel
6. Place two small drops of glue on edge of acrylic
7. Spread glue evenly using disposable tool
8. Place glass substrate onto acrylic
9. Allow glue to dry using glass weight for positioning
10. Coat exposed glass with 100 microliters of PDMS using positive displacement pipette
11. Insert base layer into vacuum spin coater
12. Spin coat at 1500 RPM for 2 minutes to achieve 10 micrometer PDMS film
13. Remove base layer from spin coater
14. Wipe excess PDMS from acrylic using acetone and paper towel
15. Bake base layer with PDMS in oven at 65°C for 2 hours
16. Pipette 1 milliliter of PDMS onto glass slide
17. Spread PDMS evenly using second glass slide until it oozes out sides
18. Cure PDMS on hot plate at 150°C for 10 minutes
19. Cut cured PDMS into rectangle matching MY-133-V-2000 device dimensions
20. Punch reservoir holes in PDMS using midlayer acrylic mold
21. Cut square viewing window in PDMS to complete gasket
22. Remove cured MY-133-V-2000 device from UV oven
23. Remove channel from negative mold and place channel-side up on flat substrate
24. Remove base layer with cured PDMS from 65°C oven
25. Place base layer onto substrate
26. Place substrate with channel and base layer into oxygen plasma cleaner
27. Set vacuum pressure to 200 millitorr
28. Set radio frequency level to high
29. Surface treat channel and glass substrate for 30 seconds
30. Remove microchannel and base layer from plasma cleaner
31. Place MY-133-V-2000 channel side down into rectangular cutout of base layer acrylic using forceps to contact PDMS
32. Place PDMS gasket on top of MY-133-V-2000 device
33. Align holes in PDMS gasket with reservoirs in the device
34. Place unfinished device on raised platform above bench
35. Extract 3 milliliters of acrylic cement using syringe
36. Distribute acrylic cement on base layer to form thin even coating, avoiding channel seepage
37. Place mid-layer acrylic piece on base layer, aligning holes with MY-133-V-2000 channel reservoirs
38. Clamp mid-layer tightly onto base layer and hold for 2 minutes for cement hardening and bonding
39. Remove pressure from the device
40. Apply acrylic cement to midlayer at anticipated top layer contact points
41. Place top acrylic layer onto midlayer
42. Align holes with reservoirs
43. Allow assembly to rest on bench for 2 minutes for cement drying
44. Add 10 microliters of food dye or deionized water into one reservoir hole
45. Insert tube into opposite reservoir
46. Connect tube end to vacuum trap
47. Turn vacuum on to pull dye/water through channel
48. Check under microscope for leaks in channel or reservoir
49. Remove dye from both reservoirs using vacuum
50. Rinse reservoirs with ethanol
51. Place ethanol in one reservoir
52. Pull ethanol through channel using vacuum
53. Allow ethanol to sit at room temperature for 10 minutes
54. Spray channel thoroughly with ethanol
55. Place device into sterile polystyrene dish
56. Wrap dish tightly with Parafilm for storage

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Induce anesthesia with isoflurane
2. Shave the scalp
3. Wipe down skin with Betadine three times
4. Coat animal's eyes with sterile petroleum jelly
5. Administer postoperative analgesia
6. Make 1 cm midline sagittal incision into scalp
7. Expose skull landmarks through incision
8. Prepare tail beginning 1 cm from base
9. Shave 1 cm along ventral surface of tail starting 1 cm from base
10. Wipe shaved tail region with Betadine three times
11. Secure mouse in stereotaxic frame
12. Maintain mouse temperature at 37°C using thermistor-controlled heating pad
13. Monitor temperature using rectal thermometer
14. Attach sterile 27-gauge needle to 1cc syringe on stereotaxic arm
15. Position needle at 2.5mm lateral to BMA on skull surface
16. Rotate syringe to initiate bur hole on skull surface
17. Apply gentle downward force using frame
18. Remove needle from stereotaxic arm after hole initiation
19. Complete bur hole manually until inner table perforation sensation
20. Place paraffin wax paper under the tail
21. Make transverse incision along shaved tail surface
22. Collect two to three large drops of arterial blood on wax paper
23. Apply pressure with sterile gauze to stop bleeding
24. Draw 17 microliters of blood sample into Hamilton syringe
25. Attach syringe to pump
26. Secure syringe to stereotaxic arm on frame
27. Angle stereotaxic arm to 5 degrees medially relative to vertical axis
28. Position needle tip over bur hole
29. Lower needle 3.5 millimeters into brain
30. Wait 2 minutes
31. Withdraw needle 0.5 millimeters to final depth of 3 millimeters
32. Wait two minutes
33. Withdraw needle half a millimeter to position tip at three millimeters depth
34. Wait five minutes for brain re-expansion around needle
35. Inject 7.5 microliters of blood at rate of one microliter per minute
36. Wait five minutes after injection
37. Inject 7.5 microliters of blood at a rate of one microliter per minute
38. Leave needle in place for 25 minutes to allow blood clotting
39. Slowly withdraw needle after clotting period
40. Rinse needle with hot water to prevent clotting
41. Remove mouse from frame
42. Close tail and scalp incisions with veterinary surgical glue
43. Turn off anesthesia
44. Place wet food pellets on cage bottom for easier access
45. Place mouse in warmed recovery chamber with food and water access
46. Return mouse to home cage when ready

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Isolate RNA using a method that preserves small RNAs
2. Resuspend isolated RNA at concentration ≥2 mg/ml
3. Mix 25 μg total RNA with 0.5 μg 5'P-Cy3-dye in 20 μl reaction volume
4. Add mixture to 1X HEPs buffer
5. Supplement reaction with T4 RNA Ligase 1D TT and ATP
6. Scale down RNA amount and reaction volume if less RNA is available
7. Add carrier (e.g., yeast tRNA) if RNA is dilute to aid precipitation
8. Incubate reaction on ice in refrigerator for 2-24 hours
9. Add sodium acetate to 0.3 M final concentration
10. Add three volumes of ethanol
11. Precipitate RNA overnight at -20°C
12. Pre-hybridize slides in filtered solution (3X SSC, 0.1% SDS, 0.2% BSA) at 37°C for 30-60 minutes
13. Submerge slides in water several times
14. Submerge slides in isopropanol
15. Dry slides using centrifuge with slide adapter (100xg, 5 minutes, 22°C)
16. Rinse lifter slips in water
17. Rinse lifter slips in ethanol
18. Air-dry lifter slips
19. Place slide in microarray hybridization chamber base
20. Position lifter slip on slide to cover probe area and white strips
21. Contact slide in dark
22. Spin down precipitated labeled RNA
23. Wash RNA pellet once with 70% ethanol
24. Air dry RNA pellet
25. Prepare hybridization solution using 1/15 to 1/100 volume purified labeled reference DNA per RNA sample
26. Dissolve RNA pellet completely with approximately 60 microliters hybridization solution
27. Add 20 microliters water to each sample during incubation
28. Add 20 microliters of water to each humidifying well in microarray hybridization chamber base
29. Add mixture of labeled RNA and reference DNA to slide
30. Use thin pipette tip to touch edge of lifter slip and apply suction to draw solution into space between slip and slide
31. Place hybridization chamber cover over base
32. Seal chamber with metal clips
33. Place entire chamber inside provided plastic bag
34. Place chamber cassette in container with wet paper towel
35. Cover container with plastic wrap and place in 37°C humid cell culture incubator for 24 hours
36. Disassemble hybridization chamber cassettes one by one
37. Submerge slide and lifter slip in 2X SSC buffer at 22°C
38. Wash slides twice in 0.8X SSC buffer
39. Wash slides three times in 0.4X SSC buffer for 1-2 minutes total
40. Dry slides using centrifuge with slide adapter
41. Scan slides with microarray scanner
42. Quantify spot intensities using software (e.g., Blue Fuse)
43. Inspect individual spots to exclude abnormal artifacts
44. Rinse slide in water
45. Immerse slide in staining dish containing prewarmed 1 mM NaOH and 0.1X SSC at 62°C
46. Incubate slide in solution for 10-20 minutes
47. Repeat incubation in solution a second time
48. Wash slides several times in water for up to 60 minutes with gentle shaking at 22°C
49. Dry slides using centrifuge
50. Store slides at 22°C

1. Pre-chill mortar and pestle with liquid nitrogen

3. Add sufficient liquid nitrogen to completely submerge sample using ladle
4. Freeze samples in liquid nitrogen for 10-30 seconds

6. Break tissue into smaller fragments using vertical crushing motion
7. Complete tissue grinding using circular grinding motion
8. Repeat freezing and grinding cycle twice more (total three cycles)

11. Store samples at -80°C until ready for extraction

13. Fill volumetric flask to 10 mL line with ultra pure water to dissolve acid
14. Transfer stock solution to clean 15 mL glass tube with PTFE-lined cap
15. Seal tube with plastic paraffin film
16. Store sealed tubes at 4°C for one week
17. Prepare 50 ml of SCCA extraction solution by diluting internal standard stock solution to 0.05 mg/ml in ultra pure water
18. Add 250 microliters of internal standard stock solution to 49.75 milliliters of ultra pure water
19. Prepare multi-point calibration standards for SCCAs of interest with ≥5 concentrations spanning expected sample range
20. Dilute each SCCA to target concentration in new micro-centrifuge tubes using ultra pure water to 1 ml final volume
21. Remove samples from storage
22. Place samples on ice
23. Weigh 100 milligrams of sample for SCCA extraction
24. Measure sample mass as close to 100mg target as possible
25. Transfer weighed sample to clean 1.5ml micro-centrifuge tube
26. Record mass of each sample for SCCA normalization
27. Add 1 milliliter of extraction solution to each sample tube
28. Vortex samples for 10 seconds to mix well
29. Incubate samples at room temperature for 1 hour
30. Vortex each tube for 10 seconds every 15 minutes during incubation
31. Vortex samples for final mixing and transfer to micro-centrifuge
32. Centrifuge samples at 4°C and 10,000×g for 10 minutes to precipitate solid material
33. Prepare syringe-mounted disc filters
34. Ensure correct attachment of filters to syringes
35. Prepare one syringe-equipped disc filter per sample (including standards)
36. Filter supernatants into clean micro-centrifuge tubes
37. Close tube containing filtrate
38. Discard syringe filter apparatus
39. Transfer 1 mL of sample to each capillary electrophoresis (CE) vial while avoiding splashing into vial neck
40. Place CE cap securely on each vial after sample transfer
41. Set up SCCA detection run according to text protocol

43. Note vial positions for auto-sampler programming
44. Initiate sample separation by opening Control menu in software and selecting Run Sequence
45. Monitor photodiode array (PDA) trays to ensure proper separation
46. Open first chromatogram file

1. Set up and label up to eight 500 microliter tubes
2. Add up to 1 mg protein sample in up to 112 microliters volume to each tube
3. Add 30 microliters of 5x sample buffer to each protein sample

6. Heat vials at 95°C for 5 minutes to denature samples

8. Remove gel free 8,100 cartridge from foil pouch

14. Pipette 100 microliters of gel-free running buffer into each collection chamber
15. Using an 8-channel pipetter (100-200 μL), remove and discard buffer overflow from sample loading chambers
16. Load denatured samples into loading chambers using 8-channel pipetter
17. Place cartridge into GelFree 8,100 fractionation station
18. Lower electrode arrays and close station lid
19. Navigate to list of pre-programmed methods using touchscreen GUI
20. Press method button to select appropriate pre-programmed method
21. Press retrieve button and enter method number using onscreen keypad
22. Press apply button to load selected method
23. Press channel button and select channels for run
24. Press done button to confirm channel selection
25. Ensure safety lid is closed with green indicator light
26. Press start button to begin experiment
27. Open lid of instrument
28. Use innate channel pipette to transfer 150 microliters of liquid from collection chambers to collection tubes or multi-well plate
29. Add 100 microliters of gel free running buffer per channel to collection chambers
30. Pipette buffer up and down twice in collection chambers
31. Add 100 microliters of gel free running buffer to collection chambers
32. Partition eight protein samples (200 micrograms each) into 12 fractions per sample
33. Take aliquot of each of 12 fractions from bovine liver homogenate sample
34. Run aliquots on standard 1D gel
35. Silver stain the gel

2. Count number of cells and dilute suspension in complete growth media to obtain 1 million cells in maximum 1.5 milliliters volume

7. Incubate plate to initiate spheroid formation

9. Fill cell chamber with 9 milliliters of growth media using 10-milliliter syringe with long needle
10. Place bioreactor in 3D incubator at 37°C with 5% CO₂ for 24 hours

12. Transfer detached spheroids to tissue culture dish

14. Transfer washed spheroids to culture dish

18. Fill entire bioreactor with fresh growth media

31. Ensure spheroids descend to bottom center of bioreactor

34. Transfer spheroids to microcentrifuge tubes

36. Centrifuge at 500g for 5 minutes
37. Dispose of media
38. Add 200 microliters of HBSS to wash spheroid and remove FBS
39. Centrifuge at 500g for 5 minutes
40. Discard supernatant
41. Immerse spheroid pellet rapidly in liquid nitrogen to freeze
42. Store frozen pellet at -80°C until further processing
43. Resuspend collected spheroids in 25 microliters of 5% SDS
44. Homogenize pellet by pipetting up and down
45. Dissolve 1 microgram of samples in 10 microliters of 0.1% trichloroacetic acid

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Dry two 20-milliliter measuring flasks and two 20-milliliter beakers equipped with magnetic stir bars
2. Transfer 4.13 grams of DMAPAAQ, 0.31 grams of N,N'-methylenebisacrylamide, 0.5 grams of sodium sulfite, and 3.36 grams of sodium hydroxide to one 20-milliliter beaker
3. Dissolve the mixture in distilled water as solvent
4. Stir the solution for 30 minutes using a magnetic stir bar
5. Transfer the mixture from the beaker to one 20-milliliter measuring flask
6. Add distilled water to the measuring flask to generate a 20-milliliter solution
7. Label the solution as the monomer solution
8. Weigh 0.54 grams of ammonium peroxodisulfate
9. Weigh 7.57 grams of iron chloride
10. Transfer both compounds to a 20 milliliter beaker
11. Dissolve compounds completely in distilled water
12. Stir solution for 30 minutes using magnetic stir bar
13. Transfer mixture to 20 milliliter measuring flask
14. Add distilled water to compose 20 milliliter solution
15. Label solution as initiator solution
16. Purge solutions with nitrogen gas for 30 minutes
17. Mix solutions together in a 50 milliliter test tube
18. Stir mixture with electric stirrer
19. Place mixture in chiller maintained at 10°C for four hours
20. Take gel block out from test tube
21. Place gel block on flat cutting board
22. Shape gel block into cubic form
23. Soak gel slices in deionized water for 24 hours to remove impurities
24. Spread gel slices onto Petri dish and dry at room temperature for 24 hours
25. Place Petri dish with gel slices in oven at 50°C for 24 hours
26. Dry five 40 milliliter plastic containers
27. Measure 20 milligrams of dried gel
28. Place measured gel into each 40 milliliter plastic container
29. Add 40 milliliters of disodium hydrogen arsenate heptahydrate solution to each container at different concentrations
30. Incubate containers in stirrer at 20 degrees Celsius and 120 RPM for 24 hours
31. Collect 5 milliliter sample from each container
32. Measure equilibrium arsenic levels using HPLC
33. Configure HPLC with 4 × 200 mm analytical column, 4 × 50 mm guard column, and 4 mm suppressor
34. Add 20 mg of dried gel to dried 40 ml plastic container
35. Add 40 ml of 0.2 millimolar solution
36. Place container in stirrer at 20°C and 120 RPM
37. Collect 5 ml sample in plastic tube using micropipette
38. Analyze equilibrium arsenic level in solution using HPLC
39. Collect fragile gel pieces one at a time to prevent breakage
40. Place collected gel pieces in mesh sieve
41. Wash gel pieces minimum five times with deionized water to remove surface arsenic
42. Transfer washed fragile gel pieces into dried 40 milliliter plastic container for desorption analysis
43. Place container in stirrer at 20°C and 120 RPM for 24 hours
44. Collect 5 mL sample into plastic tube using micropipette
45. Evaluate equilibrium arsenic concentration in solution using HPLC

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Place PRDM medium in 37°C water bath
2. Remove cryovial containing day 32 HESC-derived photoreceptor progenitor cells from liquid nitrogen
3. Keep cryovial on dry ice
4. Thaw cryovial in 37°C water bath for 3-5 minutes
5. Resuspend cells in 1 milliliter of PRDM
6. Centrifuge at 130g for 4 minutes
7. Resuspend cell pellet in 1 milliliter of PRDM
8. Take 10 microliters of cell suspension
9. Mix cell suspension with 0.2% Trypan Blue following manufacturer's instructions
10. Pipette cell mixture into cell counting chamber slide
11. Determine cell number and viability using automated cell counter
12. Centrifuge remaining cell suspension at 130g for four minutes
13. Remove supernatant from centrifuged sample
14. Resuspend cell pellet in PRDM transplantation medium
15. Mechanically dissociate cell clumps using 10 microliter pipette tip until no clumps are visible
16. Load suspension into 33-gauge injection syringe and confirm extrusion through needle
17. Take an anesthetized mouse
18. Apply one drop of 1% tropicamide and 2.5% phenylephrine into eye to dilate pupil
19. Apply ophthalmic gel to cornea to prevent dryness and anesthesia-induced cataracts
20. Set up upright operating microscope with direct light path in sterile environment
21. Detach needle hub from 10 microliter glass syringe
22. Attach 33-gauge blunt needle to syringe
23. Take metal hub cover
24. Securely fasten needle onto syringe using hub cover
25. Flush needle with distilled water to test leakage and patency
26. Empty syringe
27. Set syringe aside carefully
28. Position anesthetized mouse on cushion
29. Orient treatment eye toward microscope objective
30. Apply 0.5% proparacaine hydrochloride to eye
31. Wait 30 seconds after anesthetic application
32. Apply 150 microliters of ophthalmic gel to eye
33. Affix round cover slip to eye
34. Perform preliminary eye examination observing cornea, iris, pupil, lens, and conjunctiva
35. Adjust focal plane to visualize fundus through pupil
36. Align head so optic head centers with pupil
37. Tap base of tube containing HESC-derived photoreceptor progenitors to homogenize suspension
38. Draw 2 microliters of cell mixture using 10 microliter glass syringe with 33-gauge blunt needle
39. Create sclerectomy wound using 30-gauge disposable needle positioned 2 mm behind limbus
40. Maintain needle angle at 45 degrees to prevent lens contact
41. Observe needle tip becoming visible within eye
42. Carefully retract needle
43. Discard used needle into sharp bin to prevent injuries
44. Take glass syringe and insert blunt needle into sclerectomy wound
45. Advance blunt needle without touching lens until reaching opposite retina of entry wound
46. Gently penetrate retina until pressure branch on sclera is visible
47. Inject 2 microliters of cell suspension or PRDM media into subretinal space while maintaining gentle syringe pressure
48. Wait 10 seconds to allow cells to settle
49. Gently retract needle from eye
50. Move mouse head to position eye under microscope
51. Secure head position by gently holding
52. Activate iOCT function on operating microscope
53. Select cube on OCT screen
54. Position scanning area on bleb using arrow buttons
55. Adjust OCT by sliding centering and focus for optimal quality
56. Capture OCT scan of bleb area
57. Review OCT images to check scan quality
58. Remove cover slip
59. Clean gel from eye using gauze
60. Administer antibiotic ointment to prevent infection

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Combine 10 male and 15 female adult drosophila flies in vial with rich fly food supplemented with yeast
2. Incubate vial at room temperature for 3-4 days to allow egg development into larvae
3. Use moist fine-bristle brush to roll along vial wall and collect wandering third instar larvae
4. Transfer collected larvae to glass well filled with 1X PBS
5. Wash larvae by transferring to another well filled with 1X PBS to remove food debris
6. Separate male and female larvae using forceps
7. Collect ≥10 female larvae and transfer to separate well filled with 1X PBS using forceps
8. Transfer female larvae into new vial containing fresh fly food supplemented with yeast using forceps
9. Place vial with female larvae in dark location to facilitate pupariation
10. Monitor larvae for pupariation throughout the day; circle each pre-pupa against vial and record approximate formation time
11. Melt glass tip of pester pipette
12. Melt glass tip of Pasteur pipette over Bunsen burner
13. Use forceps to pull molten tip horizontally away from pipette
14. Break off small portion of cooled pipette tip to form circular opening
15. Attach bulb to opposite end of pipette
16. Load pipette with 1X PBS solution
17. Apply small drop of water along contact zone between pupa and vial wall
18. Allow protein glue to dissolve for 1-2 minutes
19. Gently lift pupa off vial wall using moist fine brush
20. Transfer single pupa to well filled with 1X PBS
21. Grasp posterior end of pupa with one pair of forceps
22. Tear anterior portion of pupal case with another pair of forceps until pupa head is visible
23. Grip anterior tip of pupal head with forceps
24. Gently pull pupa out of pupal case
25. Separate and discard anterior half of pupa
26. Separate and discard anterior half of pupa to isolate abdominal sac
27. Grasp abdominal sac against well bottom with forceps
28. Hold thin glass pipette filled with 1X PBS
29. Aim pipette tip toward abdominal sac opening
30. Slowly pipette 1X PBS into abdomen to wash fat body cells from ovaries
31. Continue washing until ≥2/3 fat body removed or ovaries visible at sac opening
32. Transfer abdomen into glass chamber filled with fixation buffer
33. Place lid on glass chamber

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Filter medium with 0.2 micrometer filter
2. Autoclave filtered solution
3. Prepare 10 millimolar chemoattractant solution in 1 milliliter sterile medium
4. Filter chemoattractant solution with 0.2 micrometer syringe filter to remove particles and contaminants
5. Dilute filtered chemoattractant stock solution in series according to experimental protocol
6. Attach 27 gauge needle to fill an ISCA
7. Attach 27-gauge needle to 1 milliliter syringe
8. Load one syringe per concentration of filtered chemoattractant solution
9. Hold ISCA device with port facing upwards and slowly inject each chemoattractant concentration into five wells of one row until droplet appears on each port
10. Transfer 1.5 milliliter of marine or freshwater bacteria culture into 150 milliliters of appropriate bacterial culture medium
11. Place two small pieces of double-sided adhesive tape on flat surface of 200 milliliter capacity tray
12. Secure one ISCA device onto each piece of adhesive tape
13. Slowly fill deployment tray with bacterial solution using 50 milliliter serological pipette
14. Allow bacteria to respond to chemoattractant of interest for one hour
15. Remove medium from ISCA tray using a new 50 milliliter pipette
16. Position ISCA with port facing downward
17. Draw solution from each well using sterile 1 milliliter syringe with 27 gauge needle
18. Pool samples from each row of same concentration into single tube
19. Analyze samples by flow cytometry
20. Collect 5 milliliters of water per ISCA from field site
21. Filter water through 0.2 micrometer syringe filter into 50 milliliter conical tube
22. Pass syringe-filtered water through hydrophilic GP filter cartridge (first pass)
23. Collect filtrate in new 50 milliliter conical centrifuge tube after first rinse
24. Pass syringe-filtered water through hydrophilic GP filter cartridge (second pass)
25. Collect filtrate in new 50 milliliter conical centrifuge tube after second rinse
26. Filter water through 0.02 micrometer syringe filter into new 50 milliliter tube
27. Resuspend chemoattractants to desired concentrations using filtrate aliquots in individual 15 milliliter conical tubes
28. Filter resuspended chemoattractants using 10 milliliter syringes through 0.2 micrometer syringe filters into sterile 15 milliliter conical centrifuge tubes
29. Screw each ISCA onto apparatus for field deployment
30. Screw each ISCA onto piece 9 of the device enclosure
31. Close the device enclosure
32. Seal enclosure with adhesive tape
33. Secure enclosure to deployment arm and structure using bungee cords
34. Submerge enclosure completely while holding firmly to prevent excessive water movement
35. Verify no air trapped when water reaches top of enclosure
36. Seal bottom and top holes with two plugs when enclosure is full
37. Leave ISCA in place for 1 to 3 hours
38. Remove enclosure at end of sampling period
39. Remove enclosure from water
40. Place enclosure over container to drain water
41. Remove upper part of adhesive tape from front holes
42. Remove bottom plug and drain residual water after water line passes below ISCA top
43. Use 1 milliliter pipette or syringe to remove water trapped on top of each ISCA
44. Remove ISCAs from enclosure without touching upper surface
45. Use disposable wipe to remove remaining liquid on surface
46. Retrieve samples as demonstrated for laboratory sample retrieval

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Grow cells to 70-80% confluence on 10 cm tissue culture plates
2. Stimulate cells according to experimental aims
3. Remove tissue culture medium
4. Add 5 mL fixation solution
5. Incubate cells for 10 minutes at room temperature on shaking platform
6. Remove fixation solution
7. Wash cells twice with 10 mL ice-cold 1X PBS
8. Remove 1X PBS
9. Add 5 mL glycine stop fix solution
10. Incubate cells for 5 minutes at room temperature on shaking platform
11. Remove glycine stop fix solution
12. Wash cells twice with 10 mL ice-cold 1X PBS
13. Add 2 ml of cell scraping solution to culture vessel
14. Harvest cells using cell scraper
15. Transfer harvested cells to conical tube on ice
16. Centrifuge samples at 600 × g and 4°C for 10 minutes
17. Remove supernatant from centrifuged samples
18. Resuspend pellet in 1 ml of 1X cell lysis buffer
19. Incubate resuspended cells on ice for 30 minutes
20. Transfer cell suspension to ice-cold Dounce homogenizer
21. Homogenize suspension with type B pestle for 10 strokes
22. Transfer homogenized suspension to microcentrifuge tube
23. Centrifuge sample at 2,400 × g for 10 minutes at 4°C
24. Discard supernatant
25. Re-suspend pellet in 350 microliters of nuclei digestion buffer
26. Incubate samples at 37°C for 5 minutes
27. Add micrococcal nuclease and mix by vortexing
28. Incubate samples at 37°C for 5-20 minutes with mixing every 2 minutes by inversion
29. Add 7 microliters of ice-cold 0.5 M EDTA to stop micrococcal nuclease reaction
30. Centrifuge samples at 16,200 × g for 10 minutes at 4°C
31. Transfer sheared DNA supernatant to fresh microcentrifuge tube
32. Aliquot 50 microliters of supernatant into separate microcentrifuge tube for quality control
33. Combine 10-25 μg sheared cross-linked chromatin with 25 μl chromatin immunoprecipitation grade protein G magnetic beads
34. Add 10 μl immunoprecipitation dilution buffer and 1 μl protease inhibitor cocktail
35. Incubate mixture for 4 hours
36. Add 1-10 μg antibody to 1.5 ml microcentrifuge tube
37. Add distilled water to achieve 100 μl final volume in microcentrifuge tube
38. Incubate samples on end-to-end rotator for 4-12 hours at 4°C
39. Place incubated tubes on magnetic stand
40. Remove and discard supernatant after magnetic bead aggregation
41. Wash beads three times with 800 microliters of wash buffer one
42. Mix beads by inverting tube several times (per wash cycle)
43. Place tube on magnetic stand (per wash cycle)
44. Remove wash buffer after magnetic bead aggregation (per wash cycle)
45. Wash beads once with 800 microliters of wash buffer two
46. Re-suspend beads after washes
47. Add 50 microliters of elution buffer
48. Incubate samples on end-to-end rotator for 15 minutes at room temperature
49. Transfer supernatant to fresh tube using magnetic stand
50. Add six microliters of five molar sodium chloride
51. Add two microliters of proteinase K
52. Mix samples
53. Incubate samples for two hours at 65 degrees celsius

1. Shave injection site on anesthetized mouse
2. Clean shaved site with alcohol swab two times
3. Load cold 1-mL syringe with 1-2×10⁷ cells/mL and 250 μL basement membrane matrix
4. Attach 25-gauge needle to syringe
5. Load needle with tumor cell suspension
6. Press and retract syringe plunger multiple times to remove air bubbles
7. Press and retract plunger to remove air bubbles
8. Insert needle subcutaneously at injection site at 30-degree angle
9. Slowly inject 50 microliters of tumor cells with needle parallel to body
10. Withdraw needle slowly after 10 seconds while maintaining parallel position without sideways movement
11. Clean injection site gently with alcohol swab
12. Return mouse to cage
13. Excise subcutaneous tumor using sterile forceps and micro scissors under BSL-2 hood conditions while avoiding peritoneal cavity puncture
14. Wash excised tumor twice in sterile PBS
15. Mince tumor into 1-2 mm³ pieces using sterile scalpels
16. Transfer tumor pieces into Petri dish containing cold sterile DMEM basal medium on ice
17. Confirm lack of response to toe pinch
18. Apply ointment to anesthetized recipient animal's eyes
19. Place mouse on right side to expose left abdomen
20. Remove abdominal hair with depilatory cream
21. Remove depilatory cream with gauze
22. Disinfect exposed skin with three sequential 10% povidone-iodine and alcohol wipes
23. Make 1.5 cm incision in skin 1 cm left of midline below rib cage
24. Gently externalize spleen with flat-tipped forceps
25. Externalize spleen using flat-tipped forceps and sterile cotton applicator
26. Laterally retract tail of pancreas using forceps
27. Grasp one finely-minced tumor piece with sterile forceps
28. Pass fine 6-O sterile suturing needle through tumor piece
29. Insert needle containing tumor fragment into tail of pancreas
30. Pass suture slowly through pancreatic tissue
31. Completely remove needle from tissue after securing tumor fragment
32. Maintain organs externalized for 60 seconds while inspecting for hemorrhage
33. Return spleen, pancreas, and tumor to abdominal cavity using blunt forceps or cotton tip applicator
34. Place anesthetized animal on its right side for tumor access
35. Clean tumor site using clippers and alcohol swab
36. Elevate skin under tumor using forceps
37. Insert two needle array electrodes parallel to body through skin to bracket tumor
38. Set electroporator to deliver 100 microsecond pulses at 1 Hz frequency, 1500 volts per centimeter for 150 pulses
39. Use foot pedal to deliver pulses
40. Separate each set of 10 pulses by 10-second intervals for heat dissipation
41. Ensure electrodes remain in desired location and animal stays sedated after each pulse set
42. Remove electrodes after pulse delivery
43. Record actual voltage delivered as displayed on electroporator
44. Return mouse to its cage
45. Externalize orthotopic tumor using blunt-nosed forceps
46. Tightly capture tumor with platinum electrodes of Tweezertrode
47. Deliver electroporation pulses
48. Maintain tumor externalization during final pulse set

50. Return tumor to abdominal cavity
51. Close surgical incision
52. Monitor animal until full recovery

1. Cut ventilation holes into one side of a six-ounce plastic fly culture bottle
2. Use syringe to fill Petri dish lids with grape juice medium until medium forms a dome above lid lip

4. Pour 40 milliliters of prepared fly food into 100x15mm Petri dishes

6. Obtain black Plexiglas spreader for applying yeast paste to plates
7. Separate lids from bottoms of Petri dishes
8. Collect 100-150 female flies and 50-75 male flies
9. Allow flies to mate overnight in a six-ounce fly culture bottle with standard fly food
10. Transfer mated flies into a holding bottle
11. Secure grape plate with fresh yeast paste over bottle top using masking tape
12. Invert bottle to position grape plate at bottom for egg laying
13. Discard first grape plate after 24 hours
14. Discard first grape plate after 24 hours
15. Place new grape plate in holding bottle
16. Remove grape plates from bottles after 22 hours
17. Transfer grape plates to 60 × 15 mm Petri dish
18. Place new grape plate with fresh yeast paste on holding bottle
19. Clear and discard all larvae from seeded grape plate using dissecting probe
20. Incubate cleared grape plates for four hours in standard conditions
21. Pick 100 first-instar larvae per strain from grape plates
22. Place larvae on food plates
23. Place larvae gently without perforating food or burying larvae
24. Transfer larvae in groups of ten at a time
25. Distribute larvae across food plate to avoid crowding
26. Incubate food plates until ten hours before start of wandering
27. Incubate food plates until test animals are ten hours before start of wandering stage
28. Dissolve yeast in water at 1:2 ratio (yeast:water)
29. Gently pick larvae from first strain food plate using paintbrush
30. Collect larvae in Petri dish
31. Wash larvae with water to remove food residue
32. Quickly wash larvae with two milliliters of water
33. Gently dab larvae dry with laboratory wipe
34. Keep larvae in humid environment (not submerged) to avoid stress
35. Arrange three test plates side by side
36. Set five-minute timer for each test plate
37. Arrange three test plates side by side
38. Set a five minute timer for each test plate
39. Apply yeast paste to the top of the test plates and distribute a thin, even layer across all wells using a spreader
40. Gently place one larva into the center of each well
41. Start the five minute timer immediately after placing the first larvae on each plate
42. Place remaining larvae in the unoccupied wells
43. Place a Petri dish lid on top of each well
44. After timer expiration, trace larval path-lengths in yeast using a permanent marker without removing larvae, maintaining placement order and speed

9. Firmly close microcentrifuge tubes

13. Sonicate samples at 1.1 watts with pulse setting (1 second on, 1 second off) for 2 minutes
14. Remove sample one from sonication block
15. Transfer 1 milliliter from top of dispersion to clean microcentrifuge tube
16. Add deionized water to attain sample concentration of 0.02 mg/mL
17. Immediately begin sample characterization
18. Sonicate remaining samples for four minutes
19. Maintain same amplitude and pulse settings during sonication
20. Dilute sample two to 0.02 mg/mL with deionized water

30. Add deionized water to achieve 0.2 milligrams per milliliter concentration in each vial

32. Place vials in center of calibrated ultrasonic bath
33. Ensure water level is halfway up the vials
34. Sonicate sample at 80 watts for 15 minutes at room temperature
35. Transfer aliquot of sample four to clean vial
36. Dilute aliquot with deionized water to 0.02 milligrams per milliliter
37. Begin characterization of diluted sample
38. Replace ultrasonic bath water with fresh room temperature water
39. Open dynamic light scattering instrument software
40. Create size measurement file
41. Configure parameters: equilibration time, temperature, cuvette type, experiment mode
42. Save size measurement file
43. Run DLS verification measurement using standard 100nm latex beads
44. Confirm instrument performance meets standards
45. Slowly pipette 1 mL of dilute nanopowder dispersion into clean low-volume disposable cuvette while avoiding air bubbles

47. Start data collection
48. Take at least five measurements per sample

54. Set instrument wavelength range to 700 nanometers to 200 nanometers

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Harvest mixed population of cells from mouse testes
2. Load harvested cells into stay put apparatus
3. Load BSA gradient into stay put apparatus
4. Allow cells to sediment through BSA gradient
5. Collect sedimented fractions
6. Combine fractions based on cell composition
7. Secure two 2-liter cylinders to top platform
8. Secure cell loading chamber to top platform
9. Connect components with two small tubing pieces
10. Apply tube clamps to connections
11. Clamp all tubes closed
12. Seal spout on rightmost 2-liter cylinder
13. Place small stir bar in cell loading chamber
14. Place large stir bar in leftmost 2-liter cylinder
15. Place 2-liter sedimentation chamber on platform
16. Position metal anti-vortex baffle over sedimentation chamber opening
17. Place lid on sedimentation chamber
18. Apply vacuum grease to ground glass joint of three-way stopcock
19. Clamp stopcock to sedimentation chamber by connecting ground glass joints
20. Connect cell loading chamber to right outlet of stopcock with tubing
21. Close stopcock
22. Attach cell fractionation tubing to left outlet of stopcock
23. Clamp small tube at the very bottom
24. Pour 2% BSA solution into left two liter cylinder
25. Pour 4% BSA solution into right two-liter cylinder
26. Verify absence of large bubbles in tubing connecting cylinders
27. Pour Krebs buffer into cell loading chamber and fill connecting tubing to sedimentation chamber
28. Verify absence of large bubbles that might disturb gradient
29. Confirm Krebs buffer is in tube and not in cell chamber
30. Allow small amount of Krebs buffer to flow into sedimentation chamber
31. Drain almost all buffer into cell fractionation tubing
32. Fill fractionation tubing and remove large bubbles
33. Place fraction collector directly under sedimentation chamber
34. Confirm all fraction tubes are in place
35. Cover fraction tubes with plastic wrap to prevent contamination
36. Deencapsulate testes in plate containing 8 milliliters of Krebs buffer
37. Incise membrane surrounding seminiferous tubules using one pair of forceps
38. Hold membrane with second pair of forceps and push tubules out of membrane
39. Transfer 4 milliliters of Krebs containing tubules to conical tube with 25 milliliters collagenase solution
40. Shake at 33°C for 10 minutes until tubules develop spaghetti-like appearance
41. Allow tubules to settle for 5 minutes at bottom of tube
42. Pour out supernatant
43. Wash tubules twice with 25 milliliters Krebs at room temperature, allowing settling to bottom after each wash
44. Leave approximately 5 milliliters Krebs in each tube
45. Incubate tubules with trypsin
46. Agitate tubule-trypsin solution using wide-bore pipette
47. Pipette solution in and out approximately 10 times to dissociate cells
48. Filter 30 milliliter single-cell suspension through 100-micron mesh cell strainer
49. Position stopcock to direct flow from cell chamber to sedimentation chamber
50. Turn both stir bar plates to low setting
51. Pour cell suspension into cell chamber
52. Open stopcock to allow cell flow into sedimentation chamber at 10 ml/min
53. Close stopcock
54. Pour 5 ml of 0.5% BSA solution into cell chamber and drain into sedimentation chamber at 10 ml/min
55. Close stopcock and repeat BSA wash four times
56. Open clamps between cell chamber and cylinders to drain liquid into sedimentation chamber at 40 ml/min
57. Adjust flow rate to load BSA gradient over 20-30 minutes
58. Attach fraction tube to fraction collector
59. Adjust flow rate using stop cock to collect 10 milliliters per tube every 45 seconds
60. Microscopically analyze collected fractions for different cell populations
61. Transfer 10 microliters of DAPI stained cells onto slide
62. Place cover slip on slide
63. Analyze sample with fluorescence microscope
64. Pool fractions with similar cell size and nuclear morphology

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Add 1840 microliters of culture to 160 microliters of 37% formaldehyde in a 2 ml microcentrifuge tube
2. Invert tube approximately five times to mix
3. Place tube on roller drum at room temperature for 20 minutes
4. Continue fixation at 4°C with rotation overnight
5. Thaw 200 millimolar vanadyl ribonucleoside complexes (VRC) at 65 degrees Celsius for at least 10 minutes
6. Pipette 2125 microliters of buffer B into a 15 milliliter tube
7. Vortex warm VRC for five seconds to fully resuspend
8. Add 200 microliters of VRC to the 15 milliliter tube containing buffer B
9. Centrifuge meiotic sample at 21,000 x g for 1.5 minutes at room temperature
10. Aspirate supernatant carefully using vacuum tip placed opposite pellet
11. Resuspend cells in 1.5 ml cold Buffer B by pipetting up and down
12. Centrifuge at 21,000 x g for 1.5 minutes
13. Remove bulk liquid by vacuum aspiration while leaving 100 μl behind
14. Resuspend cells in 1.5 ml cold Buffer B
15. Centrifuge samples
16. Remove liquid
17. Resuspend cells in 1.5 ml cold Buffer B
18. Centrifuge at 21,000 x g for 1.5 minutes
19. Aspirate liquid completely using pipette
20. Resuspend cells in 425 microliters of digestion master mix and vortex briefly
21. Add 5 microliters of zymolyase to tube
22. Vortex for 2-3 seconds to mix
23. Place tube on roller drum and digest at 30°C for 15-30 minutes
24. Check cells by microscopy every 5 minutes after 15 minutes until 80% appear non-transparent and non-refractive
25. Centrifuge tube at 376 x g for three minutes
26. Remove supernatant completely
27. Gently resuspend cells with 1 mL Buffer B by pipetting up and down 1-2 times
28. Centrifuge tube
29. Remove buffer
30. Resuspend cells in 1 mL of 70% ethanol
31. Incubate at room temperature for 3.5-4 hours
32. Centrifuge tube of cells at 376 x g for three minutes
33. Remove 500 microliters of 70% ethanol from tube
34. Gently pipette up and down to resuspend remaining cells
35. Transfer cell suspension to low adhesion tube
36. Centrifuge low adhesion tube
37. Remove all ethanol from low adhesion tube
38. Add one milliliter of 10% formamide wash buffer to tube
39. Gently pipette up and down 2-3 times to resuspend cells
40. Incubate cells at room temperature for 20 minutes
41. Centrifuge sample
42. Remove supernatant into hazardous waste
43. Add at least 50 microliters of hybridization solution to tube
44. Flick tube to mix solution
45. Incubate at 30°C on roller drum in dark for at least 16 hours
46. Place tube into foil-covered box to protect from light
47. Centrifuge sample at 376 × g for 3 minutes
48. Remove supernatant into hazardous waste by pipetting
49. Resuspend sample in 1 mL of 10% formamide wash buffer by gently pipetting up and down 2-3 times
50. Place tube into foil-covered box to protect from light
51. Incubate at 30°C for 30 minutes
52. Centrifuge sample at 30-minute mark
53. Remove supernatant to hazardous waste by pipetting while leaving approximately 50 microliters
54. Resuspend sample in 1 milliliter of DAPI solution by gentle pipetting (2-3 up/down cycles)
55. Place tube in foil-covered box
56. Incubate at 30°C for 30 minutes
57. Centrifuge sample
58. Completely remove supernatant by pipetting
59. Resuspend pellet in 50 microliters of enzyme-free GLOX buffer
60. Mix by pipetting (3-4 up/down cycles)
61. Store in foil-covered box at 4°C until imaging

7. Remove mouse from arena after 5 minutes
8. Place mouse in holding cage
9. Thoroughly clean apparatus

19. Use same object locations as during training for each mouse

22. Allow free exploration for 10 minutes
23. Remove mouse from arena after testing trial
24. Place mouse in holding cage

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Ethically euthanize mouse according to institution-approved methods
2. Place euthanized mouse on sterile underpad
3. Press 5/45-style tweezer on orbital area to nucleate eye and induce proptosis
4. Position tweezer at posterior of eye
5. Gently pull tweezer to remove eye
6. Verify optic nerve remains intact after eye removal
7. Submerge eyes in 2-milliliter microcentrifuge tube containing 5% povidone iodine using forceps
8. Incubate eyes in povidone iodine at room temperature for 2 to 3 minutes
9. Remove eyes from povidone iodine and place in Petri dish
10. Wash eyes with Hanks' Balanced Saline Solution using sterile transfer pipette until orange color disappears (approximately 2-3 washes)
11. Use tweezers and/or Vannas scissors to pull or cut away connective tissue in extraocular muscles under microscope
12. Optionally hold cleaned eyes in cold RPE cell culture media for up to one hour
13. Transfer eyes to 2-milliliter microcentrifuge tube containing enzyme A solution
14. Incubate eyes in incubator at 37°C for 40 minutes
15. Remove eyes from enzyme A solution
16. Transfer eyes to new 2-milliliter microcentrifuge tube containing enzyme B solution
17. Place tube in incubator at 37 degrees Celsius for 50 minutes
18. Remove eyes from enzyme B
19. Place eyes in 60-millimeter Petri dish
20. Submerge eyes in complete RPE cell growth media
21. Incubate eyes at 4 degrees Celsius for 30 minutes
22. Place dish under dissecting microscope
23. Use M5S forceps and syringe needle to make incision in ora seratta section
24. Grasp inner part of incision with one forcep
25. Grasp cornea with second forcep
26. Slowly tear cornea from retina by pulling in small increments while moving down the tear
27. Remove detached iris and lens with forceps
28. Grasp outer layer of eyecup with one tweezer
29. Position arm of second tweezer between RPE and neural layers
30. Gently pull/scoop neural retina away from RPE layer and discard
31. Move RPE layer to debris-free area of dish
32. Gently scrape inside of eyecup with 5/45-style tweezer to separate RPE from choroid and sclera
33. Aspirate cells using a 3.2-milliliter sterile transfer pipette
34. Transfer aspirated cells to a new 15-milliliter centrifuge tube containing complete RPE cell growth media
35. Centrifuge cells at 300g for 10 minutes at room temperature
36. Aspirate out the supernatant
37. Resuspend pellet in warmed RPE cell growth media
38. Plate cells on 100-millimeter Petri dish
39. Incubate at 37 degrees Celsius and 5% CO2

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Weigh replicate tissue samples into labeled 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tubes
2. Record precise mass of each sample
3. Add 500 μL of 0.1 M sodium hydroxide to each tube using micropipetter
4. Close tube lids tightly and perform sonic heating for 30 minutes
5. Place tubes in preheated hot-water bath for 15 minutes
6. Centrifuge tubes at 15,000 G for 10 minutes
7. Pipette supernatant into new labeled microcentrifuge tubes using fresh pipette tips for each sample
8. Add 300 microliters of 0.1 M sodium hydroxide to pellet
9. Centrifuge samples for 10 minutes
10. Transfer supernatant from re-extracted pellet to tubes containing first supernatant
11. Add 11 microliters of 5.8 M hydrochloric acid to neutralize supernatant pH
12. Verify pH ≈7 using litmus paper
13. Add 90 microliters of 100% trichloroacetic acid
14. Confirm pH is approximately seven using litmus paper
15. Add 90 microliters of 100% trichloroacetic acid to each tube
16. Incubate tubes on ice for 30 minutes
17. Centrifuge samples at 13,000 G for 10 minutes at four degrees Celsius
18. Remove trichloroacetic acid using vacuum line and glass micropipette tip without disturbing pellet
19. Wash pellet with 100 microliters of acetone cooled to -20 degrees Celsius
20. Allow acetone to evaporate in fume hood
21. Dissolve protein pellet with one milliliter of sodium hydroxide
22. Add 160 microliters of each IGG standard solution to a 96-well plate in triplicate starting at position A1
23. Add 50 microliters of each sample solution to 950 microliters of distilled water in a new 1.5 milliliter tube
24. Add 60 microliters of each diluted sample to the 96-well plate in triplicate starting at position H1
25. Add 100 microliters of distilled water to blank and unknown sample wells
26. Add 40 microliters of Coomassie Brilliant Blue G-250 protein dye to each well using multi-channel pipette
27. Pop bubbles in wells using needle
28. Incubate plate at room temperature for 5 minutes
29. Record absorbance values at 595 nanometers using microplate spectrophotometer
30. Weigh replicates of tissue samples into 15 mL glass tubes
31. Label tubes and record exact mass of each sample
32. Add 1 mL of 0.1 M sulfuric acid to each tube
33. Close tube caps tightly
34. Place tubes in boiling-water bath for 1 hour
35. Transfer tubes to tepid water bath for cooling
36. Pour tube contents into labeled 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tubes
37. Centrifuge tubes at 15,000 G for 10 minutes
38. Transfer supernatant to new labeled 1.5 mL tubes using micropipette
39. Transfer 15 μL of each unknown sample to individual test tubes using pipette
40. Add 385 μL distilled water to each test tube
41. Add 400 microliters of 5% phenol to each standard and unknown sample test tube in fume hood
42. Add 2 milliliters of sulfuric acid to each tube, ensuring addition to solution surface
43. Vortex tubes after 10-minute incubation
44. Incubate tube for 30 minutes
45. Transfer 800 microliters from each sample tube to three 1.5 mL polystyrene semi-microcuvettes
46. Set spectrophotometer to 490 nanometers
47. Calibrate spectrophotometer using blank cuvette
48. Run each cuvette through spectrophotometer and record absorbance

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Culture transduced human breast cancer MDA-MB-231 cells, breast epithelial MCF10A cells, and breast epithelial MCF10A-Ras cells in T75 flask
2. Monitor cells until reaching 80% confluence
3. Remove medium from T75 flask
4. Wash cells with phosphate-buffered saline
5. Detach cells using 0.5% Trypsin-EDTA solution
6. Neutralize Trypsin with medium
7. Centrifuge cells at 1,000 RPM for 5 minutes to remove medium
8. Wash cells with PBS
9. Centrifuge sample again
10. Resuspend cells in approximately 200 microliters of PBS to achieve concentration ≤1×10⁸ cells/mL
11. Store cell suspension at 4°C for ≤5 hours
12. Incubate approximately 60 zebrafish embryos in a petri dish filled with egg water supplemented with 60 micrograms per milliliter of sea salts at 28 degrees Celsius
13. Remove the coriands from embryos at 48 hours post-fertilization using fine tweezers
14. Transfer embryos to a solution of 40 micrograms per milliliter tricaine in egg water for anesthesia
15. Load borosilicate microcapillary tube into micro-pipette puller
16. Pull needle using micro-pipette puller
17. Store prepared needles in needle-holder plate
18. Aspirate 15 microliters of prepared cell suspension into injection needle
19. Attach needle to micromanipulator
20. Break needle tip to 5-10 microns using fine tweezers
21. Adjust pneumatic PICO pump
22. Inject cells onto petri dish pre-coated with sterile 1% agarose
23. Transfer approximately 10 anesthetized embryos at two to three days post-fertilization onto flat injecting plate coated with 1% agarose
24. Arrange embryo positions with hair loop tool to orient embryos in same direction
25. Manually adjust injection plate position to place embryos in diagonal orientation for precise needle insertion
26. Direct needle tip at injection site and gently insert into perivitelline space between yolk sac and epidermis of zebrafish embryo
27. Inject approximately 400 mCherry-labeled tumor cells while avoiding yolk sac rupture to prevent misplaced implantation
28. Approach the duct of Cuvier from the dorsal side of the embryo with needle at 45-degree angle
29. Insert needle at dorsal starting point of duct of Cuvier where it broadens over yolk sac
30. Inject approximately 400 cells
31. Transfer injected zebrafish embryos to egg water
32. Incubate embryos at 33 degrees Celsius until further analysis
33. Transfer injected zebrafish embryo to glass-bottom polystyrene dish using Pasteur pipette
34. Remove excess egg water from dish
35. Image whole embryo body using confocal microscope at low magnification to observe tumor dissemination pattern
36. Configure confocal microscope with 488 nm laser for embryo vasculature visualization and 543 nm laser for red-fluorescent-labeled tumor cells
37. Scan embryo in 8-10 steps to acquire high-quality image

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Supplement DMEM with 5% dextran/charcoal coated FBS, 1X penicillin streptomycin, 2mM L-glutamine, and 100mM sodium pyruvate
2. Prewarm supplemented DMEM to 37°C
3. Wash HEK-293T cells in six-well tissue culture dish with PBS
4. Replace PBS with labeling media
5. Incubate cells at 37°C with 5% CO₂ for 45 minutes
6. Pipette at least 2 microliters of alkynyl fatty acid analog into bottom of 3ml conical reaction vial
7. Pipette equal volume of diluted potassium hydroxide near vial bottom edge for mixing
8. Close reaction vial lid
9. Tap vial gently to mix solutions
10. Heat reaction vial at 65°C until solution clears (≈5 min), monitoring evaporation
11. Pipette pre-warmed 20% fatty acid-free BSA
12. Pipette pre-warmed 20% fatty acid-free BSA at volume ratio of fatty acids to potassium hydroxide to fatty acid-free BSA of 1:1:50 to form 20X BSA-bound alkynyl fatty acids
13. Mix solution by pipetting up and down
14. Incubate solution for 15 minutes at 37°C
15. Label HEK-293 T-cells
16. Add 20X fatty acid BSA conjugate to starvation media
17. Adjust concentration to achieve 1% BSA with 25 µM alkynyl myristate or 100 µM alkynyl palmitate/stearate
18. Pipette 2 microliters of unlabeled fatty acid into starvation media
19. Place cells in incubator for 3-6 hours
20. Wash cells with PBS gently at room temperature
21. Harvest and lyse cells
22. Add 500 microliters EDTA-free modified RIPA buffer
23. Rotate lysates for 15 minutes at 4°C
24. Centrifuge lysates at 16,000 × g for 10 minutes at 4°C
25. Collect supernatant
26. Transfer supernatant to 1.7 milliliter microcentrifuge tubes
27. Store supernatants at -20°C
28. Adjust protein lysates (50-100 μg) to equal volume using EDTA-free modified RIPA buffer in 1.7 ml microcentrifuge tubes
29. Add STS to samples to achieve 1% final concentration
30. Prepare master mix of click reagents
31. Add master mix to lysates
32. Mix lysates by pipetting up and down
33. Incubate lysates in dark at 37°C for 30 minutes in water bath with occasional mixing
34. Mix lysates with rabbit anti-GFP antibody
35. Incubate mixture overnight with rotation at 4°C
36. Add 15-20 μl magnetic beads pre-equilibrated with 0.1% SDS RIPA
37. React mixture end-over-end at 4°C for 3 hours
38. Wash beads with lysis buffer
39. Resuspend beads in 45 μl of 50 mM HEPES buffer with 1% SDS
40. Heat beads at 80°C for 15 minutes
41. Agitate tubes every 5 minutes during heating
42. Centrifuge tubes briefly
43. Collect supernatant while warm
44. Combine 43 μl supernatant with 7 μl click reagents master mix
45. React mixture in dark at 37°C for 30 minutes

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Clear organic matter from soil surface
2. Sieving soil samples
3. Cleaning mesh with water after sieving each sample
4. Placing three individual 100 gram soil samples into beakers
5. Covering beakers with laboratory film
6. Pre-incubating covered beakers at 25 degrees Celsius for 10 days
7. Remove film from soil sample
8. Place 1 gram of soil in pre-weighed aluminum weigh boat
9. Record combined weight of moist soil and weigh boat
10. Dry samples in weigh boats at 105°C for 48 hours
11. Check sample weight every 8 hours for first 24 hours
12. Check sample weight every 6 hours until no weight loss occurs
13. Determine dry soil weight using known weigh boat weights
14. Calculate dry to moist soil weight ratio
15. Place damp paper towel at bottom of two glass vacuum desiccators with porcelain plates
16. Designate one desiccator for fumigation
17. Prepare three narrow glass vials per sample, each containing 30 grams of soil
18. Label each vial in pencil on laboratory tape
19. Place two vials per set in fumigation desiccator and third vial in other desiccator
20. Place fumigation desiccator in fume hood
21. Cover bottom of 100 milliliter beaker with boiling stones
22. Pour 50 milliliters of ethanol-free chloroform into beaker in fume hood
23. Place beaker in center of fumigation desiccator and close desiccator
24. Connect fumigation desiccator to vacuum pump
25. Turn on vacuum pump and boil chloroform for 30 seconds
26. Turn off and disconnect vacuum pump to allow air into desiccator
27. Boil chloroform for two minutes (fourth boiling)
28. Close off desiccator to maintain vacuum
29. Turn off and disconnect vacuum pump
30. Close and seal other desiccator
31. Close and seal desiccator
32. Store desiccators in dark area at room temperature for 24 hours
33. Place fumigation desiccator in fume hood
34. Vent desiccator
35. Remove paper towel from desiccator
36. Place soil-filled vials into desiccator
37. Close desiccator
38. Connect vacuum pump to desiccator
39. Open vacuum to remove chloroform gas from soil samples
40. Run vacuum for five minutes
41. Vent desiccator
42. Repeat vacuum and vent cycle four additional times
43. Remove soil-filled vials from desiccator
44. Label wide-mouth polypropylene bottles for fumigated and non-fumigated subsamples
45. Label three bottles as no soil controls for titration
46. Drill hole in center of size 10 rubber stopper
47. Insert 15 centimeter glass rod into stopper hole
48. Affix 40 milliliter glass vial to glass rod with rubber band
49. Add one gram of pre-incubated soil to each of three subsample bottles
50. Place one milliliter deionized water into bottle labeled for fumigated soil
51. Place vial of fumigated soil into bottle
52. Pipette one milliliter of two molar sodium hydroxide into glass vial
53. Stopper bottle
54. Seal bottle with laboratory film
55. Repeat assembly process for remaining soil samples
56. Prepare no soil control containers without soil samples
57. Store incubation chambers in dark at room temperature for 10 days
58. Carefully remove vial after incubation
59. Remove vial of sodium hydroxide from incubation container
60. Pipette 2 milliliters of 1 molar barium chloride into vial
61. Add one drop of phenolphthalein to vial
62. Add magnetic stir bar to vial
63. Begin stirring mixture on stir plate
64. Slowly add 0.1 normal hydrochloric acid from burette until solution changes from red to colorless
65. Record volume of hydrochloric acid added
66. Repeat titration for all incubation chambers, noting no soil values separately
67. Calculate carbon dioxide mineralized during soil incubation using titration data and dry-moist soil weight ratio
68. Calculate microbial biomass carbon for each sample set
69. Perform seven additional rounds of fumigation and incubation on fumigated samples
70. Calculate mineralized carbon dioxide after each fumigation-incubation cycle
71. Determine labile organic carbon and potential turnover time for fumigated samples using non-linear regression

4. Begin perfusing liver with warm HBSS minus at 4 ml/min flow rate

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Split 2 milliliters of human plasma into two 1-milliliter aliquots: one for metabolite/protein corona and one for plasma metabolome characterization
2. Incubate 1 milligram per milliliter of nanomaterials in human plasma for 1 hour at 37°C with gentle mixing at 500 rpm in thermo mixer
3. Pellet nanomaterials by centrifugation at 4,000 × g for 15 minutes at 4°C
4. Collect plasma supernatant
5. Resuspend pellet in 1 mL of 10X PBS buffer
6. Vortex vigorously for 2 minutes to remove unbound proteins
7. Centrifuge solution at 4,000 × g for 15 minutes at 4°C
8. Remove supernatant carefully without disturbing pellet
9. Resuspend pellet in 1 mL of ABC buffer
10. Resuspend pellet in 1 mL of ABC buffer
11. Vortex mixture vigorously for 2 minutes
12. Perform centrifugation
13. Discard supernatant
14. Dissolve pellet in 20 μL ABC buffer containing 10 mM dithiothreitol
15. Incubate solution at 56°C for 30 minutes
16. Add 2 micrograms of sequencing grade trypsin to 20 microliters of ABC buffer containing 0.1% surfactant
17. Incubate digest solution at 37 degrees Celsius for 16 hours
18. Add 20 microliters of 55 millimolar iodoacetamide in 100 millimolar ABC to alkylate sample
19. Incubate at room temperature for 20 minutes
20. Add 20 microliters of 0.1 molar hydrochloric acid to cleave surfactant
21. Leave sample at room temperature for 10 minutes
22. Take control plasma and supernatant from nanomaterial plasma incubation
23. Place samples on ice
24. Aliquot 50 microliters from each sample into separate vials
25. Dilute samples tenfold with distilled water
26. Transfer 50 microliters of diluted sample to new vials after vortexing
27. Add 200 microliters of chloroform
28. Add 250 microliters of methanol
29. Add 350 microliters of distilled water
30. Vortex mixture vigorously for two minutes
31. Centrifuge at 20,800 × g at 4°C
32. Continue centrifugation for 10 minutes
33. Take 500 microliters of supernatant
34. Check inlet and outlet ends of capillaries for intactness and absence of visible breakages
35. Place new neutral coated capillary into CESI instrument following manufacturer guidelines
36. Flush separation capillary in forward direction with 100 millimolar hydrochloric acid using manuscript conditions
37. Flush separation capillary with BGE (background electrolyte) using manuscript conditions
38. Flush separation capillary with distilled water using manuscript conditions
39. Place new bare fused silica capillary into CESI instrument
40. Flush separation capillary in forward direction with 100% methanol
41. Flush both separation and conductive capillaries with BGE to ensure droplet formation at outlet ends
42. Flush capillary with distilled water
43. Flush capillary with 0.1 molar sodium hydroxide
44. Flush capillary with distilled water
45. Flush capillary with BGE
46. Couple CESI to SEMS
47. Resuspend nanomaterial-exposed and unexposed plasma samples in 430 microliters of distilled water
48. Vortex samples vigorously for two minutes
49. Filter samples through 0.1 micrometer membrane filter
50. Add 5 microliters of internal standards to 95 microliters of filtrate
51. Vortex mixture vigorously
52. Centrifuge at 16,100 × g and 4°C
53. Perform CESI-MS analysis

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Enter nucleotide sequence in empty box
2. In Basic Options box, select minimum free energy and avoid isolated base pairs
3. In Output Options box, select interactive RNA secondary structure plot
4. Click Proceed button
5. Select three different 21 to 24-nucleotide-long sequences within mRNA of interest
6. Prioritize sequences in regions lacking extensive secondary structures and located in 3-prime untranslated regions
7. Select regions with guanidine/cytosine ratio close to 50% and lacking nucleotide tandem repeats
8. Design 2-prime-methoxy modified RNA oligonucleotides bearing biotin moiety at 3-prime end
9. Ensure oligonucleotides are fully complementary to selected target mRNA regions
10. Use online tool to adjust RNA hybrid melting temperature to 60-65°C
11. Optimize linguistic sequence complexity using online tool
12. Select Nucleotide BLAST
13. Insert ASO sequence into query box
14. Select target organism database
15. Keep BLAST parameters as default
16. Initiate BLAST analysis
17. Mix 2 μg reporter gene with 20 μL transfection reagent
18. Add transfection mixture to HEK293 cells (70% confluency in 10-cm dish)
19. Incubate transfected cells at 37°C for 48 hours
20. Remove culture medium using serological pipette
21. Wash cells twice with 10 milliliters of PBS pre-warmed to 37 degrees Celsius
22. Add six milliliters of PBS to dish
23. Place dish on ice
24. Expose cells to UV light at 100 millijoules per square centimeter in UV-crosslinker
25. Scrape off cells and PBS from dish and transfer to 15-milliliter tube
26. Centrifuge cells at 250 × g for 10 minutes at 4°C
27. Remove supernatant using pipette
28. Resuspend cell pellet in 2 ml pre-chilled lysis buffer by pipetting up/down 5-6 times while maintaining tube on ice
29. Transfer lysate to 5 ml tube using pipette while keeping tube on ice
30. Sonicate lysate for 3 rounds (20-second bursts at 10-micron amplitude with 30-second ice-cooling intervals between rounds)
31. Transfer lysate to 2 ml tubes
32. Centrifuge lysate at 15,000 × g for 10 minutes at 4°C
33. Collect supernatant and transfer to new tube
34. Equilibrate 1 mg oligo(dT)-coupled magnetic beads in 500 μl lysis buffer
35. Remove tube from rotator
36. Place tube on magnetic rack
37. Remove lysis buffer
38. Combine 4 mg HEK293 protein extract with oligo(dT)25-coupled magnetic beads
39. Mix samples vigorously
40. Incubate mixture for 10 minutes at 25°C
41. Place tubes on magnetic stand for 10 seconds
42. Remove supernatant
43. Keep supernatant on ice for subsequent recovery rounds
44. Add 500 microliters of wash buffer A to beads
45. Vortex mixture for five seconds
46. Collect beads using magnet
47. Wash beads twice with 500 microliters of wash buffer B
48. Add 30 microliters of 10-millimolar Tris-HCl to beads
49. Incubate tube at 80 degrees Celsius for two minutes with continuous shaking at 1,000 RPM
50. Transfer tube to magnetic stand and collect eluate within 10 seconds to prevent RNA rebinding
51. Add 30 microliters of streptavidin-coupled magnetic beads to 1 mL binding and wash buffer containing 0.1 mg/mL E. coli tRNA
52. Equilibrate bead mixture on rotator for 1 hour at room temperature
53. Wash beads three times with 750 microliters binding and wash buffer
54. Vortex tube containing washed beads
55. Remove binding and wash buffer from tube
56. Resuspend beads in 30 microliters buffer
57. Store resuspended beads on ice until use
58. Dilute approximately 35 micrograms of total protein from poly(A) isolation in 100 microliters of binding and wash buffer
59. Add 200 picomoles of appropriate antisense oligonucleotide
60. Incubate mixture at 70 degrees Celsius for 5 minutes to facilitate annealing
61. Remove heat block from device and place at room temperature for 10 minutes to cool slowly
62. Add 30 microliters of equilibrated streptavidin-coupled magnetic beads to sample
63. Incubate mixture for 30 minutes at 25 degrees Celsius with constant shaking at 950 rpm
64. Place tube on magnetic stand and remove supernatant
65. Wash beads three times with 750 microliters of pre-warmed binding and wash buffer at 55 degrees Celsius
66. Add 20 microliters of 10-millimolar Tris-HCl
67. Place sample at 90 degrees Celsius with constant shaking at 950 rpm for 10 minutes to elute RNA
68. Place tube in magnetic stand
69. Immediately collect eluate

1. Add 2.054 milliliters of deionized water to a 100 milliliter glass beaker

5. Stir mixture for five minutes or until CMC sodium salt dissolves using plastic/plastic-coated tools

9. Stir entire mixture at 1,000 RPM for five minutes

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Anesthetize cockroaches on ice until immobile for three minutes
2. Pick up insect using thumb and index finger to access ventral side
3. Use dissecting scissors to cut tip of hind leg coxa between coxa and trochanter
4. Position 10-microliter micropipette at incision site
5. Squeeze abdomen gently while drawing up bleeding hemolymph
6. Collect hemolymph from five cockroaches into single microcentrifuge tube
7. Centrifuge pooled hemolymph for 10 seconds
8. Combine 10 microliters of hemolymph with 50 microliters of 1x insect saline buffer
9. Centrifuge diluted hemolymph for 10 minutes to separate hemocytes
10. Transfer supernatant to new tube
11. Quantify total protein concentration using microvolume UV-Vis spectrophotometer and adjust to 6 mg/μL
12. Anesthetize cockroaches on ice
13. Transfer one cockroach to dissection plate loaded with cold 1x insect saline buffer
14. Secure cockroach ventral side up using insect pins
15. Dissect abdomen using fine tweezers
16. Remove entire gut
17. Transfer gut to fresh dish containing 1x insect saline buffer
18. Combine all diluted reagent with diluted double-stranded RNA simultaneously
19. Vortex and incubate mixture immediately after combination
20. Mix 4 microliters of double-stranded RNA solution with 10 microliters of insect saline buffer (control)
21. Mix 4 microliters of double-stranded RNA solution with 10 microliters of extracted enzymes from hemolymph or midgut juice
22. Add 2 microliters of EGTA to create enzyme-inhibited control
23. Add 2 microliters of RNase-free water (alternative to EGTA addition)
24. Incubate samples at 25°C for ≥1 hour
25. Add 200 microliters of extraction reagent
26. Add 40 microliters of chloroform
27. Vortex samples
28. Centrifuge samples for 10 minutes
29. Transfer 150 microliters of supernatant to new tube
30. Add 150 microliters of isopropanol to precipitate RNA
31. Incubate samples on ice for 15 minutes
32. Centrifuge samples
33. Discard supernatant
34. Wash RNA pellets twice by adding 200 microliters of 70% ethanol to each pellet and centrifuging
35. Dry double-stranded RNA pellets using centrifugal vacuum concentrator for three minutes
36. Resuspend pellets in 10 microliters of RNase-free water
37. Check integrity of treated double-stranded RNA using 1.5% agarose gel
38. Prepare bolus with either 4 microliters of double-stranded RNA lipoplexes or 250 nanograms of naked double-stranded RNA
39. Grab cockroach by wings using flexible forceps
40. Slowly pipette RNA solution droplet close to cockroach mouthparts
41. Observe cockroach ingesting droplet
42. Provide water bottles to cockroaches after final feeding
43. Assess insects daily for mortality and remove non-moving cockroaches

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Label a 15 milliliter conical tube for tissue sample
2. Add 2.5 milliliters of type four collagenase mixture and 30 units of elastase to the tube
3. Move lung to clean 1X HBSS well
4. Swirl lung in HBSS with forceps to remove remaining blood
5. Transfer lung to empty 3.5 centimeter tissue culture plate
6. Mince lung with scissors
7. Rinse plate with 2.5 milliliters of HBSS
8. Transfer HBSS with lung pieces to prepared 15 milliliter conical tube containing collagenase/elastase cocktail
9. Incubate tube for 75 minutes at 4°C on rocker/rotating wheel
10. Label one 50 ml centrifuge tube and one 10 cm tissue culture plate per mouse (including additional plates for dilutions if required)
11. Bring tube volume to 10 ml with 1X HBSS, pour contents over 70 μm cell strainer into 50 ml conical tube
12. Place 70 micrometer cell strainer into 50 milliliter conical tube
13. Use plunger of 1 milliliter syringe to gently grind sample through strainer
14. Centrifuge tube for 5 minutes at 350 × g
15. Discard supernatant after centrifugation
16. Wash pellet twice with 10 milliliters of 1X HBSS
17. Resuspend pellet in 10 milliliters of 60 micromolar 6-thioguanine complete culture media
18. Plate samples in 10 centimeter cell culture plates
19. Incubate plates at 37°C and 5% CO₂ for 5 days
20. Pour culture media off plates into appropriate waste container
21. Add 5 ml undiluted methanol to each plate
22. Incubate plates with methanol for 5 minutes at room temperature
23. Swirl methanol to ensure full plate coverage during incubation
24. Pour methanol off plates into appropriate waste container
25. Rinse each plate with 5 ml distilled water
26. Add 5 ml of 0.03% methylene blue per plate
27. Incubate plates with methylene blue for 5 minutes at room temperature
28. Swirl methylene blue solution to ensure full plate coverage during incubation
29. Pour methylene blue into appropriate waste container
30. Rinse each plate with 5 ml distilled water
31. Turn plate upside down
32. Blot plate against paper towel to remove excess liquid
33. Place plate on its lid for overnight air drying
34. Remove labeled lids from plates while ensuring clear sample identification
35. Line up stained lung plates on clean light surface
36. Take picture of plate collection in well-lit area while minimizing reflections
37. Take several pictures from multiple angles
38. Crop image to exclude lids and background elements
39. Open image in Fiji ImageJ software
40. Convert image to black and white using Image→Adjust→Color Threshold menu path
41. Set thresholding method to 'Default'
42. Set threshold color to 'Black and White'
43. Set color space to 'Lab'
44. Deselect 'Dark background' option
45. Select circle tool for analysis area
46. Draw consistent circular analysis area across all plates to maximize analyzed area while minimizing edge noise
47. Analyze selected circle to determine percentage of white area
48. Click 'Analyze' and select 'Analyze particles'
49. Set particle size range: 0 to infinity
50. Set circularity range: 0 to 1
51. Check 'Summarized' box and confirm analysis
52. Move circle to next plate by grabbing center
53. Repeat analysis for relocated circle
54. Copy and paste percent area result into spreadsheet
55. Average percent area results across images for each plate

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Open thoracic cavity
2. Clear blood from vasculature through intraoral perfusion of PBS with heparin
3. Inject casting agent into left ventricle to perfuse circulation system
4. Remove perfused brain and fix in Paraformaldehyde
5. Dehydrate and clarify brain tissue with ethanol and methyl salicylate
6. Anesthetize mouse via intraperitoneal injection of ketamine and xylazine diluted in saline
7. Confirm full anesthesia by paw pinch test
8. Make chest incision below xiphoid process using scissors
9. Cut through diaphragm
10. Cut between dorsal and ventral segments of ribcage
11. Fold up sternum and adjacent chest wall
12. Secure folded tissue with hemostat to expose pericardial sac
13. Open pericardial sac using tweezers if necessary
14. Open pericardial sac with tweezers to expose heart
15. Begin perfusing PBS with 5 units/mL heparin at 37°C using pump set to 100 mmHg
16. Insert blunt 22-gauge needle connected to pump outflow into left ventricle near heart apex
17. Cut open right atrium to enable blood outflow and continue perfusion until blood is removed from circulation
18. Mix 5 mL of diluent with 4 mL of filtered compound
19. Add 450 μL of curing agent to mixture
20. Mix casting agent solution immediately before use
21. Draw casting agent solution into 10 mL syringe
22. Attach blunt 20-gauge needle to syringe
23. Fill attached needle with solution
24. Insert needle into left ventricle through existing hole
25. Secure needle in place with hemostat clamp
26. Manually inject casting agent solution into left ventricle at 3 ml/min
27. Monitor for perfusion indicators: casting agent in intestine/liver vasculature, distal limb discoloration, nose/tongue discoloration
28. Readjust needle position if necessary to achieve proper perfusion
29. Remove brain
30. Place brain in 4% paraformaldehyde overnight at 4°C
31. Dehydrate brain in 25% ethanol at room temperature (initiating 7-day ethanol series)
32. Immerse perfused brain in methyl salicylate for 2 days at room temperature to clarify tissue around blood vessels
