question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Rinse cell culture with PBS
2. Detach cells using 0.25% Trypsin and HBSS
3. Neutralize trypsin reaction with Complete RPMI Medium after 5 minutes
4. Transfer cells to conical tube for centrifugation
5. Resuspend cell pellet in fresh HBSS
6. Adjust cell concentration to 2×10⁶ cells/mL in HBSS after counting
7. Draw cell suspension into 1 mL syringe equipped with 18-gauge needle
8. Place sterile surgical drape over heating pad
9. Confirm sedation level by toe pinch
10. Remove hair from left flank of mouse
11. Apply vet ointment to eyes
12. Swab implantation site with 5% povidone iodine antiseptic
13. Make 1-1.5 cm incision on left flank using sterile scalpel without penetrating peritoneum
14. Separate dermis from peritoneum using scissors
15. Dab incision site with sterile cotton gauze to remove blood
16. Grasp mouse head with left hand
17. Support mouse flank with right hand
18. Locate spleen through peritoneum
19. Palpate mouse from underneath using one finger
20. Maintain firm hold on animal to provide tension across peritoneum and kidney
21. Have assistant deliver 0.1 milliliters of Renca cells through intact peritoneum into kidney center
22. Hold needle in place for 5-10 seconds to reduce cell backflow
23. Close incision with thin layer of cyanoacrylate tissue adhesive
24. Make midline incision with scissors starting at mid-abdomen through ribcage, neck, and salivary glands
25. Carefully remove ribs using scissors and forceps without compromising lung tissue
26. Remove connective tissue to expose trachea
27. Fill 3 ml syringe with 18-gauge needle using 10% India Ink in PBS
28. Thread silk suture under trachea using forceps
29. Tie loose suture knot around trachea
30. Insert needle into trachea and inflate lungs with 1-1.5 ml India Ink solution
31. Tighten suture knot to prevent ink backflow
32. Remove needle and excise connective tissue to extract inflated lungs
33. Fix lungs in 15 ml conical tube containing 5 ml Fekete's solution in chemical fume hood at room temperature
34. Remove de-stained lungs after 24-48 hours and carefully dissect lobes in 35×10 mm petri dish
35. Count white tumor nodules under dissecting microscope

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Open webpage shown in video
2. Input approximately 60 base pairs of sequence flanking desired edit site
3. Select C. Elegans genome and protospacer adjacent motif according to protocol
4. Click submit button
5. Choose top-ranked target sequence closest to edit site on results page
6. Centrifuge lyophilized sgRNA tube at room temperature at 12,000 g for 1 minute
7. Add 60 microliters of nuclease-free TE buffer to the tube
8. Resuspend sgRNA by pipetting up and down 15-20 times gently using p200 pipette
9. Centrifuge lyophilized ssODN tube
10. Resuspend ssODN in nuclease-free water to 100 µM concentration
11. Centrifuge reagents at maximum speed and 4°C for 2 minutes
12. Add reagents in specified order to nuclease-free PCR tube
13. Mix contents gently and thoroughly using p20 pipette
14. Incubate tube at room temperature for 10 minutes
15. Allow injected P0 animals to recover at room temperature for 1-2 hours on OP50-seeded 35 mm NGM plate
16. Transfer single injected P0 animals to individual OP50-seeded 35 mm NGM plates using platinum wire worm pick
17. Use fluorescent microscope to identify P0 plates containing F1 progeny expressing mCherry in pharynx
18. Select three P0 plates containing the most mCherry-positive F1 animals
19. Use worm pick to single 8-12 mCherry-positive F1 animals per selected P0 plate (total 24-36 animals)
20. Allow individual F1s to self-fertilize and lay eggs at room temperature for 1-2 days
21. Transfer F1 worms using worm pick into individual PCR strip tube caps containing 7 microliters of worm lysis buffer
22. Centrifuge PCR tubes at maximum speed and room temperature for 1 minute
23. Freeze PCR tubes at -80°C for 1 hour
24. Lyse frozen worms in thermocycler using specified program
25. Set up PCR master mix according to table specifications
26. Add 21 microliters of PCR master mix into clean PCR tubes
27. Add 4 microliters of worm lysis solution to PCR tubes
28. Mix reaction thoroughly by pipetting
29. Run PCR program following manufacturer's guidelines
30. Purify PCR reaction products
31. Purify PCR reactions using DNA clean and concentrate kit following manufacturer's instructions
32. Dilute DNA by adding 10 microliters of water to spin column
33. Set up restriction enzyme master mix
34. Add 10 microliters of master mix to each cleaned PCR reaction
35. Incubate tubes at 37 degrees Celsius for 1-2 hours
36. Separate digested PCR products on 1.5% agarose gel using 1x TAE buffer at 120 volts
37. Examine enzyme-digested samples for presence of bands indicating potential edited animals
38. Use remaining 3 microliters of worm lysis for PCR repetition
39. Load completed PCR reactions on 1% agarose gel
40. Separate and extract band using gel extraction kit
41. Perform Sanger sequencing using F1 primer
42. Select 8-12 F2 animals from verified heterozygous edited animals
43. Carry out genotyping and sequence verification according to text protocol

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Add 0.5 milliliters of trypsin to cancer cell culture in tissue culture hood
2. Count cancer cells using hemocytometer
3. Dilute cells to concentration of 5 × 10³ cells per milliliter
4. Transfer cell suspensions to clearly labeled sterile universal tubes
5. Resuspend cells by gentle inversion to prevent clumping
6. Add 200 microliters of cell culture to each well of 96-well plate using pipette
7. Add 200 microliters of 1X PBS to each empty well in 96-well plate
8. Add 200 microliters of 1X PBS to each empty well to prevent evaporation
9. Incubate cells in incubator at 37 degrees Celsius
10. Check cell morphology using bright-field microscopy after 24-hour incubation
11. Incubate spheroids for additional 48 hours to develop 3D cellular architecture
12. Place collagen, 5X culture medium, one-molar sodium hydroxide, and one 20-milliliter tube on ice
13. Add 10.4 milliliters of cold collagen into chilled culture tube slowly to avoid bubbles
14. Add 1.52 milliliters of cold sterile 5X culture medium gently to avoid bubbles
15. Add 72 microliters of cold sterile one-molar sodium hydroxide just before use and keep solution on ice
16. Mix solution gently by pipetting while avoiding bubbles
17. Leave mixture on ice until use
18. Remove 190 microliters of supernatant from 96-well plate using pipette angled toward well side
19. Add 100 microliters of collagen mix to each well by pipetting down well side
20. Incubate plate in incubator for at least 10 minutes
21. Incubate plate in incubator for at least 10 minutes to allow collagen polymerization
22. Add inhibitors at 2X concentration to five milliliters of culture medium
23. Pipette 100 microliters of medium gently down the side of each well to avoid spheroid disturbance
24. Observe and image each spheroid by bright-field microscopy
25. Observe and image spheroids by bright-field microscopy at 0h, 24h, 48h, and 72h timepoints
26. Return plate to incubator
27. Place plate in tissue culture hood
28. Place 96-well plate in tissue culture hood
29. Gently replace supernatant with 100 microliters of 1X PBS
30. Repeat PBS wash step three additional times
31. Remove final PBS wash from each well
32. Add 100 microliters of 4% formaldehyde in 1X PBS to each well
33. Transfer 96-well plate to lab bench and cover with foil
34. Incubate plate for 24 hours at room temperature
35. Remove formaldehyde solution from wells
36. Replace with 1X PBS
37. Repeat PBS wash three additional times
38. Replace PBS with 100 microliters of freshly prepared Triton X-100 solution
39. Incubate plate for 30 minutes at room temperature
40. Prepare blocking solution during incubation
41. Prepare blocking solution with 50 milliliters of 1X PBS and 0.05% skimmed milk powder in 50-milliliter tube
42. Mix blocking solution thoroughly
43. Remove Triton X-100
44. Wash three times with 1X PBS
45. Add 100 microliters of blocking solution to each well
46. Incubate for 15 minutes
47. Dilute anti-mouse IgG acetylated tubulin antibody in blocking buffer at 1:100 ratio
48. Centrifuge primary antibody-blocking buffer mix for 5 minutes at 15,682 × g
49. Remove blocking solution from each well
50. Add 25-50 microliters of antibody-blocking buffer supernatant to each well
51. Incubate in dark at room temperature for 1 hour
52. Remove antibody solution from each well
53. Wash wells three times with 100 microliters of 1X PBS
54. Dilute secondary antibody in blocking buffer at recommended concentration including additional fluorescent stains
55. Centrifuge secondary antibody solution for 5 minutes at 15,682 × g
56. Remove blocking solution from each well
57. Add 25-50 microliters of secondary antibody supernatant to each well
58. Incubate in dark for 1.5 hours at room temperature
59. Remove secondary antibody solution from wells
60. Wash wells three times with 100 microliters of 1X PBS per wash
61. Lift individual collagen plugs by suction using a plastic 200-microliter pipette
62. Transfer collagen plugs onto center of glass slide
63. Add one drop of suitable mountant to cover collagen plug
64. Position coverslip on top of sample
65. Store slide at room temperature in the dark
66. Leave all reagents on ice until ready for use to prevent premature collagen polymerization
67. Take extra care when handling formaldehyde during fixation step following health and safety guidelines

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Cut and gently pull back outer skin of peritoneum to expose thoracic cavity
2. Open diaphragm by cutting rib cage to expose heart and lungs
3. Fill 5 milliliter syringe with EDTA-HBSS solution
4. Connect 25 gauge needle to syringe
5. Insert needle into right ventricle
6. Use forceps to secure myocardial tissue around needle
7. Gently inject solution while maintaining constant pressure
8. Remove and discard draining lymph nodes
9. Collect lung tissue and transfer to Petri dish on ice
10. Fill GentleMACS tube with 5 ml of lung collagenase digestion solution
11. Chop lung tissue into small pieces
12. Transfer chopped tissue into tube using razor blade
13. Run GentleMACS dissociation program M_lung_01
14. Incubate sample at 37°C for 30 minutes
15. Shake tube every 5 minutes during incubation to resuspend tissue fragments
16. Run GentleMACS dissociation program M_lung_02
17. Transfer sample to 15 milliliter conical tube
18. Centrifuge cells at 335 × g for 10 minutes at 4°C
19. Discard supernatant
20. Add 2 milliliters of CK lysing buffer to pellet
21. Incubate for 1 minute at room temperature to lyse red blood cells
22. Wash cells with 13 milliliters of cold PBS supplemented with 0.5% BSA
23. Discard supernatant
24. Resuspend pellet in 5 milliliters of cold PBS with BSA
25. Pass cell solution through 100 micrometer nylon mesh
26. Determine total cell number by trypan blue exclusion
27. Fill 15 ml conical tube with 5 ml collagenase solution per lung
28. Add chopped lung tissue to collagenase solution in tube
29. Incubate lung tissue at 37°C for 1 hour
30. Vortex tissue every 15 minutes during incubation to re-suspend fragments
31. Disrupt digested tissue by passing sample 6-8 times through 3 ml syringe with 20-gauge needle while avoiding bubble formation
32. Wash single cell suspension for 10 minutes at 200 × g at 4°C
33. Discard supernatant
34. Resuspend cells in 400 microliters of separation buffer
35. Block Fc-mediated nonspecific binding with 0.5 µg anti-CD16/32 antibody per 10^6 cells
36. Incubate cells with antibody for 30 minutes at 4°C
37. Wash cells with 10 milliliters of cold separation buffer
38. Resuspend cells in 400 microliters of cold separation buffer
39. Add 100 microliters of CD11C microbeads to cell suspension
40. Mix cell-bead solution thoroughly
41. Incubate cell-bead solution for 15 minutes in refrigerator
42. Remove unbound beads by washing with 10 milliliters of cold separation buffer
43. Discard supernatant after washing
44. Resuspend cell pellet in 1 milliliter of cold separation buffer
45. Load samples into AutoMACS Pro separator
46. Run POSSELD program on AutoMACS Pro separator
47. Incubate enriched CD11C-positive cell suspension with anti-CD11C PEI7 and MHC Class II antibodies
48. Wash cells with cold PBS plus BSA
49. Resuspend cells in 0.5 milliliters of PBS plus BSA
50. Pass cell suspension through 40 micrometer nylon mesh filter
51. Add purified CD11C-positive cells to FACS tube for quantitation
52. Add 100 microliters of PBS plus BSA to five milliliter collection tubes
53. Sort filtered cell suspension by FACS while maintaining refrigerated temperature

1. Wash rootletin-eGFP and rootletin-mScarlet cells two times with 10 milliliters of PBS per wash
2. Dilute Violet cell dye to 500 nanomolar in PBS and Far Red cell dye to 200 nanomolar in PBS
3. Label rootletin-eGFP cells with diluted Violet cell dye and rootletin-mScarlet cells with diluted Far Red cell dye for one minute at room temperature
4. Stop labeling reactions by adding 10 milliliters of DMEM
5. Incubate reactions with DMEM for five minutes
6. Wash labeled cell cultures and unlabeled parental cell culture with 10 milliliters of fresh PBS
7. Incubate all cultures with one milliliter of prewarmed trypsin
8. Transfer detached cell solutions to individual 15 milliliter conical tubes
9. Centrifuge cell solutions (implied by tube transfer purpose)
10. Gently resuspend dye-labeled pellets in one milliliter of PBS
11. Resuspend unlabeled cell pellets in one milliliter of DMEM
12. Return all cell samples to cell culture incubator
13. Mix 500 microliters of Violet dye labeled cells with 500 microliters of Far Red dye labeled cells in a new 15 milliliter tube
14. Sediment unmixed labeled cells by centrifugation
15. Resuspend pellets in one milliliter of fresh DMEM
16. Place cells back into incubator
17. Collect mixed cells by centrifugation
18. Add 700 microliters of 50% 1450 PEG to pellet in drop-wise manner over 30 seconds
19. Add 10 milliliters of serum-free DMEM drop-wise over 30 seconds
20. Place tubes into incubator for 10 minutes
21. Sediment mixed cells by centrifugation
22. Gently resuspend pellet in one milliliter of complete medium
23. Filter all cells through a 70 micrometer strainer into individual FACS tubes
24. Create forward scatter versus side scatter plot in cytometer software
25. Create doublet discrimination plot in cytometer software

27. Create gates to identify fused cells
28. Run single positive Violet dye labeled cells
29. Run single positive Violet dye labeled cells to confirm no spillover into Far Red channel
30. Run single positive Far Red dye labeled cells to confirm no signal in Violet channel
31. Run fusion sample to confirm fused cells are visible with current gates
32. Align sorting stream centrally into eight-well imaging dish containing 100 microliters of growth medium
33. Sort double positive fused cells directly into dish
34. Place dish into cell culture incubator
35. Incubate dish for 2 to 16 hours
36. Replace cell culture supernatant with 200 microliters of 4% paraformaldehyde
37. Incubate in paraformaldehyde for 15 minutes at room temperature
38. Wash cells three times with 200 microliters of PBS per wash
39. Permeabilize cells in 200 microliters of 0.1% nonionic surfactant
40. Incubate in permeabilization agent for 10 minutes at room temperature
41. Block nonspecific binding with 200 microliters of 3% bovine serum albumin in PBS
42. Incubate in blocking solution for 30 minutes at room temperature
43. Label with antibodies of interest in 150 microliters of staining buffer
44. Wash cells two times with 300 microliters of PBS for five minutes per wash

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Pre-coat six-well plates with 0.5% gelatin
2. Incubate plates in humidified chamber at 37°C and 5% CO₂ for two hours
3. Transfer aseptically removed canine blood vessels to Petri dishes filled with ice-cold HBSS
4. Remove adherent tissue and fat from vessel exterior using surgical scissors while maintaining vessel integrity and visibility
5. Close vessel branches using ligatures
6. Remove closed branches using surgical scissors or scalpel
7. Insert curved Halsted mosquito forceps into one vessel end
8. Clamp forceps tip at opposite vessel end
9. Retract forceps slowly to invert vessel
10. Complete inversion until vessel is completely inside out with endothelial layer exposed
11. Place purse string sutures at both ends of vessel to close completely
12. Use ligature ends to manipulate inverted vessel
13. Transfer inverted vessel to 50 milliliter tube
14. Rinse vessel twice with HBSS to remove erythrocytes or residual freezing medium
15. Add 30 milliliters of collagenase type two and dispase in canine endothelial cell growth medium
16. Incubate tube at 37 degrees Celsius with gentle agitation for vessel digestion
17. Remove vessel from tube
18. Centrifuge cell suspension at 250 × g for 5 minutes
19. Resuspend cell pellet in CECGM
20. Seed 2 ml of suspension per well in 1-3 gelatin-coated wells
21. Culture cells at 37°C in humidified 5% CO₂ atmosphere
22. Change culture medium twice weekly
23. Wash cells with pre-warmed HBSS to remove dead/non-attached cells
24. Add 200 microliters of recombinant cell dissociation enzyme to each well
25. Incubate dish for 5 minutes or until cell detachment
26. Transfer cells to 15 milliliter tube
27. Add 10 milliliters of CECGM with 10% FCS to stop trypsinization
28. Centrifuge at 250×g for 5 minutes
29. Transfer cells to gelatin pre-coated plate or flask for continued culture
30. Culture cells in CECGM on 0.5% gelatin pre-coated T75 flask
31. Centrifuge cell suspension at 250 × g for 5 minutes
32. Discard supernatant
33. Resuspend cells in 1 ml culture medium
34. Dilute 10 μl cell suspension 1:1 with 0.4% Trypan Blue
35. Count cells using automated cell counter
36. Plate 4 × 10⁵ viable cells in new T75 flask
37. Add 10 milliliters of pre-warmed CECGM to flask
38. Culture flask at 37°C in humidified atmosphere with 5% CO₂
39. Collect ≥1×10³ cells as pellet for RNA isolation
40. Add 20 microliters of sample preparation reagent to pellet
41. Incubate mixture for 1 minute to lyse cells
42. Store lysed sample at -70°C

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Prepare 5 mL culture of E. coli in LB medium with antibiotics carrying template sRNA expression phagemid
2. Grow culture overnight at 37°C with shaking
3. Prepare two PCR reactions using 10-50× recommended DNA template amount and high-fidelity polymerase
4. Set up one PCR reaction with forward primer and one with reverse primer
5. Combine two PCR reactions in a microcentrifuge tube
6. Anneal products by heating to 98°C in boiling water bath
7. Turn off heat source immediately
8. Allow water bath to slowly cool to room temperature over 1-2 hours
9. Add 1 microliter of DpnI restriction enzyme to mixture
10. Incubate at 37°C for one hour (or manufacturer-recommended time for complete digestion)
11. Transform chemically competent E. coli cells
12. Transform chemically competent E. coli cells with 1-5 microliters of annealed PCR product
13. Isolate single colonies by selective plating on LB plates with antibiotics
14. Collect small quantity of cells from single transformed colony using 200 microliter pipet tip
15. Add collected cells to 50 microliters of nuclease-free water in microcentrifuge tube
16. Mix cell suspension by pipetting
17. Mix sample by pipetting
18. Lyse cells by heating to 95 degrees Celsius for 2 minutes using benchtop thermocycler or boiling waterbath
19. Perform PCR using 1 microliter of lysed cells as DNA template under specified conditions
20. Inoculate 5 ml culture with verified sRNA clone
21. Grow inoculated culture overnight
22. Combine 750 microliters of overnight culture with 250 microliters of 60% glycerol in screwcap cryotube
23. Prepare 5 mL overnight culture of E. coli carrying sRNA expression phagemid
24. Prepare 5 mL overnight culture of E. coli carrying M13KO7 helper plasmid
25. Co-transform chemically competent E. coli with 1 μL sRNA expression phagemid and 1 μL helper plasmid
26. Plate transformed cells on LB-agar with antibiotics for dual selection
27. Prepare 10 milliliter culture from single colony of co-transformed strain
28. Centrifuge culture at 3300 x g for 10 minutes
29. Collect supernatant
30. Filter supernatant through 0.2 micron filter
31. Store packaged phagemid filtrate at four degrees Celsius
32. Inoculate single colony into 5 ml LB medium with antibiotics
33. Incubate culture overnight at 37°C with shaking
34. Dilute F+ target cells 1:100 in 5 ml selective LB medium
35. Incubate diluted culture for ~2 hours until OD600 reaches 0.3
36. Add M13-packaged phagemids to target cells at 1:100 ratio
37. Incubate infection mixture at 37°C with shaking for 30-60 minutes
38. Assay sRNA silencing phenotype

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Cut 0.5 mm diameter tungsten rod into 25 mm lengths using wire clippers
2. Clean rods in acetone using sonication for 15 minutes
3. Rinse rods with deionized water for a few seconds
4. Transfer sodium hydroxide solution to a wash bottle
5. Fill separatory funnel with 1.5 molar sodium hydroxide solution
6. Place copper FEP catcher block inside wide-based glass beaker
7. Position copper cathode above catcher block
8. Space cathode with insulating standoffs
9. Set DC power supply current to 200 milliamps
10. Place tungsten rod in holder
11. Connect positive power supply terminal to 4-40 holding screw
12. Insert tungsten rod through hole in copper cathode
13. Extend tungsten rod approximately 12 millimeters beyond cathode hole
14. Connect negative power supply terminal to copper cathode
15. Adjust drip rate from separatory funnel to match hole drip rate at approximately one drip every three seconds
16. Wait for copper cathode reservoir to fill
17. Turn on DC power supply to initiate etching
18. Remove bottom FEP from catcher block using pliers or tweezers
19. Loosen 4-40 screw on holder
20. Gently pull upper FEP out of holder
21. Rinse etched rod with acetone
22. Rinse etched rod with deionized water
23. Store FEPs in desiccator
24. Select and examine FEP under microscope, discarding bent or irregular specimens
25. Place Faraday cup 2 cm from FEP tip
26. Connect high voltage supply to SHV feedthrough on FEP holder
27. Connect high voltage supply to SHV feedthrough on FEP holder
28. Connect picoammeter to BNC feedthrough attached to Faraday cup
29. Pump down setup to pressure ≤1×10⁻⁴ millibar
30. Gradually increase bias voltage on FEP
31. Monitor electron beam current on Faraday cup via picoammeter
32. Increase high voltage current in incremental steps upon detecting field emission
33. Increase high voltage current in 50-volt incremental steps
34. Record average electron beam current at each voltage step
35. Maintain current below 1 microamp during voltage increase
36. Continue voltage increase until reaching maximum value with current at few hundred nanoamperes
37. Operate testing setup in field emission mode at 5 nanoamperes for 1 hour to condition tip
38. Repeat current versus voltage scan

1. Add 50 grams of air-dried soil sample (sieved to 2 mm) into 250 ml conical polypropylene centrifuge tube
2. Record soil mass to at least four significant figures
3. Add 50 ml of 1.85 g/cm³ SPT solution to centrifuge tube
4. Cap tube tightly
5. Shake tube vigorously by hand for 60 seconds to break non-water-stable aggregates
6. Secure tightly sealed tube to platform shaker
7. Shake tube for 2 hours at 40-120 rpm
8. Reduce standing height of soil layer
9. Remove tube from shaker
10. Shake tube vigorously by hand to agitate denser aggregated material
11. Equalize centrifuge tube masses by adding SPT solution where required
12. Shake tubes vigorously by hand for 30 seconds after SPT addition
13. Centrifuge tubes for 10 minutes at 3,000 g in swinging bucket centrifuge
14. Test supernatant density by drawing 5 milliliters with pipette and measuring mass with balance
15. Adjust SPT volume if required to achieve desired density
16. Shake tubes again if density adjustment was performed
17. Centrifuge tubes again if density adjustment was performed
18. Attach 1-liter sidearm flask to vacuum pump
19. Attach 1-liter sidearm flask to vacuum pump
20. Place 110mm glass fiber filter (0.7μm pore) in 12cm porcelain Buchner funnel
21. Seal funnel to sidearm flask using conical rubber gasket
22. Set up vacuum line manifold for simultaneous sample processing
23. Attach second 1-liter sidearm flask to vacuum pump with rubber stopper
24. Connect 0.5-meter aspiration tubing to rubber stopper
25. Gently aspirate supernatant
26. Gently aspirate supernatant including suspended top-layer material along sides of centrifuge tube
27. Plunge aspiration tube tip quickly in deionized/distilled (DDI) water
28. Draw approximately five milliliters of DDI water through aspiration line by applying vacuum
29. Remove rubber stopper after aspiration
30. Remove rubber stopper and aspiration tube attachment from side arm flask
31. Pour flask contents into Buchner funnel while maintaining vacuum pump operation
32. Rinse flask with DDI water, swirl, and pour contents into Buchner funnel
33. Repeat rinsing and pouring until all residues are removed from flask
34. Re-suspend soil pellet in 50 milliliters of SPT
35. Vigorously shake centrifuge tube for 60 seconds by hand to break up pellet
36. Centrifuge tube for 10 minutes at 3,000 g
37. Add 50 milliliters of DDI water into centrifuge tube containing heavy fraction pellet
38. Shake tube vigorously by hand for 60 seconds while breaking up hard pellet
39. Centrifuge tube for 10 minutes at 3,000 g
40. Aspirate supernatant
41. Scrape soil from centrifuge tube into clean labeled glass beaker or jar
42. Pour DDI water into centrifuge tube to loosen remaining soil
43. Shake centrifuge tube
44. Add slurry from tube to glass container
45. Rinse centrifuge tube with DDI water
46. Transfer washing from tube back to glass container
47. Place glass container in drying oven set to 40-60°C
48. Dry sample until constant dry weight is reached (24-72 hours)
49. Fill Buchner funnel containing light fraction material with DDI water
50. Filter contents through glass fiber filters
51. Repeat washing procedure twice after water filtration completes
52. Turn off vacuum pump
53. Remove funnel from sidearm flask
54. Rinse particles from filter into labeled glass beaker using DDI water from wash bottle while holding funnel horizontally
55. Place glass container in drying oven set to 40-60°C
56. Dry until constant weight is achieved (24-72 hours)
57. Scrape dried material from container into plastic weigh boat
58. Record mass to fourth decimal place
59. Transfer sample to labeled storage vial or bag

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Sterilize tweezers with 70% ethanol
2. Place one glass coverslip into well of 24-well plate
3. Add 500 microliters of vitronectin solution into each well containing coverslip
4. Remove coating solution after incubation
5. Wash coverslips with one milliliter of PBS
6. Incubate RPE-1 cells in 1 ml of 0.05% trypsin at 37°C
7. Resuspend RPE-1 cells in 6 ml of DMEM medium to inactivate trypsin
8. Centrifuge cells at 150 G at room temperature for 5 minutes
9. Resuspend cells in 1 ml of culturing medium
10. Seed 500 microliters of cell suspension onto coated coverslip
11. Pulse cells with 10 micromoles of EdU
12. Incubate with EdU for 30 minutes at 37 degrees Celsius
13. Remove medium containing EdU
14. Chase cells with 10 micromoles of thymidine
15. Incubate with thymidine for 10 minutes at 37 degrees Celsius
16. Treat cells with 250 micromoles of hydrogen peroxide for one hour after thymidine removal
17. Incubate washed cells in 1 ml CSK extraction buffer for 5 minutes at room temperature
18. Fix cells in 0.5 ml of 3.6% paraformaldehyde solution for 10 minutes at room temperature
19. Wash cells with 1 ml PBS containing 0.05% Triton X-100 to remove paraformaldehyde
20. Remove solution and wash cells twice with 1 ml blocking buffer
21. Add 1 ml blocking buffer
22. Gently rock plate for 10 minutes at room temperature
23. Remove blocking buffer
24. Add 500 microliters of Click reaction cocktail containing picolyl-azide 647
25. Wash cells two times with PBS containing 0.05% Triton X-100 for 10 minutes at room temperature
26. Incubate washed cells in 1 milliliter of blocking buffer for 30 minutes at room temperature
27. Incubate cells in primary antibody in 250 to 500 microliters of blocking buffer for 2 hours at room temperature
28. Wash cells two times with Triton X-PBS
29. Wash cells in blocking buffer
30. Apply secondary antibody in 250-500 microliters of blocking buffer
31. Incubate secondary antibody at room temperature for two hours with gentle rocking
32. Wash cells in PBS and blocking buffer
33. Counterstain nuclei of washed cells in DAPI
34. Incubate DAPI stain for 10 minutes at room temperature
35. Wash cells with PBS for five minutes at room temperature
36. Dip coverslips into distilled water to remove salt crystals
37. Mount cover glass onto microscope slides in 10 microliters of mounting medium
38. Store stained slides at four degrees Celsius
39. Use epifluorescence microscope with DAPI, FITC, and Cy5 filter sets
40. Open image files in Fiji software
41. Open DAPI image
42. Select Process > Enhance Contrast
43. Set saturated pixels to 0.35%
44. Select Process > Binary > Convert to Mask
45. Select Process > Binary > Fill Holes
46. Select Analyze > Analyze Particles with size 10-infinity
47. Click Show All in ROI Manager
48. Open RPA2 image
49. Click Process menu and select Find Maxima
50. Set prominence value to highlight foci
51. Click Measure button in ROI manager
52. Divide rawintden column value by 255 to calculate nuclear single-stranded DNA foci

4. Resuspend pellet in 1 milliliter pasteurized spring water for second centrifugation
5. Transfer 500 microliters of conditioned medium per culture to individual wells of a glass spot plate
6. Transfer one Stentor cell into each well containing medium
7. Feed each Stentor culture with five microliters of prepared Chlamydomonas every 48 hours

9. Count new Stentor cells before each feeding
10. Adjust volume of delivered Chlamydomonas based on cell count
11. Gently pipette culture medium a few times to detach Stentor from meniscus of each well
12. Use one milliliter pipette to carefully aspirate media from wells without removing cells
13. Add 500 microliters of fresh conditioned media to each well
14. Add 20 milliliters of pasteurized spring water to autoclaved sterilized wide mouth glass jar
15. Carefully detach Stentor cells from bottom of each well
16. Collect all Stentor cells with one milliliter pipette
17. Gently transfer collected cells to glass jar
18. Add fresh pasteurized spring water to jar every 48 hours to maintain Stentor density at 20 cells/mL
19. Feed Stentor cells with 200-1000 microliters of prepared Chlamydomonas
20. Transfer entire jar contents to two-cup glass container when culture volume reaches 90% capacity
21. Feed transferred culture with 2 milliliters prepared Chlamydomonas per 100 milliliters of culture
22. Add pasteurized spring water every 4-5 days to maintain Stentor density at 20 cells/mL
23. Inspect cultures weekly
24. Inspect cultures under 5X dissecting microscope for rotifers, fungus, and other growth
25. Remove contaminating microorganisms and abnormally shaped/colorless Stentor cells using one milliliter pipette
26. Assess if glass container is approximately 90% full to initiate splitting
27. Add 25 milliliters of pasteurized spring water to culture
28. Detach Stentor from glass using 50 milliliter pipette
29. Transfer approximately 50% of culture to new two-cup glass container
30. Use needle puller to make needles from capillary tubes

32. Place droplet on slide
33. Add two microliters of 4% methyl cellulose to slow Stentor motion
34. Place droplet under dissecting microscope
35. Hold glass needle parallel to cutting surface to prevent breakage
36. Gently press contracted Stentor with side of needle

41. Collect all cells from tube using 200μl pipette in single 125μl draw
42. Dispense cells into tube containing 500μl of 25% sucrose solution
43. Start stopwatch for sucrose treatment timing
44. Flick spin cells in tube rack for 1 minute
45. Collect all cells in single 200μl draw and hold in pipette tip
46. Hold cells in pipette tip until stopwatch reaches 2 minutes sucrose treatment
47. Eject Stentor cells into microcentrifuge tube with 1 ml pasteurized spring water
48. Perform three flick spin washes
49. Add 100 microliters of pasteurized spring water into one well of a glass spot plate
50. Collect one Stentor cell in four microliters of culture media using a 20 microliter pipette tip
51. Deposit the droplet onto the center of a 22x22 mm coverslip
52. Place four additional droplets around the central droplet
53. Invert coverslip and gently place it over the well containing pasteurized spring water

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Pick 30 to 40 young adult worms into watch glass dish containing 500 microliters of 1x M9 and levamisole
2. Remove and discard most media to eliminate transferred bacteria
3. Add 500 microliters fresh 1x M9 and levamisole
4. Rinse worms by gently drawing and dispensing media with pipette
5. Remove and discard most media after rinsing
6. Repeat wash cycle (steps 3-5) two to three times
7. Leave worms in approximately 100 microliters media for ≤8 minutes
8. Transfer worms to 25-millimeter-by-75-millimeter microscope slide coated with 0.001% poly-L-lysine using glass or polyethylene pipette
9. Remove excess media until approximately 15 microliters remains on slide
10. Position two 26 1/2-gauge needles crossed in scissor formation under dissecting microscope
11. Cut worms behind pharynx using crossed needles to release germlines
12. Complete dissection of all worms within five minutes
13. Place 22-millimeter-by-40-millimeter coverslip perpendicularly over slide with ends hanging off
14. Freeze slides on pre-chilled aluminum block maintained on dry ice for at least 20 minutes
15. Place chilled pencil on top of coverslip to prevent displacement from ice expansion
16. Flick off coverslips with a pencil
17. Dip slide into jar of fresh ice-cold methanol for 1 minute
18. Gently wipe edges of slide surrounding sample
19. Apply 150 microliters of formaldehyde fixative at room temperature for 5 minutes
20. Touch slide to paper towel at 90-degree angle to drain fixative
21. Block slides twice for 15 minutes at room temperature
22. Absorb solution into paper towel
23. Block slides twice for 15 minutes at room temperature in Coplin jar with 50 ml PBT/BSA
24. Block slides with PBT/BSA solution containing 10% normal goat serum
25. Gently wipe edges surrounding slide
26. Apply 100 microliters of solution to each slide
27. Gently agitate slide to spread solution
28. Incubate for one hour at room temperature in humid chamber
29. Place slide on paper towel to let solution run off
30. Gently wipe slide edges
31. Apply one drop of blocking reagent to 14mm×14mm space
32. Incubate slides for one hour at 37°C in humid chamber
33. Gently wipe slide edges
34. Dilute primary antibodies using antibody diluent
35. Apply 40 microliters of diluted primary antibody solution per 14-by-14-millimeter space on slides
36. Place slides in humid chamber
37. Incubate slides overnight at 4°C
38. Wash slides twice for 5 minutes each with 50 ml of 1x wash buffer A at room temperature in Coplin jar
39. Set Coplin jar on orbital shaker at 60 rpm
40. Place slides on paper towel to drain wash buffer
41. Gently wipe slide edges
42. Prepare 40-microliter solution containing plus and minus probes
43. Add probe solution to each 14x14 mm space
44. Incubate slides in humid chamber for 1 hour at 37°C
45. Wash slides twice for 5 minutes each with 50 mL of 1x wash buffer A at room temperature in Coplin jar
46. Set Coplin jar on orbital shaker at 60 rpm
47. Dilute ligation buffer 1:5 with ultrapure water
48. Dilute ligase 1:40 using diluted buffer to prepare ligation solution
49. Place slide on paper towel to drain wash buffer
50. Gently wipe slide edges
51. Add 40 μL ligation solution to each 14x14 mm space
52. Incubate slides in humid chamber for 30 minutes at 37°C
53. Add 40 microliters of freshly prepared light-protected amplification solution to each 14-by-14-millimeter space
54. Incubate slides in humid dark chamber at 37°C for 1 hour 40 minutes
55. Wash slides twice for 10 minutes each with 50 milliliters of 1x wash buffer B
56. Wash slides once for 1 minute with 50 milliliters of 0.01x wash buffer B
57. Add 10 microliters of mounting medium to each sample
58. Gently lay coverslip on sample allowing mounting medium to spread
59. Paint nail polish around coverslip edge to seal while avoiding movement
60. Let nail polish harden for at least 20 minutes at room temperature protected from light
61. Ensure sufficient water is present in chamber
62. Ensure slides are level inside chamber
63. Image extruded gonads on confocal microscope
64. Quantify images using Fiji/ImageJ workflow

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Pour 274.6 grams of 70% methanesulfonic acid into beaker in fume hood
2. Start stirring solution with stir bar
3. Add 300 milliliters of deionized water to solution
4. Continue stirring solution for 1-2 minutes to mix thoroughly
5. Combine 20.595 grams of 70% MSA, 150 milliliters of one-molar lead methanesulfonate, and 1.23 grams of sodium acetate in a beaker
6. Dilute mixture to 300 milliliters with deionized water
7. Stir mixture for one to two minutes to form electrolyte solution with sodium acetate additive
8. Polish bare graphite electrode with P100-grit aluminous sandpaper until no impurities are visible
9. Rinse polished electrode with deionized water
10. Prepare 1M hydrochloric acid solution by adding 20.83 grams of 35% hydrochloric acid to 200 milliliters of deionized water and stirring well
11. Soak graphite electrode in hydrochloric acid solution for at least eight hours
12. Rinse graphite electrode with deionized water
13. Dry graphite electrode with low-lint laboratory wipe
14. Polish nickel electrode with P100-grit aluminous sandpaper
15. Rinse nickel electrode with deionized water
16. Dry nickel electrode with low-lint laboratory wipe
17. Wrap polytetrafluoroethylene tape around one side of each electrode, leaving portion exposed for battery tester connection
18. Dissolve 3.03 grams of potassium nitrate in 300 milliliters of deionized water
19. Immerse exposed sides of both electrodes in 0.1-molar potassium nitrate solution
20. Place reference silver-silver chloride electrode in solution
21. Connect electrodes to potentiostat (graphite as positive electrode, nickel as negative electrode)
22. Apply 1.80 volts versus silver-silver chloride to positive electrode
23. Apply -1 volt versus silver-silver chloride to positive electrode
24. Rinse electrodes
25. Dry electrodes
26. Connect pretreated electrodes to custom electrode positioning board
27. Place positioning board on beaker equipped with stir bar
28. Fill beaker with electrolyte until solution reaches appropriate level
29. Place beaker assembly on stirring hot plate
30. Connect battery tester to electrodes
31. Cover beaker cell with plastic wrap to prevent evaporation
32. Weigh two positive electrodes and record their masses
33. Place a negative electrode in the center position of a Haring-Blum cell apparatus
34. Place one positive electrode in the assembly near the negative electrode
35. Place the other positive electrode at a distance several times greater than the distance between the first positive electrode and the negative electrode
36. Immerse the electrode in the electrolyte of interest
37. Connect the electrodes to a battery tester
38. Start the test by charging the battery assembly
39. Charge battery assembly with constant current density of 20 milliamperes per square centimeter for 30 minutes
40. Rinse positive electrodes with deionized water
41. Allow positive electrodes to dry in ambient air overnight
42. Weigh positive electrodes
43. Calculate amount of metal plated on each electrode
44. Rinse electroplated graphite electrode with deionized water
45. Allow electrode to dry at room temperature
46. Slice electrode into samples using diamond saw
47. Cold-mount electrode sample
48. Fix mounted sample in polisher
49. Mechanically polish sample with 14-mm silicon carbide sandpaper
50. Mechanically polish sample with 8-mm silicon carbide sandpaper
51. Mechanically polish sample with 3-mm silicon carbide sandpaper
52. Rinse sample with deionized water
53. Rinse sample with deionized water
54. Dry sample with nitrogen gas
55. Polish sample with 1-micrometer diamond suspension
56. Polish sample with 0.05-micrometer alumina slurry in deionized water
57. Deposit platinum layer on polished sample
58. Attach sample to platform with copper tape
59. Acquire SEM images of electroplated material

Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Dilute 2.2 molar sucrose to prepare six 10-50% sucrose gradients
2. Fill 30 mL syringe with approximately 16 mL of 10% sucrose solution
3. Place syringe on syringe pump and connect to needle
4. Verify absence of air bubbles in syringe, tubing, and needle
5. Wipe needle tip to remove residual solution
6. Position needle tip at bottom center of tube using motorized stage
7. Set syringe pump to 2 mL/min flow rate for 2.3 mL volume
8. Dispense 2.3 mL of 10% sucrose solution
9. Lower motorized stage after dispensing
10. Repeat steps 2-9 for all six gradients
11. Add 20% sucrose solution to tube bottom
12. Add 30% sucrose solution to tube bottom
13. Add 40% sucrose solution to tube bottom
14. Add 50% sucrose solution to tube bottom
15. Collect CD1 mouse embryos at embryonic day 12 and place them in 10 cm plate containing ice-cold HBSS on ice
16. Transfer one embryo under dissection scope to six cm plate containing ice-cold HBSS
17. Fix embryo head position using 21-23 gauge needles by penetrating through eyes at 45° angle and applying force to secure needles to plate
18. Position needles at 45-degree angle
19. Secure needles to plate
20. Remove skin and skull using #5 forceps (working middle to sides)
21. Cut olfactory bulbs
22. Remove meninges to expose cortical tissues
23. Dissect cortical tissues using curved forceps
24. Transfer dissected tissues into 2-3ml neurobasal medium on ice
25. Collect tissues from multiple embryos as required
26. Add cycloheximide to neurobasal medium containing dissected tissues to final concentration of 100 micrograms per milliliter
27. Incubate mixture at 37 degrees Celsius for 10 minutes
28. Centrifuge tissue at 500 x g for five minutes at four degrees Celsius
29. Discard supernatant
30. Prepare ice cold PBS supplemented with 100 micrograms per milliliter cycloheximide
31. Wash tissues twice with prepared ice cold PBS solution
32. Add 500 microliters of cell lysis buffer supplemented with 4 microliters of RNase inhibitor
33. Pipette mixture up and down to resuspend tissue
34. Lysate tissue gently using insulin needle
35. Incubate lysate on ice for 10 minutes with brief vortexing every 2-3 minutes
36. Centrifuge lysate at 2000 x g for 5 minutes at 4°C
37. Transfer supernatant to new centrifuge tube on ice
38. Repeat centrifugation at 13,000 x g
39. Transfer supernatant to new centrifuge tube on ice
40. Measure RNA concentration in tissue lysate using UV-Vis spectrophotometer
41. Load tissue lysate samples onto sucrose gradients by slowly dispensing to ultracentrifuge tube walls
42. Gently place sucrose gradients in ultracentrifuge buckets
43. Ultracentrifuge samples at 190,000 x g and 4°C for 90 minutes
44. Place empty ultracentrifuge tube on tube piercer and gently penetrate tube
45. Fill 30 milliliter syringe with 25 milliliters of 60% sucrose chase solution
46. Gently press syringe to fill empty tube and pass chase solution through 254 nanometer UV monitor
47. Press auto zero on UV monitor to register detection baseline
48. Press Play on software to begin recording
49. Pause recording on software after baseline establishment
50. Retract chase solution ensuring no residual solution remains in system
51. Load sample tube on tube piercer
52. Penetrate tube with needle from bottom
53. Begin recording on software
54. Verify absence of air bubbles in line
55. Start syringe pump
56. Start fraction collector for polysome fraction collection
57. Set fraction collector to 30-second interval
58. Collect fractions into 1.5 milliliter tubes
59. Separate cortical lysate containing RNA from eight embryos using sucrose gradient fractionation into 12 fractions
60. Identify ribosomal subunits (40S, 60S) and complexes (80S monosome, polysomes) in fractions using UV absorbance peaks at 254 nm
61. Use syringe pumps to eject sucrose
62. Wash system with RNase-free water during fractionation and sample collection

1. Fill vessels slowly to avoid overflow and spillage
2. Ensure vessels are completely full using syringes after initial bubble removal
3. Take culture vessels out of plastic packaging
4. Label each vessel on the rim according to cell type/line, control/treatment status, and relevant information
5. Stabilize vessel and carefully open fill port without touching cap peg or O-ring
6. Place fill port cap on ethanol pad with peg/O-ring facing up
7. Load vessel with 10 mL of appropriate complete media using sterile serological pipet
8. Carefully place cap on fill port
9. Ensure syringe port caps and fill port are securely closed
10. Place filled vessels in incubator to prime for cell culture
11. Retrieve media-primed vessels from incubator
12. Retrieve media-primed vessels from incubator
13. Load T25 suspension culture flask with 10 mL cell culture from stock
14. Add 10 mL stock cell culture to 15 mL conical tube for syringe loading
15. Unscrew syringe port caps to remove them from vessel
16. Ensure stock stopcocks are in open position
17. Stabilize vessel and carefully open fill port without touching cap peg or O-ring
18. Place fill port cap on ethanol pad with peg/O-ring facing up during filling
19. Verify stopcocks are in open position
20. Empty vessel media into waste container using serological pipette
21. Aspirate media using sterile glass pipette attached to vacuum system
22. Avoid touching oxygenation membrane during aspiration to prevent damage
23. Tightly close 50 mL conical tube containing prepared stock cell culture
24. Gently invert conical tube several times to thoroughly mix contents
25. Draw up 10 mL of cell culture stock from 50 mL tube using fresh sterile serological pipet
26. Pick up vessel
27. Tilt vessel to position fill port towards top
28. Dispense cell culture stock into vessel through fill port while tilting vessel back down to avoid spilling, ensuring pipette does not contact oxygenation membrane
29. Place fill port cap back on vessel without touching O-ring
30. Remove first 3 mL syringe from its package
31. Remove first 3 mL syringe from package
32. Pump first syringe a few times to loosen
33. Attach first syringe to syringe port while ensuring fully depressed
34. Tightly close 15 mL conical tube containing 10 mL aliquot cell culture
35. Gently invert conical tube a few times to mix contents
36. Remove second 3 mL syringe from package
37. Pump second syringe a few times
38. Semi-submerge second syringe in cell culture from 15 mL conical tube
39. Draw up some cell culture with second syringe
40. Dispense syringe entirely back into tube to minimize air bubbles
41. Draw up 3 mL of cell culture with second syringe
42. Attach filled second syringe to remaining syringe port
43. Sanitize syringes and fill port area with ethanol pad while avoiding oxygenation membrane
44. Repeat syringe attachment process for remaining vessels
45. Recover remaining cell culture from 15 mL conical tube if insufficient volume in 50 mL conical tube for last vessel
46. Close syringe port stopcocks to prevent cell/bubble migration into vessels
47. Flip vessel upside down
48. Strike vessel side repeatedly to collect initial bubbles
49. Flip vessel back to upward position
50. Angle vessel slightly away to float bubbles upward
51. Maneuver bubbles under port using empty syringe
52. Open both syringe port stopcocks
53. Gently strike vessel to float bubbles into empty syringe
54. Suck large bubbles with empty syringe while depressing full syringe to maintain vessel pressure
55. Repeat bubble removal process
56. Close syringe port stopcocks after bubble removal
57. Maintain syringes attached with equal volume for subsequent bubble removal
58. Wipe rotating base surface and ribbon cable with 70% ethanol
59. Verify ribbon cable connection to power supply
60. Place rotating base inside incubator
61. Position power supply outside incubator
62. Attach SMG treatment vessel by aligning threads and turning rotating peg counterclockwise
63. Confirm secure attachment of treatment vessel
64. Fill incubator water tray with autoclaved RO water to maintain ~100% humidity
65. Calibrate incubator rotation speed to match cell sedimentation velocity
66. Check for bubble formation every few hours
67. Remove bubbles at least once daily
68. Stop rotation of apparatus
69. Disassemble apparatus
70. Remove treatment vessel by holding vessel stationary while turning rotating peg clockwise
71. Transfer control flask and treated vessel to sterile biological safety cabinet
72. Flip each vessel (excluding control flask) downward
73. Gently strike downward-facing vessel to resuspend cells
74. Reposition vessel on its side with fill port toward bottom
75. Strike repositioned vessel to guide cells toward fill port
76. Unscrew syringes from both ports
77. Dispense syringes into biohazard waste
78. Open stopcocks on syringe ports
79. Dispense waste into biohazard container
80. Open stopcocks on syringe ports
81. Remove fill port cap
82. Draw up vessel contents using sterile 10 mL serological pipette
83. Tilt vessel during emptying process
84. Dispense control and treatment group contents into labeled 15 mL conical tubes
85. Close conical tubes
86. Gently invert tubes several times to mix contents
87. Determine cell culture concentration and viability for subsequent assays

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Weigh 100 milligrams of poly(lactic-co-glycolic acid) (PLGA)
2. Transfer weighed PLGA into scintillation vial
3. Dissolve PLGA in 5 milliliters of dichloromethane by vortexing
4. Place homogenizer in beaker containing 50 milliliters of 1% polyvinyl alcohol (PVA) solution
5. Position homogenizer close to bottom of beaker without touching
6. Turn on homogenizer to appropriate speed
7. Add PLGA solution to beaker for 1 minute of homogenization
8. Pour polyvinyl alcohol PLGA micro-particle solution into beaker containing 100 milliliters of 0.5% PVA solution on stir plate in chemical hood
9. Stir solution for at least four hours
10. Pour particle solution into 50 milliliter conical tubes
11. Collect particles by centrifugation
12. Replace supernatant with 20 milliliters of de-ionized water
13. Re-suspend particles by vortexing
14. Bring final volume in each tube to 50 milliliters with de-ionized water
15. Wash particles two more times using demonstrated procedure
16. Dissolve 200 milligrams of PLGA in 5 milliliters of dichloromethane
17. Place sonicator probe in beaker containing 50 milliliters of 1% PVA solution on ice without touching beaker bottom
18. Begin sonication at 12 watts for 2 minutes
19. Add PLGA solution to beaker during sonication
20. Pour 1% polyvinyl alcohol PLGA nano-particle solution into beaker containing 100 milliliters of 0.5% PVA solution
21. Place beaker on stir plate in chemical fume hood for 4-hour solvent evaporation
22. Pour evaporated particle solution into 50 milliliter conical tubes
23. Centrifuge tubes to remove micro-particles
24. Remove supernatants after centrifugation
25. Transfer nano-particles to high-speed centrifuge tubes for three washes in de-ionized water
26. Re-suspend particles in 1 milliliter of fresh de-ionized water
27. Add film casting solution to achieve 2.5 mg/mL particle concentration
28. Transfer 10 mL aliquots of particle suspension into 75x50 mm rectangular Petri dishes
29. Transfer 15 mL aliquots of particle suspension into 100x100 mm square Petri dishes
30. Remove dried films using tweezers after overnight drying
31. Trim film edges with scissors
32. Place short edges of film between two neoprene rubber pieces
33. Mount film on aluminum blocks of automated thin film stretching device
34. Secure film using Allen wrench to screw metal grips on rubber
35. Mount all four film edges onto aluminum blocks for 2D stretching
36. Measure and record film length between aluminum blocks for 1D or 2D stretching
37. Calculate required stretch distance in one or two directions based on desired fold stretch
38. Place film-loaded stretching device in 90°C oven with water-containing beaker for 10-minute thermal conditioning
39. Let stretched film cool to room temperature for 20 minutes
40. For 1D stretching: Cut film out of stretching device at edges
41. For 2D stretching: Cut out and save center square of uniformly stretched film
42. Place films in 50 mL conical tubes (maximum two films per tube)
43. Add approximately 25 mL de-ionized water to each tube for vortexing
44. When films dissolve: Wash particles three times in de-ionized water as demonstrated
45. After last wash: Re-suspend particles in approximately 1 mL fresh de-ionized water per tube
46. Freeze particles at -80°C for 1 hour or overnight for lyophilization
47. Re-suspend lyophilized micro/nano-particles at 20 mg/mL in freshly prepared MES buffer with vortexing
48. Transfer 100 μL of particle solution to polypropylene micro centrifuge tube containing 900 μL MES buffer
49. Add 100 μL of freshly prepared EDC NHS solution to tube
50. Mix particles by vortexing
51. Incubate particles on inverter at room temperature for 30 minutes
52. Collect particles by centrifugation
53. Re-suspend micro-particle pellets in 1 mL PBS by vortexing
54. Re-suspend nano-particles in 1 mL PBS by 5-second sonication at 2-3 watts
55. Add 8 micrograms of signal one protein and 10 micrograms of anti-mouse CD28 antibody to micro-particles
56. Add 16 micrograms of signal one protein and 20 micrograms of anti-mouse CD28 antibody to nano-particles
57. Bring final volume in each tube to 1.1 milliliter with PBS
58. Incubate particles overnight on inverter at 4 degrees Celsius
59. Wash particles three times in de-ionized water

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Remove spent medium from wells
2. Rinse cells in each well with 1 milliliter of 1X DPBS
3. Incubate stem cell colonies in each well with 1 milliliter of 0.5 millimolar EDTA at 37°C for approximately 5 minutes
4. Check colony morphology for curled edges with loose gaps
5. Aspirate EDTA from wells
6. Gently rinse cells in each well with 6 milliliters of medium A containing 6 microliters of 10 millimolar rock inhibitor Y27632 solution
7. Gently tap bottom of dish to rinse cells
8. Observe cells under microscope to confirm embryo body formation
9. Collect embryo bodies into 15 milliliter centrifugal tube using 10 milliliter pipette
10. Wait 5 minutes for embryo bodies to settle to bottom
11. Carefully aspirate supernatant
12. Re-suspend embryo bodies in 6 milliliters of fresh medium A
13. Transfer suspension into new low-adhesion well of six-well plate
14. Culture in 37 degrees Celsius incubator
15. Coat 10 cm dish with 3 ml of 0.1% fish gelatin solution
16. Incubate coated dish for ≥1 hour at 37°C in 5% CO₂ incubator
17. Remove gelatin solution from dish
18. Rinse dish once with 5-6 ml of 1X DPBS
19. Collect embryo bodies into 15 milliliter centrifuge tube
20. Wait 5 minutes for embryo bodies to settle at tube bottom
21. Re-suspend embryo bodies with 15 milliliters of medium B
22. Transfer suspension to coated 10 centimeter dish
23. Culture in 37°C 5% CO₂ incubator for one week
24. Centrifuge cells at 200G for five minutes at room temperature
25. Aspirate supernatant carefully
26. Re-suspend cells with two milliliters of fresh medium C
27. Transfer cell suspension into new low-adhesion well of six-well plate
28. Incubate plate in 5% CO₂ incubator at 37°C
29. Replace spent medium with 2 milliliters of medium C per well
30. Examine plate under microscope to identify branched microglia adhering to bottom of low adhesion well
31. Gently replace medium C in each well with 1 mL Accutase
32. Incubate at 37°C for 3 minutes
33. Collect microglia cells into 15 mL tube using 5 mL pipette
34. Centrifuge cells at 200G for 5 minutes at room temperature
35. Discard supernatant
36. Suspend microglia with 1 mL of medium E
37. Obtain human microglia from human embryonic stem cells and harvest on day 56
38. Culture human embryonic stem cells in stem cell medium until cell density reaches 80-90%
39. Add 1 mL Dispase per well to cultured cells
40. Incubate at 37°C for 5 minutes
41. Add 1 mL Medium D per well after Dispase removal
42. Cut cells into small pieces using 10 μL pipette
43. Gently collect all cell pieces
44. Centrifuge tube at 200G for 5 minutes
45. Remove supernatant
46. Gently re-suspend cells in 200 microliters of cold matrix
47. Transfer 1.5 milliliter micro centrifuge tube to incubator
48. Prepare 10 cm dish with 15 ml of medium D
49. Re-suspend matrix with medium D
50. Shake Petri dish gently
51. Place dish in incubator for five days
52. Replace medium with 3 milliliters of Dispase
53. Aspirate Dispase after 5 minutes incubation
54. Add 15 milliliters of medium E
55. Introduce harvested microglia to digested retinal organoids
56. Gently swirl plate to aggregate organoids to center of dish
57. Replace medium E with medium F
58. Transfer organoids with medium F to new suspension dish

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Place female rat on dissection table
2. Hold ankle firmly between thumb, index finger, and middle finger
3. Sever calcaneal tendon using 12-centimeter straight blunt scissors

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Add fetal bovine serum to RPMI 1640 to achieve 10% final concentration
2. Prepare 10 milliliters of challenge medium for each PAMP in 50 milliliter tubes by adding PAMP to culture medium
3. Spray mouse trunk with 70% ethanol and wipe with paper towel
4. Position mouse on its back slightly tilted onto its right side
5. Cut away fur along mouse's left side between front and back legs using dissecting scissors
6. Grab peritoneum with forceps and carefully make an incision without damaging spleen
7. Remove spleen with forceps and place into sterile 50 milliliter tube containing approximately 10 milliliters of culture media on ice
8. Transfer spleen with 2 milliliters of culture medium to sterile Petri dish
9. Homogenize spleen by grinding between frosted portions of two sterile slides
10. Pipette 2 milliliters of culture medium containing splenocytes through 40 micron nylon cell strainer into 50 milliliter tube
11. Add 8 milliliters of cold culture medium to 50 milliliter tube containing splenocytes
12. Determine cell concentration per milliliter using hemocytometer
13. Add 1 × 10⁶ cells per well to six-well culture plates
14. Add 3 ml of culture medium or challenge medium to wells
15. Incubate plates at 37°C and 5% CO₂ for 3 hours
16. Scrape cells from well bottom using P1000 micropipette with 1,000 microliter tip
17. Transfer cell-containing medium to 15 milliliter tube using same pipette tip
18. Pellet cells via centrifugation at 167 × g for 5 minutes at room temperature
19. Remove supernatant from pelleted cells
20. Wash cell pellet with 5 milliliters of PBS
21. Repellet cells via centrifugation at 167 × g for 5 minutes
22. Remove supernatant after second pelleting
23. Add 600 microliters of lysis buffer to cell pellet
24. Isolate RNA from splenocytes using RNA extraction kit
25. Perform on-column DNA digestion
26. Synthesize cDNA for samples using reverse transcription kit
27. Use 10 microliters of RNA per sample in 20 microliter total reaction volume
28. Transfer mRNA from control samples to 0.5 milliliter tube for cDNA preparation
29. Add 10 microliters of 2X reverse transcriptase master mix
30. Add 10 microliters of water to reaction tube after reverse transcription
31. Add 45 microliters water to four 0.5 milliliter tubes labeled 10⁻¹, 10⁻², 10⁻³, 10⁻⁴
32. Transfer 5 microliters from starting concentration to 10⁻¹ tube using P20 micropipette
33. Mix 10⁻¹ tube by pipetting and transfer 5 microliters to 10⁻² tube
34. Mix contents of 10⁻² dilution tube
35. Transfer 5 microliters from 10⁻² dilution tube to 10⁻³ dilution tube using P20 micropipette
36. Mix contents of 10⁻³ dilution tube
37. Transfer 5 microliters from 10⁻³ dilution tube to 10⁻⁴ dilution tube using P20 micropipette
38. Mix contents of 10⁻⁴ dilution tube
39. Add 0.5 microliters of primer probe assay to reaction
40. Add 5 microliters of gene expression master mix to reaction
41. Add 2 microliters of water to reaction
42. Add 2.5 microliters of cDNA to reaction
43. Pipette RNA samples into 96-well qPCR plate
44. Incubate primer probe assays
45. Load plate into qPCR machine
46. Select quantitation mode, relative standard curve, and TaqMan chemistry in qPCR instrument setup
47. Change reaction volume to 10 microliters in qPCR instrument parameters
48. Enter relevant information into qPCR plate layout

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Fill home cages with fresh sawdust
2. Add piece of cotton wool for enrichment
3. House animals in home cage for 1-week acclimation period
4. Maintain consistent 12-hour light/dark cycle
5. Provide ad libitum access to rodent chow and water
6. Initiate application of stressors on 4-week-old mice in UCMS room after acclimation
7. Place mice with home-cage counterparts in empty cage
8. Fill cage with water at 24±1°C to 1 cm depth
9. Maintain mice in wet cage for 4 hours
10. Transfer each mouse to individual drying cage equipped with heat lamp, heating pad, and paper towel bedding
11. Place thermometer in transient drying cage
12. Verify temperature does not exceed 37°C
13. Maintain mouse in drying cage until completely dry and invigorated
14. Return mice to home cage with same counterparts
15. Pour water maintained at 24±1°C into home cage
16. Continue pouring until sawdust achieves moderate dampness
17. Transfer mice to transient cages for drying after 4-hour exposure
18. Place mice with home-cage counterparts into sterile cage containing fresh sawdust
19. Tilt cage at 45 degrees against wall for four hours
20. Transfer five mice with home-cage counterparts from home cage to empty cage for four hours
21. Transfer mice with home-cage counterparts from home cage to unfamiliar scent-marked cage (previously housed by different mice for ≥3 days)
22. Keep mice in unfamiliar scent-marked cage for four hours
23. Place each mouse separately in a clean mouse restrainer for four hours
24. Return mice to home cage with same counterparts
25. Transfer mice in home cage with specific counterparts to UCMS room
26. Keep light on for 24 consecutive hours
27. Return UCMS group cages from UCMS room to housing room
28. Start administration of putative therapeutic pharmacological agents
29. Remove each mouse from home cage
30. Place each mouse individually in cage with fresh sawdust and cotton wool
31. Prepare one bottle with distilled water and another with 2% sucrose solution
32. Weigh both bottles
33. Set bottles at both ends of cage lid
34. Place rodent chow between the two bottles
35. Allow mice ad libitum access to solutions and food for specified duration (24-144 hours)
36. Weigh bottles to estimate consumption from each bottle
37. Switch positions of nozzles to counterbalance spatial bias
38. Weigh bottles daily to estimate consumption from each bottle

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Add 45 milliliters of glacial acetic acid to a 250 milliliter round bottom flask containing a magnetic stir bar
2. Stir solution at room temperature
3. Add one equivalent each of hydrazine and ethyl acetoacetate while stirring
4. Equip flask with reflux condenser
5. Heat reaction flask to 120 degrees Celsius in oil bath while stirring for three hours
6. Remove magnetic stir bar using stir bar retriever
7. Concentrate reaction mixture using rotary evaporator at 60°C
8. Add 20 mL deionized water to reaction flask
9. Transfer solution to separatory funnel
10. Extract aqueous layer three times with 30 mL ethyl acetate
11. Combine organic layers in separatory funnel
12. Wash combined organic layers two times with 50 mL brine
13. Dry combined organic layers over anhydrous sodium sulfate for 1 hour
14. Remove sodium sulfate by gravity filtration
15. Remove solvent using rotary evaporator under reduced pressure at 35°C
16. Add 1 equivalent pyrazolone, 1 equivalent benzaldehyde, 0.6 equivalents magnesium oxide, and magnetic stir bar to oven-dried 100 mL round bottom flask
17. Maintain nitrogen atmosphere during flask setup
18. Add 40 mL anhydrous acetonitrile using airtight syringe
19. Equip reaction flask with reflux condenser
20. Heat reaction flask to 120°C in oil bath while stirring for 12 hours
21. Monitor reaction progress by thin-layer chromatography using 2:1 petroleum ether:ethyl acetate eluent
22. Cool reaction flask to room temperature after complete pyrazolone consumption
23. Filter off magnesium oxide using celite plug
24. Remove excess acetonitrile using rotary evaporator under reduced pressure at 35°C
25. Purify residue by silica gel column chromatography eluting with petroleum ether and ethyl acetate
26. Transfer crude product to 100 mL Erlenmeyer flask equipped with magnetic stir bar
27. Add minimum volume of 95% ethanol to flask
28. Place flask on hot plate and heat with stirring
29. Maintain gentle boil with stirring until complete dissolution of solid
30. Remove flask from hot plate
31. Cool flask slowly without agitation to crystallize alpha-arylidiene
32. Add one equivalent each of alpha-imino-gamma-butyrolactone hydrobromide, magnesium sulfate, triethylamine, and a magnetic stir bar to oven-dried 100 ml round bottom flask
33. Assemble reaction apparatus under Nitrogen atmosphere
34. Add 36 ml anhydrous dichloromethane to reaction flask using airtight syringe
35. Stir reaction mixture at room temperature for 1 hour
36. Add 1.1 equivalent thiophene-2-carbaldehyde to solution
37. Stir reaction mixture for 12 hours
38. Monitor reaction progress by TLC using 4:1 petroleum ether/ethyl acetate eluent until lactone consumption is complete
39. Filter reaction mixture using 30-50 micron filter paper
40. Add 5 mL deionized water to mixture
41. Separate organic layer from aqueous phase
42. Extract aqueous phase twice with 30 milliliters of dichloromethane
43. Combine organic layers in separatory funnel
44. Wash combined organic layers twice with 50 milliliters of brine
45. Dry combined organic layers over anhydrous sodium sulfate for one hour
46. Remove sodium sulfate by gravity filtration
47. Remove solvent using rotary evaporator under reduced pressure at 35 degrees Celsius

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Thinly spread a small amount of epoxy on both sides of double-sided tape using a razor blade
2. Laser-cut epoxy-coated tape to create four channels
3. Sandwich epoxy tape between four-hole slide and PEG coverslip to create flow chip
4. Apply gentle pressure along channel length using pipette tip to create seal
5. Cure epoxy for minimum one hour
6. Align chip so channel openings are positioned at centers of adapters
7. Place two transparent acrylic spacers on top of chip
8. Apply firm pressure in middle of block
9. Screw at ends of each spacer
10. Screw inlets into threaded holes on other side of bracket
11. Monitor sealing condition through transparent acrylic block
12. Flow 200 microliters of wash buffer into chamber to check for leakage
13. Aggressively push additional 200 microliters of wash buffer to remove bubbles if present
14. Add 40 microliters of BSA to flow chamber to prevent nonspecific binding
15. Incubate chamber in humidity chamber for 10 minutes
16. Add 40 microliters of Tween 20 to flow chamber
17. Incubate for 10 minutes to reduce nonspecific binding
18. Wash channel with 200 microliters of wash buffer
19. Add 40 microliters of Streptavidin to flow chamber
20. Incubate flow chamber for 20 minutes
21. Wash flow chamber with 200 microliters of wash buffer
22. Add 40 microliters of hybridized protein G-TGT to flow chamber
23. Incubate for 20 minutes
24. Wash with wash buffer
25. Add 40 microliters of protein G-TGT top strand
26. Incubate for 20 minutes to complete unhybridized TGT bottom strand
27. Wash with wash buffer
28. Add 40 microliters of P-selectin-Fc to flow chamber
29. Incubate flow chamber for 60 minutes
30. Wash flow chamber with wash buffer
31. Fill 5 mL glass syringe with rolling buffer
32. Tap sides of syringe to dislodge bubbles
33. Push dislodged bubbles out as they float toward tip
34. Insert sterile needle into 200 mm polyethylene tubing
35. Connect needle to glass syringe
36. Fix syringe onto syringe pump
37. Tilt syringe pump to elevate plunger side
38. Insert end of tube into flow chamber inlet
39. Take 1-2 ml of cell suspension sample
40. Centrifuge sample to pellet cells
41. Remove supernatant medium
42. Gently resuspend cell pellet in 500 μl rolling buffer
43. Disconnect tubing from flow chamber inlets and outlet
44. Pipette 40 μl cell suspension into flow chamber
45. Reconnect tubing to flow chamber
46. Start syringe pump at desired flow rates
47. Observe cell rolling using dark field microscope with 10X objective
48. Add 40 microliters of DNA-PAINT imager strand prepared in DNA-PAINT buffer to the channel
49. Perform total internal reflection fluorescence microscopy using conditions specified in manuscript
50. Add permanent imager strand
51. Incubate for 120 seconds in T50M5 buffer
52. Wash the channel
53. Infuse 200 microliters of wash buffer
54. Program microscope to scan 400 × 50 image area
55. Split raw data into individual TIP files (max 10,000 images/file) using ImageJ

1. Connect ball valve to one end of four-inch long, 1/8 size threaded pipe
2. Identify assembled unit as particle hopper

8. Position tubing around the 2-inch pipe
9. Insert HEPA filter inside short tubing
10. Ensure flow direction through ball valve
11. Connect vacuum generator to other ball valve using threading

16. Open ball valve closest to HEPA filter
17. Open ball valve closest to vacuum generator
18. Close ball valve closest to vacuum generator
19. Close ball valve closest to HEPA filter
20. Close main and regulator valves on air tank to stop flow

23. Take PIVEC base with well facing up
24. Connect cell culture insert adapter to top of PIVEC base

30. Insert 1/4 inch barbed adapters into cassette inlet and outlet

34. Connect 1/2 inch outer diameter tubing (incomplete action)
35. Apply aluminum foil for insulation
36. Secure insulation with tape
37. Connect 1/2 inch outer diameter tubing to adapter on top of PIVEC
38. Remove porous tubing from sterile water
39. Place porous tubing within tubing on top of PIVEC
40. Transfer system to fume hood
41. Clamp PIVEC on ring stand

43. Connect PIVEC to aerosol system
44. Connect PIVEC to aerosol generator outlet using 1/4 inch diameter tubing attached to porous tubing
45. Connect PIVEC exit to 1/4 inch diameter tubing leading to HEPA filter and vacuum pump via Y connector
46. Turn on valves
47. Set vacuum pump flow rate to 0.5 liters per minute
48. Expose cells to copper nanoparticles for ten minutes
49. Close valves

51. Take out cell culture insert using tweezers
52. Place insert in sterile well plate
53. Put insert back into carbon dioxide incubator
54. Aspirate media from PIVEC using dropper

57. Determine particle number concentration of copper nanoparticle aerosols using filter-based scanning mobility particle sizer

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Preheat six PDMS stamps to 70 degrees Celsius
2. Place six-well glass-bottom plate on dry bath pre-heated to 75 degrees Celsius
3. Pour 400-microliter drop of pre-heated low-melting agarose onto bottom of each well
4. Gently place one PDMS stamp over each agarose drop
5. Cool setup for 5-10 minutes at room temperature followed by 20 minutes at 4 degrees Celsius
6. Gently peel off PDMS from each well
7. Place embossed plate in light curing system for 3 minutes
8. Fill UV-sterilized macro-wells with sterile PBS
9. Cover plate and wrap with Parafilm for storage at 4°C
10. Remove PBS from each well
11. Gently load 50 microliters of cells of interest onto each hydrogel array
12. Allow cells to settle by gravity for 15 minutes
13. Add 6-8 aliquots of 500 microliters fresh medium to macro-well rim outside hydrogel array
14. Incubate cells at 37°C and 5% CO₂ with humidity for spheroid formation
15. Transfer spheroids into biosafety hood on ice
16. Lean pipette tip on edge of macro-well plastic bottom adjacent to hydrogel array
17. Remove all medium from around hydrogel array
18. Lean gel-loading pipette tip on edge of array tank carefully
19. Remove medium from array tank without disturbing micro-chambers and spheroids
20. Add two aliquots of 150 microliters collagen mixture at array edge using pre-frozen fine pipette tip
21. Return spheroids to incubator for 1 hour for extracellular matrix gelation
22. Add 400 microliters of pre-warmed 1% low-melting agarose on top of solidified matrix gel
23. Incubate plate covered at room temperature for five to seven minutes followed by two minutes at four degrees Celsius for agarose gelation
24. Lean pipette on edge of macro-well plastic bottom and gently add two milliliters of complete medium to each well
25. Load hydrogel micro-chamber array plate onto motorized inverted microscope stage equipped with 37°C and 5% CO₂ humidified incubator
26. Use 4x or 10x objective to pre-determine image acquisition positions across all wells to cover entire array area
27. Acquire bright-field images every 2-4 hours for 24-72 hours to track cell invasion from spheroid into extracellular matrix
28. Export time-lapse images from acquisition software in tagged image file format to dedicated folder
29. Open graphic user interface
30. Click Load Bright Field
31. Adjust segmentation parameters for spheroids and invasion areas
32. Click Region Of Interest Segmentation to create borders around spheroids
33. Press Delete or Add buttons to correct segmentation errors
34. Manually correct segmentation errors
35. Click Tracking to number spheroids across time-lapse images
36. Click Measurement to generate parameter spreadsheet
37. Save spreadsheet in appropriate format for data processing and statistical analysis

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Grow flies on standard food diet under 12-hour light/dark cycle at 25°C
2. Anesthetize flies with carbon dioxide 1-2 days before experiment
3. Separate anesthetized flies into four groups of 25
4. Store flies overnight in food vials without yeast at 25°C and 70% humidity under 12-hour light/dark cycle
5. Prepare custom Perspex T-maze
6. Check tube fittings to ensure airtight seal
7. Place custom copper grids inside training tubes
8. Clean copper grids regularly
9. Replace copper grids if oxidized
10. Attach copper grids to electric stimulator wires using crocodile clips
11. Apply volt meter to verify shock delivery
12. Secure maze with G clamps
13. Attach T-maze to air pump tubing to prevent air leakage
14. Maintain mild airflow at 2 liters per minute
15. Dilute methyl cyclohexanol (MCH) at 1:67 ratio in mineral oil
16. Dilute octanol (OCT) at 1:100 ratio in mineral oil
17. Pipette 30 microliters of diluted odor into odor cup
18. Prepare custom odor cup and cover odor block with perforated-top plastic tube
19. Place odor cup into odor block
20. Set up dim red light environment for experiment
21. Introduce flies into training tube attached to T-maze
22. Allow flies to adapt to tube and airflow for 90 seconds
23. Present first odor (MCH) with 60V electric shocks (12 pulses of 1.25 seconds, 3.75s intervals)
24. Allow 32-second rest period after shock presentation
25. Present second odor (OCT) for 60 seconds without shock
26. Initiate 32-second rest period
27. Turn T-maze apparatus on its side
28. Gently tap bottom of T-maze on mouse mat to transfer flies from training chamber to central chamber
29. Move flies to choice point of T-maze
30. Simultaneously expose flies to both odors
31. Maintain odor exposure for 120 seconds
32. Trap flies in choice tubes
33. Slide central chamber upward to secure traps
34. Collect flies from central compartment
35. Collect flies from each T-maze arm
36. Transfer collected flies to separate food vials
37. Label vials with fly identity and odor choice
38. Count flies to quantify memory retention
39. Collect flies after training
40. Transfer flies from T-maze to yeast-free food vials
41. Store flies in dark at 25°C and 70% humidity
42. Use custom-built maze for simultaneous training of several fly batches
43. Administer five cycles of training to flies
44. Maintain flies at 18°C and 70% humidity in darkness until testing
45. Move flies to 25°C for acclimatization
46. Allow flies to acclimatize for at least one hour at 25°C
47. Assess long-term memory 24 hours after training
48. Clean odor cups with hot water and odorless detergent
49. Apply 10 microliters of SigmaCoat to dried odor cups
50. Heat odor cups in microwave oven to solidify SigmaCoat
51. Introduce 100 flies into the T-maze for odor acuity test
52. Move flies to choice point after 90 seconds
53. Allow flies 2 minutes to choose between pure odors and air
54. Collect and count flies after odor choice period
55. Introduce flies into shock chamber for reactivity test
56. Administer 60V DC electric shock after 90 seconds of rest
57. Collect and count escaped flies after 2-minute choice period
