question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Layer 3 milliliters of 0.05% Tween 20 on Trichoderma mycelium culture
2. Use rake to release conidia from conidiophores without pressing on mycelium to prevent hyphae damage
3. Recover conidia solution using micropipette and transfer to 15 milliliter tube for counting
4. Deposit 100 microliters of spores (3×10⁶ spores/mL concentration) onto 9 cm Petri dish containing PDA medium
5. Add 10 grams of 2 mm diameter glass beads to plate using sterile spatula
6. Gently shake plate forward and backward
7. Spin and rotate plate in 90° segments until fully rotated to distribute spores across agar surface
8. Incubate plate at 30°C until growth inhibition analysis
9. Weigh desired quantity of garlic powder into individual 50 milliliter tubes using sterile spatula
10. Add 10 milliliters of 45°C PDA to each tube and invert several times to distribute powder evenly
11. Pour homogenized suspensions into 5cm diameter Petri dishes and allow solidification at room temperature
12. Use 5 mm diameter sterile stainless steel tube to plot circles in center of agar in control and antifungal-treated dishes
13. Use sterile toothpick to dispose of agar cylinders
14. Use new 5 mm diameter sterile stainless steel tube to plot 15-20 random circles in fungal plates
15. Use sterile toothpicks to transfer spore/hyphae/mycelium-covered cylinders into empty regions of PDA plates
16. Pour 10 milliliters of PDA medium into lid of 5 cm diameter Petri dish containing antifungal compounds or PDA medium alone
17. Allow agar to solidify at room temperature
18. Use 50 milliliter centrifuge tube as calibration tool to make PDA circle in center of agar
19. Use sterile spatula to remove agar around circle
20. Use 5 mm diameter sterile stainless steel tube to plot circle in center of medium in lid
21. Use sterile toothpick to discard agar cylinder
22. Use 5 mm diameter sterile stainless steel tube to extract plugs from prepared fungal plates
23. Transfer spore/hyphae/mycelium-coated plugs into assay plate lids using sterile toothpick

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Set differential pressure to 4 Pascal (30 mTorr) via feedback system
2. Turn on ozone generator by passing dry air through ultraviolet lamp
3. Set ozone flow rate to 0.1 standard liter per minute
4. Set relative humidity of bag to designated values
5. Set chamber temperature to 25 ± 0.1°C
6. Connect instrument inlets to environmental chamber
7. Start self-developed software by clicking Start button
8. Check real-time data on self-developed software with feedback control
9. Dissolve ammonium sulfate in high purity water using 100 milliliter volumetric glass
10. Atomize ammonium sulfate solution at 3 standard liters per minute flow rate
11. Pass aerosol flow through diffusion drier to achieve 10% relative humidity
12. Pass dried aerosol through bipolar charger and differential mobility analyzer for electric mobility-based size selection
13. Withdraw 1 milliliter of isoprene solution using syringe
14. Rinse syringe three times with isoprene solution prior to final withdrawal
15. Place syringe into syringe injector
16. Insert syringe needle tip through rubber seal into round bottomed flask
17. Preheat flask to 90 ± 1°C using heating tape
18. Turn on syringe injection and set to appropriate value
19. Introduce flow of 2 standard liters per minute of purified air
20. Start aerosol measurement software
21. Create new file by clicking 'Create a New File'
22. Click 'Okay' button to record particle number diameter distributions exiting the bag
23. Measure aerosol flow using high-resolution time-of-flight aerosol mass spectrometer
24. Start data acquisition software by pressing 'Acquire' button on lower left panel
25. Open sampling valve of PTFE Teflon tube inside bag
26. Start data acquisition using software: Access Acquisition menu in top deck and press Start

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Thaw frozen femurs in container with water at room temperature for two hours
2. Cut 10-15 millimeter thick disks from mid diaphysis region using table top diamond band saw
3. Remove soft tissue or flesh attached to cortical bone using dissection kit
4. Cut femur cross-sections along longitudinal axis using diamond wafering blade at low speed under wet conditions to obtain cuboidal sections
5. Clean specimens in solution of 1.5% anionic cleaner and 5% bleach for 20 minutes in ultrasonic cleaner
6. Coat mold walls with release agent
7. Mix acrylic resin and hardener in beaker
8. Place cut cortical bone specimens into molds with scratch surface facing downwards
9. Pour acrylic resin mix into prepared specimen holders
10. Remove air bubbles trapped during mixing
11. Let specimens cure for 4-5 hours
12. Cut embedded specimens into 5 mm thick disks at low speed to expose scratch surface
13. Mount specimens onto aluminum disks (34 mm diameter, 5 mm height) using cyanoacrylate adhesive
14. Wrap specimens in HBSS-soaked gauze
15. Refrigerate specimens at 4°C until further use
16. Grind bovine cortical bone specimens at room temperature using 400 grit silicon carbide paper for 1 minute
17. Grind bovine cortical bone specimens at room temperature using 600 grit silicon carbide paper for 5 minutes
18. Maintain grinder polisher at base speed of 100 rpm for 400 grit step
19. Maintain grinder polisher at base speed of 150 rpm for 600 grit step
20. Machine grind bovine cortical bone specimens at room temperature using 800 grit silicon carbide paper for 15 minutes
21. Machine grind bovine cortical bone specimens at room temperature using 1200 grit silicon carbide paper for 15 minutes
22. Maintain grinder polisher at base speed 150 rpm, head speed 60 rpm, and 1 pound operating load
23. Polish specimens sequentially with 3μm, 1μm, and 0.25μm diamond suspension solutions on hard perforated non-woven cloth for 90 minutes each at room temperature
24. Maintain 1 pound operating load with polisher base speed at 300 rpm and head speed at 60 rpm during diamond suspension polishing
25. Polish specimen with 0.05μm alumina suspension solution on soft synthetic rayon cloth for 90 minutes at 1 pound load
26. Set polisher base speed to 100 rpm and head speed to 60 rpm at room temperature during alumina suspension polishing
27. Place specimens in beaker with deionized water
28. Keep beaker in ultrasonic bath for 2 minutes
29. View surface features using optical microscope or SEM imaging
30. Calibrate Rockwell indenter tip using polycarbonate
31. Place cortical bone specimen on stage
32. Select scratch test site using integrated optical microscope
33. Apply linear progressive load with start load of 30 millinewtons
34. Set end load to 30 newtons
35. Set loading rate to 60 newtons per minute
36. Set scratch length to three millimeters
37. Perform series of scratch tests on short, longitudinal, bovine cortical bone specimens
38. Wet specimen surface with HBSS after every set of 3-4 scratch tests to maintain hydration
39. Analyze scratch test data using non-linear fracture mechanic modeling

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Dissect 6-8 hippocampi from postnatal day 1 pups using HBSS medium in Petri dish
2. Chop hippocampi into smaller pieces using dissection scissors
3. Transfer chopped hippocampi from Petri dish to 15 mL tube
4. Wash hippocampi twice with 5 mL HBSS
5. Leave hippocampi in 4.5 mL HBSS after washing
6. Add 0.5 mL of 2.5% trypsin to 4.5 mL HBSS containing hippocampi
7. Incubate tube in 37°C water bath for 15 minutes with inversion every 5 minutes
8. Wash hippocampi with HBSS three times for five minutes per wash
9. Add two milliliters of HBSS to hippocampi
10. Pipette hippocampi up and down 15 times with Pasteur pipette
11. Triturate tissue 10 times with fire-polished Pasteur pipette
12. Rest tube for five minutes until chunks settle to bottom
13. Repeat trituration with remaining chunks until most disappear
14. Transfer supernatant containing dissociated neurons to plating dishes using one milliliter pipette tip
15. Add culture directly to pre-incubated plating medium
16. Shake plate gently
17. Check plating dishes with light microscope 2-4 hours after seeding
18. Use fine tip forceps to flip cover slips
19. Transfer cover slips to glial cell feeder dishes containing preconditioned neuronal culture medium
20. Position cover slips with wax dots side facing downwards
21. Feed neurons with 1 mL fresh neuronal culture medium every 7 days
22. Flip cover slips on third day in vitro
23. Position cover slips with wax dot side facing up in glial cell feeder dish with conditioned medium
24. Mix 0.25 micrograms of CRE-BFP DNA with 0.5 micrograms of ankyrin-G GFP DNA in a 1.7 milliliter tube
25. Add 100 microliters of optimum to the DNA mixture tube
26. Mix the DNA-optimum tube and rest it on a rack
27. Mix 3 microliters of transfection reagent with 100 microliters of optimum in a new 1.7 milliliter tube
28. Incubate transfection reagent mixture for 5 minutes at room temperature
29. Mix 100 microliters of DNA mixture with 100 microliters of transfection reagent mixture
30. Leave mixture for 5-10 minutes on a rack
31. Add 50 microliters of DNA mix on top of each cover slip by inserting pipette tip below medium without touching cover slips while pipetting slowly
32. Transfer dish slowly to incubator
33. Incubate dish for 30-45 minutes
34. Flip cover slips onto home glial feeder dish with wax dot side facing down
35. Return dish to incubator
36. Open Z series images in Fiji
37. Generate maximum projection of images
38. Subtract empty cover slip background signal from image
39. Draw line along AIS with width fully covering the structure, starting before signal rise and stopping after signal drop
40. Measure mean pixel intensity along the line
41. Export intensity data to spreadsheet
42. Run MATLAB script to generate final figure

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Add 2 microliters of 1 mg/mL antibody to 200 microliters of cell lysates
2. Incubate lysate-antibody mixture at 4°C for 1 hour while rocking
3. Wash protein A-Sepharose beads 2-3 times by adding lysis buffer
4. Centrifuge beads at 4°C for 30 seconds to 1 minute at 5000 × g
5. Remove supernatant from beads using pipet
6. Resuspend beads in buffer
7. Add 30 microliters of 50% protein A-Sepharose bead slurry in lysis buffer to lysates
8. Incubate lysate-bead mixture at 4°C for 1 hour while rocking
9. Perform touch spin at 4°C to pellet beads
10. Remove supernatant after bead pelleting
11. Wash beads three times with 1 mL bead wash buffer including touch spinning
12. Wash beads once with 1X kinase reaction buffer
13. Perform touch spin prior to buffer removal
14. Remove maximum buffer without disturbing beads
15. Add entire reaction mixture to kinase sample
16. Incubate reaction at 30 degrees Celsius
17. Place reaction on ice
18. Add 7.5 microliters of 5X Laemmli sample buffer
19. Heat reaction at 100 degrees Celsius for 3-5 minutes in heat block
20. Load 20 microliters of sample per well onto 10-15% SDS-PAGE gel
21. Shield gel apparatus to limit phosphorus-32 radiation exposure
22. Run SDS-PAGE gel to separate kinase and substrate by molecular weight
23. Remove gel from glass and aluminum plates
24. Place gel in 50 milliliters of Coomassie stain
25. Shake gel on orbital shaker at 50 RPM for one hour
26. Transfer gel from stain to fixing solution
27. Place foam pieces or knotted laboratory wipes in container with gel
28. Rock gel in 600-700 milliliters of fixing solution on orbital shaker at 50 RPM overnight
29. Remove gel from fixing solution
30. Soak gel in 200 milliliters of methanol with gentle agitation
31. Continue agitation for 1-2 minutes until gel turns milky white
32. Wet a 14 cm × 14 cm piece of qualitative filter paper with methanol
33. Place methanol-treated filter paper onto slab gel vacuum dryer
34. Lay gel onto filter paper with front side facing up
35. Cover gel carefully with plastic wrap
36. Turn on and run vacuum dryer to apply vacuum
37. Roll out air bubbles with soft rubber brayer after vacuum attainment
38. Continue vacuum drying run for 90 minutes
39. Remove dried gel
40. Attach autorad marker (e.g., phosphorescent ruler or dot) to filter paper on gel side
41. Place gel in cassette containing intensifying screen
42. Use Geiger counter to check radioactive signal intensity
43. Place X-ray film on top of dried gel inside film cassette with intensifying screen
44. Remove film from cassette after exposure
45. Develop film in medical/X-ray film processor
46. Mark bands corresponding to protein standards on film
47. Label protein standards and reaction lanes for reference
48. Excise bands from gel corresponding to bands of interest on film
49. Place excised bands in seven milliliter scintillation vials
50. Add four milliliters of scintillation fluid to vials
51. Count bands using liquid scintillation counter
52. Confirm scintillation counter is set to phosphorus-32 energy spectrum window

1. Thaw and gently resuspend mitochondrial vesicles (mitoplasts) in 5 ml ice-cold Buffer A
2. Keep sample chilled
3. Determine protein concentration using BCA assay according to manufacturer's instructions
4. Perform BSA dilution series in ultra pure water to construct standard curve
5. Dilute sample 20-100 times with ultra pure water to fit BSA standard range
6. Adjust protein concentration to 16 mg/ml by diluting with additional Buffer A
7. Fragment mitoplasts via sonication: 7 pulses of 15 seconds each at 70-100 J/impulse using 3.9mm microtip
8. Incubate sample on ice during fragmentation
9. Sediment membrane fragments by ultracentrifugation at 54,000 g for 16 hours or 98,000 g for 5 hours at 4°C
10. Decant supernatant
11. Transfer frozen sediment from centrifuge tube to homogenizer
12. Resuspend mitochondrial membrane pellet in Buffer B using small Dounce homogenizer
13. Transfer suspension to 50 mL conical tube
14. Remove sample from ice
15. Equilibrate sample and solutions to room temperature
16. Add chloroform saturated with 2M Tris-HCl (pH 8.5) at 1:1 ratio
17. Tightly close tube cap
18. Vigorously shake sample for 20 seconds
19. Centrifuge mixture at 8,400 g
20. Centrifuge mixture at 8,400 g for 5 minutes at room temperature
21. Transfer upper cloudy aqueous phase to 1.6 milliliter microtubes
22. Add protease inhibitors to sample
23. Centrifuge samples at 13,000 g for 30 minutes at room temperature
24. Equilibrate 5 ml anion exchange Q column attached to fast protein liquid chromatography system with 50 ml of Q column buffer at 5 ml/min flow rate until absorbance at 280 nm and conductivity stabilize
25. Load supernatant on equilibrated column at 1 ml/min flow rate
26. Load supernatant onto equilibrated column at 1 mL/min flow rate
27. Monitor until absorbance at 280 nm stabilizes at background level
28. Apply 25 mL linear gradient of Q column elution buffer (0% to 100%) at 0.5 mL/min flow rate
29. Collect 1 mL fractions during gradient application
30. Equilibrate Superose 6 Increase column attached to liquid chromatography system with 48 milliliters of SEC buffer at 0.5 milliliters per minute flow rate
31. Apply sample to column
32. Run size-exclusion chromatography at 0.25 milliliters per minute flow rate
33. Collect 0.25 milliliter fractions during chromatography run
34. Run 10 microliters of peak fractions (identified by UV 280 nm absorbance) on SDS-PAGE
35. Stain SDS-PAGE samples with Coomassie Blue
36. Collect 0.25 milliliter fractions
37. Run 10 microliters of peak-corresponding fractions from UV 280 nm absorbance trace on SDS-PAGE
38. Stain samples with Coomassie Blue
39. Assay fractions corresponding to first major F1 peak for ATP hydrolytic activity and azide sensitivity using Pullman ATPase assay
40. Perform BCA assay to determine protein concentration
41. Keep purified F1-ATPase at room temperature
42. Store purified F1-ATPase at room temperature
43. Use purified F1-ATPase within three days after purification for downstream applications

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Heat samples in slide rack at 60 degrees Celsius for 60 minutes
2. Immerse slides in two 40-minute changes of solvent in fume hood
3. Rehydrate samples two times in 100% ethanol and one time in 70% ethanol for five minutes per treatment
4. Place slides into boiling antigen retrieval solution in pressure cooker on high for 10 minutes using tongs
5. Transfer pressure cooker from heat into sink
6. Immediately run cold tap water over pressure cooker lid
7. Allow slides to equilibrate for 20 minutes in antigen retrieval solution
8. Wash samples two times in 0.01 molar PBS on orbital shaker for five minutes per wash
9. Draw circles around kidney tissue samples using hydrophobic pen
10. Apply 60 microliters of blocking solution (10% chicken serum in 5% bovine serum albumin)
11. Incubate blocking solution for 30 minutes at room temperature
12. Replace blocking solution with 60 microliters of primary antibody solution
13. Incubate primary antibody in humidity chamber overnight at 4°C
14. Wash slides on orbital shaker in PBS for two minutes per wash
15. Add 60 microliters of fluorophore-conjugated secondary antibody to each slide
16. Incubate slides for 40 minutes at room temperature
17. Wash slides in PBS
18. Treat slides in 0.3% Sudan black in 70% ethanol
19. Wash slides in tap water to remove precipitate
20. Immerse slides in PBS for 10 minutes
21. Mount slides onto confocal glass coverslips with three 60 microliter drops of DAPI-supplemented mounting solution
22. Seal coverslips with nail polish
23. Allow slides to cure for 24 hours at room temperature
24. Store slides at 4°C protected from light until confocal imaging
25. Open Trainable Weka Segmentation plugin in ImageJ
26. Drop first image onto ImageJ toolbar
27. Click Image → Color → Split Channels
28. Click Color → Merge Channels
29. Assign color to each channel in popup box
30. Check 'create composite' and 'keep source images' boxes
31. Click OK in merge channels dialog
32. Draw region of interest around glomerular tuft using primary antibody staining as guide
33. Initiate Gaussian blur filter on DAPI image via Process > Filters > Gaussian blur
34. Set Gaussian blur parameter to sigma=1-2
35. Launch Trainable Weka Segmentation tool via Plugins > Segmentation
36. Trace intact nuclei using freehand tool for Class 1
37. Delineate background areas using line tool for Class 2
38. Create new class for extranuclear DNA features
39. Outline DAPI-stained extranuclear DNA using line tool
40. Train classifier and generate probability map
41. Toggle display to obtain binary image with selected objects highlighted in white
42. Duplicate image via Image > Duplicate menu
43. Access threshold tool via Image > Adjust > Threshold
44. Adjust threshold using mouse until only extracellular DNA is visible
45. Convert image to binary via Process > Binary > Make Binary
46. Copy previously generated glomerular region of interest
47. Restore selection using Control+Shift+E keyboard shortcut
48. Initiate particle analysis via Analyze Particles > OK

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Transfer nuclei pellet re-suspended in 450 microliters of clean water to a clean 1.5 milliliter microcentrifuge tube
2. Add 60 microliters of 10X DPN2 restriction enzyme buffer
3. Mix sample well
4. Incubate samples with 15 microliters of 10% sodium dodecyl sulfate at 37 degrees Celsius for one hour with agitation
5. Quench reaction by adding 75 microliters of 20% triton X-100 and incubating as demonstrated previously
6. Aliquot 10 microliters from sample as undigested control
7. Store undigested control at 4 degrees Celsius
8. Add 400 units of DPN2 to remaining sample
9. Incubate sample overnight at 37°C with agitation for chromatin digestion
10. Add additional 200 units of DPN2 to sample
11. Incubate sample at 37°C with agitation for 4 hours to complete digestion
12. Heat inactivate restriction enzyme
13. Heat-inactivate restriction enzyme by incubating sample at 65°C for 20 minutes
14. Add 80 microliters of 10% sodium dodecyl sulfate (if enzyme is not heat-inactivated)
15. Incubate sample after SDS addition
16. Add 375 microliters of 20% Triton X-100 to sample
17. Mix sample by swirling after Triton X-100 addition
18. Aliquot 10 microliters of sample as digestion control
19. Transfer sample to clean 50 milliliter conical tube
20. Adjust sample volume to 5.7 milliliters with molecular grade water
21. Mix sample by swirling
22. Add 700 microliters of 10 X T4 ligase buffer
23. Add 60 units of T4 DNA ligase
24. Incubate sample overnight at 16 degrees Celsius
25. Add 30 microliters of 10 milligrams per milliliter Proteinase K to 3C sample
26. Incubate at 65 degrees Celsius overnight with agitation
27. Add 30 microliters of 10 mg/mL RNase A to sample
28. Mix sample by swirling
29. Add 7 mL phenol chloroform to samples
30. Mix samples by shaking
31. Centrifuge sample at 3,270 G for 15 minutes at room temperature
32. Collect aqueous phase and transfer to clean 50 mL conical tube
33. Add equal volumes of chloroform
34. Mix sample by shaking
35. Centrifuge sample
36. Transfer aqueous phase to clean 50 mL conical tube
37. Add 7.5 mL molecular grade water
38. Add 35 mL 100% ethanol
39. Add 7 μL of 1 mg/mL Glycogen
40. Mix sample thoroughly
41. Freeze sample at -80°C
42. Adjust volume of control samples to 500 microliters using molecular grade water
43. Add 2 microliters of 10 mg/ml RNase A
44. Mix samples by flicking the tube
45. Add 1 milliliter of phenol chloroform to control sample tubes
46. Mix samples by shaking
47. Centrifuge tubes
48. Transfer aqueous phase to clean 1.5 ml microcentrifuge tube
49. Add equal volume of chloroform to aqueous phase
50. Centrifuge mixture
51. Collect aqueous phase
52. Remove sample from freezer
53. Centrifuge sample at 3,270 G for 30 minutes
54. Add 10 milliliters of chilled 70% ethanol
55. Break up DNA pellet
56. Centrifuge sample and remove supernatant
57. Partially air-dry sample at room temperature
58. Resuspend pellet in 150 microliters of 10 mM Tris-HCl (pH 7.5)
59. Pipette suspension up and down to mix

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Test pneumococcal isolate against negative control latex reagent
2. Test pneumococcal isolate with latex pool reagents until positive reaction is observed
3. Test isolate with groups and types present in positive pool using manufacturer's key
4. Test isolate with required factor latex reagents and interpret results using manufacturer's key
5. Label a 2 ml round-bottom microcentrifuge tube
6. Add 975 μl glycine buffered saline using 1000 μl pipette
7. Add 25 μl pneumococcal antiserum
8. Invert tube 5 times to mix
9. Dilute 120 microliters of polystyrene latex particles in 1080 microliters of sterile physiological saline
10. Invert mixture five times to mix
11. Pipette 1000 microliters of latex suspension into 1000 microliters of antiserum solution
12. Invert combined solution five times to mix
13. Transfer mixture to 37°C incubator and load onto slowly rotating wheel
14. Centrifuge particles at 1,100 × g for 15 minutes
15. Remove supernatant using 1000 microliter pipette
16. Discard supernatant
17. Resuspend pellet in 2 milliliters of sterile saline
18. Centrifuge mixture again at 1,100 × g for 15 minutes
19. Discard supernatant
20. Resuspend washed pellet in 1 milliliter of glycine buffered saline
21. Transfer suspension to 5 mL screw-cap tube
22. Add 1 mL glycine buffered saline
23. Add 2 mL glycine buffered saline with 0.2% BSA
24. Add 40 μL of 10% sodium azide preservative
25. Test reagent using positive and negative control serotype isolates
26. Store quality control-passed reagents at 4 degrees Celsius
27. Select single colony using sterile loop
28. Inoculate one third of non-selective blood agar plate
29. Streak plate to obtain single colonies
30. Incubate plate overnight at 37°C in 5% CO₂ atmosphere
31. Remove plate from incubator
32. Invert negative control latex reagent several times
33. Pipette 15 microliters of reagent onto labeled microscope slide
34. Collect bacterial culture using sterile 1 microliter loop until loop is half full
35. Smear inoculum from loop onto slide near negative control reagent drop
36. Mix inoculum into latex reagent drop using loop
37. Lift slide at both ends and gently rock back and forth
38. Continue rocking slide for one minute while preventing drop spreading
39. Read results against dark background
40. Use a new sterile loop for each reagent
41. Rock slide for one minute before reaction assessment
42. Use manufacturer's key to determine groups/types in positive pool mixture
43. Test groups/types comprising the pool with isolate until positive reaction is observed
44. Refer to manufacturer's key to identify required factor tests when group is identified
45. Refer to key to determine final serotype after factor test results are obtained

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Place one cotton ball in bottom of 4cm diameter × 21cm height conical plastic pot
2. Fill pot with steam-pasteurized soil mixture to 2cm below rim
3. Insert plastic stake designating genotype into pot
4. Plant one seed of corresponding cotton genotype in pot
5. Cover seed with additional soil
6. Transfer pots to growth chamber set at 28°C with 16-hour fluorescent/incandescent photoperiod
7. Place water emitters into each pot
8. Use automatic watering system to water pots twice daily
9. Create small depression in soil next to each plant
10. Add 1 mL reniform nematode suspension into each depression
11. Use scissors to remove most fully expanded leaves from plants
12. Squeeze pot and slide soil into hand to remove plants
13. Agitate root system in 10L tap water container to remove soil
14. Rinse root system briefly in container of clean tap water
15. Use scissors to remove cleaned root system 1cm below soil line
16. Place roots into 120ml plastic container
17. Place root system below soil line into 120 ml plastic specimen container
18. Cover root system with approximately 30 ml red food coloring solution
19. Microwave specimen container until staining solution begins to boil
20. Replace food coloring solution with 100 ml tap water after cooling to room temperature
21. Place cover on specimen container and refrigerate at 4°C
22. Place cotton ball in bottom of new conical plastic pot and partially fill with peat moss potting medium
23. Place one harvested vegetative shoot with root system in pot
24. Firmly add potting medium to fill pot
25. Place labeled plastic stake in pot to designate cotton genotype
26. Place tray of pots into plastic container of water
27. Water plants briefly to moisten potting medium
28. Place pots in growth chamber set to 28°C with 16-hour photoperiod
29. Partially fill six-liter plastic pot with potting medium (after ~30 days)
30. Transfer plant into six-liter pot while firmly adding potting medium
31. Remove root sample from specimen container
32. Use stereo microscope at 20X magnification to count female nematodes attached to root system
33. Place root system on paper towels for 10 minutes to remove excess moisture
34. Weigh root system to determine fresh root weight

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Connect silicone tubing between reservoir, pump, and bubble trap to establish fluid pathway
2. Fill reservoir with 70% ethanol
3. Turn pump on at 1 milliliter per minute flow speed
4. Turn pump on at 1 milliliter per minute flow speed
5. Let 70% ethanol circulate to sterilize tubing
6. Empty reservoir
7. Pump ethanol out of system
8. Fill reservoir with sterile distilled water
9. Fill reservoir with sterile distilled water
10. Pump water through system to remove residual alcohol
11. Pump water out of system
12. Fill reservoir with 45 milliliters of freshly prepared medium
13. Circulate medium to fill all tubing
14. Allow medium to adapt (equilibrate)
15. Inject mouse with 500 units of heparin intraperitoneally
16. Place mouse on dissection board after 10 minutes
17. Confirm appropriate anesthesia level by toe pinch
18. Sterilize fur with 70% ethanol
19. Pinch and sterilize fur with 70% ethanol
20. Use scissors to open abdominal cavity, exposing vena cava and diaphragm to visualize heart
21. Make lateral incisions from bottom to top of ribs
22. Reflect thoracic cage over animal's head
23. Observe heart beating
24. Insert 21-gauge needle attached to 5ml syringe containing sterile tyros buffer through diaphragm into inferior vena cava
25. Ensure blood exit pathway from vena cava at needle insertion site
26. Perfuse buffer gently with constant pressure into vena cava until heart partly loses red color
27. Switch syringe to new 5ml syringe of buffer
28. Transfer heart into 10 cm Petri dish within laminar flow hood
29. Add fresh PBS supplemented with antibiotics and antimycotic
30. Using dissecting microscope, micro scissors, and forceps, remove all non-cardiac tissue while preserving ascending aorta to bifurcation with brachiocephalic artery and pulmonary veins 2 mm proximal to heart
31. Cut 2 mm off tip of heart apex
32. Cut 2 mm off tip of heart apex to access left ventricular lumen
33. Place perfusion chamber in hood using silicone tubing
34. Attach 5 ml syringes filled with medium to needle #1 (mitral valve) and needle #2 (aortic valve)
35. Fill perfusion chamber with 20 ml of medium
36. Position heart on rotation stage in perfusion chamber between two blunted needles
37. Adjust height of rotation stage
38. Ligate aorta with silk 7.0 suture
39. Ligate left atrial appendage with silk 7.0 suture
40. Insert needle number one through pulmonary vein into left atrium
41. Place suture proximal to needle entry in left atrium
42. Insert needle number two into left ventricle
43. Transfer medium from perfusion chamber to 50 milliliter tube
44. Seal needle to myocardium using biocompatible glue
45. Inject medium into heart using needle number one syringe
46. Check heart for leakage
47. Confirm medium exits from needle number two and blood perfuses out

7. Measure weight of dried support piece

9. Add 26.2 g colloidal silica and 8.39 g tetraethylammonium hydroxide to 250 ml polypropylene bottle

11. Stir mixture with magnetic stirrer for 20 minutes at room temperature

13. Stir mixture with magnetic stirrer for 20 minutes at room temperature
14. Combine Solution B with Solution A in 250 ml bottle
15. Cap bottle and shake vigorously by hand for 5 minutes
16. Stir mixture with magnetic stirrer for 24 hours at room temperature
17. Pour synthesis gel into Teflon-lined autoclave

19. Quench autoclave

17. Perform chloranil test to confirm secondary amine protection
18. Drain solvent mixture and wash resin twice with 2:1 DCM:DMF mixture
19. Add 10 milliliters of 2% hydrazine solution in DMF to the resin
20. Shake mixture for one hour
21. Perform Kaiser test to confirm primary amine deprotection
22. Mix 4 equivalents Fmoc-amino acid, 3.95 equivalents HBTU, and 15 equivalents DIPEA
23. Add one-to-one mixture of DCM and DMF to cocktail
24. Sonicate cocktail until complete dissolution
25. Sonicate cocktail until complete dissolution
26. Verify complete mixing and activation of amino acid, coupling agent, and DIPEA
27. Wait 3-5 minutes to ensure carboxylic acid activation
28. Add activated reaction mixture to vessel containing resin
29. Allow coupling reaction to proceed for 2-4 hours at ambient temperature
30. Perform Kaiser or chloranil test to confirm successful coupling
31. Deprotect Fmoc group from amino acid after coupling confirmation
32. Add 10 milliliters of 20% 4-Methylpyridine solution in DMF to deprotect Fmoc group
33. Perform Kaiser test to confirm successful deprotection
34. Wash resin with 10 milliliters of DMF for 5 minutes
35. Wash resin again with 10 milliliters of DMF for 5 minutes
36. Wash resin with 10 milliliters of DCM for 10 minutes
37. Add 10 equivalents of desired carboxylic acid functionality
38. Add 9.5 equivalents of HBTU
39. Add 12 equivalents of DIPEA
40. Sonicate cocktail until complete dissolution
41. Add cocktail to vessel
42. Carry out reaction for at least five hours
43. Drain solvent from vessel
44. Wash resin with 8 mL DMF for 2 minutes
45. Wash resin twice with 8 mL DCM for 5 minutes each
46. Drain solvent from vessel
47. Dry resin under vacuum for 15 minutes
48. Add 14 milliliters of TFA to 0.5 milliliters of water
49. Add 0.5 milliliters of Triisopropylsilane to mixture
50. Add cleavage cocktail to resin
51. Shake mixture for two to four hours at room temperature
52. Collect solution in 50 milliliter round bottom flask
53. Concentrate TFA in vacuo to one to two milliliters
54. Collect solution in 50 milliliter round bottom flask
55. Concentrate TFA to 1-2 milliliters using rotary evaporator at reduced pressure and 40°C
56. Add concentrated TFA solution dropwise to 15 milliliters anhydrous cold ether to precipitate PPA
57. Add 5 milliliters anhydrous cold ether to original flask containing concentrated TFA solution
58. Sonicate flask to recover additional solids
59. Combine sonicated mixture with ether solution from precipitation step
60. Cover flask and refrigerate overnight to maximize precipitation
61. Collect precipitated material by vacuum filtration
62. Use fine or medium pour size center disk filter funnel for filtration

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Add 1 mL of ice-cold 0.9% sodium chloride to 1.5-mL tube containing larvae
2. Close tube lid
3. Vertically flip tube to wash larvae
4. Place tube on ice for 30 seconds
5. Remove sodium chloride solution using 1-mL pipette
6. Centrifuge samples at 2,000 g for 1 minute at 4°C
7. Remove residual solution using 200-μL pipette and immediately freeze sample in liquid nitrogen
8. Label side of 2-mL screw-cap bead tube with ethanol-proof marker
9. Tare mass of labeled bead tube using analytical balance (0.01 mg accuracy)
10. Remove 1.5-mL sample tube from liquid nitrogen dewar using long forceps
11. Grab frozen tube while wearing nitrile gloves and invert it
12. Sharply pound inverted tube lid against benchtop to dislodge frozen pellet
13. Pour dislodged pellet into pre-tared 2-mL screw-cap bead tube
14. Measure combined mass of larval pellet and bead tube
15. Place sample tube into liquid nitrogen
16. Place sample tubes in -20°C benchtop cooler
17. Add 0.8 mL pre-chilled 90% methanol containing 2 μg/mL succinic-D4 acid to each sample tube
18. Return samples to -20°C benchtop cooler
19. Add 0.8 mL pre-chilled 90% methanol containing 2 μg/mL succinic-D4 acid to empty bead tube for negative control
20. Homogenize samples for 30 seconds at 6.45 meters per second using bead mill homogenizer in 4°C temperature control room
21. Return homogenized samples to -20°C benchtop cooler
22. Transfer cooler to -20°C freezer for at least one hour
23. Centrifuge tubes at 20,000 g for 5 minutes at 4°C to remove precipitate
24. Transfer 600 microliters of supernatant to new 1.5-milliliter microcentrifuge tube without disturbing precipitate
25. Open all sample tubes and place them in vacuum centrifuge
26. Dry samples at room temperature until all solvent is removed
27. Prepare solution of 40 mg/mL methoxylamine hydrochloride (MOX) in anhydrous pyridine
28. Store MOX and anhydrous pyridine in desiccator
29. Dry 1-mL glass syringe with heat gun for 5 seconds
30. Insert syringe needle into bottle of anhydrous pyridine
31. Withdraw 1 mL of anhydrous pyridine using syringe
32. Add withdrawn pyridine to microfuge tube containing 40 mg MOX
33. Flush bottle of anhydrous pyridine with argon
34. Seal the bottle
35. Return sealed bottle to desiccator
36. Dissolve MOX in pyridine by incubating in thermal mixer at 35°C for 10 minutes at 600 rpm
37. Add 40 microliters of 40 mg/mL MOX in anhydrous pyridine solution to dried sample
38. Vortex sample for 10 seconds
39. Centrifuge sample briefly
40. Incubate sample in thermal mixer
41. Centrifuge sample at 20,000 g or maximum speed for 5 minutes
42. Transfer 25 microliters of supernatant to autosampler vial with 250-microliter deactivated glass insert
43. Add 40 microliters of MSTFA containing 1% TMCS to vial
44. Place cap on autosampler vial
45. Seal vial using crimper tool
46. Incubate sample at 37°C for 1 hour with shaking at 250 rpm
47. Add 3 microliters of fatty acid methyl ester (FAMES) standard solution to vial using robotic autosampler

1. Culture B16-F10 GFP melanoma cells
2. Centrifuge cells and transfer to 1.5 mL micro tube
3. Centrifuge cells again in micro tube
4. Remove supernatant except thin layer above pellet
5. Resuspend cells into thick slurry
6. Remove tip cap from 33-gauge needle on Hamilton syringe
7. Load 20 μL slurry on top of tip chamber
8. Pull back plunger to load 5 μL slurry into syringe
9. Shave the mouse head

11. Rinse with water
12. Fix the proximal edge of the ear on the tip of an index finger using adhesive tape

16. Anesthetize mouse using humidified oxygen and isofluorane mixture

24. Remove adhesive tapes from ear using curved tweezers

26. Wash ear with Ringer's buffer
27. Apply primary antibodies targeting extracellular matrix molecules in blocking buffer to exposed ear

29. Incubate sample for 15 minutes
30. Wash ear twice using approximately five milliliters of Ringer's buffer per wash
31. Apply secondary antibodies or streptavidin in blocking buffer
32. Apply cover slip
33. Incubate for 15 minutes
34. Wash twice
35. Fold open dermal area into the EM mencia conki
36. Dry outer unopened ear dermis with sterile wipes
37. Place ear on stack of glass slides
38. Apply 0.5 microliters of surgical glue to anterior and posterior dorsal edges to immobilize ear

40. Apply cover slip

42. Use fluorescent stereo microscope with two lens to image ear

44. Set peristaltic pump to 1 microliter per minute speed
45. Activate peristaltic pump to deliver buffer to chamber

47. Adjust gain fluorescence intensity, magnification, and exposure time
48. Image multiple fields containing tumor cells and stained extracellular matrix proteins

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Set up control and experimental genetic crosses in egg-lay dishes
2. Keep flies in darkness for at least two days prior to timed egg collections
3. Transfer adults to new grape agar plates decorated with fresh yeast paste
4. Allow adults to lay eggs on plates for four hours
5. Incubate four-hour collection plates overnight at 25 degrees Celsius
6. Add 16 grams of fly agar and 700 milliliters of deionized water by heating
7. Prepare 17.5 milliliters Nipagen in 95% ethanol
8. Heat agar solution until almost completely dissolved
9. Add Nipagen solution to agar solution
10. Heat mixture until agar completely dissolves
11. Cool agar solution briefly
12. Load 10 cm plates with 15 ml agar solution
13. Allow agar to gel
14. Put lids on plates and dry at room temperature for hours/overnight
15. Verify agar is firm and resists crumbling when cut
16. Use 1.5 cm cork borer to make central hole in agar gel per plate
17. Fill holes neatly with fresh yeast paste
18. Bend a curve into the tip of a plastic microspatula
19. Dip microspatula tip in yeast paste to make it adhesive to larvae
20. Pick up first instar larvae individually with microspatula
21. Place larvae on agar plate close to food mound
22. Check assay plate to verify one larva is transferred per microspatula delivery
23. Label assay plate after loading larvae
24. Cover assay plate after loading larvae
25. Deliver larva using microspatula tip
26. Label assay plate
27. Cover assay plate
28. Place covered plate in darkness with lid side up at room temperature
29. Examine each plate daily until all larvae die or pupate
30. Remove and discard yeast food from central well of each plate
31. Flood each plate with tap water
32. Use soft paintbrush to rub debris off agar surface
33. Replace water and brush debris repeatedly until plate is clean
34. Rinse plate with deionized water
35. Cover plate
36. Let plate dry at room temperature overnight
37. Prepare TIFF file image of plate using black background to visualize tunnels as bright white lines
38. Quantitate tunneling in ImageJ using Oval tool to define agar gel area (excluding plastic edge of petri plate)
39. Apply automatic threshold to produce reverse image where tunnels appear as black lines
40. Use Color picker and Paintbrush tools to white out dark regions containing no tunnels
41. Select 'Set scale' under Analyze pull-down menu and click to remove scale
42. Go to 'Set measurements' window under Analyze menu and check 'Area' and 'Limit to threshold' options
43. Press Control+M keys to measure black area (representing tunneled area) in pixels
44. Uncheck 'Limit to threshold' option
45. Use Wand tool to define central hole
46. Press Control+M to measure central hole pixel value
47. Subtract 1.5cm diameter hole pixel value from measured central hole value
48. Add difference value to previously obtained tunneling pixel value
49. Use Color picker tool to prepare area limitation
50. Use Paintbrush tool to add black bar limiting quantified area

24. Place wafer into sputter deposition system
25. Evacuate chamber to pressure of 5 × 10⁻⁶ millitorr

28. Deposit aluminum at 400 nm thickness with 300 watts power

42. Expose photoresist to 375 nm UV light at 150 mJ/cm² dose

Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Place silicon wafer on spin coater
2. Rinse wafer with acetone during initial cycle of spin program
3. Execute second cycle of spin program to evaporate acetone and dry wafer
4. Apply photoresist to wafer at start of spin cycle
5. Spin coat wafer using specified conditions
6. Soft bake photoresist-coated wafer at 110°C for 2 minutes using high uniformity hot plate
7. Place substrate onto vacuum chuck of mask aligner
8. Insert lithographic mask into holder tray

12. Turn on UV lamp to expose substrate through lithographic mask

20. Degas PDMS-covered master in vacuum desiccator until no bubbles are visible

22. Cure stamp at 65°C for 1.5 hours
23. Cut cured PDMS stamp out of master
24. Trim stamp to appropriate size
25. Store stamp in covered container feature-side up to protect from dust
26. Treat round glass cover slips with oxygen plasma for 10 minutes
27. Rinse cover slips with deionized water
28. Dry cover slips with stream of nitrogen gas
29. Rinse cover slips with ethanol
30. Dry cover slips with stream of nitrogen gas

32. Deposit 150 angstroms gold without venting evaporator between layers
33. Rinse PDMS stamp with ethanol
34. Dry PDMS stamp thoroughly with nitrogen gas
35. Apply stamping solution dropwise to PDMS stamp until fully coated
36. Dry stamp thoroughly with nitrogen gas
37. Gently press stamp onto gold substrate
38. Allow monolayer to form for 15 seconds

40. Ensure substrate is submerged
41. Gently press stamp onto submerged gold substrate
42. Allow monolayer to form for 15 seconds
43. Rinse stamped substrate with ethanol
44. Dry substrate with nitrogen gas

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Harvest spinal cord via laminectomy
2. Cut spinal cord transversely into 1 cm segments
3. Dissect paraventricular region from each spinal cord segment
4. Mince tissue into 1 mm pieces
5. Enzymatically dissociate minced tissue
6. Separate intact cells by centrifugation through discontinuous density gradient
7. Plate cell suspension
8. Sterilize rat's skin and remove dorsal surface using large dissection scissors to expose vertebral column
9. Hold dissection scissors perpendicular to dorsal surface and transversely cut vertebral column above hind limbs
10. Use small scissors to longitudinally cut dorsal muscle over vertebral column in rostral direction to expose spinous processes
11. Insert small blunt bone cutting instrument extraorally into lateral aspect of spinal canal between spinal cord and vertebral column starting at exposed caudal end
12. Place blade shallow and parallel to spinal cord
13. Make small cuts into lamina
14. Peel away lamina to expose spinal cord
15. Continue removing lamina in rostral direction to expose thoracic and cervical spinal cord
16. Using blunt tissue forceps, gently lift spinal cord from vertebral column
17. Sever spinal roots with micro scissors to release cord
18. Place excised spinal cord in Petri dish containing ice-cold sterile rat dissection buffer
19. Rinse tissue with ice cold dissecting buffer
20. Cut spinal cord transversely into 1cm segments using scissors
21. Hold tissue segment with fine forceps
22. Use micro scissors to carefully remove white matter and most gray matter
23. Isolate periventricular region of spinal cord
24. Transfer dissected periventricular tissue to 10cm sterile Petri dish containing ice cold rat dissection buffer
25. Mince dissected periventricular tissue into 1 cubic millimeter pieces using micro scissors
26. Add 5 milliliters of Earl's balanced salt solution to vial of papain from dissociation kit
27. Incubate vial at 37 degrees Celsius for 10 minutes until papain dissolves and solution appears clear
28. Add 500 microliters of Earl's balanced salt solution to vial of DNAs
29. Gently mix DNAs solution
30. Add 250 microliters of DNA solution to vial containing papain
31. Gently mix papain-DNA solution
32. Add approximately 0.2 grams minced tissue to vial
33. Place vial on rocker platform at 37 degrees Celsius for 45 minutes to one hour
34. Triturate mixture with 10 milliliter pipette to dissociate tissue pieces
35. Yield cloudy cell suspension
36. Transfer cell suspension into sterile 15 milliliter conical tube
37. Centrifuge at 300 G for 5 minutes at room temperature
38. Prepare OVO moid solution by mixing 2.7 milliliters of Earl's balanced salt solution with 300 microliters of reconstituted albumin OVO MOID inhibitor solution in 15 milliliter conical tube
39. Add 150 microliters of DNA solution to OVO moid solution
40. Mix solution by inverting tube
41. Discard supernatant from pelleted cells
42. Immediately resuspend cell pellet in diluted DNA albumin inhibitor mixture
43. Add 5 ml of albumin inhibitor solution to a 15 ml tube
44. Gently layer cell suspension on top of albumin inhibitor solution using a 5 ml pipette
45. Centrifuge sample at 70 g for 6 minutes at room temperature
46. Discard supernatant and resuspend cell pellet in 1 ml of prewarmed rat EFH medium
47. Count live cell density using a hemocytometer
48. Plate cells into T25 culture flask at density of 10 cells/μl in EFH medium
49. Incubate flasks at 37°C with 5% CO₂ for one week without disturbance

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Pour half cup of each scented sand into four cups
2. Arrange cups equidistant from entrance door in task area according to software-assigned positions
3. Bury full piece of reward in pseudo-randomly chosen correct scented cup
4. Bury half piece of reward in identically scented sample cup
5. Bury rewards at least halfway to bottom of sand cup
6. Present sample odor to rat
7. Place rat into sample chamber passing intervening delay chamber
8. Lower cup containing half reward into chamber using bent spatula
9. Remove cup as soon as rat begins eating reward
10. Lift cardboard room divider to delay chamber
11. Lift divider to MTS task area
12. Remove rat from chamber
13. Place rat in transfer cage
14. Remove rat quickly after incorrect choice
15. Start four-minute ITI timer after rat transfer
16. Shield transfer cage to blind rat from next setup
17. Vacuum spilled sand during rest period
18. Clean chamber with 70% isopropyl alcohol
19. Remove uneaten rewards
20. Replenish sand cups as needed
21. Prepare next pseudo-randomly selected sample cup with half reward
22. Set up task area for next trial
23. Return rat to home cage after session completion
24. Start testing next rat
25. Conduct two trials where rat only encounters decline cup in task room
26. Load decline cup with sand of different color and texture
27. Load decline cup with one-quarter sized reward
28. Conduct 10 forced versus choice sessions per rat
29. Monitor rat's initial turn direction in delay chamber (left toward decline cup or right toward MTS test)
30. Calculate rat's forced-trial accuracy after two sessions
31. If accuracy <40% or >70%, cease forced versus choice testing
32. If accuracy <40% or >70%, return to DMTS testing
33. Clean test chamber with isopropanol after each trial

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Dissolve 30.29 grams of tris HCl powder in 100 milliliters of deionized water
2. Transfer solution to 250 milliliter volumetric flask
3. Fill flask to line with deionized water
4. Mix solution to obtain 1M tris HCl buffer
5. Prepare 20 milliliters of 0.1M tris HCl buffer by dilution from stock
6. Adjust pH to 8.5 using 1M hydrochloric acid while monitoring with pH meter
7. Dilute 0.02 milliliters of 1 mg/mL 100nm nanodiamond suspension to 1 milliliter with pH 8.5 tris buffer
8. Stir mixture for 10 minutes to obtain 0.02 mg/mL nanodiamond suspension
9. Dissolve 20 milligrams of dopamine hydrochloride in two milliliters of pH 8.5 tris buffer
10. Vortex mixture for 30 seconds
11. Add 5, 7.5, or 10 microliters of dopamine solution to nanodiamond solution to achieve final dopamine hydrochloride concentration of 50, 75, or 100 milligram per milliliter
12. Vigorously stir mixture at 25 degrees Celsius for 12 hours in the dark
13. Transfer suspension of polydopamine-coated nanodiamonds to 1.5 milliliter centrifuge tube
14. Centrifuge at 16,000 g for two hours
15. Remove supernatant
16. Wash nanodiamonds three times with 1 ml portions of deionized water at 16,000 g for one hour per wash
17. Add 200 microliters of deionized water to washed solids
18. Sonicate mixture for 30 seconds to redisperse polydopamine-coated nanodiamonds
19. Serially dilute 40 microliters of polydopamine-coated nanodiamonds suspension twice with deionized water
20. Dissolve 100 milligrams of silver nitrate in 10 milliliters of deionized water by vortexing
21. In fume hood, add one molar aqueous ammonia to silver nitrate solution dropwise until yellow precipitate forms, periodically shaking solution
22. Continue adding ammonia until precipitate disappears to obtain diamine silver hydroxide solution
23. Add 4.3 or 6.4 microliters of diamine silver solution to 40 microliters of diluted nanodiamond dispersion
24. Adjust mixture volume to 100 microliters with deionized water
25. Sonicate mixture for 10 minutes
26. Centrifuge dispersion at 16,000 g for 15 minutes to remove free silver ions
27. Discard supernatant
28. Wash silver nanoparticle-decorated polydopamine-coated nanodiamonds by centrifugation: 3 cycles in 100 microliter deionized water at 16,000 g for 5 minutes per cycle
29. Add 100 microliters deionized water to nanodiamonds
30. Redisperse nanodiamonds by sonication for 30 seconds
31. Redisperse samples by sonication for 30 seconds
32. Characterize nanodiamonds with UV-Vis spectroscopy scanning from 250 to 550 nanometers
33. Deposit 5 microliters of silver nanoparticle-decorated nanodiamonds on plasma-cleaned carbon-coated copper grids
34. Let grids sit for 3 minutes
35. Wick away excess solution with filter paper
36. Apply drop of deionized water to each grid
37. Let water sit on grid for 15 seconds
38. Wick away water with filter paper
39. Repeat washing cycle (steps 6-8) three times
40. Let grids air dry
41. Visualize samples with transmission electron microscopy

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Discard medium from 100 milliliter culture dish containing 80% confluent adipose-derived stem cells
2. Wash cells with five milliliters of PBS
3. Add one milliliter of PBS containing one millimolar EDTA
4. Incubate at 37 degrees Celsius for two minutes in humidified incubator with 5% carbon dioxide
5. Tap culture plate gently after two minutes
6. Add four milliliters of fresh medium
7. Transfer cells to 15 milliliter conical tube
8. Centrifuge cells at 250g for two minutes at room temperature
9. Remove supernatant without disturbing pellet
10. Re-suspend pellet in 10 milliliters of medium
11. Count cells using hemocytometer
12. Transfer 2.5 × 10⁵ cells to new 15 mL conical tube
13. Centrifuge at 2400 × g for 15 minutes
14. Aspirate supernatant immediately after centrifugation
15. Add 500 microliters of chondrogenic differentiation medium (CDM) to pellet
16. Add 50 micrograms per milliliter of freshly-prepared L-ascorbic-acid 2-phosphate to pellet
17. Loosely cap tubes
18. Incubate tubes at 37°C in humidified 5% CO₂ incubator for three weeks
19. Aspirate supernatant from centrifuged pellet
20. Re-suspend pellet in 10 microliters of CDM
21. Pipette re-suspended cells into center of 24-well plate well to form micromass
22. Place plate into incubator
23. Add 500 microliters of CDM to well by pipetting against plate wall to avoid disrupting micromass
24. Incubate micromass for three weeks
25. Harvest spheroid pellets using a pipette
26. Wash harvested pellets with 1X PBS
27. Fix spheroid pellets by immersion in 4% paraformaldehyde for 24 hours
28. Discard fixative solution
29. Rinse fixed pellets with deionized water
30. Place one layer of gauze onto tissue cassette
31. Transfer fixed spheroid pellets onto gauze using pipette
32. Cover pellets by folding gauze
33. Close tissue cassette lid
34. Dehydrate spheroid pellets by immersion in 70% ethanol for five minutes at room temperature
35. Continue dehydration through series of ethanol concentrations for five minutes each
36. Immerse pellets in 100% xylene for 15 minutes
37. Immerse spheroid pellets in paraffin at 56 degrees Celsius for embedding
38. Cut five micron-thick sections from paraffin-embedded cell pellet
39. Perform sectioning using rotary microtome
40. Float sections in 50% ethanol
41. Transfer sections to 50 degree Celsius water bath
42. Mount floating sections onto slides

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Sterilize surgical instruments, gauze sponges, and cognitive applicators using autoclave
2. Store surgical tools in 70% ethanol during surgery
3. Air dry surgical tools on sterile gauze immediately before use
4. Spray work area with 70% ethanol two hours before surgery
5. Inject mouse with 0.05 milligrams per kilogram of buprenorphine
6. Anesthetize mouse using anesthetic vaporizer with 3% isoflurane and 20% oxygen gas mixture
7. Adjust oxygen amount using flow meter
8. Test anesthesia depth via toe or tail pinch
9. Maintain anesthesia at 2% isoflurane
10. Apply artificial tears to mouse's eyes with caution to avoid eye damage during surgical procedure
11. Place mouse on right side in lateral position
12. Shave left side area between eye and ear base using animal clippers
13. Cleanse area by alternating Betadine solution and 70% ethanol using cotton tip applicators with light rubbing
14. Place mouse onto heating pad connected to rectal probe
15. Insert rectal probe using mineral oil to maintain 37°C body temperature
16. Position mouse on right side under microscope and secure with tape
17. Cut window in gauze sponge and cover surgical area
18. Make vertical skin incision between left eye and left ear base using fine straight scissors
19. Retract surgical area using curved hemostats
20. Apply sterile PBS to tissue using cotton tip applicators if excessive drying occurs
21. Make horizontal incision on temporal muscle using spring scissors
22. Separate temporal muscle from skull by slow pulling with forceps
23. Perform subtemporal craniotomy at zygomatic-squamosal junction using 18-gauge needle while holding jawbone with curved forceps
24. Remove skull fragments with bone rongeurs to expose middle cerebral artery (MCA)
25. Ligate distal MCA using small vessel cauterizer
26. Reposition temporal muscle to original anatomical position
27. Close incision site using 5-0 nylon sutures
28. Discontinue anesthesia
29. Remove rectal probe
30. Inject mouse subcutaneously with second dose of buprenorphine
31. Return mouse to cage maintained at 37°C with heating panel
32. Provide recovery gel to mouse
33. Wait 2-3 hours after surgery
34. Inject mouse subcutaneously with buprenorphine every 3-5 hours for 24 hours
35. Deeply anesthetize the mouse
36. Perfuse mouse with 4% TTC solution
37. Remove brain and place into 4% paraformaldehyde solution for overnight fixation
38. Position brain in sectioning block
39. Slice brain into 1mm thick sections using razor blades
40. Place brain slices next to millimeter scale ruler and photograph using digital camera connected to dissecting microscope
41. Open desired photo in ImageJ software
42. Select straight line tool
43. Draw straight line between ruler margins
44. Open 'Set Scale' in Analyze menu
45. Set known distance to 1 millimeter
46. Select freehand circle tool
47. Outline contralateral hemisphere with circle
48. Measure outlined area via Analyze menu
49. Outline ipsilateral hemisphere excluding stroke area
50. Measure outlined ipsilateral hemisphere area
51. Repeat measurement steps for 1mm slices
52. Sum stroke areas across slices to calculate volume

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Remove culture medium from mouse embryonic stem cells
2. Wash cells twice with 2 milliliters of sterilized PBS
3. Completely remove PBS from culture dish
4. Add 2 milliliters of cell detachment medium to dish
5. Incubate culture dish at 37 degrees Celsius
6. Add 8 milliliters of EB differentiation medium to dish
7. Pipette medium up and down 15-20 times to dissociate colonies
8. Transfer cell suspension to 10-milliliter tube
9. Centrifuge cells at 300×g at room temperature for 5 minutes
10. Carefully remove supernatant after centrifugation
11. Resuspend cell pellet with EB differentiation medium
12. Invert lid of 15-centimeter culture dish
13. Invert lid of 15-centimeter culture dish
14. Deposit 20-microliter drops of resuspended cells on inverted lid using 200-microliter multichannel pipette
15. Invert lid carefully and place onto bottom portion of culture dish
16. Incubate dish with hanging drops for three days
17. Wash lid gently with 10 milliliters of PBS
18. Transfer PBS containing embryoid bodies to 50-milliliter plastic tube
19. Let tube sit for 30 minutes at room temperature for sedimentation
20. Carefully remove supernatant
21. Gently resuspend embryoid bodies in 10 milliliters of fresh EB differentiation medium
22. Transfer suspension to 10-centimeter bacteriological Petri dish
23. Check embryoid body formation using inverted microscope for roundness and size homogeneity
24. Collect embryoid bodies (EBs) from two or three 10-centimeter dishes into a 50-milliliter plastic tube
25. Centrifuge EBs at 300 × g for 5 minutes at room temperature
26. Remove supernatant and resuspend EBs in 10 milliliters of PBS
27. Centrifuge EBs at 300 × g at room temperature for 3 minutes
28. Remove supernatant and add 2 milliliters of trypsin-EDTA to pellet
29. Incubate tube at 37°C for 15 minutes
30. Pipette suspension up and down every 3 minutes during incubation to obtain single-cell suspension
31. Add 8 milliliters of EB differentiation medium to stop trypsin reaction
32. Add paraformaldehyde to cell suspension to achieve 1% final concentration
33. Incubate cells for 10 minutes at room temperature under rotation
34. Quench formaldehyde by adding glycine to achieve 0.125 M final concentration
35. Snap-freeze cell pellets in liquid nitrogen
36. Gently resuspend cell pellet in freshly prepared ice-cold lysis buffer
37. Place tube on ice
38. Centrifuge cells at 1,000 × g for 5 minutes at 4°C
39. Discard supernatant and wash pellet with 500 microliters of cold lysis buffer
40. Resuspend pellet in 1.5-ml tube with 50 microliters of 0.5% SDS in 1× buffer two
41. Place tube in heating block at 62°C
42. Remove tube from heating block
43. Add digestion buffer
44. Mix well by pipetting while avoiding excessive foaming
45. Add digestion buffer
46. Mix by pipetting while avoiding excessive foaming
47. Incubate tube at 37 degrees Celsius
48. Add 25 microliters of digestion buffer
49. Mix by inverting
50. Take 8 microliters as undigested control sample
51. Store undigested control at -20 degrees Celsius
52. Add aliquots of restriction enzyme Mbol
53. Incubate at 37 degrees Celsius under rotation after each enzyme aliquot
54. Take 8 microliters as digested control sample
55. Add 80 microliters of TE buffer to both control samples
56. Add 10 microliters of proteinase K to both control samples
57. Incubate samples at 65 degrees Celsius for one hour
58. Run 20-microliter aliquots of samples on 0.6% agarose gel
59. Shear DNA to 150-700 base pairs using sonicator
60. Transfer sheared DNA to new safe-lock tube and pool multiple sonications
61. Add warmed DNA purification beads (1.8× sample volume)
62. Resuspend mixture to combine beads with DNA
63. Incubate mixture for 5 minutes
64. Collect beads using magnetic rack
65. Wash beads twice with 1 ml freshly prepared 80% ethanol while on magnetic rack
66. Air-dry beads at room temperature for 2-3 minutes
67. Elute DNA by resuspending beads in 1× Tris buffer

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Add 5% sulfinitated polyetheretherketone fibers to 250 milliliter round bottom flask
2. Dissolve fibers in dimethylacetamide solvent
3. Shake flask for 10 minutes to settle ionomer polymers
4. Place mixture into silicon oil bath with magnetic stir bar
5. Vigorously stir solution at 500 rpm for 24 hours at 80°C
6. Filter 30 milliliters of solution through 0.45 micrometer PTFE filter into circular 18 centimeter diameter glass dish
7. Remove bubbles using air blower
8. Place dish in oven at 90 degrees Celsius for 24 hours to generate approximately 50 micrometer thick freestanding membrane
9. Fill dish with warm distilled water
10. Wait 10 minutes for membrane detachment
11. Immerse membrane in 1M sulfuric acid at 80°C for 2 hours
12. Wash membrane three times with 1L distilled water (10 minutes per wash at room temperature)
13. Dissolve 10% by weight FAA-3 ionomer in NMP solvent at room temperature with 500 RPM stirring for 2 hours
14. Filter approximately 30 ml of solution through 100 micron pore strainer into 18 cm glass Petri dish
15. Place dish in 100°C oven for 24 hours after air bubble removal
16. Extract dried membrane using hot distilled water
17. Activate membrane in 1 L sodium hydroxide solution for 2 hours
18. Wash activated membrane three times with 1 L distilled water per wash
19. Position 3 cm thick PMMA plate with electrode facing up
20. Place rubber gasket and spacer onto electrode
21. Place cation exchange membrane
22. Place anion exchange membrane on opposite side of gasket
23. Place silicon gasket and spacer onto each membrane
24. Position second PMMA plate onto spacers and gaskets
25. Secure stack using digital wrench drive at 25 newton meter force with nuts, bolts, and washers
26. Place iridium/ruthenium-coated titanium mesh electrode at end of each plate
27. Connect electrodes to source meter using crocodile clips
28. Add 5 liters of 0.01 M sodium chloride solution to a large container connected to a peristaltic pump
29. Add 5 liters of 0.6 M sodium chloride solution to a separate large container connected to a peristaltic pump
30. Stir both sodium chloride solutions continuously at room temperature
31. Add 0.05 M rinse solution to a third container
32. Connect all three containers to the RED assembly via rubber tubes through the peristaltic pump and pressure gauges
33. Set rinse solution flow rate to 50 mL/min
34. Set salt solutions flow rate to 100 mL/min
35. Check tubing to eliminate crossflow or leakage

Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Dissolve 11 mg DPPC and 1.7 mg DSPE-PEG2000 in chloroform in glass round bottom flask
2. Place round bottom flask in 50°C water bath in chemical fume hood
3. Evaporate organic solvent using stream of argon gas
4. Place flask in desiccator under vacuum overnight to desiccate lipid film
5. Rehydrate lipid film with 5.5 milliliters of PBS
6. Heat solution in 45°C water bath until lipid film dissolves
7. Transfer lipid solution into 7-milliliter vial
8. Sonicate solution with probe sonicator for 2 minutes at 20% amplitude
9. Divide solution into two vials (2.5 milliliters each)
10. Discard remaining 0.5 milliliters of solution
11. Divide solution into two vials (2.5 milliliters each)
12. Discard remaining 0.5 milliliters of solution
13. Add 2.5 milliliters of PBS to each vial
14. Place both vials in 0°C ice water bath
15. Add 50 microliters of DDFP to each vial
16. Sonicate each vial in ice water bath at 25% amplitude in pulsed mode for 60 seconds
17. Transfer PSNE solution to 20mL scintillation vials and add 5mL PBS per vial to achieve 10mL final volume
18. Assemble extruder and prime according to manufacturer directions
19. Pipette 10mL deionized water into top sample port of extruder
20. Cap extruder opening and tighten vent valve
21. Slowly open nitrogen gas line to increase pressure for membrane extrusion
22. Collect sample from extruder outlet tubing
23. Dissolve 2.4 grams of BSA powder in 10 milliliters of deionized water to prepare 24% BSA solution
24. Dilute 1 gram of APS powder in 10 milliliters of deionized water to prepare 10% APS solution
25. Mix reagents in plastic mold in specified order: 2.1 ml acrylamide solution, 1.2 ml tris buffer, 0.1 ml 10% APS, 4.5 ml 24% BSA solution, and 3.6 ml deionized water
26. Heat the solution to 40 degrees Celsius in a water bath
27. Place the solution under vacuum for one hour to degas the liquid
28. Remove the solution from the vacuum
29. Add 480 microliters of PSNE suspension
30. Thoroughly mix the solution by gently swirling the plastic chamber
31. Thoroughly mix contents by gently swirling plastic chamber
32. Add 12 microliters of TEMED (tetraethyl ethylene diamine) in chemical fume hood to polymerize acrylamide
33. Place chamber in 12°C water bath for two hours
34. Position focused power transducer and imaging probe in acrylic tank filled with degassed deionized water, aligning imaging plane
35. Position imaging probe in acrylic tank filled with deionized water
36. Align imaging plane to overlap with transducer focal volume
37. Position PSNE-loaded polyacrylamide hydrogel at focus of power transducer
38. Vaporize PSNE within hydrogel using short acoustic pulses
39. Apply 15-second continuous wave ultrasound exposure for bubble-enhanced ablation

Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Transfer brains from 5-10 neonatal mice to Petri dish containing buffer A
2. Remove cerebellum, brainstem, meninges, and capillary fragments from each brain
3. Transfer cerebrals to second Petri dish containing buffer A
4. Cut cerebrums into small pieces using sterile scalpel
5. Homogenize tissue by aspirating and expelling with 10 ml pipette until no clumps appear
6. Transfer suspension to 50 ml centrifuge tube
7. Centrifuge brain tissue at 250 × g for 5 minutes at room temperature
8. Discard supernatant
9. Dissolve pellet in 4.5 ml buffer A with 1.5 ml 0.75% w/v solution
10. Dissolve pellet in 4.5 milliliters of buffer A with 1.5 milliliters of 0.75% weight per volume collagenase dispase
11. Incubate tissue and collagenase solution for 45 minutes at 37°C in water bath with occasional agitation
12. Coat four wells of 12-well plate with 0.1 milligrams per milliliter collagen in 50 millimolar acetic acid
13. Allow collagen to adhere to wells at room temperature for one hour
14. Add 15 milliliters of buffer A to stop digestion
15. Thoroughly resuspend pellet in buffer A solution
16. Centrifuge at 250 × g for 10 minutes
17. Discard supernatant
18. Add 10 milliliters of sterile 25% w/v BSA in PBS to tissue
19. Centrifuge at 1000 × g for 20 minutes at 4°C
20. Carefully discard supernatant
21. Discard supernatant after centrifugation
22. Dissolve pellet in 15 milliliters of buffer A
23. Transfer solution to fresh 50 milliliter centrifuge tube
24. Centrifuge at 250 × g for 5 minutes at room temperature
25. Wash collagen-coated plate twice with PBS
26. Leave two wells empty after washing
27. Add 2 milliliters of growth media to two other wells
28. Discard supernatant and resuspend cell pellet
29. Resuspend pellet in 4 milliliters of growth media containing 4 μg/mL puromycin
30. Plate 2 milliliters of cell suspension into each of two empty collagen-coated wells
31. Incubate at 37°C for 45 minutes
32. Cultivate GPE neo fibroblasts in gelatin-coated flasks containing GPE neomed medium
33. Grow GPE Neo fibroblasts for 24 hours
34. Remove 10 milliliters of media from GPE Neo Fibroblast culture
35. Add sterile polybrene solution to achieve final concentration of 8 micrograms per milliliter
36. Pass supernatant mixture through 0.45 micron filter to remove cellular fragments
37. Aspirate media from endothelial cell cultures
38. Add 2 milliliters of supernatant-polybrene mixture to wells
39. Remove supernatant-polybrene mixture from wells
40. Wash cells twice with PBS
41. Add fresh growth medium to cells
42. Passage confluent cells at 1:2 ratio onto collagen-coated plates
43. Transfer stable cell lines to collagen-coated tissue culture flasks
44. Add 3 milliliters of trypsin-EDTA to flasks
45. Incubate at 37°C for 5-15 minutes until cell layer disperses
46. Add 5 milliliters of medium to flask
47. Seed cells into new flasks at ≤1:4 split ratio
48. Harvest cells using trypsin
49. Centrifuge cell suspension at 250 × g for 5 minutes at room temperature
50. Resuspend cell pellet in 6 ml of freezing medium
51. Pipette 1.5 ml of cell suspension into each of four cryo vials
52. Place cryo vials into Nalgene freezing container filled with isopropanol
53. Store cryo vials at -80°C for one day
54. Transfer cryo vials to long-term storage under liquid nitrogen vapor

11. Incubate tissue and collagenase solution for 45 minutes at 37°C in water bath with occasional agitation
12. Coat four wells of 12-well plate with 0.1 milligrams per milliliter collagen in 50 millimolar acetic acid
13. Allow collagen to adhere to wells at room temperature for one hour
14. Add 15 milliliters of buffer A to stop digestion
15. Thoroughly resuspend pellet in buffer A solution
16. Centrifuge at 250 × g for 10 minutes
17. Discard supernatant
18. Add 10 milliliters of sterile 25% w/v BSA in PBS to tissue
19. Centrifuge at 1000 × g for 20 minutes at 4°C
20. Carefully discard supernatant
21. Discard supernatant after centrifugation
22. Dissolve pellet in 15 milliliters of buffer A

35. Add sterile polybrene solution to achieve final concentration of 8 micrograms per milliliter

38. Add 2 milliliters of supernatant-polybrene mixture to wells
39. Remove supernatant-polybrene mixture from wells

43. Transfer stable cell lines to collagen-coated tissue culture flasks

46. Add 5 milliliters of medium to flask

48. Harvest cells using trypsin

1. Dislodge semi-adherent cells from flask via rigorous pipetting in laminar flow hood
2. Count cells using hemocytometer
3. Transfer 125% of required cells to 50 milliliter conical tube
4. Pellet cells at 300 G for 5 minutes
5. Aspirate supernatant from sample
6. Resuspend pellet at 1.7 × 10⁶ cells/mL in serum-free Schneider's medium
7. Prepare sterilized and primed automated liquid handling system
8. Add 10 microliters of cell suspension to each well of 384-well plate pre-treated with 250 ng/well dsRNA
9. Spin plates at 300 × g for 1 minute to settle cells
10. Incubate plates at 25°C for 45 minutes
11. Add 20 microliters of complete Schneider's medium to each well
12. Add 20 microliters of complete Schneider's medium to each well
13. Spin plates at 300 G for one minute
14. Store plates in humidified sealed containers lined with water-soaked paper towels
15. Incubate at 25 degrees Celsius for three days
16. Filter crude vaccinia virus stock through 0.8 micron syringe filter
17. Dilute virus in Schneider's medium with 2% serum to target multiplicity of infection (MOI) of 2
18. Prime sterilized WellMate tubing system with diluted virus until air bubbles are removed
19. Remove medium from plates of double-stranded RNA treated Drosophila cells using sterilized multichannel aspirator with vacuum manifold
20. Dispense 30 microliters of diluted virus into each well
21. Centrifuge plates at 300 G for one minute
22. Incubate plates in humidified container at 25 degrees Celsius for 48 hours
23. Aspirate media from plate in BSL-2 biosafety cabinet
24. Add 15 microliters of 4% formaldehyde fix to each well using multichannel pipette
25. Incubate at room temperature for 15 minutes
26. Remove liquid with multichannel aspirator
27. Add 30 microliters of PBST to each well using WELLMADE
28. Incubate at room temperature for 10 minutes
29. Perform second PBST wash for 10 minutes in same manner
30. Remove liquid after incubation
31. Add 30 microliters of PBST with 2% VSA blocking solution
32. Incubate at room temperature for 10 minutes
33. Add 15 microliters of primary antibody diluted in blocking solution to each well using multi-channel repeat pipetter
34. Spin down plates at 300 G for one minute
35. Seal each plate with clear ceiling film
36. Incubate at 4°C overnight
37. Wash cells with PBST three times for 10 minutes each using WellMate and multi-channel aspirator
38. Add 15 microliters of fluorescently labeled secondary antibody with nuclear stain to each well using multi-channel pipetter
39. Cover plate with foil to protect from light
40. Incubate plates at room temperature for one hour
41. Wash cells with PBST three times for 10 minutes each as previously performed
42. Add 30 microliters of PBST to cells
43. Seal plate with clear sealing film
44. Cover sealed plate with foil
45. Store sealed plates at 4°C for up to three weeks before imaging

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Use osteochondral autograph transfer system to create 4.75-millimeter diameter defect in lateral condyle of euthanized sheep
2. Use orthopedic hammer to create 10-millimeter deep defect and retrieve osteochondral explant from condyle
3. Prepare calcium phosphate cement supplemented with 40 micrograms per milliliter of BMP-2
4. Load cement paste into 3-milliliter syringe
5. Inject cement into explant defect using 18-gauge needle
6. Turn on micro-tomography X-ray machine
7. Place explant tube on sample holder
8. Set scan resolution to 10.7 micrometers
9. Set exposure time to 1,200 milliseconds
10. Install 1-millimeter aluminum filter
11. Set voltage to 80 kilovolts and current to 125 microamperes
12. Configure image averaging: three images per 0.45-degree rotation increment
13. Use integrated segmentation wizard to train deep learning model for bone-cement distinction
14. Select representative zone containing bone, cement, and background from reconstructed micro-CT images
15. Segment first frame of selected zone
16. Generate deep learning model in model tab and select 3D U-Net routine
17. Right-click generated model and set parameters: depth=5, patch size=32x32, Adadelta algorithm, stride ratio=0.25, 10X data
18. Use segmented frame to train deep learning model
19. Click train button to initiate model training
20. Define second frame after training completion
21. Automatically segment defined frame using predict function
22. Click export to publish trained model
23. Apply model to entire micro-CT dataset by selecting: segment > exported model > segment full dataset
24. Place sheep osteochondral bone explant in 40-milliliter glass vial
25. Add 25 milliliters of dehydration solution to glass vial
26. Place glass vial on rotating wheel for one hour at room temperature
27. Replace dehydration solution with 25 milliliters of xylene
28. Place glass vial with xylene on rotating wheel for one hour at room temperature
29. Add 10% benzoyl peroxide
30. Add 10% dibutyl phthalate
31. Add 450 microliters of N,N-Dimethylaniline diluted 1:20 in propanol
32. Place glass vial at -20°C overnight
33. Place explant into medium-sized embedding mold in plastic box
34. Pour methylmethacrylate benzoyl peroxide, dibutyl phthalate, N,N-aniline solution into mold
35. Ventilate mold with nitrogen flow for five minutes
36. Close box hermetically
37. Place box at 4°C for 48 hours for MMA polymerization and hardening
38. Remove resin containing explant from mold
39. Cut polymethyl methacrylate block containing explant along long axis using diamond saw to generate 1.5 mm thick section at 3,000 RPM with 3 mm/min speed
40. Grind section with silicon carbide paper using ascending grit numbers
41. Carbon coat thick section with 10-nanometer carbon film
42. Mount section on aluminum stub
43. Create conductive silver paint bridge between section top and stub
44. Place stub on SEM stage
45. Close SEM chamber and initiate vacuum
46. Turn on electron beam
47. Adjust SEM settings
48. Configure SEM to operate in backscattered electron mode
49. Set gray level standard to 25 for carbon
50. Set gray level standard to 225 for aluminum
51. Set gray level standard to 253 for silicon
52. Acquire images of specimen
53. Acquire images in backscattered electron mode
54. Convert gray levels to calcium content using backscattered electron image
55. Plot calcium distribution content of image
56. Place sample section on Raman micro spectrometer stage
57. Calibrate wavenumber for accuracy
58. Align laser prior to measurement
59. Configure laser to 785-nanometer wavelength at 30 milliwatts power
60. Set integration time to 20 seconds
61. Set spectral range to 350-1800 cm⁻¹ with 1200 lines per millimeter grating
62. Place sample in optical system
63. Identify nanoindentation location
64. Move sample under indentation device
65. Set indentation depth to 900 nanometers
66. Set loading/unloading speed to 40 millimeters per minute
67. Set 15-second pause between loading and unloading
68. Set Poisson coefficient to 0.3 for bone tissue
69. Start nanoindentation

21. Pour out and dispose of supernatants

23. Reweigh tubes using analytical balance and report final pellet weight for each tube
24. Add 1 milliliter of cold S30 buffer with 2 millimolar DTT per 1 gram of cell mass to pellets

52. Mix each well by pipetting up and down
