question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Wear mask
2. Wear hair cover
3. Wear clean lab coat or scrubs
4. Place anesthetized mouse on clean surface separated from surgery area
5. Fit mouse with nose cone delivering 1.5% isoflurane with 21% oxygen
6. Inject buprenorphine subcutaneously into dorsal cervical thoracic area
7. Inject heparin intraperitoneally
8. Apply ophthalmic ointment to eyes
9. Remove hair from ventral abdomen using clippers
10. Move mouse to heated water blanket in surgery area
11. Fit mouse with nose cone delivering isoflurane
12. Position mouse in dorsal recumbency
13. Secure limbs to table with surgical tape
14. Monitor body temperature using rodent-specific rectal thermometer
15. Thoroughly disinfect abdomen
16. Perform toe pinch test to ensure full anesthesia
17. Wear sterile gloves and aseptically drape surgical site
18. Make 3-5 cm ventral midline abdominal incision in skin using #15 scalpel blade
19. Separate 0.5 cm of skin edge from underlying muscle fascia
20. Continue incision through abdominal wall along linea alba using spring-loaded micro scissors
21. Place retractor
22. Place sterile gauze pads moistened with warm sterile saline around operating area
23. Remove small intestine from abdominal cavity
24. Flip intestine cranially and to animal's left and place on moistened pads
25. Place moistened gauze pad over tissues to prevent desiccation
26. Locate superior mesenteric artery ventral to inferior vena cava caudal to celiac artery and cranial to renal artery
27. Dissect superior mesenteric artery away from surrounding connective tissue
28. Place atraumatic microvascular clip along base of SMA at abdominal aorta branch point
29. Verify small intestine ischemia by observing color change from pink to pale white
30. Return viscera to original position within abdominal cavity
31. Remove retractor
32. Cover incision with moist gauze
33. Remove the occluding clip
34. Verify restoration of blood flow by observing mesenteric pulsation and flushed color
35. Apply warm sterile saline intraperitoneally
36. Close abdominal muscles using 6-0 polyglactin 910 suture
37. Administer bupivacaine topically along muscle incision line
38. Close skin with surgical staples or wound clips
39. Transfer mouse to recovery chamber on circulating water blanket
40. Allow mouse to recover for 90 minutes
41. Monitor mouse for signs of pain or distress during recovery
42. Collect liver lobes (left lateral, left median, right median)
43. Collect both kidneys
44. Cut left kidney longitudinally
45. Cut right kidney in cross-section
46. Collect entire length of small and large intestine
47. Fold small intestines into Z-shape to segment duodenum (top), jejunum (middle), and ileum (bottom)
48. Identify colon as remaining intestinal part
49. Flush lumen of intestinal segments with saline using 10-mL syringe affixed with 20-gauge angiocatheter
50. Cut length of intestines using micro dissecting scissors
51. Lay each intestinal segment flat with luminal side facing up
52. Apply 10% buffered formalin dropwise to mucosa using 3-mL syringe affixed with 27-gauge needle to coat entire length
53. Roll each intestinal segment circumferentially around a toothpick with proximal portion forming inner part and lumen facing inside
54. Place individual Swiss rolls spiral face up in separate labeled tissue cassettes
55. Place tissues in labeled vials filled with 10% buffered formalin for fixation at room temperature

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Identify distal stomach and pylorus
2. Hold pylorus with forceps while snipping away mesentery
3. Perform blunt dissection with back of scissors to scrape away adherent pancreas
4. Use scissors to remove seven centimeters of proximal intestine without tearing
5. Place intestinal segment into ice-cold DPBS
6. Place intestinal segment into ice cold DPBS without calcium or magnesium
7. Attach blunt end 18-20 gauge needle to 5-10 milliliter syringe
8. Flush fecal contents with ice cold DPBS using prepared syringe
9. Divide intestine into three centimeter segments
10. Remove mesentery and longitudinal muscle from segments
11. Take a cotton swab
12. Break off approximately 4 cm of cotton swab
13. Separate wooden stick from cotton tip
14. Soak wooden stick in DPBS
15. Slip intestinal segment onto wetted stick
16. Remove adherent mesentery using needle nose forceps
17. Make two longitudinal nicks along intestine attachment site using razor blade or scalpel
18. Wet cotton tip with DPBS
19. Rub wetted cotton tip longitudinally along muscle to loosen LMMP
20. Move cotton tip horizontally to tease LMMP from circular muscle
21. Discard LMMP unless harvesting EGCs
22. Cut intestines into 0.5 cm pieces using fine scissors
23. Collect tissue pieces in 30 ml ice-cold EDTA HEPES DPBS solution
24. Rock tissue solution at 4°C for 10 minutes at 60 tilts/minute
25. Pre-moisten 5 ml plastic pipette by pipetting EDTA HEPES DPBS buffer up and down once
26. Triturate tissue-buffer suspension 20 times using pre-moistened pipette
27. Pour mixture through 100 micron nylon cell strainer to collect tissue
28. Transfer strained tissue to new 50 ml tube containing 30 ml ice-cold EDTA HEPES DPBS using needle nose forceps
29. Rock tissue solution at 4°C for 10 minutes at 60 tilts/minute (second incubation)
30. Perform gentle trituration using pre-moistened 5 ml pipette
31. Pour mixture through 100 micron nylon cell strainer and discard flow-through
32. Transfer tissue from nylon strainer to 15 ml conical centrifuge tube containing 5 ml cell recovery solution
33. Rock tube for 25-30 minutes at 4°C
34. Gently triturate 10 times
35. Gently triturate tissue 10 times to dissociate enteric glial cells from lamina propria
36. Filter dissociated material through 40 micron filter and collect filtrate in clean 50 milliliter tube
37. Rinse tissue on nylon filter with 1 milliliter of DPBS
38. Discard tissue
39. Transfer 5-6 milliliters of filtrate to clean 15 milliliter conical centrifuge tube
40. Centrifuge filtrate at 2,000 × g for 5 minutes at 4°C in swinging bucket centrifuge
41. Resuspend glial cell pellet in ≥1 milliliter resuspension buffer by pipetting gently with 500 microliter pipette tip without introducing bubbles
42. Plate enteric glial cells
43. Plate enteric glial cells
44. Add 200 microliters of cell suspension to each well of laminin-coated six-well plate containing glial growth media
45. Add 100 microliters of cell suspension to each well of laminin-coated 12-well plate containing glial growth media

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Place pre-prepared mold in aluminum foil-covered glass Petri dish
2. Prepare 30-40 ml PDMS mixture by weighing 1:10 ratio of curing agent to elastomer
3. Mix PDMS continuously for approximately 5 minutes until mixture appears white and nearly opaque
4. Pour PDMS mixture into Petri dish containing mold
5. Degas PDMS in desiccator until no bubbles remain
6. Bake mold with liquid PDMS in oven or on hot plate at 70°C until rubber-like consistency is obtained
7. Cut device out by tracing around features with scalpel while pushing forward (not down) due to mold fragility
8. Remove cutout PDMS device from mold
9. Place PDMS device upside down in new Petri dish
10. Punch holes in device using blunt 20-gauge syringe needle based on imprinted pattern
11. Insert cleaned PDMS and coverslip into plasma cleaner
12. Close plasma cleaner valves and turn on power, vacuum, and pump
13. Run plasma cleaner for approximately 1 minute
14. Set RF to high and allow controlled air entry using fine valve
15. Monitor viewing window until color changes from purple to pink, then run plasma cleaner for 50 seconds and turn RF off
16. Maintain pump operation for 1 minute after RF deactivation
17. Gradually open main valve to reintroduce air into plasma cleaner
18. Press PDMS surface onto cleaned coverslip for bonding
19. Confirm bond integrity by testing for detachment when pulling coverslip slightly
20. Secure 90-degree blunt needle with pliers
21. Insert one end of needle into Tygon tube
22. Insert other end of needle into punched hole of device
23. Repeat needle-tubing assembly process for remaining device holes
24. Apply small amount of immersion oil to surface of copper cold stage
25. Spread oil using lint-free wipe to create thin layer
26. Place clean sapphire disc on created oil layer
27. Apply droplet of immersion oil onto center of sapphire disc
28. Position PDMS device onto oil droplet with features aligned over viewing hole of cold stage
29. Secure tubing to outer walls of aluminum box housing adhesive tape
30. Inject 4-5 microliters of antifreeze protein solution into inlet channel using glass syringe
31. Close lid of cold stage
32. Initiate temperature control program
33. Set temperature to -25°C
34. Increase temperature gradually at rate of 1°C per 5 seconds toward sample melting point
35. Switch microscope to 10x or 20x objective
36. Grow single crystal by slightly decreasing temperature until crystal ends meet channel walls
37. Switch microscope to 50x objective
38. Inject antifreeze protein solution into channels and observe fluorescence intensity increase
39. Slowly inject buffer solution into second inlet of microfluidic device
40. Observe decrease in fluorescent signal using NIS-Elements Imaging Program
41. Inject THF-water solution (1:15 molar ratio) into microfluidic device
42. Slowly increase temperature until all ice melts while preserving hydrates
43. Hold temperature at 1°C for 3 minutes
44. Set temperature to -2°C
45. Observe hydrate abundance in microfluidic channels under inhibitor-free conditions
46. Inject antifreeze protein or inhibitor into microfluidic channel using glass syringe
47. Adjust temperature to maintain crystal stability
48. Allow inhibitor molecules to adsorb to crystal surface for several minutes
49. Perform solution exchange by injecting inhibitor-free solution into channel
50. Capture images of crystal before and after solution exchange
51. Analyze fluorescence intensity on crystal and in solution using imaging program
52. Form and isolate a single crystal in microfluidic channels
53. Exchange solution around the isolated crystal

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Select similar-sized Triticum aestivum (wheat) seeds
2. Disinfect seeds with 8% hydrogen peroxide solution for 15 minutes
3. Rinse disinfected seeds thoroughly with deionized water
4. Place seeds on humid filter paper in dark at room temperature for 5-day germination
5. Select 9 uniform-sized germinated seedlings
6. Transfer seedlings to plastic beakers containing 250 milliliters of 1/4 strength Hoagland's solution
7. Cultivate seedlings in growth chambers for 7 days under temperature cycle: 14 hours at 22°C and 10 hours at 27°C
8. Prepare centrifuge tubes A and B with 50 milliliters of 1/4 strength Hoagland's solution
9. Dissolve PFOA and PFOS in methanol
10. Dilute dissolved PFOA/PFOS with sterile nutrient solution to prepare stock solution
11. Add stock solution to tube B at 100 micrograms per liter of PFOA and PFOS
12. Separate wheat seedling roots into two equal parts using tweezers
13. Insert separated roots into tubes A and B respectively
14. Seal both tubes with aluminum foil
15. Culture tubes in incubator for 7 days with 14 hours at 22°C and 10 hours at 27°C cycle
16. Collect wheat seedlings after 7-day incubation
17. Separate seedlings into shoots and roots using scissors (3 parts: spiked solution shoots/roots, unspiked solution shoots/roots)
18. Freeze-dry plant samples in lyophilizer at -55°C for 48 hours
19. Homogenize freeze-dried samples
20. Weigh root and shoot samples
21. Collect spiked and unspiked solution samples
22. Add 2 milliliters of sodium carbonate buffer to 15-milliliter polypropylene tube
23. Add 1 milliliter of tetrabutylammonium hydrogen sulfate to tube
24. Add 5 milliliters of methyl tert-butyl ether to tube
25. Add homogenized root or shoot sample to tube
26. Shake tube at 250 RPM for 20 minutes
27. Centrifuge tube at 2000 g for 10 minutes at room temperature
28. Pool similar treatment extracts
29. Evaporate pooled extracts to dryness in gentle nitrogen stream
30. Reconstitute dried extracts with 5 milliliters methanol
31. Vortex solution at constant speed for 30 seconds
32. Condition PestiCarb cartridge with 5 milliliters 0.1% ammonium hydroxide in methanol
33. Condition cartridge with 5 milliliters water
34. Condition cartridge with 5 milliliters methanol
35. Add 5 milliliters extracting methanol solution through PestiCarb cartridge to remove pigments
36. Elute cartridge with 5 milliliters methanol into sample tubes
37. Evaporate 10 milliliters methanol solution to dryness
38. Reconstitute with 200 microliters methanol
39. Centrifuge sample at 10,000 g for 20 minutes at room temperature
40. Condition polar-enhanced polymer extraction cartridge with 5 milliliters of methanol and 5 milliliters of water
41. Add sample solution (1 milliliter spiked solution or 50 milliliters unspiked solution) through cartridge
42. Elute target PFAAs with 10 milliliters of methanol
43. Evaporate extract using gentle nitrogen stream
44. Reconstitute evaporated extract with 200 microliters of methanol

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Dilute peptide-plasmid DNA mixture with ultrapure water until solution becomes clear
2. Dilute mixture to final volume specified in protocol
3. Transfer 800 microliters of diluted solution into cuvette
4. Configure zetasizer with 633 nm helium neon laser
5. Set zetasizer to 25°C with 173° backscatter detection angle
6. Measure hydrodynamic diameter and polydispersity index of complexes using zetasizer
7. Transfer solution into folded capillary cell for zeta potential measurements
8. Set measurement parameters: dielectric constant, refractive index, and viscosity of water at 25°C
9. Deposit 10 microliters of complex solution onto freshly cleaved mica surface
10. Leave mica to air dry overnight in covered plastic Petri dish
11. Acquire image of complexes in air at 25°C using silicon cantilever (spring constant: 1.3 N/m) in tapping mode
12. Choose at least three leaves for transfection to serve as triplicates for gene expression quantification
13. Load 100 microliters of complex solution into a 1-milliliter needleless plastic syringe for transfection of one leaf
14. Position syringe tip on abaxial surface of leaf
15. Press syringe tip against leaf slightly and depress syringe plunger slowly while exerting counterpressure with gloved index finger
16. Label infiltrated leaves for identification
17. Incubate transfected plant for 12 hours in growth chamber
18. Excise whole transfected leaf
19. Place excised leaf in 1.5 ml microcentrifuge tube
20. Add 100 microliters of 1X Lysis Buffer per tube
21. Prepare lysates of non-transfected control leaves in triplicate
22. Grind leaf using homogenization pestle
23. Incubate lysate at 25 degrees Celsius for 6-10 hours
24. Centrifuge lysate at 12,470 × g in microcentrifuge for 1 minute
25. Transfer 20 microliters of cleared lysate to well in 96-well microplate
26. Add 100 microliters of 1× Renilla Luciferase assay substrate to well
27. Mix substrate and lysate by slow pipetting
28. Place microplate in multi-mode microplate reader
29. Initiate luminescence measurement
30. Cut edges of whole leaf to aid removal of air spaces
31. Remove plunger from 10 milliliter syringe
32. Place excised leaf or leaf section in syringe
33. Replace plunger and push gently to bottom of syringe without crushing leaf
34. Draw water into syringe until approximately half-filled
35. Point syringe upwards
36. Push plunger to remove air from syringe through tip
37. Cover tip of syringe
38. Pull plunger down slowly to expel air from leaf
39. Repeat degassing process until leaf appears translucent
40. Cover surface of microscope slide
41. Cover microscope slide surface with adhesive tape
42. Cut square area in tape using blade to accommodate leaf sample
43. Peel square tape piece off with forceps to create specimen chamber
44. Place degassed leaf in chamber with abaxial surface facing upward
45. Fill remaining chamber area with water
46. Seal chamber with glass cover slip
47. Secure cover slip edges with adhesive tape
48. Configure confocal microscope with 40x or 63x water immersion objective at 488 nm excitation

18. Stop timer when all four paws enter goal box section

18. House rats in overcrowded conditions (six rats per cage instead of three) for 18 hours
19. Place rats in cage tilted 45 degrees along vertical axis for three hours
20. Food deprive rats for 18 hours
21. Water deprive rats for 18 hours with exposure to empty water bottles after one hour
22. Place rats in cage with wet bedding for eight hours during night cycle
23. Expose rats to continuous lighting for 24 hours in reverse light-dark cycle twice weekly
24. Place rats in 40°C environment for five minutes during night cycle
25. Perform sucrose preference test to confirm depression development

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Draw 30 milliliters of whole blood from forearm vein
2. Split blood evenly into two non-polystyrene 50 milliliter conical tubes
3. Dilute blood in each tube with 10 milliliters of PBS
4. Add 25 milliliters of histopaque to each tube containing blood and PBS, avoiding mixing
5. Centrifuge tubes for 30 minutes at 400 Gs, room temperature, with no brake
6. Aspirate plasma layer after centrifugation
7. Transfer opaque interface layer to fresh 50 milliliter tube
8. Wash cells in 20 milliliters PBS plus CDTA for 5 minutes at 250 Gs and 4°C
9. Discard supernatant after washing
10. Resuspend pellet in 10 milliliters PBS plus CDTA
11. Vortex cell suspension for 10 seconds to mix well
12. Dilute 200 microliters cell suspension in 300 microliters PBS plus EDTA
13. Add 500 microliters trypan blue to diluted cell suspension
14. Spin down cells three times for 10 minutes at 120 Gs
15. Suspend pellet in nine milliliters of sterile water for three seconds
16. Add one milliliter of 10X PBS
17. Wash and count cells
18. Dilute cells in easy set buffer at 5×10⁷ cells per milliliter
19. Incubate cells with 50 microliters per milliliter of monocyte enrichment cocktail for 10 minutes at 4°C
20. Vortex cept monocyte enrichment beads for 30 seconds during last minute of incubation
21. Add 50 microliters per milliliter of beads to cells
22. Incubate bead-cell suspension for 5 minutes
23. Adjust cell solution volume to 2.5 milliliters using eep buffer
24. Place tube in eep magnet for 2.5 minutes at room temperature
25. Pour buffer containing non-binding monocytes into fresh sterile tube
26. Wash unbound cells once with PBS plus EDTA
27. Transfer washed cells to multi-well plate
28. Add 1 ml culture media per 1×10⁶ cells to culture plate
29. Culture cells under experimental conditions for 6 days to induce macrophage differentiation
30. Replace half of media with fresh media after 3 days
31. Harvest cells for experimental analysis after 6 days

1. Remove flask containing cells from cell culture incubator
2. Check cell confluence on inverted microscope equipped with automatic image acquisition system
3. Move to biosafety hood
4. Aspirate culture medium using 10-milliliter plastic pipette under sterile conditions
5. Gently discard aspirated medium into waste bottle
6. Add 10 milliliter calcium-magnesium-free (CMF) solution to flask
7. Close flask
8. Place flask in cell culture incubator for 10 minutes
9. Remove flask from incubator
10. Discard CMF solution from flask
11. Add 2 milliliter trypsin-EDTA solution to flask
12. Gently rock flask manually to ensure solution coverage
13. Incubate flask for 10 minutes
14. Add 10 ml culture media supplemented with 10% heat-inactivated bovine serum
15. Carefully wash the flask
16. Collect the cell suspension
17. Transfer cell suspension into conical 50-ml tube
18. Centrifuge the sample
19. Check pellet formation after centrifugation
20. Aspirate supernatant
21. Resuspend cell pellet in 6 ml serum-supplemented culture medium
22. Pipette cell suspension up and down repeatedly to break clumps
23. Place culture insert in well of 12-well plate using sterile tweezers
24. Apply pressure along insert edges with tweezers to fix insert to plate surface
25. Fill each insert compartment with 70 microliters of cell suspension (3.5 × 10⁴ cells/chamber)
26. Check under microscope at 4× magnification for cell leakage from compartments
27. Incubate plate for 24 hours
28. Remove inserts with sterile tweezers without breaking cell monolayers
29. Transfer insert onto absorbent paper
30. Wash cells by adding one milliliter medium per well, avoiding direct addition on cell monolayers to prevent disruption
31. Close plate and gently rock manually
32. Aspirate medium
33. Replace with one milliliter fresh medium per well
34. Visualize cell-free gaps under microscope
35. Acquire images at time zero
36. Withdraw media from wells
37. Add PBS supplemented with calcium and magnesium
38. Shake plate
39. Remove PBS
40. Add test compounds to wells
41. Incubate plate

43. Observe cell migration for each sample under microscope at 4× magnification
44. Acquire microscope images after 15, 20, and 24 hours incubation in same areas as time zero
45. Select images for each sample at all time intervals when experiment finishes
46. Create zip file of selected images
47. Open automated Image Analysis Wimasis program
48. Upload zip file using lowest magnification
49. Calculate mean value of all samples at time zero

51. Calculate mean value and relative standard error for all samples at each time point
52. Perform statistical analysis using two-way ANOVA test
53. Aspirate medium from each compartment
54. Wash compartments once
55. Add 70 microliter of media supplemented with 5 micromolar AG1478
56. Incubate plate for 30 minutes
57. Remove insert using tweezers

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Prepare yeast extract with supplements without adenine (YES), Pombe Glutamate Medium (PMG), and PMG without adenine
2. Mix media components according to specified table
3. Add G418 to media after autoclaving if necessary
4. Stir media for 5-10 minutes
5. Aliquot media into 90 milliliter Petri dishes
6. Store plates at 4 degrees Celsius
7. Use cloned rpt4 positive with 5' and 3' UTRs and silent mutation
8. Perform error-prone PCR with primers p5 and p6 and error-prone polymerase in 50 microliter total volume
9. Inoculate 10 milliliters of YES medium with yeast
10. Incubate culture for >16 hours to saturation
11. Dilute cells to OD600 0.2 using 200 milliliters of YES medium
12. Incubate culture at 30°C with shaking for 5-6 hours to OD600 0.6-0.8
13. Calculate volume of cells equivalent to OD600 of 30
14. Dispense cells into four 50ml conical tubes
15. Chill tubes on ice for 10 minutes
16. Harvest cells by centrifugation at 1050 G, 4°C for 3 minutes
17. Place microfuge tubes with cassette DNA and 10 electro cuvettes on ice
18. Discard supernatant while keeping tubes on ice
19. Add 15ml of 1.2M sorbitol
20. Gently shake tubes to resuspend cells
21. Centrifuge at 1050 G, 4°C for 3 minutes
22. Discard supernatant and perform second sorbitol wash
23. Discard supernatant after second centrifugation
24. Resuspend cells and transfer to one 15ml conical tube
25. Add 1.2 molar sorbitol up to 2.4 milliliters
26. Keep tube of cells on ice
27. Add 200 microliters of sorbitol-suspended cells to tube containing fusion PCR construct
28. Mix sample well
29. Transfer sample to electro cuvette
30. Add 600 microliters of 1.2-molar sorbitol to each electroporation cuvette
31. Spread transformed cells onto four YES plates
32. Incubate plates at 30 degrees Celsius for 24 hours
33. Perform replica plating on YES plates containing G418
34. Incubate replica plates for three additional days
35. Perform replica plating from YES plus G418 plates to YES without adenine plates
36. Perform replica plating from YES plus G418 plates to PMG without adenine plates
37. Incubate replica plates at 30°C for 1-2 days until colonies on YES without adenine plates show white coloration
38. Compare growth on YES without adenine and PMG without adenine plates
39. Select colonies showing pink or white coloration on YES without adenine plates with concurrent growth on PMG without adenine plates
40. Exclude colonies without growth on PMG without adenine plates (false positives)
41. Pick each colony and add to 10 microliters of SPZ solution containing 2.5 mg/ml zymolyase 100T
42. Incubate colonies at 37°C for at least 30 minutes
43. Use 1 microliter of incubated solution as template for colony PCR
44. Mark colonies with XhoI-cut PCR products and patch them to YES plus G418 plates
45. Isolate genomic DNA (gDNA) from fission yeast cells
46. Sequence PCR products

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Prepare yeast extract with supplements without adenine (YES), Pombe Glutamate Medium (PMG), and PMG without adenine
2. Mix media components according to specified table
3. Add G418 to media after autoclaving if necessary
4. Stir media for 5-10 minutes
5. Aliquot media into 90 milliliter Petri dishes
6. Store plates at 4 degrees Celsius
7. Use cloned rpt4 positive with 5' and 3' UTRs and silent mutation
8. Perform error-prone PCR with primers p5 and p6 and error-prone polymerase in 50 microliter total volume
9. Inoculate 10 milliliters of YES medium with yeast
10. Incubate culture for >16 hours to saturation
11. Dilute cells to OD600 0.2 using 200 milliliters of YES medium
12. Incubate culture at 30°C with shaking for 5-6 hours to OD600 0.6-0.8
13. Calculate volume of cells equivalent to OD600 of 30
14. Dispense cells into four 50ml conical tubes
15. Chill tubes on ice for 10 minutes
16. Harvest cells by centrifugation at 1050 G, 4°C for 3 minutes
17. Place microfuge tubes with cassette DNA and 10 electro cuvettes on ice
18. Discard supernatant while keeping tubes on ice
19. Add 15ml of 1.2M sorbitol
20. Gently shake tubes to resuspend cells
21. Centrifuge at 1050 G, 4°C for 3 minutes
22. Discard supernatant and perform second sorbitol wash
23. Discard supernatant after second centrifugation
24. Resuspend cells and transfer to one 15ml conical tube
25. Add 1.2 molar sorbitol up to 2.4 milliliters
26. Keep tube of cells on ice
27. Add 200 microliters of sorbitol-suspended cells to tube containing fusion PCR construct
28. Mix sample well
29. Transfer sample to electro cuvette
30. Add 600 microliters of 1.2-molar sorbitol to each electroporation cuvette
31. Spread transformed cells onto four YES plates
32. Incubate plates at 30 degrees Celsius for 24 hours
33. Perform replica plating on YES plates containing G418
34. Incubate replica plates for three additional days
35. Perform replica plating from YES plus G418 plates to YES without adenine plates
36. Perform replica plating from YES plus G418 plates to PMG without adenine plates
37. Incubate replica plates at 30°C for 1-2 days until colonies on YES without adenine plates show white coloration
38. Compare growth on YES without adenine and PMG without adenine plates
39. Select colonies showing pink or white coloration on YES without adenine plates with concurrent growth on PMG without adenine plates
40. Exclude colonies without growth on PMG without adenine plates (false positives)
41. Pick each colony and add to 10 microliters of SPZ solution containing 2.5 mg/ml zymolyase 100T
42. Incubate colonies at 37°C for at least 30 minutes
43. Use 1 microliter of incubated solution as template for colony PCR
44. Mark colonies with XhoI-cut PCR products and patch them to YES plus G418 plates
45. Isolate genomic DNA (gDNA) from fission yeast cells
46. Sequence PCR products
47. Compare obtained sequence with wild-type sequence
48. Identify mutations through sequence comparison
49. Re-introduce identified mutations into wild-type cells

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Spray mouse skin with 70% ethanol
2. Open skin and expose leg muscle using sterile dissecting scissors
3. Cut muscle alongside leg to view bone clearly
4. Gently remove bone without damaging it
5. Place bone into tube containing 4% paraldehyde
6. Place tube on ice
7. Place bone sample tubes at 4°C in cold room on orbital shaker for minimum 24 hours
8. Retrieve fixed bone tissue from orbital shaker
9. Wash bone tissue with 1X PBS twice for 3 minutes each
10. Place bone specimen in new container with 20% EDTA at pH 8
11. Incubate container on orbital shaker at 140 RPM for 30 minutes at room temperature
12. Replace EDTA solution with fresh 20% EDTA
13. Agitate container on orbital shaker for 30 minutes
14. Add fresh EDTA to container
15. Incubate container on orbital shaker at 4°C overnight
16. Retrieve samples from EDTA container
17. Wash samples two times with 1X PBS to remove EDTA residues
18. Place bone in 70% ethanol
19. Incubate in 70% ethanol for 15 minutes at 140 RPM
20. Discard 70% ethanol solution
21. Add 90% ethanol solution
22. Incubate in 90% ethanol for 15 minutes at 140 RPM
23. Place dehydrated bone tissue in new container with xylene
24. Incubate in xylene for 20 minutes at 140 RPM
25. Replace used xylene with fresh xylene
26. Incubate in fresh xylene for 20 minutes
27. Place cleared bone sample in embedding cassette
28. Begin wax infiltration
29. Incubate sample at 60°C for 30 minutes
30. Discard used wax and add new wax
31. Incubate sample at 60°C for 30 minutes
32. Replace wax with fresh wax
33. Incubate sample at 60°C for 45 minutes
34. Position bone in mold with desired orientation
35. Solidify wax on cold surface
36. Store FFPE block at 4°C until sectioning
37. Spray all work surfaces and instruments with decontaminating solutions to remove RNases
38. Set up water bath with double distilled water at 42 degrees Celsius
39. Clean microtome blade with 70% ethanol to remove oil
40. Decontaminate microtome blade to remove RNases
41. Secure blade on microtome
42. Set microtome clearance angle to 10 degrees
43. Spray tweezers, brushes, and probes with decontaminating solutions
44. Store decontaminated tools in ice bucket
45. Prepare ice bath by adding double distilled water to ice bucket
46. Place FFPE block on microtome
47. Set microtome to trim thickness of 14 micrometers
48. Trim sample until tissue is visible
49. Hydrate FFPE block by placing on ice bath
50. Place FFPE block on ice bath for hydration
51. Secure hydrated sample in microtome specimen clamp and align with blade
52. Set microtome section thickness to five micrometers
53. Start tissue sectioning
54. Collect sectioned tissue using cold tweezers and brushes
55. Place tissue section in 42°C water bath
56. Place tissue on glass slide
57. Incubate slide at 42°C for three hours
58. Dry slide overnight in oven at room temperature
59. Store prepared slide in slide box at room temperature

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Add 25 mm magnetic stir bar and 113 ml deionized water to 500 ml beaker
2. Stir deionized water at highest rate without vortex formation
3. Slowly add 13.05 g anhydrous Zirconium Chloride to water in small increments
4. Add 53.29 g glucose to solution
5. Add 3.73 g Yttrium nitrate hexahydrate to solution
6. Increase stirring rate to 700 RPM until all solids dissolve
7. Add 42 ml propylene oxide to solution
8. Decrease stirring to 150 RPM after sufficient mixing
9. Continue stirring until magnetic stir bar stops due to gel formation
10. Remove parafilm from beaker
11. Decant liquid on top of gel
12. Add 300 milliliters of absolute ethanol to beaker containing gel
13. Tightly cover beaker with parafilm
14. Leave covered beaker at room temperature for 24 hours
15. Remove gel from beaker
16. Transfer gel to two-liter porcelain evaporating dish using laboratory spatula
17. Break gel into pieces with spatula
18. Spread gel pieces over surface of evaporating dish
19. Allow gel pieces to dry under ambient conditions
20. Grind dried xerogel into fine powder using agate mortar and pestle
21. Place one gram of xerogel powder into 13mm cylindrical pellet press die
22. Apply 22 kilo Newtons of force using hydraulic press for 90 seconds to form pellet
23. Slowly release force from hydraulic press
24. Carefully remove pellet from pellet die
25. Place xerogel pellet onto YSZ plate and load into center of tube furnace
26. Blow Argon at 1/3 working tube volume per minute for ≥15 minutes while venting outlet to fume hood
27. Program tube furnace temperature controller to heating schedule while maintaining constant Argon flow
28. Start heating program and verify furnace follows schedule
29. Remove pellet from tube furnace after program completion
30. Cut 50 milligram piece from centered xerogel pellet using utility knife
31. Grind xerogel into fine powder using agate mortar and pestle
32. Place approximately 50 milligrams of fine powder into Alumina sample cup
33. Heat sample in thermogravimetric analyzer at 10°C/min from ambient to 1200°C with 100 mL/min air flow
34. Place centered xerogel pellet in alumina crucible
35. Place crucible into box furnace at 700°C for 2 hours
36. Remove hot crucible from furnace using stainless steel crucible tongs
37. Cool crucible to room temperature for 1 hour
38. Remove porous white YSZ scaffold from crucible

36. Pierce IVC with 30-gauge needle to initiate venotomy

41. Place two 11-0 stay sutures to connect donor aortic cuff to proximal and distal apex of recipient aortotomy
42. Perform anastomosis with 2-3 continuous 11-0 nylon sutures between donor aortic cuff and anterior wall of recipient aortotomy
43. Turn spleen graft to recipient's left side
44. Anastomose donor aortic cuff to posterior wall of recipient aortotomy
45. Initiate venous anastomosis between donor portal vein and recipient IVC
46. Apply four to five bites of continuous sutures per side for venous anastomosis
47. Suture posterior wall within vessel lumen
48. Close anterior wall using same continuous suture
49. Suture posterior wall within vessel lumen
50. Close anterior wall using same suture
51. Complete anastomosis with knot placed on lower corner outside IVC
52. Release vessel clamps

55. Close skin layer with 5-0 nylon suture in interrupted pattern

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Weigh 100 grams of Taiwanese green propolis
2. Grind propolis into fine powder using spice grinder (no large particles)
3. Set out five flasks and add 100 milliliters of various ethanol concentrations to each
4. Mix 10 grams of ground propolis into ethanol solution in each flask
5. Incubate flasks at 25°C with shaking at 250 RPM for 48 hours
6. Filter ethanol extracts using 25 micrometer pore size filter paper
7. Reconstitute filtrates to original volume of 100 milliliters with 95% ethanol using volumetric flask
8. Store reconstituted extracts at -20°C until ready to use
9. Concentrate 10 milliliters of extract using vacuum evaporation at 40 degrees Celsius for 10 minutes
10. Bake dry matter at 45 degrees Celsius for 24 hours
11. Reconstitute dry matter sample with 10 milliliters of 95% ethanol
12. Filter extract using 0.22 micrometer syringe filter and collect filtrate for HPLC analysis
13. Prepare 1 liter of mobile phase methanol-to-water solution at 88.8:11.2 ratio
14. Prepare serial dilutions of propolin standard concentrations using mobile phase as solvent
15. Inject 20 microliters of each standard concentration into reverse phase columns
16. Sequence injections from lowest to highest concentration
17. Set HPLC column temperature to 30 degrees Celsius
18. Set UV detector wavelength to 280 nanometers
19. Set flow rate to 1 milliliter per minute and recorder time to 20 minutes
20. Analyze standards in triplicate
21. Inject 20 microliters of each extract into reverse phase column
22. Set HPLC column temperature to 30 degrees Celsius
23. Set HPLC flow rate to 1 milliliter per minute
24. Set UV detector wavelength to 280 nanometers
25. Set recorder time to 20 minutes
26. Analyze extracts at least three times
27. Use standard curve to determine concentration
28. Add 10 microliters of diluted ethanol extract (0.156-640 μg/mL) to wells of 96-well plate
29. Adjust well volumes to 100 microliters using broth while maintaining 5% DMSO concentration
30. Inoculate 100 microliters of bacterial culture into each well of 96-well plate
31. Incubate plate at 37°C for 48 hours
32. Measure bacterial growth turbidity at 590 nm using microplate reader to determine MIC
33. Inoculate 10 microliters of liquid culture from no-growth wells onto agar plate
34. Incubate agar plate at 37 degrees Celsius for 24 hours

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Clean 3x3 cm glass plates with soap and hot water
2. Place glass plates in beaker with ethanol
3. Place beaker in ultrasonic bath for 10 minutes
4. Dry glass plates with tissue and air blower
5. Place glass plates in UV ozone photoreactor
6. Treat glass plates in UV ozone photoreactor for 20 minutes to remove organic residues
7. Blow air on one glass plate
8. Place glass plate on spin coater
9. Deposit 0.5 milliliters of polyamide solution onto glass plate to cover entire surface
10. Spin coat alignment layer
11. Mark glass plates on non-coated side with distinctive signs to recognize homeotropic and planar alignment layers
12. Place coated glass plate on hot plate at 110°C for 10 minutes to remove majority of solvent
13. Place all coated glass plates in oven at 180°C for one hour to cure polyamide layer
14. Place coated glass plate face-down on velvet cloth
15. Apply uniform soft pressure with two fingers
16. Drag glass plate straight along velvet surface
17. Blow air on glass plate
18. Mix UV curing glue with 20μm diameter glass beads
19. Place two glue drops at adjacent corners of planar-aligned glass plate
20. Place two additional glue drops 5mm from other corners
21. Assemble homeotropic-aligned plate atop first plate with 4mm edge gap
22. UV cure assembly for 2 minutes
23. Place cell on hot plate with homeotropic side upwards
24. Set hot plate temperature to 110 degrees Celsius
25. Place LC mixture on edge of cell to melt solid and initiate capillary flow
26. Add more LC mixture at edge until cell is fully filled
27. Slowly cool cell to 90 degrees Celsius to reach nematic phase
28. Polymerize mixture under UV light at 90 degrees Celsius for 30 minutes
29. Place polymerized cell on hot plate at 130 degrees Celsius
30. Allow cell to cool to room temperature
31. Place razor blade at one edge of cell
32. Push razor blade between glass plates to open cell
33. Lift film corners using razor blade
34. Gently peel film from glass plate
35. Cut a strip along the molecular director of the film
36. Clamp film sample using self-closing tweezers
37. Position clamped sample to allow 1.7 centimeters of film free to move
38. Hold sample vertically
39. Direct LED beam perpendicular to sample for self-oscillation observation
40. Observe film attached to glass substrate between crossed polarizers above diffused white light source to verify splay alignment

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Culture HeLa cells in supplemented DMEM at 37°C in 5% CO₂ atmosphere
2. Replace culture medium with 15 mL prewarmed fresh medium when cells reach 80% confluence
3. Add 15 μL of 100 mM EU to each dish to achieve 1 mM final concentration
4. Add 7.5 μL of 100 mM 4SU to experimental and no UV control sample dishes
5. Cover dishes with foil
6. Culture cells for 16 hours at 37°C in 5% CO₂ atmosphere
7. Remove culture medium from dishes
8. Wash each dish three times with 5 ml PBS per wash
9. Remove residual PBS completely from dishes
10. Place experimental and no four SU control sample dishes on ice
11. Remove lids from dishes
12. Irradiate cells with UV cross-linker at 365 nm and 2 J/cm²
13. Add 1 milliliter of prelysis buffer to each dish
14. Scrape cells using rubber cell lifter
15. Collect prelysis suspension in 15 milliliter tube
16. Adjust total volume of suspension to 6 milliliters with prelysis buffer
17. Add equal volume of R lysis buffer to suspension
18. Pass cell lysate through narrow-needle syringe repeatedly until clear and homogeneous
19. Incubate lysate at 4°C with gentle rotation for 1 hour
20. Dilute lysate in 20 volumes of dilution buffer and divide into 15 mL fractions in 15 mL ultrafiltration tubes (10 kDa cutoff)
21. Centrifuge tubes at 4000 × g and 4°C for ~15 minutes using swinging bucket rotator until concentrated to <1 mL
22. Add 14 mL dilution buffer to each concentrated lysate fraction
23. Repeat concentration process (centrifugation at 4000 × g, 4°C)
24. Combine fractions and concentrate to 6 mL volume using described centrifugation process
25. Add reaction mix to 6 mL of pre-cleared lysate
26. Mix lysate and reaction mixture thoroughly
27. Add 162.5 microliters of reducing reagent
28. Mix solution with reducing reagent thoroughly
29. Incubate mixture at room temperature on orbital shaker at 800 rpm for 2 hours
30. Quench reaction by adding 5 mM EDTA
31. Incubate quenched mixture on orbital shaker at room temperature for 5 minutes
32. Split reaction mixture between two 50 milliliter tubes containing four volumes of pre-chilled methanol
33. Incubate mixture at -30 degrees Celsius for 30 minutes
34. Centrifuge at 4000 × g and 4 degrees Celsius for 15 minutes
35. Discard supernatant and add 1-2 milliliters of pre-chilled methanol to pellet
36. Pipet up and down to completely suspend pellet with no visible chunks
37. Repeat centrifugation and resuspension process twice
38. Carefully remove residual methanol without disturbing pellet
39. Transfer cleaned up and reconstituted sample to prepared streptavidin agarose beads
40. Incubate sample with beads at 4°C with gentle rotation overnight
41. Centrifuge beads at 4000 × g for 5 minutes
42. Transfer supernatant to a new tube
43. Collect 20 microliters of sample for quality control
44. Wash beads with 10 mL of wash buffer A
45. Incubate beads with wash buffer A at 12 rpm and room temperature for 10 minutes
46. Centrifuge beads at 4000 × g for 5 minutes
47. Remove supernatant
48. Repeat wash and centrifugation process once more (Steps 6-9)
49. Repeat wash process using wash buffer B
50. Repeat wash process using wash buffer C
51. Add 400 microliters of prepared biotin elution buffer to 400 microliters of washed, settled beads
52. Incubate mixture on orbital shaker at 1500 rpm and room temperature for 20 minutes
53. Incubate mixture on orbital shaker with heat block at 1500 rpm and 65°C for 10 minutes
54. Centrifuge beads at 7800 × g for 1 minute and collect eluted RNP
55. Add 400 microliters of fresh biotin elution buffer to beads
56. Repeat incubation and centrifugation process to collect second eluate
57. Combine both eluates in a single 15 milliliter tube
58. Add three volumes of dilution buffer to combined RNP eluates
59. Concentrate sample to approximately 40 microliters using 0.5 mL ultrafiltration tube
60. Add 0.5 μg/μL RNase A to sample
61. Incubate sample at 37°C for 2 hours

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Obtain 4-6 week old BALB/c mice after euthanizing with sterile surgical scissors
2. Cut off fresh fibula, tibia, and femur from hind limb
3. Peel off epithelial tissue using tweezers
4. Remove soft tissue using tweezers
5. Rinse leg bones twice with sterile 10 mM PBS solution in 6-cm dish to remove blood
6. Immerse bones in 75% ethanol for 3 minutes
7. Rinse bones twice with PBS
8. Store clean bones in sterile 50 ml centrifuge tube with sterile PBS at -80°C
9. Thaw frozen bones at room temperature
10. Freeze bones at -80 degrees Celsius for one hour
11. Subject bones to more than two freeze-thaw cycles for cell lysis and tissue breakdown
12. Place bones in sterile 50 milliliter centrifuge tube
13. Fill tube with 0.5 normal HCl
14. Incubate overnight at room temperature on orbital shaker with gentle shaking
15. Decant hydrochloric acid solution completely
16. Rinse bones under running water for one hour
17. Wash bones with distilled water for 15 minutes on orbital shaker
18. Completely decant solution
19. Place demineralized bones in 50 ml centrifuge tube with 1:1 methanol-chloroform mixture
20. Wrap tube with tin foil to prevent light exposure and chloroform decomposition
21. Place tube on orbital shaker for 1 hour
22. Transfer bones with tweezers to new tube containing methanol wrapped with tin foil
23. Remove methanol completely from tube
24. Wash bones with distilled water twice for 15 minutes each on orbital shaker
25. Decant final wash water and proceed under sterile conditions
26. Rinse bones in 6 cm dish with sterile PBS for 3 minutes
27. Add 40 mL sterile 0.05% TE solution into 50 mL centrifuge tube
28. Incubate bones in TE solution for 23 hours at 37°C in CO₂ incubator
29. Discard TE solution
30. Rinse bones twice with sterile PBS supplemented with 90 µg/mL ampicillin and 90 µg/mL kanamycin for 15 minutes each on orbital shaker
31. Replenish with 40 mL sterile PBS with antibiotics after decanting final wash

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Add 500 microliters of Tween 20 to one liter of PBS
2. Mix washing solution vigorously
3. Prepare blocking solution (3% Blocker A in PBS)
4. Stir blocking solution gently
5. Sterilize blocking solution by filtration
6. Add 5 milliliters of blocking solution to 10 milliliters of PBS for assay diluent
7. Thaw monoclonal mouse anti-MeCP2 antibody on ice
8. Dispense 25 microliters of coating solution into bottom corner of each well using multichannel pipette
9. Dab 96-well plate gently on each side to ensure coating solution covers bottom of wells
10. Seal plate with adhesive foil
11. Incubate sealed plate overnight at 4°C
12. Retrieve 96-well plate from refrigerator
13. Remove foil from plate
14. Remove antibody coating solution by flicking into waste container
15. Tap plate on paper towel to remove residual solution
16. Add 125 microliters of blocking solution to each well
17. Seal plate with adhesive foil
18. Place plate on orbital microplate shaker
19. Set shaker to 800 RPM
20. Incubate for 90 minutes at room temperature
21. Thaw one vial of MeCP2 or Tat-MeCP2 protein stock solution, mouse brain lysates, and HDF lysates on ice
22. Dilute protein stock solution in clean tubes according to manuscript Table 2 specifications
23. Dilute mouse brain lysate samples using 1-20 micrograms per 25 microliters of lysis buffer
24. Dilute HDF lysate samples using 0.25-1 microgram per 25 microliters of lysis buffer
25. Remove blocking solution from wells by flicking into waste container
26. Tap plate on paper towel to remove residual solution
27. Wash plate three times with 150 microliters washing solution (add solution and immediately remove)
28. Add 25 microliters of standards and samples to 96-well plate wells
29. Seal plate and incubate for 4 hours at room temperature with 800 RPM shaking
30. Thaw unlabeled polyclonal rabbit anti-MeCP2 detection antibody on ice
31. Dilute detection antibody in assay diluent solution at 1:6,000 ratio
32. Remove standards and samples from 96-well plate by flicking into waste container
33. Tap plate on paper towel to remove residual solution
34. Wash plate three times with 150 microliters washing solution (add and immediately remove)
35. Add 25 microliters unlabeled detection antibody solution to each well using multichannel pipetter
36. Seal plate and incubate for 1 hour at room temperature with constant shaking at 800 RPM
37. Obtain specific secondary antibody from refrigerator
38. Place secondary antibody on ice
39. Dilute secondary antibody in assay diluent solution
40. Mix diluted secondary antibody gently
41. Flick 96-well plate into waste container to remove unlabeled detection antibody
42. Tap plate on paper towel
43. Wash plate three times as previously described
44. Add 25 microliters of secondary antibody to each well using multichannel pipetter
45. Seal plate
46. Incubate sealed plate for one hour at room temperature with constant shaking at 800 RPM
47. Remove free secondary antibody by flicking into waste container and tapping plate on paper towel
48. Wash plate three times as previously described
49. Add 150 microliters of 1X Tris base Gold Read Buffer with surfactant (containing tripropylamine) to each well using reverse pipetting technique
50. Place 96-well plate on microplate platform of electrochemiluminescence detection system
51. Begin capturing data immediately using built-in CCD camera to record signal counts (relative light units proportional to light intensity)

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Place one embryo in dish containing dissection media coded with silicon
2. Hold embryo with abdomen against silicon using forceps under microscope
3. Insert tip of second forceps into central canal of rostral spinal cord
4. Close forceps to slightly tear dorsal tissue
5. Repeat tearing operation towards caudal side until entire spinal cord is opened
6. Position embryo with open spinal cord against silicon
7. Detach rostrad part of spinal cord
8. Separate spinal cord from body
9. Pinch rostrad part of spinal cord
10. Pull embryo by head to extract spinal cord
11. Pull away remaining meninges and DRGs attached to spinal cord
12. Flatten spinal cord on silicon
13. Remove dorsal half of cord using scalpel by cutting along middle of each side
14. Cut middle part of spinal cord into ten pieces
15. Transfer spinal cord pieces to new tube
16. Collect spinal cord pieces at bottom of tube
17. Replace HBSS with 1 ml Ham F-10 medium
18. Add 10 µl trypsin
19. Incubate tube for 10 minutes at 37°C
20. Transfer fragments to new 15 ml tube containing 800 µl complete L-15 medium
21. Add 100 µl of 4% BSA to tube
22. Add 100 µl DNAs and titrate mixture twice
23. Let fragments settle for 2 minutes
24. Collect supernatant in fresh 15 ml tube
25. Add 2 ml of 4% BSA to bottom of supernatant tube
26. Centrifuge at 470 × g for 5 minutes
27. Remove supernatant after centrifugation
28. Resuspend cell pellet with 2 ml complete L-15 medium
29. Split cell suspension into two 15 milliliter tubes
30. Add 2 milliliters of L-15 PH medium to each tube
31. Slowly add 2 milliliters of density gradient solution to bottom of each tube to create interface
32. Centrifuge tubes at 830 x gravity for 15 minutes at room temperature
33. Aspirate 1-2 milliliters of cells from density gradient/medium interface
34. Aspirate 1-2 milliliters of cells at the density gradient solution/medium interface
35. Transfer aspirated cells to a fresh 15 milliliter tube
36. Adjust volume to ten milliliters with L-15 PH medium
37. Add two milliliters of 4% BSA cushion to bottom of tubes
38. Centrifuge at 470 x gravity for 5 minutes at room temperature
39. Remove supernatant after centrifugation
40. Resuspend pellet in 3 milliliters of complete L-15 medium
41. Repeat centrifugation at 470 x gravity for 5 minutes at room temperature
42. Remove supernatant after second centrifugation
43. Resuspend cell pellet in two milliliters of complete culture medium
44. Dilute motor neurons to 5,000-10,000 cells in 500 microliters of culture medium per well in 24-well plate
45. Remove laminae solution using P-1000 pipet tip
46. Immediately transfer diluted motor neurons in culture medium to coated plates to avoid drying
47. Re-suspend 1 microgram of DNA in 50 microliters of neuronal culture medium
48. Vortex DNA solution for 5 seconds
49. Re-suspend 1.5 microliters of magnetic beads in 50 microliters of neuronal culture medium
50. Add 50 microliters of bead solution to 50 microliters of DNA solution
51. Incubate DNA-bead mixture for 20 minutes at room temperature
52. Withdraw 100 microliters of culture medium from target well
53. Transfer 100 microliters of DNA-bead mixture to each well
54. Incubate 24-well plate for 20-30 minutes on magnetic plate at 37°C

Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Seed fibroblasts into 24-well plates at 50,000 cells per well in 1 mL medium
2. Incubate plate overnight at 37°C and 5% CO₂
3. Prepare MMC media by mixing Ficoll 70, Ficoll 400, and ascorbic acid into 10% FCS DMEM
4. Incubate media in water bath to disperse crowders
5. Sterilize media using 0.2 micrometer filter
6. Aspirate spent media from fibroblasts
7. Replace aspirated media with freshly made MMC media
8. Incubate cells at 37°C for 6 days with MMC media changes every 3 days
9. Prepare Sirius Red solution by dissolving 0.2 grams of Direct Red 80 powder in 200 milliliters of distilled deionized water with one milliliter of acetic acid
10. Aspirate MMC media from cells
11. Add 300 microliters of Sirius Red solution to each well
12. Return cells to incubator for 90 minutes
13. Gently rinse Sirius Red solution with tap water
14. Allow plate to air dry overnight
15. Add 200 microliters of 0.1 molar sodium hydroxide to each well to extract Sirius Red
16. Shake plate for 5-10 minutes
17. Transfer 100 microliters of extracted stain to transparent 96-well plate
18. Measure absorbance at 620 nm using microplate reader
19. Wash each well with 200 microliters of PBS
20. Fix cells with methanol at 4°C for 10 minutes
21. Add 3% BSA to wells
22. Incubate plate for 30 minutes at room temperature
23. Aspirate blocking solution
24. Add 200 microliters of anti-collagen one antibody to each well
25. Incubate plate for 90 minutes
26. Aspirate antibody solution
27. Wash wells three times with PBS (5 minutes per wash)
28. Add 200 microliters of Goat Anti-Rabbit-FITC Secondary Antibody and DAPI to each well
29. Cover plate with aluminum foil and incubate for 30 minutes at room temperature
30. Discard secondary antibody and DAPI
31. Repeat PBS washes
32. Visualize fluorescence under microscope
33. Wash cells twice with PBS
34. Add 40 microliters of lysis buffer into each well
35. Scrape cell layer with pipette tip
36. Transfer protein lysate into microcentrifuge tubes
37. Load 10 micrograms of protein into each well of 4-12% Bis-Tris protein gel
38. Perform SDS-PAGE electrophoresis at 200 volts for 30 minutes
39. Transfer proteins to nitrocellulose membrane using western blot at 90 volts for 90 minutes while avoiding bubble formation between gel and membrane
40. Block membrane with 10 milliliters of blocking buffer
41. Incubate membrane with primary antibodies at 4°C overnight
42. Wash membrane five times with 0.1% TBS Tween 20 for 5 minutes per wash
43. Incubate membrane with species-appropriate secondary antibody for 1 hour at room temperature
44. Repeat washing procedure with 0.1% TBS Tween 20
45. Visualize fluorescence using imaging system
46. Measure RNA concentration using micro volume spectrophotometer
47. Prepare cDNA synthesis kit
48. Perform first strand DNA synthesis
49. Mix cDNA with 10 microliters of SYBR Green Supermix
50. Transfer mixture to custom 96-well plate pre-coated with oligonucleotide primers
51. Adjust total volume to 20 microliters per well with water
52. Run RT-PCR according to manuscript directions

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Place eight-day-old specific pathogen-free embryonated chicken eggs in rotating egg tray with stamped ends facing upward
2. Set rotating tray inside egg incubator set to 36°C with 50% humidity
3. Incubate eggs for 48 hours
4. Place incubated eggs in egg rack with stamped ends up
5. Transfer egg rack to laminar flow hood
6. Turn off room and hood lights
7. Hold stamped end of egg lightly to egg candler to expose vasculature of chorioallantoic membrane (CAM) and air sack
8. Make a mark between two major blood vessels with a pencil
9. Make a hole at egg tip above air sack at stamped end using sterile push pin (3mm deep)
10. Make a second hole at pencil mark using sterile push pin (3mm deep)
11. Squeeze air from safety bulb
12. Press safety bulb firmly against hole above air sack
13. Slowly release bulb to apply suction at pencil mark hole
14. Confirm air sack displacement to pencil mark using candler
15. Make both holes deeper if air sack displacement unsuccessful
16. Deepen both holes slightly
17. Reapply suction using safety bulb
18. Hold egg with one hand and position rotary tool with 1.5-1.6 inch cutoff wheel attachment
19. Make two transverse cuts (2 cm length, 1 cm apart) through shell without touching CAM
20. Make longitudinal cuts at ends of transverse cuts by lightly touching cutoff wheel to shell
21. Slide sterile forceps under and parallel to shell piece
22. Grab shell piece with forceps and cleanly remove it
23. Place basement membrane protein mixture (e.g., matrigel) on ice to prevent polymerization
24. Use 4-5 ml of 2 mM EDTA solution per flask to detach tumor cells
25. Place flask in tissue culture incubator for 5 minutes
26. Resuspend detached cells in medium
27. Count cell number
28. Transfer volume required for 500,000-2 million cells to new tube
29. Centrifuge tube at 500 × g for 5 minutes
30. Discard supernatant
31. Resuspend cell pellet in 40 μl PBS containing calcium
32. Peel tape off the egg
33. Drop sterile 1 mm silicone ring from cryogenic vial through opening onto CAM
34. Pipette 60 microliter solution of cells, PBS plus plus, and basement membrane mixture into center of silicone ring
35. Retape the egg window
36. Carefully move egg tray back to incubator
37. Allow tumors to grow for 5-7 days
38. Place underpad and biohazard bag in hood
39. Prepare 35 mm × 10 mm plates containing 1 ml PBS plus plus and paraformaldehyde containers
40. Insert pointed end of sterile dissection scissors into egg at hole near stamped end and cut around length of entire egg
41. Extract tumor from CAM using dissection scissors and forceps
42. Place tumor in 35 mm × 10 mm plate
43. Measure and weigh tumor
44. Fix tumor in paraformaldehyde for paraffin embedding
45. Mince harvested tumor in plate using dissection scissors
46. Transfer minced tumor to 15 mL centrifuge tube
47. Remove PBS plus plus from tube by pipetting
48. Add 2 mL of 1 mg/mL collagenase solution in PBS plus plus
49. Mix solution by inverting tube several times
50. Incubate tube at 37°C for 30 minutes to several hours
51. Add 5 mL medium to tube and mix by pipetting 20-40 times
52. Transfer supernatant to fresh tube after sedimentation
53. Centrifuge at 500 × g for 5 minutes and discard supernatant

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Select, trim, and label the panicle
2. Emasculate trimmed panicle by dipping into 48°C water bath
3. Pollinate emasculated panicle using pollen-shedding panicle
4. Harvest seeds two weeks after cross-pollination
5. Remove one cell for watering
6. Fill tray with four by nine cells using Metro Mix 360 soil
7. Lightly mist the cells
8. Sow seeds on soil surface (one seed per cell)
9. Cover seeds with 0.5 cm thick soil layer
10. Mist soil surface to dampen
11. Water flats from bottom (maintain 1-2 times/day; avoid overwatering)
12. Set growth chamber daytime temperature to 31°C
13. Set growth chamber nighttime temperature to 22°C
14. Configure pre-dawn treatment at 15°C (8:30 AM to 9:00 AM)
15. Set 12-hour light/dark schedule
16. Set relative humidity to 50%
17. Select primary panicle with one to five flowers in bloom
18. Cut off tip of panicle
19. Remove flowers from lower section of proximal middle and base sections using fine forceps
20. Cut off tip and trim flowers from base of panicle (for panicles with one-tenth to one-fifth flowers in bloom)
21. Retain proximal middle section and cut off distal middle section (for panicles with one-fourth to one-half flowers in bloom)
22. Trim bristles with surgical scissors
23. Retain uppermost mature floret for each spikelet
24. Retain uppermost mature floret for each spikelet
25. Remove all bloomed flowers
26. Remove all immature flowers
27. Leave approximately 20-30 evenly distributed flowers per panicle
28. Mark one side of each flower with red permanent marker
29. Record number of florets on panicle
30. Flag panicle with emasculation date, duration, and temperature
31. Remove all tillers of each plant
32. Set water bath to 48°C for heat treatment
33. Dip trimmed panicles (3-4 at a time) in preheated water bath for 3-6 minutes while avoiding stem breakage and preventing flag leaf contact with water
34. Remove panicles from water bath
35. Gently shake excess water from panicles
36. Cover emasculated panicle with customized micro-perforated bread bag
37. Secure bag with twist tie
38. Place plants back in growth chamber until following day
39. Move emasculated female parents and non-emasculated male parents from growth chamber to lab
40. Mark newly bloomed or blooming flowers with red permanent marker
41. Record total number of bloomed flowers per panicle using red permanent marker
42. Record date of pollination and male parent name on plant tag
43. Detach one male panicle for pollination
44. Gently rub detached male panicle against emasculated panicle
45. Discard used male panicle after pollination
46. Repeat panicle rubbing until each emasculated panicle is pollinated by 2-3 male panicles
47. Select male flower with blooming yellowish-white anthers using forceps
48. Transfer pollen by physically touching anthers to stigma of female parent flower
49. Dip forceps in 95% ethanol between different male parents
50. Wipe forceps with Kim wipes after ethanol dip to prevent pollen carryover
51. Cover panicle of female parent with customized micro perforated bread bag
52. Secure bread bag at base using twist tie
53. Harvest seeds 14-16 days after pollination
54. Place plant tag in same envelope as harvested seeds
55. Discard seeds without red markings
56. Dry seeds at 30°C for two days
57. Store dried seeds in lab or seed chamber
58. Remove physes when ready for analysis if necessary

52. Secure bread bag at base using twist tie
53. Harvest seeds 14-16 days after pollination
54. Place plant tag in same envelope as harvested seeds
55. Discard seeds without red markings
56. Dry seeds at 30°C for two days
57. Store dried seeds in lab or seed chamber
58. Remove physes when ready for analysis if necessary

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Cut 3 cm length of round glass tube (ID: 580 μm, OD: 1 mm) using tungsten carbide scriber
2. Cut 2 cm length of round glass tube (ID: 200 μm, OD: 330 μm) using tungsten carbide scriber
3. Place 1 ml of ODTS in 1.5 ml centrifuge tube
4. Immerse one end of middle tube in ODTS for 10 seconds
5. Flush tube with nitrogen gas from untreated end until dry
6. Cut 0.5 mm × 0.5 mm notch on edge of plastic Luer hub of 20 gauge half-inch blunt needle
7. Cut 0.5 mm × 0.5 mm notch on hub of second needle
8. Cut 1 mm × 1 mm notch directly across from first notch on hub of second needle
9. Place middle tube lengthwise on standard glass microscope slide with hydrophobic ODTS-coated end extending ~1 cm past narrow end of slide
10. Insert inner tube into untreated end of middle tube leaving ~1 cm of inner tube outside middle tube
11. Apply epoxy to fix tubes in place along center line of slide
12. Adjust position of inner tube to be approximately concentric to middle tube
13. Place singly-notched needle over end of inner tube aligned with notch
14. Fix singly-notched needle with epoxy to form low-viscosity oil inlet
15. Fix double-notched needle over junction between inner and middle tubes
16. Seal needle hubs with epoxy around tubes and to glass substrate
17. Fit middle tube into big notch and inner tube into small notch of high-viscosity inlet needle
18. Fit 20 mm polyethylene tubing (ID 0.86 mm) over hydrophobic end of middle tube
19. Draw 0.8 milliliters of glycerol tinted with blue dye into a 1 milliliter syringe
20. Draw 0.8 milliliters of light paraffin oil into a second 1 milliliter syringe
21. Connect glycerol syringe to high-viscosity aqueous solution inlet via 0.86 mm ID polyethylene tubing
22. Connect liquid paraffin syringe to low-viscosity oil inlet
23. Mount both syringes on syringe pumps
24. Mount device vertically over 35 mm Petri dish using two-finger clamp and lab stand
25. Adjust device position to position outlet tubing 2 mm above dish bottom
26. Pour liquid paraffin into Petri dish to immerse device outlet
27. Add equivalent quantity of liquid paraffin to second 35 mm Petri dish
28. Set glycerol syringe pump flow rate to 2 microliters per minute
29. Set liquid paraffin syringe pump flow rate to 6 microliters per minute
30. Run both pumps to initiate glycerol droplet generation
31. Monitor droplet generation using camera
32. Wait approximately one minute for glycerol and liquid paraffin flows to stabilize
33. Remove initial Petri dish from device outlet
34. Place second liquid paraffin-filled dish under device outlet

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Place adult male mouse (≥8 weeks old) in supine position
2. Disinfect ventral abdomen with 70% ethanol
3. Make v-shaped incision in abdominal pelvic region using sterile scissors
4. Pull epididymal fat pad using sterile forceps to locate testes
5. Dissect testes using scissors
6. Place harvested testes in sterile 100-mm Petri dish containing PBS
7. Cut slit in tunica albuginea using fine-tipped scissors
8. Tear tunica albuginea open using forceps
9. Force seminiferous tubules out of tunica albuginea
10. Transfer decapsulated seminiferous tubules to new Petri dish
11. Add sterile PBS to cover bottom of Petri dish
12. Gently separate tubules using forceps without tissue damage
13. Tape dish onto dissecting microscope stage
14. Turn on transillumination
15. Move tubule bundles with fine forceps to observe light absorption and scatter patterns
16. Lift tubule of interest using hooked-tip forceps
17. Cut tubule segment using microdissection scissors
18. Collect tubule segment using pipette in 10 microliters of PBS
19. Place tubule segment onto microscope slide
20. Press 20x20 mm cover glass onto tubule while avoiding excessive cell squashing
21. Place prepared slide onto phase-contrast microscope stage
22. Place filter paper onto edge of cover glass
23. Use 40x microscope objective to verify cell stages
24. Dip slide into liquid nitrogen for 10 seconds
25. Use scalpel to flip cover glass off cells
26. Place slide in 90% ethanol for 2-5 minutes
27. Air dry slide
28. Store slide at room temperature for up to several days
29. Transfer segments of interest into 15-mL conical tube containing ice-cold PBS using pipette
30. Remove PBS after allowing segments to sediment
31. Add 10 mL fresh ice-cold PBS
32. Mix sample by inversion
33. Allow tubules to settle for several minutes
34. Remove supernatant after settlement
35. Fix tubules in 5 milliliters of paraformaldehyde for 5 hours at 20-30 rpm at 4°C
36. Allow tubules to settle for a few minutes
37. Wash sample three times in fresh PBS at 4°C for 10 minutes per wash with rotation
38. Transfer 10 to 20 fixed tubule segments using a 1-milliliter pipette
39. Replace supernatant with 1 milliliter of blocking solution (2% BSA, 10% FBS in PBSX)
40. Incubate for one hour at room temperature with 20-30 revolutions per minute rotation
41. Wash samples with 1 milliliter of PBSX
42. Dilute primary antibody in 1% BSA in PBSX
43. Incubate diluted primary antibody at room temperature at 20-30 RPM for two hours
44. Wash tubules three times in PBSX
45. Label samples with 250 microliters of appropriate secondary antibodies
46. Incubate samples for one hour at room temperature protected from light with 20-30 rpm agitation
47. Wash tubules three times
48. Decant tubules onto microscope slide in small volume of supernatant
49. Use gel-loading tips to arrange tubules into linear strips
50. Drain excess buffer
51. Add 1-2 drops of mounting medium
52. Mount slide with coverslip

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Use fertilized quail eggs (fresh or stored at 10-15°C for ≤5 days)
2. Select only clean and undamaged eggs
3. Incubate eggs in forced-draught incubator at 50-60% humidity, 37.5°C for ~54 hours (horizontal position, rotation off)
4. Disinfect egg surface with 70% ethanol without rotating egg
5. Open eggshell using sterile surgical scissors in laminar flow cabinet while wearing gloves
6. Transfer egg content into 6-well culture plate
7. Disinfect scissors with 70% ethanol after processing each egg
8. Add approximately 5 milliliters of sterile water to gaps in 6-well plate to maintain humidity
9. Aspirate improperly-tipped embryos or unfertilized eggs using vacuum aspirator
10. Place embryos in incubator at 37°C with 80-90% humidity
11. Place sterilized silicone ring on CAM surface along small capillaries while avoiding major blood vessels
12. Apply 30 microliters of hypericin solution to silicone ring under sterile conditions
13. Keep embryos in incubator at 37 degrees Celsius in 80-90% humidity
14. Illuminate CAM with violet excitation light
15. Record hypericin and CAM tissue fluorescence with digital camera at timed intervals
16. Record CAM in white light before hypericin administration (if required)
17. Record CAM in white light before tissue fixation (if required)
18. Place CAM under optical fiber to cover entire silicone ring area with laser beam
19. Perform in vivo irradiation with 405nm laser at 285 mW/cm² fluence rate
20. Record CAM images using white light and/or fluorescent light before and after treatment
21. Fix CAM tissue in cultivation plate with 4% paraformaldehyde in PBS
22. Remove paraformaldehyde solution
23. Cut out target tissue from CAM within silicone ring
24. Wash excised CAM tissue in water for 10 minutes
25. Dehydrate tissue in 70% ethanol for 3 minutes
26. Stain tissue in eosin solution for 2 minutes
27. Dehydrate tissue in 96% ethanol for 5 minutes
28. Dehydrate tissue in 100% ethanol for 5 minutes
29. Treat tissue with xylene for 10 minutes (first treatment)
30. Treat tissue with xylene for 10 minutes (second treatment)
31. Incubate samples twice in xylene for 10 minutes in a new Petri dish
32. Transfer samples to dissolved paraffin in Petri dishes using spatula or thin brush
33. Place tissue in histological mold after 24 hours
34. Fill histological mold with paraffin embedding medium
35. Allow paraffin to solidify in refrigerator
36. Cut solidified CAM from embedding medium
37. Turn sample in tray by 90 degrees
38. Refill tray with embedding medium
39. Allow embedding medium to solidify
40. Prepare 5-10 micrometer sections using microtome
41. Mount native or 4% paraformaldehyde fixed CAM on glass slide
42. Fill embedding mold halfway with OCT medium
43. Freeze mold in liquid nitrogen or dry ice/ethanol mixture
44. Transfer CAM from glass slide onto frozen OCT medium
45. Place CAM in mold, cover with OCT medium, and freeze
46. Overflow CAM with prewarmed fixation solution (4% paraformaldehyde and 2% glutaraldehyde in PBS)
47. Remove fixation solution after 48 hours
48. Carefully separate CAM from embryo using micro scissors and fine brush
49. Wash separated CAM in PBS
50. Mount washed CAM on glass slide
51. Let mounted CAM slowly dry
52. Photograph slide using digital camera with transilluminator as homogenous white light source

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Make Y incision to peritoneal cavity using dissecting scissors and forceps
2. Excise uterus containing E9.5 embryo
3. Dissect embryos in complete DMEM growth medium
4. Remove yolk sac, placenta, and surrounding membranes using forceps
5. Rinse isolated embryos in HBSS to remove residual amniotic tissue and blood
6. Prepare fixative by adding paraformaldehyde to HBSS
7. Adjust solution to 4% paraformaldehyde working concentration
8. Add 1 milliliter of fixative solution to each well of 24-well plate
9. Place embryos into individual wells
10. Incubate embryos for 45 minutes with gentle agitation on rocker
11. Remove fixative after incubation
12. Remove fixative from embryos
13. Wash embryos thrice for 30 minutes in PBS with calcium, magnesium, and 0.1% Triton
14. Incubate embryos in blocking solution with gentle agitation for one hour
15. Incubate embryos in blocking solution with gentle agitation for one hour (second incubation)
16. Rinse embryos using fresh blocking solution
17. Prepare primary antibody solution by diluting antibodies in supplemented PBS
18. Remove blocking solution from wells
19. Add 1 mL of primary antibody solution to each well
20. Incubate plate at 4°C with gentle rotation for three days
21. Wash embryos with supplemented PBS five times for one hour on rocker at 20 RPM
22. Add 1 milliliter of secondary antibody solution to each well
23. Incubate plate with gentle rocking at 4°C in the dark for three days
24. Remove secondary antibody solution
25. Wash embryos three times for 30 minutes each in supplemented PBS
26. Prepare 4% (w/v) low melting point agarose solution in PBS with calcium and magnesium
27. Place 12-well plate in 55°C bead bath
28. Add 3 ml of agarose solution to each well
29. Transfer plate to bench top
30. Transfer embryos into individual wells using perforated spoon
31. Use pipette tips to gently embed and orient embryo centered in solution
32. Place plate at -20°C for 10 minutes for solidification
33. Remove entire agarose block from well using scalpel
34. Cut rectangular block around embryo leaving 0.3 cm on each side and extra length at caudal end
35. Apply strip of tape to vibratome specimen holder
36. Orient embryo upright in upper section of block
37. Super glue agarose block to tape for anterior-to-posterior axial sectioning
38. Fill vibratome chamber with cold HBSS
39. Fully immerse sample in HBSS
40. Surround vibratome chamber with ice
41. Set parameters on vibratome
42. Perform serial axial sectioning of embryo
43. Transfer individual sections to dish filled with HBSS using forceps

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Wash cells with 10 milliliters of PBS
2. Centrifuge sample at 1,450 × g for 5 minutes at 4°C
3. Discard supernatant
4. Lyse cells using 1 ml ice-cold Lysis Buffer A per 10^6 cells via gentle pipetting
5. Sonicate sample in 1.5-ml tube at 25% amplification for 10 seconds
6. Centrifuge sample at 20,400 × g for 15 minutes at 4°C
7. Collect supernatant
8. Transfer 1 ml supernatant into 1.5-ml microcentrifuge tubes
9. Add 40 μl prewashed Protein A-agarose beads per 1 ml lysate
10. Mix by inverting tube several times
11. Rotate sample for 30 minutes at 4°C
12. Centrifuge sample at 13,000 × g
13. Recover supernatant
14. Add 40 μl prewashed Protein A-agarose beads to pre-cleared lysate
15. Add 10 μg anti-human TERT antibody to pre-cleared lysate
16. Mix sample by inverting tube several times
17. Rotate sample at 4°C overnight
18. Centrifuge sample at 3,300 × g for 30 seconds at 4°C
19. Remove supernatant
20. Add 1 ml Wash Buffer One to beads
21. Mix by inverting tube
22. Rotate sample for 5 minutes at 4°C
23. Centrifuge sample at 3,300 × g for 30 seconds at 4°C
24. Remove supernatant
25. Repeat wash procedure (steps 3-7) three additional times
26. Resuspend beads in 60 microliters of micrococcal nuclease reaction mixture by gentle pipetting
27. Shake sample gently on turntable shaker in Peltier incubator at 25°C for 15 minutes
28. Centrifuge sample at 3,300 × g for 30 seconds at 4°C
29. Remove supernatant
30. Wash beads twice with AGC solution containing 3 mM EGTA
31. Wash beads once with Wash Buffer Two
32. Add six microliters of P-32 radiolabeled uridine triphosphate to reaction mixture
33. Mix reaction mixture well by pipetting
34. Add one microliter of template RNA to mixture
35. Resuspend beads with 20 microliters of RdRP reaction mixture
36. Shake sample gently on turntable shaker in Peltier incubator at 32°C for two hours
37. Add 5.4 microliters of 20 mg/mL Proteinase K to reaction mixture
38. Add 45.4 microliters of 2X Proteinase K Buffer to reaction mixture
39. Mix reaction mixture by pipetting
40. Incubate sample in Peltier-type incubator at 37°C for 30 minutes
41. Add 109.2 microliters of RNase-free water to sample
42. Add 200 microliters of acid phenol-chloroform to sample
43. Mix sample by vortex
44. Centrifuge sample at 21,100 × g for 5 minutes at room temperature
45. Transfer aqueous phase to new tube
46. Add 20 microliters of three molar sodium acetate (pH 5.2) to aqueous phase
47. Add four microliters of precipitation carrier to aqueous phase
48. Add 250 microliters of ethanol to aqueous phase
49. Shake tube vigorously to mix contents
50. Centrifuge sample at 21,100 × g for 20 minutes at 4°C
51. Discard supernatant after centrifugation
52. Add 300 microliters of cold 70% (v/v) ethanol (stored at -20°C) to pellet
53. Centrifuge sample at 21,100 × g for 15 minutes at 4°C
54. Discard supernatant after centrifugation
55. Air-dry pellet after supernatant removal
56. Resuspend pellet in 20 microliters of RNase-free water
57. Prepare RNase reaction mixture in new tube
58. Add 180 microliters of RNase reaction mixture to sample
59. Incubate tube for 2 hours at 37°C
60. Add 2.3 microliters of 10% SDS to sample
61. Incubate for 15 minutes at 37°C
62. Add 2 microliters of 20 mg/ml Proteinase K to sample
63. Incubate for 15 minutes at 37°C
