question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Retrieve one vial of papain powder from 4°C storage
2. Incubate papain powder vial at room temperature
3. Set aside Complete Schneider's Medium (CSM) in Eppendorf tube at room temperature
4. Add room temperature CSM to papain vial through cap using syringe to achieve 100 units/mL concentration
5. Invert vial to capture powder from cap
6. Pipette vial contents up and down
7. Bring water to 37°C in beaker using water bath
8. Place vial in 37°C water bath for 30 minutes, ensuring it doesn't float
9. Retrieve proteolytic enzyme blend aliquot from freezer
10. Incubate enzyme blend at room temperature
11. Incubate activated papain at room temperature
12. Dissect staged pupal brains in cold CSM using clean forceps
13. Dissect maximum number of brains within 10 minutes
14. Pool dissected brains in fresh drop of cold CSM
15. Cut off optic lobes by pinching at junction between optic lobe and central brain
16. Add dissected optic lobes to microtube containing 500 microliters of 1x RS solution (experimental tissue)
17. Add dissected optic lobes to separate microtube containing 500 microliters of 1x RS solution (negative control tissue)
18. Keep microtubes on ice
19. Dissect maximum number of optic lobes within one hour
20. Pool all dissected optic lobes into single microtube to minimize tissue loss during transfers
21. Remove 1x RS solution from microtube containing dissected optic lobes using P200 pipette
22. Leave sufficient solution to cover optic lobes
23. Add 500 microliters of 1x RS to side of microtube
24. Allow optic lobes to settle at bottom of microtube
25. Aspirate 200 microliters of solution from microtube
26. Expel solution mixture and observe lobe movement
27. Allow optic lobes to resettle
28. Aliquot 600 microliters of activated room temperature papain into a microtube
29. Add 4.2 microliters of room temperature proteolytic enzyme blend to 600 microliters of papain solution to achieve 0.18 WU per milliliter final concentration
30. Mix solution by pipetting up and down
31. Remove 1x RS from each sample
32. Add 300 microliters of dissociation solution to optic lobes
33. Incubate samples at 25°C and 1,000 RPM for 15 minutes in microtube ThermoMixer
34. Stop ThermoMixer at 5-minute incubation time point
35. Allow lobes to sink to bottom of microtube
36. Pipette up 200 microliters of solution and mix
37. Wait for lobes to settle after 15-minute incubation
38. Remove dissociation solution from lobes without disturbing tissue
39. Add 500 microliters of 1x RS without disturbing lobes
40. Pipette up 200 microliters of solution and gently expel
41. Allow lobes to sink to bottom and repeat solution expulsion
42. Wash sample with 500 microliters of CSM (first wash)
43. Wash sample with 500 microliters of CSM (second wash)
44. Carefully remove CSM
45. Add 200 microliters of CSM to tube
46. Disrupt tissue by pipetting up and down with P200 pipette without foaming
47. Filter cell suspension through 30 micrometer mesh cap into 5 milliliter fax tube on ice using P200 pipette
48. Ensure entire suspension passes through mesh
49. Add 500 microliters of CSM to rinse dissociation microtube
50. Filter rinse solution into fax tube
51. Carefully remove cap of fax tube
52. Collect solution underneath mesh filter with P200 pipette
53. Add collected solution to suspension in fax tube

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Assemble 12-well plate in biological safety cabinet
2. Unpack plate and inserts using sterilized forceps
3. Grab inserts at base with forceps and place into wells
4. Coat porous membrane of each insert with 90 microliters of 10 μg/μL collagen IV/fibronectin mixture
5. Incubate plate at 37°C for 24 hours in cell culture incubator
6. Pipette 1 mL of PBS into each collagen-coated insert
7. Aspirate PBS from inserts using vacuum pump
8. Pipette prewarmed DMEM F12 medium into upper and lower chambers
9. Incubate plate at 37°C and 5% CO₂
10. Aspirate medium from cell culture flask
11. Wash cells with 10 milliliters of PBS
12. Cover cells completely with 5 milliliters of trypsin EDTA
13. Incubate flask for 3-5 minutes at 37°C
14. Transfer 5 milliliters of cells into 15 milliliter tube
15. Add 5 milliliters of FCS-containing medium to stop enzymatic reaction
16. Centrifuge suspension for 3 minutes at 210 × g
17. Remove supernatant
18. Resuspend cell pellet in 5 mL medium
19. Add 10 μL cell suspension to 10 μL 0.4% trypan blue stain
20. Add 10 μL mixture to counting chamber
21. Insert counting chamber into cell counter and start program with focus verification
22. Seed 200,000 cells per upper chamber in 12-well plate
23. Incubate plate at 37°C for 14 days with media changes in upper/lower chambers every 2-3 days
24. Inoculate one colony of E. coli strain GM2163 into three milliliters of LB medium
25. Culture inoculated cells at 37 degrees Celsius for 24 hours in an incubation shaker
26. Dilute compound of interest to final concentration in DMEM F12 medium
27. Add diluted compound mixture into upper and lower chambers of 12-well plate
28. Incubate plate in cell culture incubator
29. Exchange complete medium with antibiotic-free medium after incubation
30. Measure optical density at 600 nm of overnight bacterial culture
31. Dilute culture to OD600 0.5 in 50 milliliter Falcon tube
32. Work in biological safety cabinet
33. Add 450 microliters of bacterial solution to each upper chamber of 12-well plate
34. Incubate plate for six hours at 37 degrees Celsius
35. Remove insert with forceps
36. Sample 50 microliters of medium from each lower chamber
37. Drop each sample onto separate agar plate
38. Streak solution on plates using cell spreader
39. Incubate plates at 37 degrees Celsius for 24 hours
40. Count colonies on plates
41. Treat cells with 0.5 mM and 0.15 mM glyoxal solution for 1 hour
42. Perform immunoblotting with anti-AGE antibodies to detect glycation
43. Count bacterial colonies and normalize counts to untreated control
44. Cultivate THBMECs in normal glucose medium and high glucose medium

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Culture HCT116 cell line in DMEM cell culture medium
2. Incubate cultures until six to twelve confluent plates are obtained
3. Wash cells gently with 10 milliliters of PBS to remove cell culture medium
4. Add three milliliters of ice-cold PBS supplemented with five-millimolar EDTA
5. Incubate plates for five minutes at room temperature
6. Detach cells from plates using a cell scraper
7. Collect cells in 50-milliliter tube placed on ice
8. Centrifuge at 250 G for 10 minutes
9. Measure volume of harvested cells using tube's volumetric scale
10. Resuspend harvested cell pellet in equal volume of lysis buffer
11. Lyse cells by sonication
12. Spin sample at 100,000 G for one hour at 4°C in ultracentrifuge rotor
13. Remove cleared lysate using syringe without disturbing pellet or floating layer
14. Add 2 microliters of cleared lysate into tube
15. Add 18 microliters of water to tube
16. Add 5 microliters of 5X SDS sample buffer to tube
17. Add 1 mM GTP and 20 µM paclitaxel to collected supernatant
18. Incubate mixture at 37°C for 30 minutes
19. Prepare cushion buffer by adding 600 µL glycerol to 400 µL lysis buffer
20. Supplement cushion buffer with 20 µM paclitaxel
21. Prewarm cushion buffer to 37°C
22. Add 800 microliters of cushion buffer to an ultracentrifuge tube
23. Pipette GTP-paclitaxel-supplemented lysate carefully on top of cushion buffer
24. Place tube in ultracentrifuge rotor
25. Centrifuge at 100,000 G at 30°C for 30 minutes
26. Mark outward-facing edge of tube to identify microtubule pellet location
27. Add lysis buffer next to pellet
28. Rotate tube several times to remove glycerol from pellet and tube walls
29. Aspirate and repeat glycerol washing step three times
30. Resuspend washed pellet gently using cut pipette tip in 50 microliters prewarmed resuspension buffer
31. Place tube at 37 degrees Celsius
32. Install blotting paper in plunge freezer device
33. Set plunge freezer temperature to 30 degrees Celsius
34. Set plunge freezer humidification to 100%
35. Allow device to equilibrate for 30 minutes
36. Configure plunge freezer for two applications
37. Set first application parameters: force=10, blot time=2 seconds, wait time=0 seconds
38. Place EM grids with carbon side up into metal glow discharger vehicle
39. Glow discharge EM grids at 30 milliamperes for 60 seconds
40. Cool polystyrene container assembly with liquid nitrogen
41. Prepare liquid ethane by condensing ethane gas into cold metal cup
42. Grab glow discharged grid with plunge tweezers
43. Click grid into plunge freezer
44. Position polystyrene container with liquid ethane in plunge freezer
45. Run through prepared program
46. Apply 3.5 microliters of microtubule solution to grid
47. Let plunge freezer blot grid
48. Apply 3.5 microliters of diluted protein
49. Let plunger blot and plunge freeze grid in liquid ethane

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Weigh 1000 grams of cement and 350 grams of water using electronic balance
2. Wipe five liter mixing pot with wet towel to moisten
3. Add weighed water and cement to mixing pot
4. Place pot on mixer and raise to stirring position
5. Mix at 65 rpm for 90 seconds
6. Let mixture sit still for 30 seconds
7. Scrape paste from inner wall of pot
8. Mix at 130 rpm for 60 seconds
9. Remove pot from mixer
10. Put ceramic particles into cement paste
11. Thoroughly mix ceramic particles with cement paste by hand
12. Half-fill mold with fresh cement paste
13. Place ceramic particle on top of paste in mold
14. Fill remainder of mold with cement paste
15. Wipe off excess cement paste with scraper knife
16. Vibrate mold on vibrating table for one minute
17. Seal mold surface with cling film
18. Cure specimen in curing room for 24 hours
19. Remove specimen from mold
20. Cure specimen for 28 days under same environmental conditions
21. Cut specimen through geometric center of ceramic particle
22. Immerse both specimen parts in isopropyl alcohol for 3 days to remove unbound water and terminate hydration
23. Replace isopropyl alcohol solution every 24 hours during immersion period
24. Dry both specimen parts in vacuum drying oven for 7 days at 40°C
25. Smear demolding paste on inner surface of two cylindrical plastic molds using finger
26. Place sample piece into each mold with examination surface facing downward
27. Weigh 50 grams of low-viscosity epoxy resin in paper cup
28. Add five grams of hardener to epoxy resin
29. Manually stir epoxy-hardener mixture for two minutes
30. Place mold and paper cup into cold mounting machine
31. Start vacuum on machine
32. Pour epoxy resin into mold until merged with samples
33. Maintain mold in machine for 24 hours to harden epoxy resin
34. Remove bottom of each mold
35. Squeeze out sample from mold
36. Store sample in vacuum drying oven
37. Grind sample with silicon carbide paper and alcohol using automatic polishing machine
38. Attach flannelette to turn table of polishing machine
39. Polish sample with diamond paste (3, 1, 0.25 micrometers) at 150 rpm
40. Remove debris using ultrasonic cleaner with alcohol
41. Place sample in plastic box with examination surface facing up
42. Store plastic boxes in vacuum dry oven
43. Spray thin layer of gold foil on examination surface using automatic sputter coater in vacuum environment
44. Place adhesive tape strip on sample side to connect testing surface and opposite surface
45. Position sample on test bench with testing surface facing upward
46. Move sample to focus on region one
47. Vacuum SEM chamber
48. Switch to backscattered electron mode
49. Set magnification to 1000X
50. Adjust brightness and contrast
51. Move SEM lens along aggregate boundary direction
52. Position SEM lens at new location
53. Capture SEM image
54. Acquire minimum 15 images for statistical analysis
55. Repeat SEM imaging process on region two
56. Repeat SEM imaging process on region three
57. Apply ImageJ pretreatment with best fit filter
58. Apply 3x3 median filter three times to reduce noise
59. Manually capture ceramic particle boundaries
60. Manually capture the boundary of the ceramic particle
61. Cut the ceramic particle boundary region from the original image
62. Set different threshold values to roughly determine the upper threshold of pore phases
63. Segment the image and compare with the original image for threshold evaluation
64. Obtain the gray-scale distribution of the remaining image region
65. Choose two approximately linear parts of the curve above the predetermined upper threshold
66. Fit the two linear curve parts with linear regression
67. Set the intersection point of fitted curves as the exact upper threshold value
68. Use the determined threshold value to perform image segmentation
69. Compare the binary segmented image with the original gray-scale image for final threshold validation
70. Convert the gray-scale image to a binary image with white representing pore phase and black representing solid phase

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Position one peristaltic feeder tube per tissue bath around rollers of peristaltic pump head
2. Secure tubes with retaining stops by tightening compression cams
3. Lock keys around tubes
4. Place free ends of peristaltic feeder tubes into one liter container of physiological saline (PSS)
5. Start pump to allow PSS to continually perfuse into tissue baths
6. Place pregnant human myometrium biopsy sample into clear silastic dissection dish filled with PSS
7. Place dissection dish under microscope
8. Orient biopsy to visualize serosa and decidua edges
9. Secure tissue to dish with dissection pins
10. Identify regions of myometrium free of scar tissue, serosa, decidua, and adherent fetal membranes using 10X objective
11. Blunt dissect sample with large scissors until two adjacent tissue layers separate, revealing muscle planes
12. Secure each corner of tissue pieces with additional pins
13. Inspect muscle sheets for parallel-running fibers
14. Harvest tissue strips along longitudinal axis using dissection scissors while grasping initial cut edge and aligning with muscle fiber direction
15. Pin both ends of each strip to straighten and secure specimens to dish while avoiding tissue overstretch
16. Attach aluminum tissue clips to each strip end
17. Trim excess tissue to achieve approximately 5 mm length between clips
18. Transfer tissue strips to experimental tissue baths
19. Attach one end of each strip to force transducer and other end to fixed hook in tissue chamber
20. Verify tissue strips are positioned at base of each hook in bath
21. Open recording software
22. Right-click on y-axis and configure scale to maximum 10, minimum 0
23. Repeat scale configuration for each recording channel
24. Click record to initiate live recording
25. Turn micromanipulators to manually stretch tissue until tension reaches 0.2 grams
26. Reaffirm tissue strips are positioned at base of each hook
27. Allow tissues to equilibrate for approximately two hours until spontaneous contractions arise
28. Place feeder tubing for first tissue bath into glass laboratory bottle containing lowest concentration of experimental reagent diluted in PSS
29. Record time of reagent application in recording software and laboratory notebook
30. Sequentially place feeder tubing into glass laboratory bottles containing next higher concentrations in series
31. Return feeder tubes to bottle of PSS to wash out system
32. Click stop to end live recording

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Ask participant to sit comfortably in chair
2. Clean participant's skin by rubbing preparation paste in hand regions for electrode placement
3. Remove excess preparation paste with clean gauze pad
4. Attach EMG surface electrodes in belly-tendon montage on hand muscle of interest
5. Connect ground electrode
6. Turn on magnetic stimulator
7. Charge capacitor by pressing corresponding charge button
8. Place figure-of-eight TMS coil on participant's scalp over motor cortex region
9. Hold TMS coil at 45-50° angle relative to inter-hemispheric fissure with handle oriented backwards
10. Discharge magnetic stimulator using trigger button, foot switch, or software program after charging
11. Set stimulation intensity to low level
12. Monitor EMG amplifier for motor evoked potentials (MEPs)
13. Increase stimulation intensity if no MEP is detected
14. Increase stimulation intensity until MEP is clearly present
15. Repeat stimulation by pressing trigger button
16. Move coil radially in 1cm steps around initial site to find spot with largest MEP response
17. Apply single TMS pulses during coil movement
18. Move coil again to secure hotspot position
19. Mark hotspot position and coil orientation with non-permanent skin marker
20. Connect cables to rubber electrodes
21. Place electrodes inside sponge bags
22. Ensure electrode size and sponge bag size match
23. Soak sponge bags on both sides with isotonic sodium chloride solution, avoiding excessive soaking to prevent salt bridges or dripping
24. Find head markings for motor cortical hot spot
25. Separate hair around the marked area
26. Clean skin with swab soaked with 40-50% alcohol or skin preparation paste
27. Remove excess cleaning agent with swab
28. Clean area again with isotonic sodium chloride solution
29. Dry the area
30. Place one electrode on head marking for motor cortex area of interest, ensuring sponge maintains direct skin contact
31. Place second electrode for unilateral stimulation
32. Place second electrode over contralateral supra-orbital area for unilateral stimulation
33. Place second electrode on opposite motor cortex (following head marking ipsilateral to study limb) for bilateral stimulation
34. Route all electrode cables toward participant's back to avoid disturbance during stimulation and ease device connection
35. Cover head twice with elastic bandage circularly in mediolateral direction to stabilize M1 electrode
36. Cover head with remaining bandage circularly in anterior-posterior direction to stabilize both electrodes
37. Fix end of bandage and secure cables with adhesive tape on participant's neck or shirt
38. Connect electrode cables to non-invasive brain stimulation device
39. Switch on brain stimulation device
40. Adjust device settings (stimulation type, intensity, duration, ramping parameters, frequency spectrum) for transcranial random noise stimulation
41. Inform participant about potential side effects associated with brain stimulation
42. Start stimulation and check for continuity during ramping up and throughout session
43. Disinfect electrodes and sponges with 40 to 50 percent alcohol
44. Thoroughly rinse electrodes and sponges in water
45. Let materials dry before storing

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Dissolve succinyl glutarate modified PEG in 4 milliliters of Tris buffered saline
2. Sterilize PEG solution using 0.22 micron filter
3. Mix 500 microliters of fibrinogen stock and 250 microliters of PEG stock in culture well of six-well plate
4. Incubate mixture for 20 minutes in 5% CO₂ humidified incubator at 37°C
5. Mix 250 microliters of ASC-CSM with pegylated fibrinogen solution
6. Add 1 milliliter of thrombin stock solution
7. Triturate mixture 1-2 times using pipette
8. Transfer cell-gel mixture to 12-well plate
9. Incubate in 5% CO₂ humidified chamber at 37°C for 10 minutes
10. Wash PEG fibrin gel twice with HBSS
11. Incubate with α-MEM supplemented with 10% FBS in 5% CO₂ humidified incubator at 37°C
12. Mix ASC-CSM with type 1 collagen extracted from rat tail tendons
13. Use 2N sodium hydroxide to adjust pH to 6.8
14. Fibrillate the collagen mixture
15. Add fibrillated collagen-ASC-CSM mixture to a 12-well plate
16. Incubate in 5% CO₂ humidified incubator at 37°C for 30 minutes
17. Verify fibrillation completion
18. Incubate collagen-ASC-CSM gels in 5% CO₂ humidified incubator at 37°C for up to 11 days
19. Observe cell migration from CSM into gel over 11 days using standard microscopy
20. Prepare 1 mL of 7.5 mg/mL collagen mixture
21. Transfer collagen mixture to six-well tissue culture insert
22. Incubate collagen mixture for 30 minutes at 37°C
23. Lay 200 μL of 5 mg/mL ASC-CSM in culture medium over collagen surface
24. Prepare PEG fibrin gel using 250 microliters of cell culture medium
25. Layer pegylated fibrinogen thrombin solution over ASC-CSM-collagen layers
26. Incubate constructs for 30 minutes in 5% CO₂ humidified incubator to achieve gelation
27. Place 1 milliliter of medium in upper chamber over construct
28. Place 3 milliliters of medium in lower chamber

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Expel three 50-microliter drops of media at top, middle, and bottom of glass slide using media-filled filter syringe
2. Transfer 5-10 third instar larvae or early pupae to top media drop using dissecting probe
3. Agitate larvae/pupae gently in media with dissecting probe to remove food/debris
4. Turn single larva/pupa on its side using dissecting probe
5. Identify translucent bilateral testes in posterior third of body to confirm male
6. Discard animals lacking testes (females)
7. Transfer clean male larva/pupa to second media drop
8. Repeat transfer until 3-4 male larvae/pupae are in second media drop
9. Grasp animal at mid-region anterior to testes using forceps
10. Tear animal in half gently using second pair of forceps
11. Secure posterior end of animal on glass slide using forceps
12. Push down on cuticle adjacent to forceps using scalpel edge
13. Move scalpel toward cut end of animal to expel internal organs
14. Gently tease apart testes from surrounding organs
15. Transfer testes individually by inserting scalpel edge under fat body, lifting tissue from media, and moving to third drop
16. Use prewetted glass Pasteur pipette for transfer if insufficient fat body remains attached for scalpel lifting
17. Trim excess fat body from testes using sharp end of scalpel in third media drop, leaving small rim around edges
18. Deposit 30 microliters of Schneider's medium at center of 50mm gas-permeable culture dish using filter syringe
19. Transfer up to five prepared testes to medium drop using scalpel tool or prewetted Pasteur pipette
20. Position testes centrally on gas-permeable membrane using scalpel tool
21. Place four drops of halocarbon oil around medium drop using 1ml syringe, positioned at coverslip corners
22. Align 22mm glass coverslip corners with oil drops and lower coverslip onto media/oil
23. Place culture dish under dissecting microscope
24. Insert corner of delicate task wipe under coverslip
25. Wick away media until coverslip contacts surface of largest testis
26. Place drop of immersion oil on glass coverslip above testes
27. Turn over dish
28. Place dish on microscope stage
29. Move 40X or 60X microscope objective into oil below testes
30. Use transmitted light to locate single testis
31. Bring testis into focus
32. Place 0.5 milliliters of 8% paraformaldehyde in PBSTx into one well of a nine-well dissecting dish
33. Transfer up to 10 testes to the well using a glass Pasteur pipette
34. Ensure testes are positioned at the bottom of the well
35. Transfer testes to a new well containing 0.5 milliliters of PBSTx after 20 minutes
36. Wash testes by agitating gently for five minutes
37. Wash testes two additional times in new wells with fresh PBSTx
38. Transfer testes from dissecting dish to a 1.5 milliliter microcentrifuge tube containing diluted primary antibody
39. Incubate tube overnight at 4°C with gentle rocking or nutation
40. Transfer testes from nine well plate to glass microscopy slide using Pasteur pipette
41. Use scalpel edge to push testes down onto slide surface if necessary
42. Wick away excess liquid from testes using corner of delicate task wipe
43. Remove as much liquid as possible from testes
44. Apply 30-50 microliter drop of microscopy mounting medium to testes
45. Place 22 millimeter coverslip onto drop of mounting medium
46. Allow coverslip to settle and mounting medium to spread under coverslip
47. Apply gentle pressure to coverslip if necessary to squeeze out excess mounting medium and allow coverslip to rest on testes

1. Reconstitute microcarrier beads at 6×10⁴ beads/mL concentration in PBS
2. Sterilize microcarrier bead suspension via autoclave
3. Aliquot 1 mL of complete medium into round-bottom FACS tubes
4. Add 20 μL of room-temperature autoclave-sterilized microcarrier bead suspension to each FACS tube

34. Unscrew objective from microscope

38. Place number zero thickness glass coverslip onto holder surface
39. Secure plastic window to microscope stage using tape

46. Hold strain device in upright position

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Thaw matrigel aliquot on ice at 4°C
2. Prepare gel mixture with PBS sterile water, sodium hydroxide, matrigel, and rat tail type 1 collagen
3. Mix gel components on ice in specified sequence
4. Incubate final gel solution at 4°C for 1 hour
5. Place 12mm diameter 8μm cell culture inserts into 12-well plate using sterilized forceps
6. Count cells
7. Resuspend cells in serum-free media at 5×10⁶ cells/mL concentration
8. Add 100μL cell suspension to 900μL gel solution
9. Mix cell-gel solution thoroughly by pipetting gently up and down
10. Add 150μL final mixture to each insert
11. Transfer inserts to 37°C 5% CO₂ incubator for 30 minutes polymerization
12. Add 100 microliters of serum-free media on top of gel for static condition
13. Add 1,200 microliters of serum-free media under insert for static condition
14. Add 100 microliters of serum-free media under insert for flow condition
15. Add 650 microliters of serum-free media above gel for flow condition
16. Avoid creating air bubbles beneath insert during flow condition setup
17. Place plate in 37°C 5% CO₂ incubator for 24 hours
18. Add 500 microliters of 1x PBS per well into a new 24-well plate
19. Remove remaining medium from upper portion of flow transwell wells
20. Remove gel from inserts using cotton swabs by wiping membrane surface to eliminate non-VA cells
21. Wash inserts by placing them in 1x PBS-filled 24-well plate for 15 seconds
22. Remove PBS from wells
23. Add 500 microliters of 4% paraformaldehyde underneath each insert
24. Incubate inserts in paraformaldehyde for 30 minutes at room temperature to fix transmigrated cells
25. Remove PFA (paraformaldehyde fixative)
26. Rinse once with 500 microliters of 1× PBS to remove residual fixative
27. Add 500 microliters of 0.5% Triton X-100 solution beneath inserts
28. Incubate with Triton X-100 solution for 10 minutes at room temperature
29. Cut membranes out of insert using razor blade
30. Place membranes into 100 microliters of 2 μg/mL DAPI in 1× PBS solution with underside facing down
31. Wrap plate in aluminum foil
32. Place plate on shaker at 150 RPM for 30 minutes at room temperature
33. Wash membranes in 500 microliters of PBS
34. Place membranes back on shaker for 10 minutes
35. Repeat PBS wash two additional times to remove free dye
36. Place membranes on glass slides with transmigrated cells facing up
37. Add mounting solution to membranes
38. Cover membranes with cover slips
39. Count DAPI-stained nuclei and calculate number of invaded cells according to manuscript protocol

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Prepare solution of lipids in chloroform at 1 mg/mL concentration with total mass 0.6 mg
2. Wrap glass vial containing lipid mixture with metal foil to protect from ambient light
3. Place metal foil-wrapped sample vial inside glass beaker
4. Evaporate chloroform solvent in rotary evaporator for 3 hours at 7 kPa vacuum and 80 rpm
5. Add 600 microliters of phosphate-buffered saline solution onto dried lipid film
6. Add six microliters of glycerol to sample
7. Seal glass vial with Parafilm
8. Cover sealed vial with metal foil for light protection
9. Store vial at four degrees Celsius overnight
10. Sonicate glass vial containing solution for one minute using ultra-sonication bath at room temperature
11. Remove Parafilm and pipette solution until visually uniform small lipid vesicles form
12. Aliquot 30 microliters of small lipid vesicle solution into individual 0.5 milliliter plastic tubes
13. Thaw plastic tube containing aliquot of frozen suspension of small lipid vesicles at room temperature
14. Vortex tube four times for 1-2 seconds using vortex mixer at maximum speed
15. Place 5 microliters of small lipid vesicle suspension onto surface of uncleaned glass coverslip to form small round droplet
16. Place glass coverslip in vacuum desiccator for 20 minutes
17. Store dried lipid film at room temperature for 4 minutes
18. Pipette 50 microliters of 10 millimolar HEPES buffer onto dry lipid film
19. Wait 5 minutes for GUV-MLV complex formation
20. Center glass coverslip on microscope stage
21. Pipette 300 microliters of HEPES buffer onto coverslip
22. Position droplet center above microscope objective
23. Transfer 50 microliters of GUV-MLV solution into HEPES solution
24. Wait 25 minutes for GUV-MLV complexes to adhere to coverslip
25. Round edges of borosilicate glass capillaries by placing capillary ends into candle flame
26. Pull at least three glass capillaries using automatic laser puller to achieve tip opening of approximately 0.3 microns diameter
27. Filter 5 mM calcium chloride solution in HEPES buffer using 0.2-0.5 micron syringe filter
28. Back-fill each micropipette with 8 microliters of filtered calcium chloride solution using Microloader
29. Connect micropipette holder to micromanipulator
30. Mount micropipette into micropipette holder tightly
31. Connect injection pump to system
32. Connect capillary holder using supply tube
33. Start injection pump
34. Adjust injection pump settings to 20 hectopascal injection pressure
35. Set compensation pressure to 5 hectopascal
36. Set microscope to bright-field mode
37. Configure microscope for differential interference contrast
38. Position micropipette above droplet containing GUV-MLV complexes using coarse micromanipulator
39. Locate the tip of the micropipette above the objective
40. Bring the micropipette down to the droplet using the coarse micromanipulator
41. Set the microscope to fluorescence mode
42. Position the micropipette tip at a distance of three microns from the membrane surface
43. Inject calcium chloride solution from the micropipette to allow MTPs to form
44. Translate micropipette tip around GUV surface using fine micromanipulator
45. Maintain constant distance between GUV surface and micropipette tip
46. Continue calcium ion injection during manipulation
47. Turn off injection pump to stop microinjection

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Mix 105 milliliters of ethylene glycol
2. Mix 15 milliliters of ultrapure water
3. Add 4.1 grams of urea
4. Add 5.0 grams of nickel nitrate hexahydrate
5. Cover the solution
6. Sonicate solution for 30 minutes in ice-water bath at 40 kHz frequency and full power without pulse
7. Transfer 20 milliliters of sonicated precursor solution into microwave reaction vial
8. Seal reaction vessel with locking lid and PTFE liner
9. Place vial inside microwave reactor
10. Set reactor program to heat as fast as possible using maximum power until target temperature is reached
11. Apply variable power to maintain reaction temperature for 13 to 30 minutes
12. Vent reaction chamber with compressed air until solution temperature reaches 55°C
13. Transfer post-reaction solution to 50 milliliter centrifuge tubes
14. Centrifuge tubes at room temperature for four minutes
15. Decant supernatant
16. Resuspend nickel hydroxide nanosheets in 25 milliliters of ultrapure water
17. Centrifuge suspension again using previous parameters
18. Decant supernatant after second centrifugation
19. Cover centrifuge tubes with tissue or paper towel
20. Dry nanosheets in sample oven at 70°C for 21 hours under ambient atmosphere
21. Suspend small amount of nickel hydroxide powder in 1 milliliter of ethanol using water bath sonicator
22. Drop cast nickel hydroxide and ethanol mixture on SEM stub
23. Evaporate ethanol by heating SEM stub in sample oven at 70 degrees Celsius
24. Add 25 milligrams of nickel hydroxide nano sheets into sample tube
25. Degas and dry sample under vacuum at 120 degrees Celsius for 16 hours
26. Transfer sample tube from degassing port to analysis port
27. Fill zero background powder XRD holder sample well with nickel hydroxide
28. Collect powder X-ray diffractograms using copper K-alpha radiation source
29. Set two theta range to 5-80 degrees
30. Set step increment to 0.01 degrees
31. Equip attenuated total reflectance (ATR) attachment to FTIR spectrometer
32. Press nickel hydroxide powder between two glass slides to create pellet
33. Place nickel hydroxide pellet on silicon ATR crystal
34. Acquire FTIR spectrum between 400-4000 cm⁻¹ (average of 16 scans at 4 cm⁻¹ resolution)

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Dissect E14.5 mouse embryos from uterus
2. Store embryos in L15 medium on ice
3. Transfer embryo to microscope
4. Isolate brain from embryo
5. Remove cephalon by cutting along medial part of cephalic vesicles
6. Remove meningeal sheath
7. Make dorso-ventral cut rostral to mesencephalic flexure
8. Make dorso-ventral cut caudal to mesencephalic flexure
9. Make rostral-caudal cut along dorsal midline using microdissection knife
10. Expose underlying ventral midbrain tissue
11. Make first rostral-caudal cut lateral and parallel to ventral midline
12. Make second rostral-caudal cut lateral and parallel to ventral midline
13. Remove dorsal midbrain tissue
14. Divide ventral midbrain tissue into explants using microdissection knife
15. Store explants in L-15 medium containing 5% FBS on ice
16. Transfer telencephalic vesicles to microscope
17. Remove thalamus by cutting between thalamus and striatum using forceps
18. Make medial-lateral cut rostral to striatum
19. Remove rostral structures including olfactory bulb
20. Repeat cutting procedure for tissue caudal to striatum
21. Position remaining tissue slice for coronal view of striatum
22. Identify striatum by its slightly more transparent tissue characteristic
23. Isolate striatum tissue
24. Cut striatum into explants using microdissection knife while avoiding darker tissue near midline containing ganglionic eminence neurons
25. Add a drop of prepared collagen to a cover slip in a four-well dish
26. Incubate at 37°C and 5% CO₂ for 30 minutes to gelatinize collagen
27. Transfer dopaminergic or sal explants to gelatinized collagen using pipette with 200 microliter tip
28. Position explants in close proximity using needle, maintaining distance of approximately one explant diameter
29. Remove excess medium
30. Add 20 microliters of prepared collagen on top of explant
31. Reposition displaced explants using needle
32. Solidify collagen at room temperature for 15 minutes
33. Transfer to 37°C incubator with 5% CO₂ for 30 minutes
34. Add 400 microliters of explant medium and incubate at 37°C for 2-3 days
35. Add 4% paraformaldehyde to fix explants
36. Incubate fixed explants at room temperature for 1 hour
37. Wash fixed explants in PBS at room temperature three times (15 minutes per wash)
38. Incubate explants in blocking buffer for 2 hours
39. Incubate explants overnight with primary antibody
40. Incubate samples overnight with primary antibody in blocking buffer at 4°C
41. Wash samples in PBS five times at room temperature for 1 hour each
42. Incubate samples with secondary antibody conjugated to fluorophore in blocking buffer overnight at 4°C
43. Cover samples with aluminum foil to minimize light exposure
44. Wash samples overnight in PBS at 4°C
45. Perform several PBS washes the next day
46. Mount explants on microscope slide using prolonged anti-fade reagent mounting medium with explant side facing down
47. Place cover slip gently on mounting medium to avoid trapping air
48. Acquire digital images of explants using epi-fluorescence microscope
49. Measure length of 20 longest neurites emerging from explant in proximal and distal quadrants
50. Calculate proximal-distal ratio using average length of 20 longest neurites per explant

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Set up microscope with lid heater, automatic stage, and EMCCD camera system
2. Fabricate polymethyl suboxane stamps in microfluidics facility or commercial foundry
3. Cut PDMS chip into 16 individual stamps using scalpel
4. Cut PDMS chip into 16 individual stamps using scalpel
5. Expose PDMS stamp and glass slide to air plasma for one minute
6. Attach plasma-treated stamp onto plasma-treated glass slide
7. Cut square area from bottom of 3.5 cm Petri dish
8. Prepare multiple modified Petri dishes
9. Prepare sterile aliquots of high and low melting point agar
10. Place three aliquots of high melting point agar onto heating block
11. Melt one aliquot of low melting point agar
12. Transfer melted low melting point agar aliquot to separate heating block
13. Take plastic dish with pre-made square opening
14. Place dish upside down with opening facing upwards
15. Cover opening with double-sided sticky tape
16. Turn dish to position sticky tape on bottom
17. Place dish onto hard surface
18. Cut opening free using scalpel
19. Fill area surrounding opening with 2 ml high melting point agar
20. Wait until agar solidifies
21. Expose PDMS stamp surface to air plasma for 20 to 60 seconds
22. Create two spacers by stacking five to nine glass slides each
23. Place single glass slide between the two spacer stacks
24. Place glass slide with PDMS stamp across the spacers
25. Adjust spacer height to achieve 1.5 millimeter space between molding surface and glass slide
26. Place drop of hot high melting point agarose onto slide near PDMS stamp
27. Slide PDMS stamp horizontally into liquid agarose
28. Pull stamp off vertically after agarose solidifies and appears opaque
29. Transfer eggs/worms with OP 50 bacteria onto agarose using fine platinum wire pick
30. Distribute one egg/worm per chamber with food using eyelash
31. Cut agar slab containing micro chambers into a square fitting dish opening
32. Use forceps to lift agar slab
33. Place agar slab upside down onto glass cover slip
34. Place cover slip into opening of plastic dish
35. Gently press cover slip down onto ring of double-sided sticky tape
36. Turn dish upside down
37. Fill gap between agro slab and aros reservoir using low melting point aros
38. Seal dish with lid and param
39. Place dish containing micro chambers onto microscope
40. Focus microscope on sample
41. Set recording parameters to 40 image frames in 20 seconds
42. Set recording interval to every half hour for 24 hours
43. Configure stage scan to visit each worm
44. Zoom out microscope to lower magnification

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Take S400 spin columns
2. Vortex columns for at least 30 seconds to resuspend resin
3. Centrifuge columns for one minute at 735 G to remove storage buffer
4. Transfer columns to clean 1.5 milliliter tubes
5. Add 100 microliters of doubly functionalized linear DNA solution to each column
6. Centrifuge columns for two minutes at 735 G to flow DNA through column
7. Pool together the flow-through
8. Vortex M280 streptavidin-coated magnetic beads for 30 seconds to resuspend
9. Transfer 4 mg of resuspended magnetic beads to a clean 1.5 ml tube
10. Place tube in magnetic rack for 1 minute to collect beads
11. Resuspend beads in 1 ml of buffer A
12. Vortex resuspended beads for 5 seconds
13. Incubate beads at room temperature for 5 minutes
14. Resuspend beads in 400 microliters of 2x buffer A by pipetting
15. Add 400 microliters of functionalized DNA solution to beads
16. Mix bead-DNA solution gently by pipetting
17. Incubate bead-DNA mixture overnight at 4°C with end-over-end rotation
18. Place samples on magnetic rack to separate beads
19. Remove supernatant from samples
20. Wash beads with buffer B
21. Wash beads with buffer C
22. Resuspend beads in 300 microliters of buffer C
23. Prepare four 75 microliter aliquots of bead suspension
24. Take one 75 microliter aliquot for downstream processing
25. Take one 75 microliter aliquot containing 1 mg DNA-bound streptavidin-coated magnetic beads
26. Remove supernatant using magnetic rack
27. Wash beads with 200 microliters loading buffer
28. Resuspend beads in 75 microliters loading buffer and mix gently by pipetting
29. Add origin recognition complex at 37.5 nM final concentration to bead-bound DNA
30. Incubate reaction for 5 minutes at 30°C with 800 rpm agitation
31. Add Cdc6 at 50 nM final concentration
32. Incubate reaction as previously demonstrated
33. Add Mcm2-7/Cdt1
34. Add Mcm2-7/Cdt1 complex at final concentration of 100 nanomolar
35. Incubate reaction for 20 minutes
36. Add Ddk at final concentration of 100 nanomolar
37. Incubate reaction for 30 minutes
38. Remove supernatant from bead-bound DNA
39. Wash bead-bound DNA with 200 microliters of high salt wash buffer
40. Mix by pipetting to ensure full bead resuspension
41. Remove wash buffer
42. Wash beads with 200 microliters of CMG buffer
43. Remove CMG buffer
44. Resuspend DNA-bound beads in 50 microliters of CMG buffer supplemented with 5 mM ATP
45. Mix all components shown on screen in one tube
46. Place mixed component tube on ice
47. Add resuspended bead-bound DNA to protein mix
48. Incubate reaction at 30°C for 15 minutes with 800 RPM agitation
49. Wash beads sequentially with HSW buffer and CMG buffer
50. Resuspend CMG-containing DNA-bound magnetic beads in 200 microliters of elution buffer
51. Incubate suspension at room temperature for one hour with 800 RPM agitation
52. Place tube in magnetic rack
53. Allow beads to collect via magnetism for five minutes
54. Carefully collect supernatant containing eluted DNA-CMG complexes without disrupting beads
55. Transfer collected supernatant to a new tube
56. Place collected supernatant in magnetic rack
57. Incubate in magnetic rack for five minutes
58. Collect supernatant
59. Transfer supernatant to new tube
60. Add 1,400 microliters of CMG buffer to 200 microliters of supernatant
61. Flow all solutions into flow cell at 0.5 bar constant pressure
62. Turn off flow in channels four and five
63. Reduce flow pressure to 0.2 bar
64. Move trapping lasers to channel one
65. Capture one bead in each optical trap
66. Move trapped beads to channel two using joystick with trigger pressed
67. Fish DNA-CMG complex by moving right bead toward/away from left bead using joystick (trigger not pressed)
68. Move beads to channel three
69. Move beads away from left bead until DNA tether is trapped
70. Move beads to channel three
71. Stop flow in all channels
72. Adjust 561nm laser power to 4 microwatts in excitation laser panel
73. Take one-frame test scan of DNA in channel three
74. Move DNA tether to channel four or five for imaging (conditional on CMG presence)
75. Turn flow back on and discard beads (conditional on CMG absence)
76. Input 2 piconewtons in force spectroscopy panel
77. Click enable button in force spectroscopy panel to start force clamp
78. Disable force clamp by clicking enable button in force spectroscopy panel
79. Click start scan button in image scan panel
80. Acquire time-lapse images of fluorescent CMG complexes every 5 seconds using 561 nm laser at 4 microwatts (objective power)

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Select plump and undamaged tartary buckwheat seeds
2. Soak seeds in 28°C water for 20 minutes
3. Place 100-200 peeled seeds into sterilized 100 ml conical flask containing 75% ethanol
4. Sterilize seeds in 75% ethanol for 30 seconds
5. Replace ethanol with 5% sodium hypochlorite
6. Decant sodium hypochlorite after 15 minutes
7. Wash seeds with sterile deionized water five times
8. Blot seeds dry with sterile bibulous paper
9. Add 10 tartary buckwheat seeds per bottle to 300 ml plant tissue culture bottles containing 50 ml MSSA medium
10. Germinate seeds in culture room at 25±1°C under light conditions
11. Select robust seedlings with two unfolded cotyledons after 7-10 days
12. Cut seedlings from roots
13. Place seedlings in sterile petri dish
14. Cut hypocotyls into 0.8-1 cm segments
15. Shear cotyledons into ~0.5 cm pieces
16. Pre-culture explants under light conditions for 24 hours
17. Thaw A. rhizogenes culture on ice
18. Evenly plate bacteria onto YEB solid medium with antibiotics
19. Incubate plate at 28°C for 12-16 hours
20. Pick monoclonal colony from incubated plate
21. Culture picked colony in new petri dish
22. Incubate new culture at 28°C for 12-16 hours
23. Inoculate monoclonal colonies in sterilized 100ml conical flask containing 20ml YEB medium with antibiotics
24. Incubate flask at 28°C with 200 RPM shaking for 16-18 hours until OD600 reaches 2.0
25. Transfer A. rhizogenes culture to sterile 50 milliliter conical tube
26. Collect bacteria by centrifugation
27. Resuspend pellet in MSSAS medium
28. Adjust suspension to optical density of 0.2
29. Infuse suspension into flask of explants
30. Remove explants from flask after 10 minutes
31. Blot explants with sterile bibulous paper
32. Place sterile 9cm diameter filter paper onto dish of MSSAAS medium
33. Overlay explants on filter paper
34. Incubate at 25°C in dark for three days
35. Transfer approximately 20 infected explants onto MSSACK medium
36. Vertically incubate plants under light conditions at 25 ± 1°C
37. Select hairy roots demonstrating white appearance and rapid growth
38. Cut selected roots into 2-3 centimeter pieces
39. Number root pieces on clean bench
40. Subculture root pieces in sterilized 100 ml conical flask containing MSSK medium
41. Incubate at 25°C and 80 rpm in dark until roots overspread flask bottom
42. Remove tawny and contaminated hairy roots
43. Select hairy roots with white appearance
44. Check roots for fluorescence under blue/UV transilluminator (if reporter gene used)
45. Select hairy roots exhibiting strong fluorescent signal
46. Dry roots with absorbent paper
47. Wrap roots in marked aluminum foil for immediate analysis
48. Lyophilize roots in liquid nitrogen for long-term storage
49. Place lyophilized roots at -80°C until investigation

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Thaw cryo vial containing tumor fragments in 37°C water bath with occasional gentle flicking
2. Mix fragments with gentle inversion and dry outside of vial
3. Spray vial with 70% ethanol and place in vial-safety hood
4. Transfer cryo vial contents to 15 milliliter conical tube
5. Add 10 milliliters of cold DMEM to conical tube
6. Invert tube to mix contents
7. Replace supernatant twice with 10 milliliter aliquots of fresh cold DMEM after fragment settling
8. Place tube on ice
9. Spray tumor region of tumor-bearing mouse with 70% ethanol
10. Use serrated forceps to pinch and lift skin surrounding tumor
11. Use scissors to make small incision
12. Separate tumor from skin while avoiding hair contamination
13. Trim remaining mouse fat pad tissue from outer surface of tumor
14. Place tumor in 15 milliliter conical tube containing 5 milliliters of cold DMEM
15. Transfer dissected tumor into sterile 10 cm Petri dish containing DMEM to prevent drying
16. Cut tumor into 1 mm³ fragments using UV-sterilized blade under aseptic conditions
17. Transfer tumor fragments to 15 ml tube containing cold DMEM on ice
18. Confirm lack of response to pedal reflex
19. Apply ointment to anesthetized recipient mouse's eyes
20. Shave abdomen around fourth mammary gland
21. Disinfect exposed skin using circular motion from center to outer edges with povidone-iodine surgical scrub
22. Remove surgical scrub using 70% isopropyl alcohol pad
23. Cover animal with sterile fenestrated surgical drape
24. Use serrated forceps to pinch and lift skin at fourth nipple
25. Make 1 cm parasagittal incision from fourth nipple toward head using scissors (blunt tips down)
26. Separate skin from peritoneum on medial incision side using cotton tip applicator
27. Gently peel mammary fat pad from skin
28. Secure skin near animal's body using sterile 27-gauge needle
29. Use serrated forceps to gently extend fat pad away from body
30. Locate inguinal lymph node at major vessel intersection and cauterize medial vessels/adjoining fat
31. Cut each cauterized vessel one at a time using micro dissecting scissors
32. Remove the section of fat pad containing the lymph node
33. Grasp the fat pad using blunt forceps
34. Insert closed tips of angled fine forceps into middle of fat pad above remaining vessel near peritenoneum using curved motion
35. Retract forceps tips and reinsert into original hole
36. Open forceps to stretch hole into small pocket
37. Remove forceps from pocket
38. Insert tumor fragment into pocket using forceps
39. Slowly open forceps tips to release tumor fragment into fat pad pocket
40. Carefully withdraw forceps from fat pad pocket
41. Collect skin edges on each side of incision with serrated forceps
42. Uncurl incision edges with claw forceps
43. Pinch incision edges together to form continuous surface
44. Hold incision sides with serrated forceps
45. Place wound clip in center of incision using applicator
46. Transfer animal to clean cage on warming surface

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Thaw cryo vial containing tumor fragments in 37°C water bath with occasional gentle flicking
2. Mix fragments with gentle inversion and dry outside of vial
3. Spray vial with 70% ethanol and place in vial-safety hood
4. Transfer cryo vial contents to 15 milliliter conical tube
5. Add 10 milliliters of cold DMEM to conical tube
6. Invert tube to mix contents
7. Replace supernatant twice with 10 milliliter aliquots of fresh cold DMEM after fragment settling
8. Place tube on ice
9. Spray tumor region of tumor-bearing mouse with 70% ethanol
10. Use serrated forceps to pinch and lift skin surrounding tumor
11. Use scissors to make small incision
12. Separate tumor from skin while avoiding hair contamination
13. Trim remaining mouse fat pad tissue from outer surface of tumor
14. Place tumor in 15 milliliter conical tube containing 5 milliliters of cold DMEM
15. Transfer dissected tumor into sterile 10 cm Petri dish containing DMEM to prevent drying
16. Cut tumor into 1 mm³ fragments using UV-sterilized blade under aseptic conditions
17. Transfer tumor fragments to 15 ml tube containing cold DMEM on ice
18. Confirm lack of response to pedal reflex
19. Apply ointment to anesthetized recipient mouse's eyes
20. Shave abdomen around fourth mammary gland
21. Disinfect exposed skin using circular motion from center to outer edges with povidone-iodine surgical scrub
22. Remove surgical scrub using 70% isopropyl alcohol pad
23. Cover animal with sterile fenestrated surgical drape
24. Use serrated forceps to pinch and lift skin at fourth nipple
25. Make 1 cm parasagittal incision from fourth nipple toward head using scissors (blunt tips down)
26. Separate skin from peritoneum on medial incision side using cotton tip applicator
27. Gently peel mammary fat pad from skin
28. Secure skin near animal's body using sterile 27-gauge needle
29. Use serrated forceps to gently extend fat pad away from body
30. Locate inguinal lymph node at major vessel intersection and cauterize medial vessels/adjoining fat
31. Cut each cauterized vessel one at a time using micro dissecting scissors
32. Remove the section of fat pad containing the lymph node
33. Grasp the fat pad using blunt forceps
34. Insert closed tips of angled fine forceps into middle of fat pad above remaining vessel near peritenoneum using curved motion
35. Retract forceps tips and reinsert into original hole
36. Open forceps to stretch hole into small pocket
37. Remove forceps from pocket
38. Insert tumor fragment into pocket using forceps
39. Slowly open forceps tips to release tumor fragment into fat pad pocket
40. Carefully withdraw forceps from fat pad pocket
41. Collect skin edges on each side of incision with serrated forceps
42. Uncurl incision edges with claw forceps
43. Pinch incision edges together to form continuous surface
44. Hold incision sides with serrated forceps
45. Place wound clip in center of incision using applicator
46. Transfer animal to clean cage on warming surface

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Select worms unfed for at least one week and 5-7 mm long
2. Transfer 15-30 worms to 100 mm Petri dish
3. Fill Petri dish two-thirds full with worm water
4. Replace Petri dish cover immediately
5. Observe worms for missing/unpigmented tissues (e.g., white heads/tails)
6. Verify worms are undamaged and not recently regenerating
7. Wipe workspace and dissecting microscope base with 70% ethanol solution
8. Allow workspace to dry completely
9. Place dish with selected worms on left side of microscope
10. Place equipment on right side of microscope: number five forceps, 31-gauge 5/16-inch insulin needle with 1-mL syringe, clean transfer pipette, and labeled 12-well plate for collecting ablated worms
11. Place box of lint-free tissue wipes, plastic wash bottle with fresh worm water, and additional transfer pipettes within easy reach
12. Position Peltier plate at center of dissecting microscope base
13. Set DC power source output to cool Peltier plate surface sufficiently for worm immobilization
14. Cut 5 cm × 10 cm piece of plastic paraffin film
15. Place paraffin film over center of Peltier plate
16. Fold lint-free tissue wipe into ~2 cm square
17. Place folded wipe on top of paraffin film
18. Hold folded wipe in place
19. Moisten wipe with worm water
20. Roll wipe flat with transfer pipette
21. Place worm dorsal side up on filter paper using transfer pipette
22. Turn light on and focus beam on worm
23. Adjust dissecting microscope focus and magnification to visualize worm eyes clearly
24. Rotate paraffin film to position worm with head toward researcher at 30-40 degrees right angle
25. Reposition ventral-side-up worm to dorsal-side-up position using dull side of forceps (conditional)
26. Readjust microscope settings to bring eyes into clear focus
27. Ensure both eyes and surrounding head tissues are within microscope field of view
28. Hold clean syringe in right hand between thumb and index finger
29. Support bottom of syringe on left thumb braced against Peltier plate
30. Verify needle bevel visibility through microscope
31. Place left index finger on surgery surface at 40-degree angle to left thumb
32. Stabilize surgery surface using finger placement
33. Position needle at 90-degree angle to eye
34. Position needle at 90-degree angle to eye with bevel facing upward
35. Penetrate tissue overlying optic cup (white unpigmented region) with needle tip
36. Remove pigmented and unpigmented tissues within optic cup by gentle right-to-left scooping
37. Ablate second eye (conditional for double-eye procedures)
38. Backload transfer pipette with small amount of worm water
39. Release worm water onto worm to lift it off filter paper
40. Draw worm into transfer pipette
41. Transfer worm to labeled 12-well plate containing fresh worm water (two-thirds full)
42. Wash worms by replacing well water with fresh worm water
43. Incubate worms in 20°C incubator protected from light while monitoring regeneration

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Covalently attach probe molecules to cantilever tip
2. Use tweezers to place silicon nitride cantilever chips on clean glass slide or Petri dish
3. Place slide with chips in 100 watt plasma chamber evacuated and flushed with oxygen
4. Operate plasma chamber at 0.25 millibar and 20 watts for 15 minutes
5. Place 2.5 milliliters of up test solution in a glass petri dish
6. Prepare a vessel containing acetone
7. Retrieve cantilevers immediately after plasma processing
8. Dip each cantilever in acetone for one second
9. Place cantilever in the Optus solution
10. Incubate cantilevers for 15 minutes at room temperature
11. Determine weight of contents in eppendorf tubes containing mPEG-NHS and di-NHS-PEG
12. Add chloroform to mPEG-NHS tube to achieve 25 mM final concentration
13. Vortex mPEG-NHS tube to dissolve powder
14. Add chloroform to di-NHS-PEG tube to achieve 0.25 mM final concentration
15. Vortex di-NHS-PEG tube to dissolve powder
16. Mix solutions to achieve 1:500 molar ratio of di-NHS-PEG to mPEG-NHS
17. Transfer mixture to glass Petri dish with lid to limit evaporation
18. Prepare two containers with 10 ml acetone and one container with chloroform for rinsing
19. Rinse cantilevers by dipping each twice in acetone and once in chloroform
20. Transfer cantilevers to polyethylene glycol solution
21. Cover dish and incubate cantilevers in solution for one hour
22. Begin with one molar sodium hydroxide volume
23. Dissolve probe molecule (poly-D-tyrosine) to 1 mg/mL concentration
24. Exchange sodium hydroxide for pH 8-8.5 sodium borate buffer
25. Perform buffer exchange immediately before functionalization
26. Prepare three containers for rinsing
27. Prepare container with poly tyrosine borate buffer solution
28. Prepare rinsing solutions: 5 ml chloroform, 5 ml ethanol, 5 ml borate buffer
29. Measure 300 microliters of poly tyrosine borate buffer solution
30. Retrieve cantilevers
31. Dip each cantilever in chloroform
32. Dip each cantilever in ethanol
33. Dip each cantilever in borate buffer
34. Place cantilevers in poly tyrosine borate buffer solution
35. Incubate cantilever chips for one hour
36. Prepare 5 ml of borate buffer in one container and 5 ml of ultrapure water in another container for rinsing
37. Prepare 20 ml of ultrapure water for storage
38. Get functionalized cantilevers
39. Dip each cantilever in borate buffer
40. Dip each cantilever in ultrapure water for rinsing
41. Immerse cantilever in ultrapure water for storage
42. Place functionalized cantilever into AFM cantilever holder suitable for liquids
43. Connect cantilever holder to AFM head
44. Pour water into fluid cell to immerse cantilever and surface
45. Lower AFM head onto liquid cell
46. Let system equilibrate for at least 30 minutes
47. Position cantilever far from surface
48. Record thermal noise spectra (minimum 10 spectra for sufficient signal-to-noise ratio)
49. Carefully approach surface for inverse optical lever sensitivity measurement
50. Push cantilever tip against hard surface
51. Record force versus piezo travel distance
52. Measure slope during contact
53. Invert slope to determine inverse optical lever sensitivity
54. Determine spring constant of cantilever by fitting harmonic oscillator to thermal noise spectrum
55. Set appropriate parameters for data collection
56. Record numerous force-distance curves for each experimental condition
57. Determine sensitivity and spring constant again to check system consistency and stability

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Harvest human endothelial EAHY 926 cells at 95% confluency
2. Wash cells with 10 milliliters of PBS to remove growth media
3. Incubate cells in trypsin-EDTA solution for detachment
4. Transfer detached cells to conical tube
5. Centrifuge cells
6. Resuspend cells in 25 ml of complete DMEM medium at 1 million cells/ml concentration
7. Place one Thermon cover slip in each well of 24-well tissue culture plate using sterile forceps
8. Orient cover slip with opaque/cell-adherent side facing upwards
9. Add 500 microliters of cell suspension to each well
10. Incubate plate at 37°C for 48 hours or until cells reach confluency
11. Use clean forceps to remove each cover slip
12. Dip cover slips in DMEM to remove soluble secreted proteins
13. Transfer washed cover slips to individual wells of fresh 24-well plate containing 490 microliters per well of complete DMEM while maintaining orientation (cells facing upward)
14. Prepare 10 milliliter culture of Staphylococcus aureus in brain heart infusion broth
15. Transfer bacterial culture into conical tube
16. Centrifuge bacteria at 5,000 times G for 10 minutes
17. Remove supernatant after pelleting cells
18. Resuspend pellet in 10 milliliters of sterile DMEM
19. Centrifuge cells again to repeat wash
20. Perform wash procedure three times total
21. Resuspend bacteria in DMEM after final wash
22. Measure optical density of bacterial suspension
23. Adjust concentration to obtain OD600 of 1.0 (approximately 10^9 CFU/mL)
24. Add 10 microliters of bacterial suspension to each well of 24-well plate containing cover slips
25. Incubate plate at 37 degrees Celsius for 15-90 minutes to allow bacterial attachment and invasion
26. Aspirate supernatant from each well and replace with 10% FBS-DMEM supplemented with Gentamycin
27. Incubate plate at 37 degrees Celsius for 60 minutes to kill extracellular bacteria
28. Wash each cover slip in DMEM three times to remove antibiotic
29. Transfer cover slips to new 24-well plate containing 500 microliters of 0.5% Triton X-100 in PBS per well
30. Maintain cover slips with cell-covered side facing up during transfer
31. Vigorously rinse each cover slip by pipetting Triton solution up and down directly onto cover slip to lyse cells and release intracellular bacteria
32. Perform serial dilutions of the lysate to quantify infecting bacteria
33. Collect lysate and plate serial dilutions on TSA plates

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Prepare 5% native polyacrylamide gel containing 0.5x TBE and 2.5% glycerol using mini protein gel system
2. Mix distilled water, TBE, ammonium persulfate, TEMED, acrylamide bis, and glycerol to prepare 30 ml gel solution for four gels
3. Immediately pass gel solution
4. Wrap polymerized gels in clear plastic wrap pre-wetted with 0.5x TBE
5. Store wrapped gels at 4°C
6. Resuspend oligonucleotides in 1x Tris-EDTA buffer
7. Adjust resuspended oligonucleotides to final concentration of 100 micromolar
8. Mix 0.6 microliters of five prime dye short oligonucleotide
9. Add 1.2 microliters of long oligonucleotide
10. Add 28.2 microliters of sodium chloride Tris-EDTA buffer to mixture in 1.5 milliliter tube
11. Place tube in boiling water for five minutes
12. Turn off heat source and allow tube to cool overnight in dark
13. Mix 30 microliters of annealed oligonucleotides with dNTPs, Klenow buffer, Klenow five prime dye tag, and double distilled water in 0.2 ml PCR tube
14. Incubate mixture at 37°C for 60 minutes in PCR machine
15. Add 3.4 microliters of 0.5 M EDTA to stop reaction
16. Heat inactivate at 75°C for 20 minutes in PCR machine
17. Dilute filled-in probes with sodium chloride Tris-EDTA buffer to 0.1 micromolar concentration
18. Store oligonucleotides at -20°C in the dark
19. Mix 20 microliters of long oligonucleotide, 20 microliters of Long R oligonucleotide, and 60 microliters of sodium chloride Tris-EDTA buffer in a 1.5 milliliter tube
20. Place tube in boiling water for five minutes
21. Turn off heat source and allow tube in water to cool overnight
22. Prepare 1 mL of 5x binding buffer by mixing Tris HCl, Sodium Chloride, Potassium Chloride, Magnesium Chloride, EDTA, DTT, BSA, and double-distilled water
23. Pre-run 5% native polyacrylamide gel in 0.5x TBE and 2.5% glycerol at 80 volts for 30 minutes to one hour or until current stabilizes
24. Mix four microliters of 5x binding buffer
25. Mix 4 microliters of 5x binding buffer, 80-200 nanograms of purified protein A, 1 microliter of 0.1 micromolar dye conjugated probe, and double-distilled water
26. Incubate mixture at room temperature in the dark for 15 minutes
27. Load entire binding reaction onto gel
28. Run gel at 10 volts per centimeter to desired distance
29. Cover gel apparatus with aluminum to maintain darkness

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Prepare sterile instruments and reagents (scissors, forceps, DPBS in 35-ml culture dishes, 70% ethanol, gauze, paper towels)
2. Clean all surfaces of dissection area
3. Cover dissection area with clean paper towel
4. Place euthanized mouse on gauze in dissection area
5. Spray 70% ethanol on mouse's abdomen
6. Make lower midline abdominal incision with sterile dissection scissors to expose bladder
7. Grasp and gently pull up bladder with forceps to identify bilateral ureterovesical junction
8. Remove bladder fundus with scissors along boundary between ureter openings
9. Transfer bladder into sterile dish containing 2 milliliters of DPBS
10. Prepare sterile instruments and reagents
11. Transfer bladder to 35-millimeter dish in biosafety cabinet
12. Wash bladder with 2 milliliters of sterile DPBS
13. Collect bladder tissues from four mice into dish with 1 milliliter of complete culture medium without charcoal-stripped FBS
14. Mince each bladder into four equal pieces using surgical blade
15. Unfold minced bladder pieces with forceps to expose urothelium surface to medium
16. Transfer bladder pieces and medium into 15-milliliter centrifuge tube
17. Wash dish twice with 2 milliliters of complete culture medium without charcoal-stripped FBS
18. Add wash to centrifuge tube and adjust total volume to 5 milliliters
19. Add collagenase and hyaluronidase mixture to tube
20. Place tube horizontally in 37°C orbital shaker incubator at 200 RPM for 30 minutes
21. Add equal volume of 10% charcoal-stripped FBS and DPBS to digested tissue
22. Transfer mixture into tube
23. Centrifuge at 200 × g for 5 minutes
24. Discard supernatant
25. Add 2 milliliters of trypsin substitute to cell pellet
26. Mix cell pellet and trypsin substitute thoroughly
27. Incubate tube at 37°C and 5% CO₂ for 4 minutes
28. Pipette cells up and down 10 times with standard P1000 tip
29. Add 5 milliliters of 10% charcoal-stripped FBS and DPBS
30. Strain dissociated cells through 100-micrometer sterile cell strainer and collect suspension in 50-milliliter tube
31. Transfer cell suspension to 15-milliliter tube
32. Centrifuge at 200 × g for 5 minutes
33. Resuspend pellet in 1 milliliter of complete culture medium
34. Count cells using hemocytometer
35. Plate 500,000 cells per well in 24-well plate with 0.5 milliliters fresh complete culture medium
36. Add 2 microliters of adenovirus to 24-well plate
37. Mix adenovirus with dissociated urothelial cells
38. Centrifuge plate at 300g for 30 minutes (spinoculation)
39. Incubate plate at 37°C and 5% CO₂ for 1 hour
40. Transfer cells and medium from well to 15-mL tube
41. Centrifuge at 200 × g for 5 minutes
42. Discard supernatant
43. Add 50 μL complete culture medium to cell pellet
44. Mix well to resuspend cells
45. Add 140 μL matrix extract to cell suspension
46. Gently mix to avoid air bubbles
47. Pipette 50 μL aliquots to create domes in prewarmed 6-well plate
48. Incubate plate for 5 minutes to solidify domes
49. Flip 6-well plate upside down
50. Continue solidification for 25 minutes
51. Add 2.5 milliliters of complete culture medium to each well
52. Place plate in incubator for organoid culture

8. Remove bladder fundus with scissors along boundary between ureter openings
9. Transfer bladder into sterile dish containing 2 milliliters of DPBS

11. Transfer bladder to 35-millimeter dish in biosafety cabinet

17. Wash dish twice with 2 milliliters of complete culture medium without charcoal-stripped FBS
18. Add wash to centrifuge tube and adjust total volume to 5 milliliters
19. Add collagenase and hyaluronidase mixture to tube
20. Place tube horizontally in 37°C orbital shaker incubator at 200 RPM for 30 minutes
21. Add equal volume of 10% charcoal-stripped FBS and DPBS to digested tissue
22. Transfer mixture into tube
23. Centrifuge at 200 × g for 5 minutes
24. Discard supernatant
25. Add 2 milliliters of trypsin substitute to cell pellet
26. Mix cell pellet and trypsin substitute thoroughly
27. Incubate tube at 37°C and 5% CO₂ for 4 minutes
28. Pipette cells up and down 10 times with standard P1000 tip
29. Add 5 milliliters of 10% charcoal-stripped FBS and DPBS
30. Strain dissociated cells through 100-micrometer sterile cell strainer and collect suspension in 50-milliliter tube
31. Transfer cell suspension to 15-milliliter tube
32. Centrifuge at 200 × g for 5 minutes
33. Resuspend pellet in 1 milliliter of complete culture medium
34. Count cells using hemocytometer
35. Plate 500,000 cells per well in 24-well plate with 0.5 milliliters fresh complete culture medium
36. Add 2 microliters of adenovirus to 24-well plate
37. Mix adenovirus with dissociated urothelial cells
38. Centrifuge plate at 300g for 30 minutes (spinoculation)
39. Incubate plate at 37°C and 5% CO₂ for 1 hour
40. Transfer cells and medium from well to 15-mL tube
41. Centrifuge at 200 × g for 5 minutes
42. Discard supernatant
43. Add 50 μL complete culture medium to cell pellet
44. Mix well to resuspend cells
45. Add 140 μL matrix extract to cell suspension
46. Gently mix to avoid air bubbles
47. Pipette 50 μL aliquots to create domes in prewarmed 6-well plate
48. Incubate plate for 5 minutes to solidify domes
49. Flip 6-well plate upside down
50. Continue solidification for 25 minutes
51. Add 2.5 milliliters of complete culture medium to each well
52. Place plate in incubator for organoid culture

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Inoculate 5 milliliters of single drop-out broth with single colony of yeast transformed with effector protein plasmid
2. Prepare negative control using yeast transformed with vector alone
3. Incubate inoculum overnight at 30°C with shaking
4. Add 180 microliters of sterile water to wells A2 through A6 of 96-well plate
5. Vortex overnight culture and add 180 microliters to well A1
6. Perform serial dilution of yeast in wells with water
7. Spot 5 microliters of each dilution onto single drop-out glucose and galactose agar plates using multi-channel pipette
8. Incubate plates at 30°C for 48 hours
9. Inoculate 100 milliliters of single drop-out broth with 1 milliliter of previously prepared yeast stock
10. Incubate inoculated broth for 16-24 hours at 30°C with shaking at 150 RPM
11. Add entire 100 milliliters of overnight culture to 900 milliliters of pre-warmed single drop-out broth
12. Incubate flask for 4-5 hours at 30°C with shaking
13. Pellet culture at 6,000 × g for 10 minutes at 4°C
14. Discard supernatant
15. Resuspend pellet in 250 milliliters of sterile water
16. Repeat centrifugation at 6,000 × g for 10 minutes at 4°C
17. Discard supernatant after repeated centrifugation
18. Resuspend pellet in 250 milliliters of 1 mM lithium acetate
19. Pellet culture by centrifugation
20. Remove lithium acetate
21. Resuspend pellet in 9.6 milliliters of 50% PEG 3350
22. Add transformation reagents as described
23. Adjust volume to 15 milliliters with sterile water
24. Invert gently to mix
25. Incubate mixture at 30 degrees Celsius for 30 minutes
26. Add 750 microliters of DMSO
27. Incubate in water bath at 42 degrees Celsius for 30 minutes
28. Pellet yeast by centrifuging at 3,000 times g for five minutes
29. Discard supernatant
30. Wash pellet with 10 milliliters of sterile water
31. Centrifuge to pellet yeast
32. Resuspend pellet in eight milliliters of water
33. Dilute sample 1:10
34. Plate 100 microliters of each dilution onto double drop-out agar
35. Plate 200 microliters of sample on double drop-out galactose agar plates
36. Incubate plates at 30 degrees Celsius for 48-96 hours
37. Patch colonies on double drop-out galactose agar
38. Incubate patches for 24-48 hours
39. Inoculate five milliliters of double drop-out broth with patch material
40. Incubate inoculum overnight at 26 degrees Celsius with shaking at 150 RPM
41. Add 180 microliters of sterile water to five wells (A2 through A6) of a 96-well plate
42. Vortex the overnight culture mix
43. Add 180 microliters of yeast culture to well A1
44. Serially dilute yeast culture using water in wells A2 through A6
45. Spot five microliters of each dilution on single drop-out glucose and galactose agar plates, including toxic effector alone control
46. Incubate plates at 30 degrees Celsius for 48 hours
47. Test effector proteins for toxicity in yeast
48. Express protein of interest in yeast under galactose-inducible promoter
49. Compare yeast growth on galactose versus glucose media
50. Conduct suppressor screen by transforming toxic strain with yeast genomic library pYAP13
51. Plate transformants on galactose agar
52. Spot suppressor clones on double drop-out galactose agar to confirm toxicity suppression

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Deposit bottom metal layer using magnetron sputtering
2. Perform titanium sputtering with pure titanium target using argon plasma at 350°C substrate temperature
3. Perform reactive sputtering of titanium nitride using argon and nitrogen mixture at 350°C substrate temperature
4. Deposit 1 micrometer thick silicon dioxide layer using plasma enhanced chemical vapor deposition
5. Check thickness of silicon dioxide layer using reflectometer
6. Coat wafer with 1.4 micrometers of positive photoresist
7. Perform 92nd dexamethazone treatment at 130 degrees Celsius
8. Spin coat wafer at 3,500 RPM
9. Soft bake wafer for 90 seconds at 95 degrees Celsius
10. Expose pattern of openings to UV light at 140 mJ/cm² using photolithography mask and exposure tool
11. Perform post-exposure bake at 115 degrees Celsius for 92 seconds
12. Perform development using developer for 62 seconds
13. Perform hard bake at 100 degrees Celsius for 90 seconds
14. Inspect resist openings for correct dimensions and alignment to markers using microscope
15. Plasma etch contact openings in silicon dioxide using triad plasma etcher
16. Wet etch sacrificial titanium layer in 0.55% hydrogen fluoride for 60 seconds
17. Rinse wafers with deionized water until water resistivity reaches five mega ohms
18. Dry wafers using rinser dryer
19. Pump down chamber to at least 2×10⁻⁶ Torr
20. Heat wafers to 60°C using lamps under vacuum
21. Evaporate 5 nm cobalt using e-beam evaporator
22. Place wafer in ultrasonic bath with tetrahydrofuran at 35°C for 15 minutes
23. Rinse with deionized water for 5 minutes
24. Dry wafer using spinner
25. Inspect wafer under microscope for resist residues
26. Perform carbon nanotube (CNT) growth using low-pressure chemical vapor deposition
27. Cool down the reactor and purge using nitrogen
28. Use scanning electron microscope (SEM) to check CNT height inside openings under 45-degree tilt
29. Inspect samples using Raman spectroscopy to determine CNT crystallinity
30. Sputter 100 nanometers of titanium using magnetron sputtering
31. Sputter 2 micrometers of aluminum without breaking vacuum
32. Perform hexa ethyl dilane treatment at 130°C after wafer cooling on cold plate
33. Spin coat wafer with 3.1 micrometers of positive photoresist at 3000 RPM
34. Soft bake wafer at 95°C for 90 seconds
35. Expose top metal pattern to exposure dose
36. Expose top metal pattern to 420 mJ/cm² dose at focus -1 using photolithography mask and exposure tool
37. Perform post-exposure bake at 115°C for 92 seconds
38. Perform 62-second puddle development process
39. Hard bake at 100°C for 90 seconds
40. Inspect resist lines for correct dimensions and overlay alignment to markers using microscope
41. Etch titanium/aluminum stack using chlorine plasma with inductive coupled plasma
42. Remove photoresist using organic solvent (e.g., N-methyl pyrrolidone) to prevent plasma damage to CNT
43. Clean wafers in 99% nitric acid for 10 minutes
44. Rinse wafers with deionized water until resistivity reaches 5 megaohms
45. Dry wafers using rinser dryer

Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Add 400 microliters of ice-cold methanol to 200 microliters of liquid sample

3. Centrifuge sample at 10,000 G for 30 minutes at 4°C
4. Transfer supernatant and collect pellet in 1.5 milliliter tubes

6. Homogenize soft tissue once for 20 seconds
7. Homogenize stiff tissue twice for 20 seconds with 5-minute interval between homogenizations
8. Place sample on ice
9. Transfer all lysate to new 1.5 milliliter tube
10. Incubate sample
11. Centrifuge sample at 10,000 G for 30 minutes at 4°C
12. Select supernatant in 1.5 milliliter tubes
13. Truncate caps of each tube
14. Add 500 microliters of nine molar hydrochloric acid to each sample
15. Transfer tubes into glass tubes
16. Close tubes tightly with caps ensuring proper sealing
17. Place sealed tubes into glass beaker partly filled with sand
18. Hydrolyze samples at 110 degrees Celsius for 16 hours in drying chamber
19. Cool samples and lyophilize overnight using high vacuum centrifuge
20. Dissolve completely dried pellets in 1 mL of 0.1 M hydrochloric acid
21. Add 50 μL of chloroform to each tube
22. Thoroughly dissolve pellet using 1,000 μL pipette
23. Transfer complete volume to new tube
24. Centrifuge samples for 10 minutes
25. Carefully collect upper aqueous phase of biphasic liquid in new tube
26. Select plastic tubes for manual solid phase extraction or glass tubes for robotic solid phase extraction
27. Pre-condition cartridges followed by centrifugation for one minute
28. Load sample onto SPE cartridges and centrifuge at 600 G for two minutes at room temperature
29. Wash cartridges with 1 mL water (3 repetitions)
30. Centrifuge cartridges for 1 minute after each water wash (3 repetitions)
31. Wash cartridges with 1 mL of 0.1 M hydrochloric acid (5 repetitions)
32. Centrifuge cartridges for 1 minute at 800 G and room temperature after each acid wash (5 repetitions)
33. Wash cartridges with 1 mL methanol (2 repetitions)
34. Centrifuge cartridges for 1 minute after each methanol wash (2 repetitions)
35. Add 1 mL of 3X replacement solution to elute arginine derivatives into 15 mL tube
36. Centrifuge for elution (2 repetitions of 1 minute each)
37. Place tubes for collection of eluates and glass tubes containing the upper phase of the biphasic liquid into respective robot positions
38. Use software application or method based on manual SPE protocol
39. Lyophilize samples overnight to completely dry and remove ammonia and water
40. Dissolve each sample in 500 microliters of NMR buffer until homogenous
41. Transfer equal amount of homogenous sample to each NMR tube
