question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Add 5 milliliters of methanol to 50 milliliter vial
2. Dissolve 2 milligrams of conjugated polymers in 2 milliliters of chloroform in 5 milliliter vial
3. Place 5 milliliter vial inside 50 milliliter vial
4. Cap 50 milliliter vial
5. Incubate at 25 degrees Celsius for 3 days to precipitate polymer microspheres
6. Dissolve 5 mg of conjugated polymers in 1 ml chloroform
7. Dissolve 30 mg of SDS in 2 ml deionized water
8. Add 100 μl of polymer mixture to SDS solution
9. Homogenize mixture at 30,000 RPM for 2 minutes
10. Allow emulsion to rest uncapped for 1 day to evaporate chloroform
11. Centrifuge dispersion in 1.5 ml tube at 2,200 × g for 5 minutes
12. Remove supernatant
13. Add 1 ml deionized water and shake vigorously
14. Repeat centrifugation, supernatant removal, and water wash three times
15. Dissolve 20 mg polystyrene and 1 mg fluorescent dye in 20 ml tetrahydrofuran
16. Add 1 ml deionized water to a 1.5 ml microtube
17. Gently pour 0.2 ml THF solution onto water layer while preventing mixing
18. Leave uncapped vial undisturbed for 6 hours to precipitate polymer microspheres
19. Dilute suspension of prepared microspheres by 10 times with poor solvent (methanol or deionized water)
20. Spin cast one drop of diluted suspension onto quartz substrate using spin coater
21. Air dry casted film until solvents evaporate completely
22. Transfer quartz substrate to optical microscope sample stage
23. Identify isolated and well-defined microsphere for micro-photoluminescence measurement
24. Set laser conditions as per text protocol
25. Irradiate microsphere with focused laser beam
26. Record photoluminescence spectrum using spectrometer
27. Take fluorescent image of irradiated microsphere
28. Place quartz substrate onto optical microscope sample stage
29. Identify single well-defined microsphere
30. Set tungsten microneedle on micromanipulation apparatus
31. Move microneedle using touch panel controller
32. Pick up identified microsphere with microneedle
33. Connect collected microsphere to another single well-defined microsphere
34. Irradiate microsphere with focused laser beam
35. Record photoluminescence spectrum using spectrometer
36. Take fluorescent image of irradiated microspheres

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Cut fresh citrus leaf with clean scissors and place in clean plastic sandwich bag
2. Chill mortar and pestle by adding small quantity of liquid nitrogen
3. Cut leaf tissue into small pieces (~1 square inch) using scissors
4. Place leaf pieces in chilled mortar to freeze immediately
5. Grind leaf tissue with mortar until liquid nitrogen evaporates and fine green powder forms
6. Chill metal spatula in liquid nitrogen for 10-15 seconds
7. Transfer ground tissue from mortar to 1.5mL micro-centrifuge tube using chilled spatula
8. Add 600 mL of nuclei lysis solution to sample using 1000 mL pipette
9. Vortex sample for 1-3 seconds
10. Incubate tube at 65°C in water bath for 15 minutes
11. Add 3 mL of RNA solution to lysate using 10 mL pipette
12. Mix tube contents by inverting 2-5 times
13. Incubate at 37°C for 15 minutes in cabinet incubator
14. Add 200 mL protein precipitation solution to sample
15. Vortex tube at high speed for 20 seconds
16. Centrifuge sample at 13000G for 3 minutes
17. Add 600 mL room-temperature isopropanol to new 1.5 mL micro-centrifuge tube
18. Remove supernatant from centrifuge tube
19. Transfer supernatant to tube containing isopropanol
20. Discard pellet
21. Invert tube to visualize DNA as thread-like strand
22. Centrifuge sample at 13000G for 1 minute at room temperature
23. Decant supernatant after centrifugation
24. Add 600 mL of 70% ethanol to pellet
25. Invert tube several times to wash pellet
26. Centrifuge tube at 13000G for one minute
27. Carefully aspirate supernatant
28. Invert tube with lid open on absorbent paper to air-dry for 15 minutes
29. Add 100 mL of DNA rehydration solution to the pellet
30. Incubate DNA suspension at 65°C in water bath for 1 hour
31. Tap tube periodically while mixing
32. Add 1 mL purified water to micro-cuvette
33. Read water as blank on spectrophotometer
34. Add 1 mL sample to micro-cuvette
35. Place micro-cuvette on spectrophotometer and obtain DNA concentration
36. Add all PCR reagents and 100 nanograms of genomic DNA to a 50mL PCR tube
37. Mix reagents by gently flicking the PCR tube
38. Perform quick centrifugation of PCR tube
39. Place PCR tube in thermocycler and run program
40. Weigh 0.4 grams of agarose powder
41. Add agarose powder to 200ml Erlenmeyer flask
42. Adjust volume to 50ml with 1X TAE buffer
43. Microwave mixture on high for 1.5 minutes
44. Shake mixture every 30 seconds until agarose dissolves completely
45. Add 2.5 mL of 10 mg/mL ethidium bromide to liquid agarose
46. Shake flask to mix ethidium bromide
47. Pour liquid agarose gel into level gel mold
48. Insert standard 12-tooth gel comb
49. Let gel cool at room temperature
50. Remove gel comb
51. Place solidified gel in electrophoresis unit filled with 1X TAE buffer
52. Load amplified DNA sample and 5μL DNA ladder in separate wells
53. Start electrophoresis unit at constant 90 volts for 35 minutes
54. Remove gel from electrophoresis unit
55. Place gel on UV transilluminator
56. Capture photographs of gel banding patterns at 302 nanometers

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Line tissue culture hood with under pad
2. Place 1L beaker with stir bar, 150mm sterile tissue culture dish, and whole kidney into hood
3. Fill beaker with 500ml of 1% SDS solution
4. Place kidney in sterile tissue culture dish
5. Remove perirenal fat by lightly shaving around renal capsule with scalpel
6. Make shallow 8-10cm incision across superior end of kidney to open renal capsule without damaging cortex tissue
7. Use two hemostat clamps to remove renal capsule by peeling away from cortex tissue
8. Bisect kidney along coronal plane using scalpel along lateral side
9. Isolate cortex tissue from both halves by carving out medullar region with scalpel
10. Dice cortex tissue into 0.5 centimeter cubed pieces
11. Remove any large visible vessels
12. Place diced cortex tissue into beaker containing SDS solution
13. Cover beaker with autoclaved aluminum foil
14. Place covered beaker on stir plate at 400 RPM outside tissue culture hood
15. Bring beaker into tissue culture hood after 24 hours of stirring
16. Bring beaker into tissue culture hood
17. Add 40 micron sterile nylon mesh cell strainer
18. Fill separate 1000 milliliter beaker with 200 milliliters of bleach
19. Place bleach-containing beaker in tissue culture hood
20. Dispose of SDS solution through cell strainer into bleach-containing beaker
21. Pipet out remaining SDS solution until only decellularized tissue and cell strainer remain
22. Transfer decellularized tissue (kidney ECM) into 30 milliliter self-standing conical tube
23. Fill conical tube with cell culture grade water until all tissue is submerged
24. Homogenize kidney ECM in conical tube using tissue homogenizer for 2 minutes until opaque solution with no visible ECM pieces is obtained
25. Submerge conical tube containing kidney ECM in liquid nitrogen
26. Store kidney ECM at -4°C overnight
27. Slightly loosen conical tube cap to allow gas exchange
28. Place tube into lyophilization machine
29. Lyophilize kidney ECM for three days or until powdered
30. Add carefully weighed porcine gastric pepsin
31. Add 0.01 normal hydrochloric acid
32. Transfer mixture to scintillation vial containing stir bar
33. Stir mixture at approximately 500 RPM
34. Continue stirring until all pepsin dissolves
35. Transfer lyophilized kidney ECM to scintillation vial
36. Agitate solution on stir plate at ~500 RPM for three days
37. Pipet required volumes of cell culture media, sodium hydroxide, and M199 supplement in tissue culture hood
38. Transfer mixture to sterile 30 milliliter self-standing conical tube
39. Mix neutralizing reagent solution with micro spatula
40. Transfer appropriate volume of stock kidney ECM hydrogel to neutralizing reagent using sterile 1 milliliter syringe
41. Gently mix solution with micro spatula until homogeneous hydrogel forms
42. Avoid air bubbles by stirring slowly and gently
43. Resuspend 300,000 cells in 10 microliters of medium per hydrogel
44. Pipet 10 microliters of cell suspension into final kidney ECM gel
45. Stir solution with micro spatula until cells are evenly distributed
46. Fill 1 mL syringe with kidney ECM hydrogel
47. Transfer hydrogel from syringe to cell culture device
48. Incubate hydrogel-filled device at 37°C for 1 hour to set gel
49. Prepare cells for transfer onto set hydrogel in culture device

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Grind 200 milligrams of plant tissue sample to powder in liquid nitrogen
2. Add 1 milliliter of RNA extraction reagent (containing guanidine thiocyanate and acid phenol) to ground tissue
3. Mix sample well by inversion until all tissue is wet
4. Centrifuge samples at room temperature for 10 minutes
5. Transfer supernatant to new 1.5 milliliter tube
6. Add 200 microliters of 1-bromo-3-chloropropane to supernatant
7. Vigorously vortex sample
8. Centrifuge sample to separate phases
9. Transfer approximately 500 microliters of upper aqueous phase to new 1.5 milliliter tube
10. Add 500 microliters of isopropanol and 50 microliters of three molar sodium acetate to sample
11. Mix sample well by inversion
12. Incubate sample at -20°C for 10 minutes
13. Precipitate RNA by centrifugation
14. Discard supernatant
15. Wash pellet twice with 500 microliters of 80% ethanol
16. Wash pellet once with 500 microliters of 99% ethanol
17. Dry pellet for 5-10 minutes
18. Dissolve pellet in 30 microliters of RNase-free water
19. Measure RNA concentration using spectrophotometer
20. Digest 20 micrograms of RNA sample in DNase water and buffer solution
21. Incubate sample for 30 minutes at 37 degrees Celsius
22. Add appropriate volume of DNase inactivation reagent to remove enzyme
23. Check quality and purity of isolated RNA by capillary electrophoresis according to standard protocols
24. Add at least 12 micrograms of each RNA sample to new tubes
25. Spike in 0.1 nanogram of methylated and non-methylated in vitro transcripts per 3 micrograms of RNA sample
26. Adjust final volume to 80 microliters per tube using RNase-free water
27. Sonicate entire volume of each RNA sample in individual sonication tubes
28. Confirm sonication efficiency and RNA concentration by capillary electrophoresis
29. Add 9 µg of each sonicated RNA sample and up to 60 µL RNase-free water into new low binding tubes
30. Heat RNA to 70°C in water bath for 10 minutes
31. Cool samples for 10 minutes in ice water bath
32. Transfer 20 µL of each sample into new low binding tube for -80°C storage as input control
33. Add 50 microliters of 10X MeRIP buffer to each sample
34. Add 10 microliters of RNase inhibitor to each sample
35. Add 10 microliters of anti-M5C antibody to immunoprecipitation tubes
36. Add 10 microliters of water to mock tubes
37. Seal tubes with parafilm
38. Incubate samples at 4°C with overhead rotation for 12-14 hours
39. Add 40 microliters of beads to one 15 milliliter tube per sample
40. Perform three 5-minute washes with 800 microliters of MeRIP buffer using overhead rotation per sample
41. Collect beads on magnet after each wash
42. Discard buffer after each wash
43. Resuspend beads in 40 microliters of fresh MeRIP buffer per sample
44. Add 40 microliters of resuspended beads to immunoprecipitation and mock sample tubes
45. Incubate tubes for two hours at four degrees Celsius with overhead rotation
46. Place tubes on magnetic rack for one minute
47. Carefully discard supernatant without disturbing beads
48. Wash beads five times in 700 microliters of MeRIP buffer supplemented with 0.01% Tween-20 for 10 minutes per wash at room temperature with overhead rotation
49. Resuspend beads in 200 microliters of proteinase K digestion buffer and 3.5 microliters of proteinase K
50. Incubate suspension for three hours at 50 degrees Celsius with shaking at 800 revolutions per minute
51. Extract RNA using 800 microliters of RNA extraction reagent via acid guanidinium thiocyanate phenol-chloroform protocol
52. Resuspend extracted RNA pellet in 20 microliters of RNase-free water

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Adjust CV-1 cell concentration to 100,000 cells per milliliter
2. Seed 2 milliliters of cell suspension into each well of six-well plate
3. Transfect cells with 0.5 ng gRNA-N2 plasmid and 0.5 ng pSTCas9 plasmid
4. Incubate transfected cells for 24 hours
5. Replace cell culture medium
6. Dilute backbone VVL15 virus with DMEM to 200,000 PFU per milliliter
7. Add 100 microliters of diluted VVL15 virus into each well of transfected CV-1 cells
8. Transfect cells with 1 microgram of shuttle donor vector for HR expression
9. Incubate transfected cells for 24 hours
10. Detach transfected CV-1 cells using cell scraper
11. Collect cell suspension into cryovial
12. Store cryovial at -80°C for future use
13. Seed 15 six-well plates with 300,000 unmodified CV-1 cells per well
14. Thaw frozen suspension of transfected CV-1 cells at 37 degrees Celsius for 3 minutes
15. Vigorously vortex cell suspension for 30 seconds to lyse cells
16. Dilute 1 microliter of lysate with 3 milliliters of DMEM
17. Add 0.5 milliliter of dilution to each plate well containing unmodified CV-1 cells
18. Return plates to incubator for 2 days
19. Identify RFP-positive plaques using fluorescence microscope with 10X objective
20. Label identified plaques underneath plate using marker pen
21. Prepare cryovial containing 200 microliters of serum-free DMEM for each positive plaque
22. Aspirate all medium from wells with labeled plaques
23. Set 200 microliter pipette to aspirate 30 microliters
24. Partially load pipette with 10 microliters of medium from cryovial
25. Scrape RFP-positive plaque and draw up cells with remaining aspiration
26. Eject harvested material into cryovial
27. Repeat scraping and ejection procedure twice more targeting same plaque
28. Prepare six-well plate with 500,000 CV-1 cells per well
29. Thaw frozen purified plaque at 37°C for 3 minutes
30. Vortex plaque vigorously for 30 seconds to make lysate
31. Add all lysate including cell debris into one well of CV-1 cells
32. Incubate plate for three days
33. Inspect cells daily under microscope
34. Harvest infected cells when >50% show cytopathic effect
35. Collect cells in 15-ml tube with culture medium using cell scraper
36. Pellet cells at 300g for three minutes
37. Discard supernatant
38. Use PCR to verify mutant VV modification
39. Freeze cell pellet at -80°C for later processing

Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Add 500 microliters of hydrogen peroxide to ticks
2. Vortex sample for 15 seconds
3. Wash ticks with 70% ethanol
4. Wash ticks with double-distilled water
5. Transfer ticks to new PCR tube
6. Allow ticks to air dry
7. Place ticks into a new PCR tube
8. Allow ticks to air dry in PCR tube
9. Mechanically disrupt tick tissues by crushing with mortar and pestle
10. Purify DNA from individual ticks using commercial DNA extraction kit following manufacturer instructions
11. Elute purified DNA to final volume of 100 microliters
12. Set up amplicon PCR in 27.5 microliter reaction volume
13. Add 5 microliters of each primer at 1 micromolar concentration
14. Add 12.5 microliters of commercially available PCR mix

16. Move tubes to thermocycler
17. Run thermocycler program listed in text protocol
18. Prepare new PCR tube for each sample
19. Combine PCR product with double-distilled water to achieve total volume of 60 microliters
20. Bring paramagnetic beads to room temperature
21. Vortex paramagnetic beads thoroughly
22. Add 48 microliters of paramagnetic beads to 60 microliters of sample
23. Incubate bead-sample mixture for five minutes
24. Place tubes on magnetic rack for five minutes
25. Wait until solution becomes clear
26. Remove supernatant
27. Place tubes on magnetic rack
28. Add 500 microliters of freshly prepared 80% ethanol to tubes
29. Pipette out liquid from tubes while retaining beads
30. Air dry samples by leaving tubes open on magnetic rack for five minutes or until small cracks appear in beads
31. Add 20 microliters of TE buffer to tubes
32. Incubate samples off magnet at room temperature for five to ten minutes
33. Place tubes back on magnet
34. Transfer supernatant to fresh tube after liquid-bead separation
35. Add 2.5 microliters of each primer to PCR reaction
36. Add 12.5 microliters of commercially available PCR Master Mix
37. Add 5 microliters of double-distilled water
38. Add 2.5 microliters of cleaned amplicon product
39. Move PCR tubes to thermocycler
40. Run thermocycler program as detailed in protocol
41. Load 4-6 microliters of PCR product per sample onto 1.5% agarose gel
42. Prepare 10 microliter qPCR reactions containing 6 microliters qPCR Master Mix, 2 microliters double-distilled water, and 2 microliters sample/standard
43. Move qPCR plate to real-time PCR instrument
44. Perform qPCR run using pre-programmed protocol
45. Create combined library following quantification

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Disinfect forceps using dry heat sterilization at 200 degrees Celsius for one hour
2. Autoclave all glassware at 121 degrees Celsius for 15 minutes
3. Disinfect forehead section of lamp by pouring 100 milliliters of 200 PPM chlorine dioxide solution onto sample area
4. Shave forehead section using electric clippers
5. Wash area with 200 milliliters of 200 PPM chlorine dioxide solution
6. Wipe area using ethanol and blue roll
7. Cover sample area with hair removal cream
8. Gently scrape off hair removal cream using scraping tool
9. Assess sample area
10. Assess sample area using scraping tool
11. Repeat hair removal process if significant hair remains
12. Rinse clean sample area with 200 milliliters of chlorine dioxide solution followed by ethanol and blue roll
13. Use sterile eight-millimeter biopsy punch to cut out eight-millimeter skin sample from prepared area
14. Remove sample using sterile forceps and number 15 blade scalpel
15. Ensure all cutaneous fat is removed from sample
16. Place samples in sterile 0.5 liter jar filled with sterile PBS
17. Transfer samples to sterile 50-milliliter tubes containing 50 milliliters of 200 PPM chlorine dioxide solution
18. Invert tube twice
19. Leave samples to sterilize for 30 minutes
20. Transfer samples to 50-milliliter tube containing 40 milliliters of sterile PBS
21. Place each skin sample in separate well of 24-well plate
22. Add 350 microliters of pre-warmed medium to well

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Streak Candida albicans strain CI4+CLP10 from glycerol stocks onto SC-U agar plate
2. Incubate plate at 30°C until colonies form (typically 48 hours)
3. Store plate at 4°C until ready to use
4. Pick single Candida albicans colony
5. Culture colony in 5ml SCU medium overnight at 30°C on rocker (200 RPM)
6. Add 10 microliters of Candida albicans overnight culture to 990 microliters of PBS at pH 7.4
7. Perform cell count using hemocytometer
8. Stain 100 million Candida with Zy dye for 10 minutes at room temperature in the dark
9. Wash Candida albicans three times in PBS to remove unbound dye
10. Centrifuge Candida at 3000 × g for 5 minutes
11. Remove supernatant after centrifugation
12. Resuspend pellet in 1 milliliter of PBS
13. Adjust final suspension concentration to 1 million cells per microliter in PBS
14. Scrape macrophages from flask
15. Transfer scraped macrophages to 50 ml Falcon tube
16. Centrifuge macrophages at 600 × g for 5 minutes
17. Obtain cell pellet after centrifugation
18. Remove supernatant from centrifuged sample
19. Resuspend pellet in 10 ml pre-warmed supplemented DMEM
20. Count cells using hemocytometer
21. Plate 1 million cells in 35 mm glass-based imaging dish
22. Add 2 ml supplemented DMEM medium to imaging dish
23. Incubate dish overnight at 37°C with 5% CO₂
24. Replace medium with 2 ml pre-warmed CO₂-independent medium
25. Add 1 µM Lysotracker Red DND-99 and supplements to medium
26. Turn on microscope heater to warm control chamber to 37 degrees Celsius
27. Turn on microscope and computer
28. Load imaging software
29. Mount imaging dish on microscope stage
30. Adjust focus to locate J774.1 macrophages
31. Optimize Trixi and DIC images by adjusting percentage of transmitted light and exposure times
32. Remove imaging dish
33. Add 3 million FITC-stained Candida albicans to dish
34. Record time
35. Return dish to microscope stage
36. Optimize FITC image appearance if necessary
37. Set up points of interest in imaging software if required
38. Commence imaging when all points are in focus and channels optimized
39. Capture FITC, TRITC, and DIC images every minute for six hours

6. Rinse model with isopropyl alcohol to remove plastic dust
7. Dry model in chemical hood for 2-3 hours
8. Spray model with transparent lacquer three times
9. Allow lacquer to air-dry for one hour between applications

16. Immerse model in absolute acetone for 48 hours in chemical hood until plastic dissolves
17. Incubate model at 60 degrees Celsius for at least four days to evaporate trapped acetone
18. Sterilize silicone model and inlet/outlet connectors using ultraviolet light or radiation
19. Coat model with 4 mL of 100 μg/mL fibronectin solution using syringe
20. Incubate coated model for 2 hours at 37°C
21. Remove fibronectin solution through outlet
22. Wash model with endothelial cell medium
23. Fill rinsed model with 4 mL of endothelial cell suspension (2.5 × 10⁶ cells/mL) using syringe
24. Secure model to rotator inside cell culture environment
25. Secure model to rotator inside cell culture incubator
26. Rotate model at one revolution per minute for 48 hours
27. Wash model with PBS using 10 milliliter plastic syringe
28. Fix cells with four milliliters of 4% paraformaldehyde for 15 minutes
29. Wash model three times with PBS
30. Store model at four degrees Celsius in four milliliters of fresh PBS
31. Connect oscillation damper to peristaltic pump using outlet tubing
32. Connect peristaltic pump to inlet of cultured carotid model
33. Merge two outlets using piece of tubing
34. Split outlet tubing into two outlet tubes
35. Add plastic clamp to each outlet tube
36. Add 300 milliliters of PBS to closed circuit container
37. Place one inlet tube and one outlet tube into PBS-filled container with clamp B and clamp C open
38. Place other inlet tube into one-liter washing container filled with distilled water
39. Place other outlet tube into empty one-liter waste container with clamp A and clamp D closed
40. Place carotid model under stereo microscope

42. Add 1.6 μg/ml fluorescent carboxylated polystyrene particles to PBS in closed circuit container when flow rate reaches 100 rpm
43. Image region of interest every 10 seconds for 1.5 hours
44. Open clamps A and D
45. Close clamps B and C
46. Flow water from washing container to waste container at 100 revolutions per minute
47. Stop pump before complete water transfer
48. Close tube clamps before inlet and after outlet of carotid model

1. Anesthetize rat via IP injection of urethane

5. Place rat in prone position on homeothermic plate
6. Insert rectal probe to maintain homeothermic plate at 37.5°C
7. Place one ear bar into approximate position within stereotaxic frame
8. Adjust animal to position ear bar in external acoustic meatus
9. Align and place second ear bar into contralateral external acoustic meatus
10. Position animal to align ear bars with tooth holder
11. Open animal's jaw using rat tooth forceps
12. Hook upper incisors over tooth holder
13. Clamp nose in place
14. Make incision in head skin 1mm right of midline, extending 10mm rostral
15. Retract skin and muscle laterally from incision
16. Expose parietal and interparietal bones
17. Scrub exposed bone surface with 20% hydrogen peroxide solution using gauze pad under microscope

22. Saturate wrapped wire with saline

25. Attach electrode array to manipulator using needle tip

27. Manually insert electrode 2mm into brain targeting inferior colus
28. Attach speaker to left hollow ear bar
29. Ensure amplifier is turned on
30. Close door of recording chamber
31. Deliver white noise bursts at maximum rate of one burst every 200 milliseconds
32. Monitor activity on each electrode using motorized micro drive
33. Slowly insert electrode array until acoustically driven activity is recorded on three most distal electrodes per shank
34. Perform acoustic stimulation protocol (300 repetitions of 50ms white noise bursts at 1s repetition rate, 70dB SPL) while recording multi-unit activity at 24.4 kHz sampling rate
35. Slowly insert electrode array 200μm into inferior colliculus to reposition electrodes at consistent recording depth
36. Repeat acoustic stimulation and neural recording protocol
37. Insert electrode array in 100 micron steps while performing acoustic stimulation and neural recording protocol until all electrodes record acoustically driven activity from three positions
38. Retract electrode array in 200 micron steps while performing acoustic stimulation and neural recording protocol until initial position is achieved
39. Manually retract electrode array
40. Rinse electrode array with distilled water to remove contamination
41. Connect electrode array to potentiostat
42. Place electrode array into testing solution
43. Clamp electrode array into place
44. Connect counter and reference electrodes to potentiostat
45. Perform sequential electrochemical impedance spectroscopy and cyclic voltammetry on all electrodes
46. Remove electrode array from testing solution
47. Rinse electrode array gently with deionized water
48. Place electrode array into enzymatic cleaning solution
49. Immerse in enzymatic cleaning solution for 24 hours
50. Repeat cleaning procedures
51. Store probe in dry protective container

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Thaw bottle of basement membrane matrix on ice or at 4°C overnight
2. Transfer resected tissue to 10-centimeter sterile Petri dish
3. Examine tissue macroscopically; document morphological characteristics (fatty/vascularized/necrotic), record size/shape, and photograph with ruler
4. Mince tissue into small pieces using sterile number 10 scalpel
5. Transfer minced tissue to 50-milliliter conical tube
6. Add 10 milliliters of 2 mg/mL collagenase IV solution
7. Seal the tube
8. Place sealed tube on orbital shaker at 37°C, 140 RPM, 30° angle for 30-90 minutes
9. Place aliquot of complete medium in bead/water bath at 37°C to pre-warm
10. Resuspend tissue every 15 minutes by vigorous mixing using 5-mL sterile pre-coated serological pipette
11. Monitor dissociation under microscope at ≥5X magnification
12. Centrifuge sample at 400 G for five minutes
13. Aspirate supernatant
14. Add 10 milliliters of AdDF+++
15. Centrifuge sample and carefully aspirate supernatant
16. Add two milliliters of red blood cell lysis buffer (if tissue pellet is partially red)
17. Incubate for five minutes at room temperature (if tissue pellet is partially red)
18. Add 10 milliliters of AdDF+++ to tube
19. Centrifuge at 400 G for five minutes
20. Discard supernatant
21. Resuspend pellet in 50 to 300 microliters
22. Discard supernatant
23. Resuspend pellet in 50-300 microliters of undiluted cold basement membrane matrix
24. Mix suspension by pipetting carefully to avoid bubble formation
25. Plate 300 microliters of basement membrane matrix dome containing organoids per well in pre-warmed six-well tissue culture plate
26. Leave plate undisturbed in hood for 5 minutes
27. Incubate plate at 37°C for 20-30 minutes for matrix solidification
28. Add 3 ml of pre-warmed complete medium dropwise to each well
29. Incubate plate at 37°C and 5% CO₂
30. Capture organoid images using 5X objective on inverted bright-field microscope
31. Lift basement membrane matrix dome into well medium using cell scraper or 1-ml pipette tip
32. Transfer floating organoid dome with medium to 15-ml or 50-ml conical tube using pre-coated pipette tip
33. Add DPBS to increase volume to at least 5 milliliters
34. Centrifuge tubes at 400 G for 5 minutes to form basement membrane matrix-organoid layer
35. Aspirate supernatant
36. Add 5-20 ml DPBS (volume dependent on number of wells pooled)
37. Mix organoid-basement membrane matrix pellet in DPBS using pre-coated sterile disposable pipette
38. Centrifuge and discard supernatant
39. Add cell dissociation reagent using coated pipette tip at three times basement membrane matrix volume
40. Resuspend organoids in dissociation reagent
41. Place tube on orbital shaker at 37°C and 140 RPM in angled position for 8-15 minutes
42. Monitor dissociation progress under microscope every five minutes to ensure organoid breakdown
43. Add AdDF+++ at volume equal to or greater than cell dissociation reagent
44. Pipette to mix organoids
45. Centrifuge at 400 G for five minutes to obtain organoid pellet
46. Discard supernatant
47. Resuspend pellet in one milliliter of AdDF+++
48. Adjust volume to 10 milliliters with AdDF+++
49. Centrifuge tube for wash step
50. Discard supernatant after wash step
51. Add required amount of basement membrane matrix to digested organoids based on split ratio
52. Mix by gently pipetting up and down to avoid bubbles
53. Immediately place mixture on ice
54. Plate 300-microliter domes of organoid-matrix suspension in pre-warmed six-well plate
55. Leave plate undisturbed in hood for 5 minutes
56. Incubate plate at 37°C with 5% CO₂ for 20-30 minutes for dome solidification
57. Add 3 mL pre-warmed complete medium to each well
58. Place plate back in incubator

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Collect green coffee fruits (120-150 days after flowering) from plantations
2. Select approximately 300 uniformly-sized healthy fruits
3. Withdraw peduncles from selected fruits
4. Dip fruits in 2% soap solution and clean thoroughly by rubbing
5. Rinse fruits three times with clean water
6. Immerse fruits in 0.5% sodium hypochlorite solution
7. Place immersed fruits on shaker at 110 rpm for 10 minutes
8. Rinse fruits three times with clean water
9. Dry fruits using sterile paper towels
10. Place fruits 55 cm from UV source in UV-enabled horizontal laminar flow station for 15-minute irradiation
11. Move fruits every five minutes during irradiation to ensure whole-surface exposure
12. Immerse newly emerged coffee berry borers in 0.5% sodium hypochlorite solution
13. Agitate insects slowly with brush for 10 minutes
14. Filter insects through muslin cloth
15. Wash insects three times with sterile distilled water
16. Remove excess water using sterile paper towels
17. Prepare group of 30 green coffee fruits
18. Prepare 30 green coffee fruits per experimental unit
19. Place prepared fruits in plastic box
20. Apply test product concentrations using portable sprayer unit
21. Spray control group of green coffee fruits with water
22. Spray experimental units sequentially with at least three units per treatment
23. Release two coffee berry borer adults in sterile hood
24. Release two disinfected coffee berry borer adults per green coffee fruit in sterile hood
25. Cover boxes after 30 minutes
26. Transfer plastic boxes with infested fruits to dark incubator/room with controlled conditions
27. Count number of borer-infested fruits and living/dead insects outside fruits in each box after specified days
28. Dissect each green coffee fruit under stereo microscope at 10x magnification
29. Count number of healthy seeds and insect-damaged seeds per fruit
30. Count different coffee berry borer biological stages
31. Observe number of dead insects per seed to determine mortality
32. Release coffee berry borer adults in sterile hood
33. Release coffee berry borer adults to disinfected green coffee fruits in sterile hood
34. Allow infestation at two-to-one ratio for three hours at 21°C
35. Select 46 infested fruits with exposed borer abdomens
36. Place selected fruits in 96-well plastic rack
37. Directly spray treatment on borers perforating fruits
38. Spray three racks three times consecutively
39. Cover racks after 30 minutes
40. Dissect green coffee fruits after 20 days
41. Dissect green coffee fruits under stereo microscope at 10x magnification
42. Count number of healthy seeds and seeds damaged by insects in each fruit
43. Count different coffee berry borer biological stages in each seed
44. Count number of dead insects in each seed
45. Release green coffee fruit adults into plastic boxes
46. Count number of coffee berry borers that fly away from boxes
47. Count number of coffee berry borers that infest green coffee fruits

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Cut 25 gauge stainless steel hypotube for implant cannula
2. Cut 0.013 inch dummy removable internal wire
3. Insert dummy wire into implant cannula until it reaches the end
4. Bend excess wire of dummy wire
5. Cut 70 centimeter flexible polymer microbore tube for injector
6. Cut 30 gauge stainless steel hypotube for injection cannula using rotary tool
7. Insert 3 millimeters of injection cannula into flexible tube
8. Glue joint between injection cannula and flexible tube to secure injector
9. Slide implant cannula onto cannula holder
10. Lower cannula holder to implantation target in rat's brain
11. Attach implanted cannula to rat skull using dental cement
12. Insert prepared dummy into implant cannula
13. Cover all implants by applying dental cement to remaining skull area
14. Attach injector to bicuculline-filled precision glass microsyringe
15. Configure injector settings to 0.35 microliters per minute flow rate
16. Set injector total volume to 0.35 microliters
17. Place rat in experimental cage
18. Remove dummy and insert injector into implanted cannula
19. Start infusion pump machine
20. Monitor tic initiation and termination times using stopwatch
21. Remove injector after one minute of injection
22. Reinsert dummy cannula slowly
23. Cut a 12 millimeter 25 gauge stainless steel hypotube to prepare cannula guide
24. Cut a 30 gauge stainless steel hypotube to prepare infusion cannula
25. Insert 0.005 inch diameter wire into infusion cannula
26. Bend inserted wire into L-shape at intended location
27. Cut eight centimeter polyethylene PE10 tube to prepare flexible catheter tubing
28. Remove inner wire from infusion cannula
29. Glue cannula guide onto 3mm overlap near bent part of infusion cannula using cyanoacrylate glue and accelerator
30. Insert horizontal part of infusion cannula onto catheter tubing
31. Eject translucent cap from pump flow moderator
32. Immerse tubing adapter in 70% alcohol
33. Attach tubing adapter to short cannula part of flow moderator until contact with white flange
34. Insert flexible catheter tubing into tubing adapter while securing long cannula part with clip stand
35. Apply cyanoacrylate glue with accelerator to all connections
36. Wrap mini osmotic pump with paper wipe
37. Fix pump vertically with opening facing upwards
38. Secure pump using clip holder stand
39. Fill pump with aCSF using 27-gauge blunt-needle syringe
40. Avoid air entry during pump filling
41. Fill long cannula of infusion tube with aCSF using 27-gauge blunt-needle syringe
42. Insert infusion tube into pump
43. Place pump in saline beaker
44. Create subcutaneous pocket in rat's back by alternatively opening and closing alcohol-sterilized hemostat under skin through mid scapular line
45. Remove pump from water bath
46. Place pump on rat's back covered with paper wipe
47. Slide cannula guide of infusion tube onto cannula holder
48. Hold pump with hemostat
49. Gently insert pump into subcutaneous pocket
50. Implant infusion cannula in target and glue to skull using gel glue
51. Fix infusion cannula ensuring catheter is kink-free to allow neck movement
52. Cover all implants with dental cement
53. Inject bicuculline into pump using 27 gauge blunt needle syringe while removing syringe to prevent air entry
54. Insert flow moderator into pump and place pump in saline beaker in 37°C water bath for 4-6 hours
55. Make incision on skin above implanted pump
56. Wash surgical pocket with room temperature aCSF and dry with gauze pads
57. Detach aCSF-filled pump from flow moderator using hemostat and discard pump
58. Detach and discard flow moderator from bicuculline-filled pump
59. Attach bicuculline-filled pump to implanted flow moderator
60. Glue incision line with tissue adhesive

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Place gallium-68 labeling kit on labeling module according to manufacturer specifications
2. Position materials in correct locations on module
3. Attach solutions from gallium-68 labeling kit to manifolds
4. Attach sterile vial with non-vented filter to labeling module output
5. Place vial in lead shield rated for positron emitters
6. Connect output of gallium-68 generator to input of labeling module
7. Dissolve 50 micrograms of peptide analog into 1.5 milliliters of 1.5 molar HEPES buffer solution
8. Transfer dissolved peptide solution to reaction vial
9. Close lead shielding around labeling module
10. Start gallium-68 DOTATATE production via tablet computer attached to labeling module
11. Remove needles with filters from glass vial
12. Close lead shield around vial
13. Place vial in dose calibrator
14. Measure activity of gallium-68 DOTATATE
15. Note activity reference time
16. Remove 0.5 milliliters of labeled peptide from vial for quality control
17. Prepare syringes for patient administration
18. Visually inspect gallium-68 DOTATATE product to ensure colorless liquid without particles
19. Measure pH of solution using pH indicator strip
20. Add 20 microliters of labeled peptide to 500 microliters of water
21. Homogenize dilution by careful shaking of vial
22. Add 5 microliters of dilution to 7cm × 1cm instant thin-layer chromatography silica gel glass fiber paper strip, 1.5cm from bottom
23. Place paper in tube containing 2mL of 50:50 methanol to 1M ammonium acetate solution, ensuring no peptide-solution contact
24. Close tube to prevent evaporation
25. Wait until solvent travels at least 5 cm above peptide application point
26. Cut chromatography paper in half
27. Place bottom and upper paper halves in separate tubes

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Dissociate submerged BME2 organoid culture
2. Aspirate media from culture plates while avoiding contact with culture
3. Trypsinize submerged BME2 organoid culture with recombinant enzyme
4. Add two milliliters of recombinant enzyme per well in six-well plate
5. Mechanically break BME2 by repeated pipetting
6. Incubate plates at 37°C in cell culture incubator for five minutes
7. Transfer suspension to 15 milliliter centrifuge tube
8. Centrifuge at 600 × g for 5 minutes at room temperature
9. Aspirate recombinant enzyme supernatant carefully without disturbing organoid pellet
10. Resuspend organoid pellet in growth medium
11. Add DNase 1 to suspension
12. Incubate suspension for 5 minutes at room temperature
13. Centrifuge at 600 × g for 3 minutes at room temperature
14. Discard media carefully without disturbing organoid pellet
15. Resuspend pellet in fresh growth medium
16. Preheat new black clear bottom 96-well plate at 37°C in cell culture incubator for 10 minutes
17. Prepare cell suspension in PBS
18. Count cells using cell analyzer
19. Aliquot cells from single-cell suspension
20. Pellet cells by centrifugation
21. Discard media
22. Keep cells on ice for approximately one minute
23. Resuspend cells in BME2
24. Tilt pre-warmed black clear bottom 96-well plate towards the body
25. Seed five microliters of cell suspension per well
26. Seed cell domes at six o'clock position of well
27. Fill outer wells at rim of 96-well plate with PBS
28. Seed cell domes in remaining inner wells
29. Incubate freshly seeded cell domes in laminar flow hood for five minutes without moving plate
30. Transfer plate to cell culture incubator
31. Incubate plate at 37 degrees Celsius for 10 minutes
32. Add 100 microliters of growth medium per well to wells containing organoids
33. Add 100 microliters of PBS to outer wells at rim of plate
34. Prepare serial drug dilution of osimertinib in low growth factor media
35. Prepare negative control with low growth factor media and 0.1% DMSO
36. Rotate organoid plate clockwise by 180 degrees to reposition organoids
37. Aspirate growth media using multi-channel apparatus
38. Add 100 microliters of control or drug solution per well
39. Incubate treated organoids at 37 degrees Celsius in cell culture incubator for five days
40. Thaw reagent overnight at 4°C
41. Equilibrate reagent in room temperature water bath for 30 minutes
42. Mix reagent by inverting
43. Add 100 microliters of reagent to each well
44. Mix thoroughly by pipetting up and down with tip positioned at organoid dome
45. Incubate for 5 minutes at room temperature in dark
46. Transfer approximately 150 microliters of lysate to new white opaque 96-well plate using multi-channel pipette
47. Incubate plate for 25 minutes at room temperature in dark
48. Record luminescence using ELISA plate reader
49. Save data in appropriate table format with raw reads, plate layout, and drug recordings

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Aspirate media from cell culture plate
2. Gently wash plate once with 1x PBS
3. Add 2 milliliters of 0.05% Trypsin solution
4. Gently rock plate to facilitate cell detachment
5. Observe cells under microscope during detachment
6. Add ample volume of FBS-containing media to quench trypsin activity
7. Transfer mixture to 50 milliliter tube
8. Count cells using a hemocytometer
9. Centrifuge sample at 200 × g for 3 minutes
10. Ensure cell pellet remains undisturbed
11. Remove media and resuspend pellet in Hank's Balanced Buffer Solution (HBSS) to achieve 5 × 10⁶ cells/mL concentration
12. Filter suspension through 70 μm cell strainer into 5 mL culture tube by placing pipette tip directly on filter and ejecting suspension
13. Count cells again using trypan blue to determine viability
14. Grab tail of immunodeficient mouse and pull into restrainer with back against slit and tail protruding from rear opening
15. Slide restraint ring inward along slit until it engages mouse's mouth and lock into place
16. Identify major tail veins for injection
17. Draw at least 200 microliters of prepared cells into a 1 mL syringe
18. Attach a 30 gauge half needle to syringe
19. Push out air bubbles from syringe
20. Wipe tail injection site with 70% ethanol
21. Pull tail straight for injection
22. Wipe tail injection site with 70% ethanol
23. Pull tail straight
24. Hold tail tip with thumb
25. Support injection point with index finger
26. Insert needle into vein and inject cells
27. Withdraw needle from vein
28. Press clean cotton ball to injection site
29. Palpate tail upwards to push residual sample into circulation
30. Release mouse from restrainer
31. Return mouse to cage
32. Record number of injection trials and number of cells injected in laboratory notebook
33. Determine viability of cells after injection
34. Spin down leftover cells
35. Rinse pellets with PBS once
36. Freeze pellets at -80°C
37. Dissect mice to determine metastasis locations
38. Place euthanized mouse on dissection station
39. Spray mouse generously with ethanol
40. Cut open rib cage to expose lungs and heart
41. Excise lungs and heart and place in PBS
42. Remove heart after entry into circulation
43. Remove the heart
44. Rinse lungs in PBS
45. Observe lung lobes under dissecting microscope
46. Count number of detectable metastases on both sides of all four lung lobes
47. Isolate individual lung metastases by excision using scissors
48. Transfer each metastasis to 70 micrometer cell strainer
49. Mince metastasis using end of sterile needle cap
50. Rinse cell strainer with several milliliters of medium
51. Collect cells passing through strainer filter
52. Transfer collected cells to culture dish (one dish per metastasis)
53. Prepare culture dishes with cells from individual metastases
54. Incubate cells at 37 degrees Celsius for at least four days without disturbance
55. Wash blood and tissue debris from dish with PBS
56. Add fresh medium to culture dish
57. Assess purity of derived cells by immuno-staining with anti-human specific protein antibodies

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Cut soft sponge into 5 cm² section (20-25 mm tall)
2. Cut groove into sponge (5-10 mm deep, width matching fish)
3. Cut 0.3 mm thick polyvinyl chloride to fit over sponge
4. Add valley fold to PVC
5. Position PVC into central groove of sponge
6. Transfer assembly to 160 mm dish with 30 mm walls
7. Fill dish with recovery tank water until PVC is fully immersed
8. Choose injection site and tag color for fish tagging
9. Prepare VIE tags using tagging kit
10. Mix elastomer materials
11. Load mixed elastomer into 3mm syringe with 29-gauge needle
12. Prepare two water tanks for anesthesia and recovery
13. Place air stone in recovery tank to mildly circulate water
14. Prepare 1:1 mixture stock of 2-methylquinoline and 99.5% ethanol
15. Prepare sufficient stock for 18 ppm 2-methylquinoline concentration in tank
16. Thoroughly mix stock solution into tank water
17. Transfer individual fish to anesthesia tank
18. Cover anesthesia tank to prevent escape
19. Wait until fish shows no reaction to water vibrations or touch
20. Remove anesthetized fish from tank by hand
21. Use cold water acclimated hand for handling
22. Lift fish from anesthesia tank and take physical measurements
23. Transfer fish back to anesthesia tank if recovery signs appear
24. Transfer small fish to surgical table positioned ventral side down in PVC groove
25. Use binocular microscope for injection if fish is very small
26. Hold large fish by hand for injection
27. Position needle with beveled side outwards and pointed side toward fish
28. Insert needle subcutaneously parallel to body at shallow depth under skin
29. Adjust needle position to ensure tag visibility
30. Inject VIE tag while withdrawing needle, stopping injection before needle bevel reaches entry point to create wide deposition area
31. Transfer injected fish to recovery tank
32. Gently circulate water by hand if recovery exceeds two minutes
33. Return recovered fish to rearing aquarium using net
34. Administer 10 ppm Elbagin in rearing aquarium for three days to prevent infection

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Harvest both testes from an eight week old male mouse
2. Place harvested tissues in 60 millimeter Petri dish containing two milliliters of ice cold PBS
3. Remove tunica albuginea from tissues
4. Gently separate testes with forceps to disperse seminiferous tubules
5. Transfer seminiferous tubules into drop of fresh ice cold PBS in 100 millimeter Petri dish
6. Use forceps to gently untangle tubules
7. Wash tubules in three additional droplets of PBS
8. Place untangled seminiferous tubules into 15 milliliter tube containing 2 milliliters of dissociation buffer
9. Incubate tube at 37°C for 20 minutes
10. Pipette tubules gently 20 times using 1000 microliter pipette
11. Return tube to incubator for 6 minutes
12. Pipette tissues 20 times after incubation
13. Return tube to incubator
14. Pipette tubules 10 times after 3 minutes or until no visible tissue pieces remain
15. Add 10 milliliters of FACS buffer to stop dissociation
16. Centrifuge tissue suspension
17. Wash with 10 milliliters fresh FACS buffer and centrifuge again to remove debris and spermatozoa
18. Re-suspend pellet in 3 ml of FACS buffer
19. Filter cell suspension through 70 micron nylon strainer into 50 ml tube
20. Transfer 10% of cells into new tube on ice as unstained negative control
21. Mix 6 microliters of DCV stain into remaining cell suspension
22. Incubate for 30 minutes at 37°C in dark with gentle shaking every 10 minutes
23. Add 5 microliters of DNase I to cells
24. Filter cells through 35 micron nylon mesh strainer into 5 ml FACS tube on ice
25. Create new experiment in flow analysis software
26. Click 'new density' to create FSC-A vs SSC-A density plot on linear scale
27. Click 'new histogram' to create DCV blue histogram plot on logarithmic scale
28. Click 'start and record' to process unstained sample
29. Adjust forward/side scatter PMT voltages to position unstained cells on FSC/SSC plot scale
30. Adjust jappy channel PMT voltage to position DCV-negative population in first decade of DCV blue histogram
31. Click 'stop' to unload unstained sample
32. Vortex and load stained sample (incomplete action)
33. Click stop to unload unstained sample
34. Vortex stained sample briefly
35. Load stained sample
36. Click next tube to create new worksheet
37. Set cytometer to acquire ≥1×10⁶ events
38. Click start and record to begin acquisition
39. Create FSC-H vs FSC-W density plot (linear scale)
40. Create DCV blue vs DCV red density plot (linear scale)
41. Use polygon tool to draw 'cells' gate on FSC-A vs SSC-A plot to exclude debris
42. Apply 'cells' gate to FSC-H vs FSC-W plot
43. Use rectangle tool to draw 'single cells' gate on FSC-H vs FSC-W plot to exclude non-single cells
44. Apply single cell gate to DCV blue versus DCV red density plot
45. Adjust plot scale to capture extended profile
46. Use polygon tool to draw DCV gate excluding unstained cells and side population
47. Apply DCV gate to DCV blue histogram plot on linear scale
48. Create second DCV blue versus DCV red density plot on linear scale
49. Back gate DCV gate onto plot to locate 1C and 4C populations
50. Create DCV blue versus DCV red density plot on linear scale
51. Use ellipse tool to draw gate on 1C population
52. Use polygon tool to gate 4C population with continuous curve
53. Create another DCV blue versus DCV red density plot on linear scale
54. Use polygon tool to create 4C one gate
55. In forward by side scatter plot, use ellipse tool to create pachytene, diplotene, leptotene, and zygotene spermatocyte gates
56. Create DCV blue versus DCV red color dot plot on linear scale to apply 4C gate and verify population continuity
57. Create forward scatter area versus backscatter area density plot on linear scale and select unified size as pure round spermatid population

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Mount clean 2 cm × 2 cm C-plane sapphire substrate on RF sputtering system sample stage with polished side facing molybdenum target
2. Pump down sample chamber to 3 × 10⁻⁶ Torr
3. Inject argon gas into system at 40 mL/min
4. Stabilize chamber pressure at 5 × 10⁻² Torr
5. Ignite plasma and set power output to 40 W
6. Reduce chamber pressure to 5 × 10⁻³ Torr
7. Manually open molybdenum target shutter
8. Deposit metal for 30 seconds while maintaining constant power output
9. Place substrate in quartz sample holder with metal film facing up
10. Position substrate at center of heating zone in calibrated tube furnace
11. Measure and place 1.5 grams of sulfur powder into alumina heating boat
12. Position sulfur boat 2 cm upstream of heating zone to achieve 120°C at 800°C substrate temperature
13. Close furnace and pump down to 5 × 10⁻³ torr
14. Flow argon gas through furnace at 130 mL/min
15. Stabilize furnace pressure at 0.7 torr
16. Ramp furnace temperature from room temperature to 800°C at 20°C/min
17. Hold furnace at 800°C until sulfur fully evaporates
18. Turn off furnace heat and cool substrate to room temperature under argon flow
19. Mount substrate in RF sputtering system with molybdenum disulfide film facing tungsten target
20. Sputter tungsten on substrate for 30 seconds using same settings as for molybdenum
21. Place tungsten-coated substrate in center of furnace heating zone
22. Place one gram of sulfur powder two centimeters upstream of heating zone
23. Sulfurize tungsten film using same parameters as for molybdenum film
24. Spin coat three drops of polymethyl methacrylate on prepared transition metal dichalcogenide film
25. Perform spin coating at 500 and 800 rotations per minute for 10 seconds each
26. Cure PMMA at 120 degrees Celsius for five minutes
27. Place PMMA-coated substrate in Petri dish filled with deionized water
28. Prepare tweezers with round, flat tips
29. Carefully peel one corner of PMMA with attached TMD film from substrate
30. Transfer substrate to 1M aqueous potassium hydroxide solution heated to 100°C
31. Peel entire PMMA TMD film from substrate using tweezers
32. Scoop PMMA TMD film from potassium hydroxide solution using clean sapphire substrate with polished side facing film
33. Transfer film to beaker of deionized water
34. Perform three sequential transfers of film to fresh deionized water to remove residual potassium hydroxide
35. Obtain 300 nm thick P-type silicon dioxide on silicon substrate patterned with titanium or gold electrodes
36. Immerse patterned substrate in deionized water and attach TMD film to substrate
37. Bake substrate with attached film at 100 degrees Celsius for 3 minutes to evaporate water
38. Cover substrate surface with PMMA to secure film attachment
39. Dry substrate in electronic drying cabinet for eight hours
40. Remove PMMA layer from substrate
41. Fabricate TMD heterostructure transistor using photolithography and reactive ion etching

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Remove medium from wells of HEK 293T cell plate
2. Wash wells of six-well plate with 1 milliliter of PBS
3. Discard PBS and transfer plates to ice
4. Add 200 microliters of lysis buffer to each well
5. Detach and break cells using cell scraper
6. Transfer cell extract from two wells into 1.5 milliliter tube
7. Transfer supernatant into pre-cooled tube
8. Calculate volume of protein extract required for 1.5 mg of proteins
9. Adjust samples to 600 microliters final volume using lysis buffer
10. Keep samples on ice until use
11. Mix beads in storage buffer by flicking the tube
12. Calculate 100 microliters of slurry medium per sample
13. Place calculated volume of slurry into magnetic rack
14. Remove storage solution from beads
15. Add one milliliter of lysis buffer to beads and invert manually
16. Remove buffer using magnetic rack
17. Repeat washing step (add lysis buffer and invert)
18. Add volume of lysis buffer equal to initial slurry volume to beads
19. Mix beads by flicking tube
20. Dispense medium uniformly into 1.5 milliliter tubes (one per sample)
21. Remove buffer using magnetic rack
22. Add 600 microliters of 0.25 mg/ml yeast tRNA solution in lysis buffer
23. Incubate for one hour at room temperature on rotating wheel
24. Remove tRNA solution using magnetic rack
25. Add 600 microliters of lysis buffer and wash by manual mixing
26. Prepare 200 micrograms of RNA oligonucleotide in 600 microliters of lysis buffer
27. Add RNA oligonucleotide solution to beads
28. Incubate mixture for one hour at room temperature while rotating
29. Remove solution from beads
30. Wash beads twice with 600 microliters of lysis buffer by rotating tubes for five minutes each at room temperature
31. Discard wash buffer
32. Separate 30 microliters from 600 microliters protein solution
33. Reserve separated protein solution as input sample for analysis
34. Add remaining protein mixture to tubes containing loaded beads
35. Incubate tubes with slow rotation overnight at 4°C
36. Remove unbound protein fraction from beads using magnetic rack

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Gather parts for Rhizobox assembly
2. Cut polyester padding to length for Rhizobox bottom
3. Lay padding on back panel above bottom spacer to prevent leaking
4. Position top panel on spacers to hold padding in place
5. Get hardware for Rhizobox assembly
6. Gather hardware for Rhizobox assembly
7. Prepare screw, two washers, and hex nut for each drilled hole
8. Place washer on front panel and tighten screw through to back panel
9. Add second washer and hex nut to fastener assembly
10. Tighten all screws thoroughly to prevent soil loss
11. Create two spacers for treatment and control patches using high-density polyethylene sheets
12. Secure a screw at the top of each spacer to allow partial insertion
13. Prepare a protective case for light deprivation and heat reduction to enclose the box
14. Build a PVC frame to hold the box at 55 degrees for directing root growth against the back panel
15. Label two small ziptop bags per Rhizobox for treatment and control patches
16. Weigh 30 grams of soil-sand substrate using scale
17. Transfer 30 grams substrate into control bag
18. Add 30 grams substrate to treatment bag
19. Weigh 1 gram nitrogen-15 labeled nitrogen source
20. Add nitrogen-15 source to treatment bag
21. Thoroughly mix substrate and nitrogen source in treatment bag
22. Insert two spacers into Rhizobox until screw prevents further motion
23. Mark depth of spacer bottom edge on Rhizobox side
24. Remove spacers from Rhizobox
25. Fill Rhizobox with substrate to marked depth using funnel, moving it back and forth slowly for uniform distribution
26. Place spacers into Rhizobox positioned 5cm from left and right sides
27. Continue filling Rhizobox with substrate until 5cm below top rim
28. Arrange slow irrigation of substrate with 150 milliliters of water using drip emitters or manual method
29. Let substrate rest several hours or overnight for water diffusion and thorough wetting around spacers
30. Remove spacers to create empty cavities for patches
31. Tape transparency film to outside of box
32. Label one patch as treatment and other as control on transparency film
33. Fill control patch using funnel with prepared control substrate
34. Fill treatment patch using funnel with prepared treatment substrate
35. Trace boundary of each patch on transparency film using permanent marker
36. Fill Rhizobox evenly with remaining substrate from large bag
37. Trace top of substrate level on transparency film using permanent marker
38. Transplant germinated seed into prepared box
39. Dig 2.5 cm deep hole at center of Rhizobox using narrow spatula
40. Place germinated seed in hole with radical oriented directly downward
41. Trace seed location on transparency attached to box
42. Cover seed and water with up to 50 ml deionized water
43. Enclose box in light-blocking case to prevent root light exposure
44. Transport box to growth frame
45. Position Rhizobox on frame to encourage growth toward back frame

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Crush 10 grams of commercial di-iodine pentoxide crystals to consistent powder using mortar and pestle
2. Spread crushed powder in ceramic crucible
3. Heat crucible at 10°C per minute to 250°C and hold for 5 minutes to remove iodic acid
4. Place 3 grams of dried di-iodine pentoxide powder in glass beaker containing magnetic stirrer
5. Add 3 grams of distilled water to beaker
6. Stir mixture for 20 minutes to form aqueous iodate solution
7. Heat solution in oven to 250°C at 20°C/minute rate
8. Maintain solution at 250°C for 10 minutes
9. Prepare aqueous iodate solution
10. Stir solution in low humidity environment until water evaporates and iodic acid dehydrate precipitates
11. Place solid iodic acid sample in differential scanning calorimeter
12. Heat sample to 250°C at 10°C per minute
13. Mix oxidizer with 80 nanometer aluminum in beaker to achieve 2 grams total weight
14. Add 60 milliliters of isopropanol to beaker
15. Sonicate mixture for 2 minutes
16. Pour sonicated mixture into glass dish in fume hood with 20% relative humidity atmosphere
17. Allow solvent to evaporate for 24 hours
18. Ground razor blade with conductive wire
19. Remove dry mixture from glass dish using grounded razor blade
20. Sieve mixture into airtight container
21. Sieving and mixing 80 nanometer aluminum and oxidizer to prepare a two-gram sample with fuel oxygen equivalence ratio of one
22. Sealing the mixture in an airtight container
23. Placing sealed container on vibration table for three minutes
24. Heat alumina crucibles to 1500 degrees Celsius for 30 minutes to remove residues
25. Weigh crucibles and record weights
26. Place 10 mg of each mixture into a crucible
27. Place sample and reference crucibles on DSC-TGA thermocouple
28. Heat sample at 10°C/min to 600°C in argon atmosphere for thermal equilibrium analysis
29. Cover one end of each quartz tube with electrical tape
30. Weigh each tube
31. Place powder sample into tubes using a small spatula
32. Tap tube on hard surface to settle powder after each addition
33. For low bulk density samples, use narrow rod to pack down powder
34. Prepare three tubes for each mixture
35. Add powder to each of three tubes until weights are within 5% and tubes are completely filled
36. Cut nickel chromium wire
37. Cut nickel chromium wire into 10 centimeter long segments
38. Bend wire segments into v shape
39. Insert v-shaped wire into each filled tube
40. Tape tube shut to secure wire and powder
41. Prepare combustion chamber with viewing ports and ventilation system routed through fume hood
42. Position high-speed camera perpendicular to flame propagation direction
43. Focus camera on sample tube
44. Adjust lens and camera placement to enable lowest resolution
45. Place ruler in camera field of view
46. Take snapshot for distance calibration
47. Reduce exposure time
48. Set up neutral density filters to prevent overexposure
49. Connect voltage generator to wires via insulated leads
50. Seal the chamber
51. Set voltage generator to 10 volts
52. Start high-speed camera
53. Turn on voltage generator to initiate reaction
54. Export video into tracking program
55. Track position of flame front as function of time
56. Plot linear trend line from distance and time data
57. Remove data points collected before reaction achieved steady state

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Boil 0.4-0.6 mm thick viscose non-woven fibrous material
2. Dry boiled viscose material
3. Iron dried viscose material
4. Cut material into 11 mm circles using metal hole punch
5. Place cut fibrous material between two slides
6. Wrap slides in foil
7. Autoclave foil-wrapped slides
8. Place inserts into wells of sterile 12-well plate
9. Place each sterilized scaffold into corresponding insert
10. Mix 2.7 × 10⁵ fibroblasts in 100 microliters fetal calf serum with 100 microliters freshly diluted thrombin per scaffold
11. Dispense thrombin-fibroblast mixture onto each scaffold
12. Add 200 microliters freshly diluted fibrinogen to each scaffold
13. Mix cell mixture within each well using gentle pipetting to distribute solution evenly over scaffold
14. Prepare test gel in control well without scaffold to track clot formation time
15. Assess clot formation using pipette tip or by inverting plate
16. Submerge organ cultures in medium supplemented with ascorbic acid and TGF-beta 1
17. Replace medium with RFAD medium containing 500 units/mL aprotinin
18. Seed 2.5 × 10⁵ mTEC suspended in 100 μL RFAD medium onto each scaffold
19. Incubate co-cultures for 24 hours
20. Replace medium with fresh medium containing 250 units per milliliter of aprotinin
21. Use forceps to separate scaffold dermal equivalent from filter
22. Embed OTCs in OCT compound
23. Freeze OTCs in gaseous phase over liquid nitrogen
24. Separate scaffold tissue from filters
25. Cut OTCs into pieces using scalpel
26. Transfer OTC pieces into 2 ml RNA-free tube containing 1 ml denaturing solution
27. Mechanically shred OTCs using fast prep instrument at 6.0 speed for 30 seconds
28. Perform second shredding cycle at 6.0 speed for 30 seconds after 2-minute rest on ice
29. Isolate RNA using acid guanidinium thiocyanate-phenol-chloroform extraction per manufacturer protocol
30. Remove membrane and separate scaffold from fibrin fibroblast mtech gel
31. Cut gel with scalpel
32. Transfer tissue into fax tube
33. Add two milliliters of collagenase dis paste
34. Place tube in 37°C water bath with magnetic stirring for 20 minutes
35. Agitate mixture by pipetting once every five minutes
36. Filter cell suspension through 70 micrometer strainer
37. Stain cells using appropriate antibodies

Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Prepare inoculum in 50 microliters of saline
2. Fill 1 mL syringe fitted with sterile bent gavage needle with inoculum

13. Insert needle into trachea until bend reaches front incisors
14. Push plunger evenly to deliver inoculum without bubbling
15. Withdraw needle from trachea immediately after delivery
16. Hold mouse upright for several seconds to facilitate lung inhalation
17. Place mouse near heating lamp for recovery until awake
18. Pin arms and legs of euthanized mouse to dissection board
19. Make midline incision on ventral side with scissors
20. Gently pull skin to expose peritoneum
21. Make incisions on peritoneum to expose abdominal organs
22. Hold tip of sternum with forceps
23. Puncture diaphragm with scissors
24. Cut diaphragm along rib cage sides with scissors parallel to dissection board
25. Cut through thoracic cavity along dorsoventral line
26. Lift ribcage on right side of heart
27. Identify lymph nodes under thymus and near tracheal blood vessel
28. Harvest lymph nodes into 1 ml digestion mix
29. Place lymph node sample on ice
30. Harvest lungs into six-well plate
31. Aspirate PBS from six-well plate kept on ice
32. Chop lung lobes into small pieces using forceps and scissors
33. Hold lymph node down with 26-gauge needle
34. Tease lymph node apart with second 26-gauge needle
35. Add 3 milliliters of digestion mix to lungs
36. Add 1 milliliter of digestion mix to lymph nodes
37. Incubate lymph nodes for 25-30 minutes at 37°C
38. Incubate lungs for 30 minutes at 37°C
39. Add EDTA to final concentration of 10 millimolar to digestion mixtures
40. Place digestion mixtures on ice
41. Pass pieces of lungs through 18-gauge needle using 3ml syringe
42. Mash lung and lymph node pieces through 70-100 micron cell strainers three times
43. Wash cell strainers with 10ml HBSS without calcium and magnesium using back end of 1ml syringe plunger
44. Centrifuge cell samples at 500 × g
45. Resuspend cell pellets in 5 milliliters of red blood cell lysis buffer
46. Incubate suspension for 3 minutes at room temperature
47. Add 10 milliliters of HBSS without calcium and magnesium to quench lysis
48. Centrifuge samples at 500 × g for 5 minutes
49. Bring lung single-cell suspension to 10 milliliters and lymph node suspension to 5 milliliters

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Document weight of human brain tissue
2. Remove meninges with forceps under microscope
3. Cut off white matter with scalpel
4. Mince brain tissue with scalpel for approximately five minutes
5. Transfer minced brain tissue to tissue grinder
6. Add 30 milliliters of isolation buffer to tissue grinder
7. Homogenize each sample with 100 strokes at 50 rpm
8. Avoid stirring in air to prevent bubble formation
9. Document time every 25 strokes and total time for 100 strokes
10. Transfer homogenate to Dounce homogenizer on ice
11. Homogenize suspension for 20 strokes while avoiding bubbles
12. Distribute brain homogenate equally into four 50 mL centrifugation tubes
13. Document total volume of homogenate
14. Rinse pestle and homogenizer with 10 milliliters of isolation buffer
15. Distribute isolation buffer into four centrifugation tubes
16. Distribute 50 milliliters of density gradient buffer into centrifugation tubes
17. Tightly close centrifuge tubes with caps
18. Vigorously shake tubes to mix homogenate, density gradient medium, and buffer
19. Centrifuge at 5,800xg for 15 minutes at 4°C
20. Discard supernatant
21. Resuspend each pellet in 2 milliliters of 1% BSA
22. Filter suspension through 300 micrometer mesh
23. Wash mesh with up to 50 milliliters of 1% BSA
24. Discard mesh
25. Distribute capillary filtrate over five 30 micrometer cell strain filters
26. Wash each filter with 25 milliliters of 1% BSA
27. Pour all filtrates over sixth filter
28. Wash each filter with 50 milliliters of 1% BSA
29. Retain cell strain filters containing capillaries
30. Discard filtrate
31. Turn filters upside down
32. Wash capillaries with 50 milliliters of 1% BSA into 50 milliliter tubes
33. Gently apply pressure with pipette tip across filter surface
34. Move pipette tip across filter to wash off brain capillaries
35. Ensure complete washing of brain capillaries from filter rim
36. Centrifuge all samples at 1,500 × g for 3 minutes at 4°C
37. Remove supernatant from centrifuged samples
38. Resuspend pellet in approximately 3 ml of isolation buffer
39. Combine all resuspended pellets from one sample in a 15 ml conical tube
40. Fill 15 ml conical tube with isolation buffer
41. Centrifuge combined sample at 1,500 × g for 3 minutes at 4°C
42. Document capillary purity using microscope at 100× magnification with camera

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Wash protein pellets twice with acetone via centrifugation at 20,500 G at 4°C for 10 minutes
2. Decant acetone from tube
3. Remove residual acetone with micro pipette
4. Add 3 mL fresh ice-cold acetone to tube using glass serological pipette
5. Mix tube contents by inverting several times
6. Air-dry pellets for 1-2 minutes (avoiding over-drying)
7. Add 1 mL of buffer B
8. Add 1 milliliter of Buffer B to dried protein pellet
9. Sonicate pellet in bath sonicator (250 W output, 15-30 second intervals) with brief vortexing
10. Perform Bicinchoninic acid (BCA) assay to measure protein concentration
11. Transfer 500 micrograms of protein sample to a 0.5 milliliter, 10 kilodaltons centrifugal filter
12. Adjust sample volume to 500 microliters with resuspension buffer
13. Centrifuge protein-buffer mixture at 14,000 G at room temperature until retentate volume is less than 100 microliters
14. Collect sample by inverting centrifugal filter into a collection tube
15. Centrifuge inverted filter at 1000 G for 2 minutes
16. Add buffer C to achieve final volume of 500 microliters
17. Add buffer C to achieve final volume of 500 microliters
18. Add 20 microliters of 500 mM dithiothreitol (DTT) to sample
19. Adjust mixture to achieve final DTT concentration of 20 mM
20. Incubate samples for 30 minutes at 37°C with shaking at 850 RPM
21. Centrifuge samples in 0.5 mL 10 kDa centrifugal filters at 14,000 G and room temperature until volume <100 μL
22. Add Buffer D to adjust volume to 500 μL
23. Repeat centrifugation at 14,000 G and room temperature
24. Invert centrifugal filter into collection tube after fourth centrifugation
25. Centrifuge inverted filter at 1000 G for 2 minutes to collect sample
26. Measure protein concentration using BCA assay
27. Place spin columns on vacuum manifold
28. Place spin columns on vacuum manifold
29. Transfer 500 microliters of freshly prepared thiol affinity resin slurry to each column using micro pipette
30. Apply vacuum to remove water
31. Repeat resin transfer and vacuum dehydration once to obtain 30 milligrams of resin per column
32. Wash resin five times with 500 microliters of ultrapure water using vacuum for water removal
33. Wash resin five times with 500 microliters of buffer E using vacuum for liquid removal
34. Add buffer C to new tube
35. Add Buffer C to 150 micrograms of reduced protein sample in a new tube
36. Adjust mixture to final volume of 120 microliters
37. Transfer protein solution to plugged spin column containing resin
38. Place cap on spin column
39. Incubate column for two hours at room temperature with shaking at 850 rpm
40. Wash resin according to manuscript protocol
41. Add 120 microliters of freshly prepared trypsin solution to the samples
42. Incubate the column overnight at 37 degrees Celsius with shaking at 850 RPM
43. Add 40 microliters of 100 millimolar Triethylammonium bicarbonate buffer to washed resin using micro pipette
44. Add 70 microliters of dissolved TMT labeling reagent
45. Incubate mixture for 1 hour at room temperature with shaking at 850 RPM
46. Store remaining TMT reagent at -80°C
47. Add 100 microliters of 100 mM ammonium bicarbonate buffer (pH 8.0, 20 mM DTT) to column
48. Incubate column on thermal mixer at room temperature for 30 minutes at 850 RPM
49. Place column on vacuum manifold and apply vacuum to elute samples into 5 mL microcentrifuge tube
50. Repeat vacuum elution step once
51. Add 100 microliters of 80% acetonitrile with 0.1% trifluoroacetic acid to column
52. Incubate column for 10 minutes at room temperature
53. Elute sample into same 5 mL centrifuge tube using vacuum manifold
54. Place eluted samples in vacuum concentrator
55. Concentrate samples until dry using vacuum concentrator
56. Store dry peptides at -80°C

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Open capsule containing silicon nitride membrane support
2. Gently squeeze capsule to loosen membrane support
3. Grasp one corner of silicon frame with thin tweezers (avoiding membrane contact)
4. Place frame into uncovered Petri dish in laminar flow cabinet
5. UV sterilize membrane for 25 to 30 minutes
6. Add 10 microliters of poly-L-lysine attachment factor
7. Incubate for 25 minutes at 37°C and 5% CO₂ with humidity
8. Fill wells of sterile 48-well plate with 200-250 microliters of 0.22 μm filtered ultra-pure water per well
9. Use fine tweezers to submerge membrane vertically
10. Perform three successive 10-second rinses in one water well
11. Place rinsed membrane vertically in empty well of sterile 96-well plate under laminar flow hood
12. Leave membrane overnight until dry
13. Place coated membrane with flat side facing up in one well of sterile four-well plate
14. Add 5×10⁴ cells in 10 microliters of culture medium to silicon nitride membrane surface without touching membrane
15. Culture cells at 37°C, 5% CO₂, and humidity
16. Add 1 ml of complete culture medium slowly down the wall of each well to cover membranes
17. Turn on automatic plunge freezer
18. Fill appropriate number of wells in 12-well plastic plate with 37°C ammonium acetate buffer
19. Remove sample from incubator
20. Use black clamp ring of quick-release tweezers to unlock tweezers
21. Grasp membrane support with unlocked tweezers
22. Vertically immerse membrane in ammonium acetate buffer solution for 5 seconds
23. Open environmental chamber door
24. Slide tweezers into forceps interlock
25. Close environmental chamber door
26. Press blot A plunge button to trigger mechanism
27. Plunge tweezers holding silicon nitride membrane into cryogen
28. Remove lid of transfer container using pre-cooled forceps
29. Press transfer button/mechanism
30. Slide and tilt tweezers out of forceps interlock
31. Place tweezers into empty cryobox slot in nitrogen-filled transfer container
32. Release black clamp ring to free membrane
33. Power on freeze drying instrument using rear panel rocker switch
34. Mount silicon nitride membrane brass holder onto supplier-provided sample transfer holder in liquid nitrogen-filled polystyrene container
35. Use rod to lock full assembly
36. Press enter to break vacuum
37. Release lid of freeze dryer chamber
38. Transfer sample into freeze dryer chamber
39. Close freeze dryer chamber lid
40. Press start to initiate freeze drying cycle

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Harvest spleens and lymph nodes from donor mice
2. Purify CD4-positive T cells from harvested tissues
3. Adoptively transfer purified donor T cells into NOD/SCID recipient mice
4. Monitor recipient mice for type one diabetes development
5. Soak donor mouse fur with 70% ethanol
6. Cut skin and retract to expose front limb muscles
7. Identify axillary lymph nodes near front limb base
8. Grasp lymph node with small tweezers
9. Remove lymph node by trimming connective tissue
10. Make 1-inch peritoneal incision with small scissors
11. Grasp spleen at center
12. Trim connective tissue and attached fat from spleen
13. Remove spleen
14. Place spleen and lymph nodes in 10 ml DMEM on ice
15. Press lymphoid organs through 70 micrometer cell strainer using end of sterile 10 milliliter syringe plunger
16. Rinse strainer with 1 milliliter of DMEM several times
17. Transfer collected cells into 15 milliliter conical tube
18. Centrifuge cells at 400 G for 7 minutes at room temperature
19. Resuspend cell pellet in 3 milliliters of ACK buffer
20. Incubate at room temperature for 5 minutes to lyse red blood cells
21. Add 12 milliliters of DMEM
22. Pellet cells by centrifugation
23. Wash cell pellet once in 10 milliliters of DMEM
24. Suspend cells in 5 milliliters of FACS staining buffer
25. Count viable cells using phase contrast microscope and hemocytometer
26. Remove approximately 1×10⁶ cells for staining control
27. Stain cells with mouse monoclonal antibodies for sorting nonactivated transgenic CD4-positive T-cells
28. Incubate samples at 4°C in the dark for 30 minutes
29. Wash cells with 3x staining volume of fax buffer
30. Resuspend cells in fax buffer at 1-2×10⁷ cells/ml for sorting sample
31. Resuspend single stain controls in 300 microliters of fax buffer
32. Pass cell sample through 35 micrometer cell strainer cap tube
33. Collect sorted cells into 3 milliliters of DMEM
34. Record absolute number of sorted cells
35. Centrifuge cells at 400 Gs for 7 minutes
36. Resuspend cell pellets in sterile phosphate buffered saline for adoptive T-cell transfer
37. Prepare 1 mL syringe with 18 gauge needle
38. Gently resuspend FACS-purified CD4-positive T-cells using prepared syringe
39. Load cell suspension into 1 mL syringe
40. Attach 27.5-gauge needle to syringe for injection
41. Restrain NOD-SCID recipient mice
42. Wipe mouse tail with 70% ethanol to disinfect injection site
43. Inject 1×10⁶ purified CD4-positive T-cells per mouse into lateral tail vein
44. Verify proper intravenous placement by monitoring plunger resistance during injection
45. Measure urine glucose using reagent strips according to manufacturer instructions
