question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Add 15 milliliters of density gradient cell separation solution into each of two 50 milliliter centrifugation tubes
2. Allow density gradient solution to warm to room temperature
3. Transfer LRSC into a 50 milliliter centrifugation tube
4. Add 1x PBS to LRSC tube up to 50 milliliters
5. Mix LRSC and PBS solution
6. Slowly layer 25 milliliters of LRSC/PBS mix on top of 15 milliliters of equilibrated density gradient solution
7. Centrifuge tubes at 700 G for 25 minutes without breaks
8. Insert pipette through plasma phase without aspirating it
9. Collect PBMC layer into new 50 ml centrifugation tube
10. Add up to 50 ml of 1x PBS to PBMCs and centrifuge for 10 minutes at 300 G
11. Aspirate supernatant from cell pellet
12. Re-suspend pellet in 40 milliliters of macrophage medium
13. Count PBMCs using Malassez chamber
14. Transfer required amount of PBMCs to centrifugation tube
15. Centrifuge cells at 300 G for 10 minutes
16. Aspirate supernatant from centrifuged cells
17. Re-suspend pellet in sorting buffer (80 μl per 10 million PBMCs)
18. Add CD14 microbeads (20 μl per 10 million PBMCs)
19. Mix sample thoroughly
20. Incubate mixture at 4°C for 15 minutes under constant agitation
21. Add 1 ml of sorting buffer per 10 million PBMCs
22. Centrifuge sample according to previous protocol
23. Aspirate supernatant from centrifuged sample
24. Re-suspend pellet in 500 μl of sorting buffer per 100 million PBMCs
25. Place magnetic column in separator's magnetic field
26. Rinse column with three milliliters of sorting buffer
27. Apply cell suspension onto column
28. Collect flow-through containing unlabeled cells for subsequent staining
29. Wash column three times with three milliliters of sorting buffer
30. Place collection tube under column
31. Remove column from separator
32. Maintain monocytes and macrophages in oxygen-controlled environment using hypoxia workstation
33. Prepare media and washing buffers under hypoxia station
34. Wait for liquid to reach correct oxygen partial pressure
35. Perform cell lysis in buffer under adapted hood
36. Load protein equivalent of 300,000 cells per sample onto 4-12% bis-tris acrylamide gels
37. Control electrophoresis duration to separate protein samples into six gel bands
38. Excise protein bands using a clean scalpel
39. Dice each excised protein band
40. Place diced bands into 500 microliter tubes
41. Wash gel slices three times in 200 microliters of 25 millimolar ammonium bicarbonate at 37 degrees Celsius for 20 minutes per wash
42. Wash gel slices once in 25 millimolar ammonium bicarbonate and acetonitrile
43. Dehydrate gel pieces with 200 microliters of 100% acetonitrile for 10 minutes
44. Incubate each gel piece with 10 millimolar DTT and 25 millimolar ammonium bicarbonate for 45 minutes at 56 degrees Celsius
45. Discard DTT solution
46. Incubate gel piece with 55 millimolar iodoacetamide in 25 millimolar ammonium bicarbonate for 35 minutes in the dark at room temperature
47. Digest proteins overnight at 37°C with Trypsin/Lys-C Mix according to manufacturer's instructions
48. Add 50 microliters of 50% acetonitrile to gel pieces for 15 minutes in low-absorption tubes
49. Add 50 microliters of 5% formic acid for 15 minutes
50. Add 50 microliters of 100% acetonitrile for 15 minutes
51. Pull and dry each fraction in low-absorption tubes to limit peptide absorption and sample loss
52. Store samples at -80°C until further analysis

1. Weigh 5-8 grams of air-dried sieved soil in 50 mL conical centrifuge tube
2. Record soil mass with four significant figures
3. Add 35-40 mL of sodium polytungstate (SPT) using graduated cylinder
4. Centrifuge at 2500 × g for 90 minutes in swinging bucket centrifuge
5. Pour off all floating and suspended material
6. Pour floating and suspended material into 250 ml polycarbonate centrifuge bottle
7. Rinse material adhering to tube walls into same polycarbonate bottle using deionized water squirt bottle while holding tube inverted
8. Set up vacuum ultrafiltration line with 0.45 micron filters
9. Moisten filters and apply vacuum before tightening funnels to prevent tears
10. Slowly pour content of polycarbonate bottle into funnel of vacuum filtration unit
11. Rinse residue from polycarbonate bottle into filtration unit
12. Add >10 mL deionized water to filtering unit (repeat ≥3 times)
13. Rinse sides of filtration funnel
14. Release vacuum on filtration line
15. Release vacuum on filtration line and remove funnel from filtration unit
16. Recover material adhering to funnel sides into labeled aluminum bowl using deionized water squirt bottle
17. Lift filter with tweezers and rinse material into same aluminum bowl using deionized water squirt bottle
18. Dry bowl contents at ≤65°C until constant weight
19. Cool bowl contents in desiccator with fresh desiccant for ≥30 minutes after ≥48 hours drying
20. Scrape material off aluminum bowl with plastic spatula
21. Record mass of free light fraction with four significant figures and transfer to storage vial
22. Add 35-40 ml of SPT solution (density: 1.62 g/cm³) to centrifuge tube containing pellet
23. Resuspend pellet in SPT solution
24. Insert ultrasonic probe 2 cm below solution surface
25. Place tube in iced water bath to prevent heating
26. Sonicate sample until reaching target energy of 280 J/ml
27. Centrifuge sample for 90 minutes at 2500 times gravity in swinging-bucket centrifuge
28. Pour all floating and suspended materials into 250 milliliter polycarbonate centrifuge bottle
29. Rinse occluded light organic material adhering to tube into same polycarbonate bottle
30. Add refrigerated deionized water to tube
31. Add refrigerated deionized water to pellet-containing tube up to 40 milliliter mark
32. Sonicate sample at 75 joules per milliliter
33. Allow content to settle until material finer than eight microns settles
34. Pipette supernatant down to 10 milliliter mark into 50 milliliter centrifuge tube
35. Place tubes containing less than eight micron fraction and greater than eight micron fraction in oven at 45 degrees Celsius
36. Add 35-40 ml of SPT (density 2.78 g/cm³) to each size fraction
37. Resuspend pellet in SPT solution
38. Centrifuge resuspended samples at 2500 × g for 90 minutes
39. Pour floating/suspended materials into 250 ml polycarbonate centrifuge bottle
40. Transfer pellets to 250 ml polycarbonate bottles using squirt bottle filled with deionized water
41. Add deionized water to polycarbonate bottles to lower density
42. Ensure liquid volume in bottles does not exceed 200 ml mark
43. Decant supernatant into used SPT jar for recycling at 5000 × g
44. Collect heavy fraction (pellet and adherent particles) in aluminum bowl
45. Dry heavy fraction at ≤105°C until constant weight
46. Store heavy fraction using light fraction preservation method

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Mix NK cell isolation cocktail solution with 6-10 milliliters of EDTA peripheral whole blood by several inversions
2. Homogenize sample on tube rotator for 5 minutes at room temperature
3. Place tube into magnetic separator for 15 minutes under sterile conditions with tube label facing magnet back
4. Transfer supernatant into new 15 milliliter tube
5. Bring final volume to 15 milliliters with complete medium
6. Wash cells by centrifugation
7. Resuspend pellet in 1 milliliter of fresh complete medium
8. Count cells
9. Adjust volume to concentration of at least 2×10⁴ NK cells per 100 microliters in complete medium
10. Gently mix isolated NK cells by pipetting at least five times
11. Aliquot 100 microliters of NK cells into appropriate wells of 96-well V-bottomed plate
12. Switch off light
13. Add anti-CD107a antibody to each well of NK cells
14. Carefully mix aliquot of K562 tumor cells (2×10⁴ cells/100μL) by pipetting at least five times
15. Transfer 100 microliters of tumor cells to each stimulation well
16. Add IL-2 and IL-15 to stimulation wells
17. Mix all wells by pipetting at least five times
18. Incubate plate protected from light for 3 hours at 37°C and 5% CO₂
19. Add freshly prepared protein transport inhibitor solution to each well with careful mixing
20. Return plate to incubator
21. Mix co-cultures carefully
22. Transfer mixed co-cultures to corresponding flow cytometry tubes
23. Add 1 milliliter of wash buffer to each tube
24. Resuspend pellets in 99 microliters of PBS and 1 microliter of fixable dead cell marker per sample
25. Vortex cells
26. Incubate for 15 minutes at room temperature in the dark
27. Wash cells in 1 mL wash buffer
28. Resuspend pellets in 84 μL wash buffer plus 16 μL cell surface antibody cocktail
29. Vortex samples
30. Incubate for 20 minutes in the dark at 4°C
31. Wash cells in 1 ml wash buffer
32. Resuspend pellets
33. Resuspend in 100 μl cold fixation solution
34. Mix by vortexing
35. Incubate for 10 minutes at 4°C in dark
36. Wash cells in 1 ml wash buffer
37. Resuspend pellets in 90 μl permeabilization buffer
38. Stain with 10 μl intracellular antibodies and mix by vortexing
39. Incubate for 30 minutes in dark at 4°C
40. Wash cells twice in 1 ml fresh permeabilization buffer
41. Resuspend samples in 400 μl fresh permeabilization buffer
42. Mix by vortexing and place cells on ice for flow cytometry analysis

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Grind defatted cotton seed meal through a 0.5 millimeter steel screen using a cyclone sample mill
2. Retrieve the ground working meal from the grinder's outlet
3. Measure 25 grams of working meal
4. Place measured working meal in blender jar
5. Add 200 milliliters of water to meal in blender
6. Mix water and meal in blender for 3 minutes
7. Centrifuge the resulting mixture
8. Freeze-dry the centrifuged mixture to isolate water-insoluble fraction
9. Place water-washed meal in a vessel
10. Add 25 grams of deionized water per 3 grams of meal to vessel
11. Add stir bar to vessel
12. Seal beaker with paraform
13. Stir mixture for two hours
14. Take prepared slurry for immediate use in bonding specimens
15. Cut wood veneer strips to dimensions: 25.4 mm wide × 88.9 mm long × 1.59 mm thick with length parallel to wood grain
16. Mark line across wood grain 25.4 mm from one end of each strip using pencil
17. Label wood veneer strips for identification
18. Unseal adhesive slurry
19. Unseal adhesive slurry container
20. Apply first layer of adhesive slurry to marked regions of wood veneer strips using brush
21. Air dry adhesive-coated strips for 10-15 minutes until tacky
22. Apply second layer of adhesive slurry over first layer using brush
23. Air dry strips again after second adhesive application
24. Overlap adhesive-coated regions of two specimen strips
25. Place overlapped specimens in press
26. Hot press specimens at specified conditions for 20 minutes
27. Recover specimens from press and transfer to incubator
28. Condition specimens in incubator at 22-23°C and 50-60% RH for 48 hours
29. Put room temperature water in a glass jar
30. Get conditioned specimens
31. Immerse specimens in water for 48 hours
32. Fill water bath with water at 63 degrees Celsius
33. Retrieve conditioned specimens
34. Immerse specimens in 63°C water bath for 4 hours
35. Recover specimens from water bath
36. Dry specimens at 22°C and 50-60% relative humidity for 18-20 hours
37. Immerse specimens in another 63°C water bath for 4 hours
38. Dry specimens for 48 hours before testing
39. Set gripping pressure to 7 MPa
40. Set crosshead speed to 1 mm/min
41. Measure and record shear strength at break for each specimen
42. Average multiple measurements for each test variable
43. Secure bonded wood specimen (30 mm by 25 mm) into grips with width wider than specimen and comparable length

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Shave the surgical knee with electric clippers
2. Vacuum the fur
3. Disinfect the shaved knee
4. Cover the animal with sterile dressing
5. Palpate the patella
6. Perform medial skin incision of the patella
7. Open knee joint by medial para patella arthrotomy under sterile conditions
8. Displace the patella
9. Displace patella laterally
10. Use sterile scalpel to peel 2-3 mm cartilage pieces from trochlea ossis femoris
11. Place cartilage biopsies into 50 ml tube containing complete medium
12. Rinse defects with sterile saline
13. Use forceps to remove residual cartilage pieces
14. Reposition patella within trochlea groove
15. Close wound in layers with single button sutures and continuing absorbable cutaneous suture
16. Seal wound with water vapor-permeable spray dressing
17. Wash sterile harvested cartilage twice in PBS
18. Centrifuge washed cartilage at 500 × g for 3 minutes at room temperature
19. Cut cartilage into 1 cubic millimeter pieces
20. Transfer cartilage pieces to new 50 mL conical tube
21. Digest cartilage pieces with 10 mL trypsin-EDTA and 10 mL PBS on shaker
22. Stop trypsinization by adding 10 mL complete medium after 30 minutes
23. Remove supernatant after centrifugation
24. Add serum-free medium with collagenase A
25. Shake mixture for 12 hours
26. Centrifuge resulting solution
27. Resuspend cell pellet in 5 mL complete medium
28. Seed cells onto 25 cm² tissue culture flasks in 5 mL complete medium
29. Grow cells in humidified incubator at 37°C with 5% CO₂
30. Wash chondrocytes twice in PBS

32. Add 5 milliliters of complete medium to stop trypsinization after cell detachment
33. Spin down cells
34. Resuspend cell pellet in complete medium
35. Seed cells in 75 square centimeter tissue culture flasks
36. Count harvested cells from in vitro culture
37. Transfer 5×10⁴ cells into 1.5 ml microcentrifuge tube
38. Centrifuge cell suspension
39. Resuspend pellet in 15 microliters of complete medium
40. Cut bilayer collagen scaffold to exact dimensions of trochlear defect
41. Place collagen matrix into 24-well plate
42. Seed chondrocytes onto porous cell adhesive side of matrix
43. Incubate cell-seeded matrices without cell culture medium for one hour
44. Create two isolated 3.6 mm diameter chondral defects in trochlear groove using sterile air operating power drill
45. Rinse defects with sterile saline while avoiding opening marrow cavity
46. Implant chondrocyte-seeded matrices with porous side face down
47. Apply fibrin glue to seal implanted matrices for secure fixation
48. Relocate patella within trochlear groove
49. Apply full range of motion to knee joint multiple times
50. Displace patella laterally while holding matrices to check fixation
51. Replace patella in position
52. Perform wound closure in layers
53. Apply spray dressing to wound

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Centrifuge cyanobacteria sample at 3,500 G for 10 minutes at 25°C
2. Discard supernatant into biological waste
3. Cover centrifuge tube with sealing film and pierce holes using sharp long-nose tweezers
4. Store tube upright at -80°C
5. Place tube upright into freeze dryer glass container
6. Place container inside -80°C freezer
7. Cool container in freezer for 5 minutes
8. Remove glass container from freezer
9. Attach rubber lid to container
10. Ensure rubber valve outlet handle points upward and is vented to atmosphere
11. Firmly attach glass container to freeze-dryer
12. Slowly turn rubber valve outlet handle to down position to expose jar to vacuum
13. Allow freeze-drying for up to 24 hours to sublimate all liquid
14. Weigh 15 to 50 milligrams of frozen sample pellets into 15 milliliter centrifuge tube using analytical balance
15. Add 300 to 600 microliters of 10% aqueous Trichloroacetic acid (TCA) to sample
16. Place sample tubes in container filled with crushed ice
17. Wipe sonicator probe with lint-free paper wipe sprayed with 70% ethanol
18. Fully immerse probe end into sample and start sonication
19. Place sample-containing centrifuge tube on ice for 1 minute
20. Repeat sonication process for cell lysis
21. Cool sample at 4°C for 24 hours
22. Centrifuge sample at 3,500 G for 15 minutes at 8°C
23. Transfer supernatant to 2 ml tube labeled 'free fraction' using micropipette
24. Add 400 microliters of 10% aqueous TCA into sample pellet
25. Break up pellet by vortex agitation or micro pipette tip
26. Centrifuge sample
27. Transfer supernatant to 2ml free fraction tube
28. Repeat pellet wash using 10% TCA acetone
29. Place open free fraction tube in centrifugal evaporator
30. Evaporate until volatile liquid is removed
31. Cover tube securely with ceiling film
32. Pierce film with holes using sharp long-nose tweezer
33. Reconstitute sample with 200 microliters of 20mM hydrochloric acid
34. Place sample in -80°C freezer
35. Add 400 microliters of 100% acetone to sample pellet using micro pipette
36. Break up pellet using vortex agitation or micro pipette tip
37. Quantitatively transfer washed/resuspended pellet to glass shell vial using 1mL micro pipette
38. Add 400 microliters acetone to sample tube to transfer remaining pellet
39. Centrifuge sample
40. Decant supernatant liquid into biological waste
41. Dry pellet in centrifugal evaporator until liquid is removed and pellet is dry
42. Add 1 mL of 6 M hydrochloric acid to bottom of hydrolysis vial
43. Insert labeled shell vials with dried samples into hydrolysis vial using tweezers
44. Ensure vials are in upright stable position
45. Attach lid to hydrolysis vial
46. Push red knob on lid to close valve
47. Attach vacuum tube to head of hydrolysis vial lid after turning on vacuum pump
48. Press green knob on lid to open valve
49. Allow vacuum pump to remove air from vial for 1 minute
50. Depress red knob on hydrolysis vial lid to close vial
51. Turn off vacuum pump and remove vacuum tube
52. Attach rubber tube to nitrogen gas tap on laboratory bench
53. Open nitrogen gas tap slightly
54. Seal tube end with thumb and monitor gas pressure buildup until escape
55. Attach rubber tube to hydrolysis vial lid head
56. Push green knob on hydrolysis vial lid
57. Time duration for gas escape
58. Push red knob on hydrolysis vial lid
59. Remove rubber tube from vial
60. Repeat entire vacuum treatment process twice
61. Place hydrolysis vial into preheated oven at 110°C
62. Hydrolyze samples at 110°C for 16-18 hours
63. Remove glass hydrolysis vial from oven using oven gloves
64. Cool vial in fume hood for 10 minutes
65. Push green knob while facing vial away to release pressure and gas

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Seed BT-474 cancer cells at density of 2 × 10⁶ cells into T-75 flask using 10 ml complete growth media
2. Culture cells until reaching 70-80% confluency
3. Discard growth media
4. Wash cell monolayer with PBS
5. Wash cell monolayer with PBS (second wash)
6. Add 5 ml pre-warmed cell culture dissociation reagent
7. Incubate at 37°C for 5 minutes or until cell detachment
8. Remove flask from incubator
9. Gently tap flask to aid cell detachment
10. Add 5 ml complete growth media with 10% FBS to quench dissociation reagent
11. Gently resuspend cells by pipetting to reduce clumps
12. Transfer cell suspension to 50-milliliter tube
13. Centrifuge at 180 G for 3 minutes at room temperature
14. Decant supernatant
15. Resuspend cell pellet in 10 ml HBSS without calcium and magnesium
16. Centrifuge cell suspension at 180 G for 3 minutes at room temperature
17. Decant supernatant to remove residual serum or dissociation reagent
18. Resuspend cell pellet in 3 ml HBSS
19. Place 100-micrometer cell strainer on fresh 50-milliliter conical tube
20. Pass cell suspension through strainer to remove cell clumps
21. Calculate number of viable cells using trypan blue
22. Dilute cell suspension with HBSS to concentration of 2.5 × 10⁶ cells/mL
23. Place tube horizontally on ice
24. Gently rock tube periodically to minimize clumping
25. Store cell suspension on ice
26. Ear-punch mouse for identification
27. Shave fur from neck region using electric clippers
28. Clean excess hair from exposed skin using tape
29. Apply ocular lubricant to eyes to prevent drying
30. Secure mouse by hooking upper incisor teeth using thread taped to surgical board
31. Tape front and hind legs to surgical board
32. Wipe neck with povidone iodine antiseptic (center to outward)
33. Perform three alternating rounds of povidone iodine and 70% ethanol disinfection
34. Perform pinch test to verify anesthesia depth
35. Lay sterile surgical drape over animal
36. Position under dissection microscope
37. Make 15-mm vertical midline neck incision with scissors from 5mm below jaw to thoracic inlet
38. Part skin and salivary glands using two pairs of angled forceps
39. Apply retractors to maintain tracheal exposure
40. Bluntly dissect muscle and fat tissue adjacent to trachea using two pairs of fine angled forceps
41. Clear surrounding fascia from a segment of the common carotid artery caudal to the carotid bifurcation
42. Separate the common carotid artery from the vagus nerve and veins
43. Isolate and clear the carotid bifurcation from surrounding nerves and fascia
44. Position fine forceps under the external carotid artery
45. Pass a 5-0 silk suture under the external carotid artery
46. Knot and tighten the suture on the external carotid artery
47. Position fine forceps under the common carotid artery
48. Pass a 5-0 silk suture under the common carotid artery
49. Tie a knot and tighten the suture proximal to the proposed injection site
50. Cut excess suture leaving approximately 10 millimeters of line
51. Cut and moisten a strip of low-lint disposable wipers
52. Cut and moisten a 10-by-5 millimeter strip of low-lint disposable wipers
53. Fold the moistened wiper strip
54. Place folded wiper strip underneath carotid artery at proposed injection site
55. Place third ligation with loose knot on common carotid artery rostral to injection site
56. Gently agitate cell suspension
57. Draw 200 microliters of cell suspension into insulin syringe with 31-gauge needle
58. Load syringe into syringe driver connected to activating foot pedal
59. Attach fine cannula with 31-gauge needle to syringe
60. Prime the line
61. Check carotid artery positioning and pressurization
62. Gently tension the end of the first ligature using fine-angled forceps
63. Hold 31-gauge needle with fine-angled forceps
64. Slowly insert needle with bevel up into blood vessel lumen while avoiding puncture
65. Inject 100 microliters of prepared cell suspension into common carotid artery at 10 microliters per second (delivering 2.5×10⁵ cells)
66. Visually confirm successful injection via blood clearance from carotid vessel
67. Withdraw needle from blood vessel
68. Immediately lift and tighten loose ligature to prevent backflow and bleeding
69. Trim excess sutures
70. Remove moistened low-lint disposable wipers
71. Rinse surgical cavity twice with 150-200 microliters of sterile water or saline using P-200 pipette
72. Check for bleeds
73. Reposition soft tissue, salivary glands, and skin over carotid artery and trachea
74. Close skin layer of incision using suture needle-holder, forceps, and 6-0 monofilament suture in continuous pattern
75. Inject 50 μg/kg buprenorphine and 1 mg/kg meloxicam via subcutaneous injection for post-surgical pain relief
76. Move animal to warm and clean cage for anesthesia recovery

2. Add 5 nM Docetaxel after cells reach 70-80% confluence (24-hour post-plating)
3. Aspirate drug-containing media
4. Add fresh Docetaxel-free media
5. Wait 1-2 weeks for clone development in flask
6. Aspirate media from flask
7. Wash cells with 15 mL PBS
8. Incubate cells with 4 mL of 0.05% Trypsin-EDTA at 37°C for 3-5 minutes to detach
9. Re-suspend trypsinized cells from flask using 8 milliliters of fresh media
10. Pool cells from all treated flasks
11. Pellet cells by centrifugation
12. Remove supernatant
13. Re-suspend cell pellet in 20 milliliters of fresh media
14. Plate cells in 150 cm² flasks
15. Add 5 nanomolar Docetaxel after 24 hours at 70-80% confluence
16. Plate cells in 150 cm² flasks
17. Treat cells with 10 nM Docetaxel after 24 hours at 70-80% confluence
18. Pool surviving clones after each Docetaxel concentration treatment
19. Plate 2,000 cells using 2 milliliters of media per well in six-well plates
20. Add increasing concentrations of Docetaxel for DU145 and 22Rv1 cell lines after 24 hours
21. Add DMSO only to one well as control at same volume as highest Docetaxel dose
22. Aspirate drug-containing media after 72 hours
23. Add fresh Docetaxel-free media
24. Incubate plates for 1-2 weeks until colonies are visible under microscope
25. Wash colonies gently with 2-3 milliliters of PBS
26. Incubate colonies with 2-3 milliliters of crystal violet solution for 20 minutes in tissue culture hood or fume hood
27. Remove standing solution and wash plates with 2-3 milliliters of water
28. Remove water and air dry plates
29. Take digital images of plates for figure representation
30. Visualize wells and manually count colonies using marker pen
31. Represent percentage of cell viability in graph

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Add 100 microliters of Proteinase K, 800 microliters of lysis buffer, and 1 microgram of carrier RNA to 1 milliliter of patient plasma
2. Pulse vortex solution thoroughly for 30 seconds
3. Incubate solution for 30 minutes at 60 degrees Celsius
4. Add 1.8 milliliters of binding buffer to the tube
5. Mix sample with 15 to 30 seconds of pulse vortexing
6. Insert silica membrane column into vacuum apparatus
7. Insert 20 milliliter tube extender into open column
8. Pour mixture into tube extender
9. Switch on vacuum pump
10. Monitor lysate filtration until complete
11. Switch off vacuum pump
12. Release pressure to zero millibars
13. Discard tube extender without contaminating columns
14. Transfer column into collection tube
15. Centrifuge column to remove residual lysate
16. Discard flow-through and add 600 microliters of wash buffer 1 to column
17. Centrifuge column with wash buffer 1
18. Discard flow-through and add 750 microliters of wash buffer 2 to column
19. Centrifuge column with wash buffer 2
20. Discard flow-through and add 750 microliters of 96-100% ethanol to column
21. Centrifuge column with ethanol
22. Transfer column to new 2 ml collection tube
23. Centrifuge column in collection tube
24. Place membrane column assembly into new 2 ml collection tube
25. Dry column at 56°C for 10 minutes
26. Transfer column to new 1.5 ml elution tube
27. Add 50 microliters of elution buffer to column
28. Incubate at room temperature for 3 minutes
29. Centrifuge solution at 20,000 × g for 1 minute to elute nucleic acids
30. Secure base plate to chip priming station
31. Adjust clip to lowest position
32. Place high sensitivity DNA chip onto priming station
33. Add 9 microliters of gel dye mix to G chip well
34. Position plunger at 1 milliliter mark
35. Close chip priming station
36. Close lock of latch until it clicks
37. Set timer to 60 seconds
38. Press down plunger until held by clip
39. Release plunger using clip release mechanism after 60 seconds
40. Wait 5 seconds after plunger retreats to 300 microliter mark
41. Slowly retract plunger to 1 milliliter position
42. Remove 9 microliters of gel dye mix from chip priming station
43. Transfer gel dye mix to bottom of high sensitivity DNA chip G well
44. Dispense 5 microliters of DNA marker into ladder well and 11 sample wells
45. Add 1 microliter of DNA ladder to DNA ladder well
46. Add 1 microliter of sample to used sample wells
47. Add 1 microliter of marker to unused sample wells
48. Place high sensitivity DNA chip horizontally in adapter
49. Vortex chip at 2,400 revolutions per minute for 60 seconds while ensuring fixation bulge remains undamaged
50. Confirm electrode cartridge is properly inserted
51. Position chip selector to double stranded high sensitivity DNA
52. Mount high sensitivity DNA chip into receptacle
53. Confirm electrode cartridge fits exactly into chip wells
54. Close instrument lid
55. Open assay menu in fragment analyzer software
56. Select double stranded DNA high sensitivity assay
57. Enter sample names into software table
58. Click start to initiate chip run
59. Remove and discard chip according to good laboratory practice

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Aspirate medium from cells
2. Wash cells with 5 milliliters of phosphate buffered saline
3. Add 0.5 milliliters of 0.025% trypsin to cells
4. Incubate cells at 37 degrees Celsius for 3-4 minutes
5. Add 9 milliliters of complete medium to deactivate trypsin
6. Transfer cells to 15 milliliter centrifuge tube
7. Centrifuge cells to form pellet
8. Dissolve centrifuged pellet in 5 milliliters of fresh medium
9. Pipette 10 microliters of re-suspended cells and mix with 10 microliters of trypan blue vital dye
10. Use hemocytometer to count living/dead cells and assess viability (target: 90% living cells)
11. Leave plate in incubator for 24 hours
12. Remove media from wells
13. Add 500 microliters of fresh RPMI 1640 medium to each well
14. Incubate plate for 24 hours to allow cell spreading on well surfaces
15. Prepare KRB buffer
16. Aspirate medium from all wells
17. Wash wells twice with pre-warmed phosphate buffered saline
18. Add 450 microliters of glucose-free KRB buffer supplemented with bovine serum albumin to each well
19. Incubate culture plate at 37°C and 5% CO₂ for 1 hour
20. Prepare serial drug dilutions in KRB buffer supplemented with 200 mM glucose (10X final concentration)
21. Pipette 50 microliters of serially diluted drugs into corresponding wells
22. Add respective serially diluted drugs in presence of 20 millimolar glucose for glucose stimulation
23. Incubate cells at 37 degrees Celsius for 90 minutes
24. Collect supernatants and transfer to labeled 1.5 milliliter centrifuge tubes
25. Transfer 10 microliters of each collected supernatant into a 96-well plate
26. Add 90 microliters of KRB to each well to achieve 1:10 dilution
27. Mix dilutions to ensure homogeneity
28. Obtain insulin standard curve for HTRF insulin assay
29. Add 10 microliters of standard insulin samples and diluted assay supernatants to 96-well plate
30. Prepare antibody mix in 1:2 donor-to-acceptor ratio using detection buffer
31. Add 30 microliters of antibody mix to each well of 96-well plate
32. Seal plate and incubate at room temperature
33. Read plate on plate reader after 2-hour incubation

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Pour 100 milliliters of distilled water into beaker containing 4 grams of sugar and 0.8 grams of agar
2. Heat mixture to 100 degrees Celsius while stirring continuously
3. Remove mixture from heat
4. Lower heat to maintain 80 degrees Celsius
5. Add 7.4 grams of flour to mixture while stirring
6. Add 2.8 grams of yeast to mixture while stirring
7. Add Moldex propionic acid solution to mixture
8. Cool mixture to 50 degrees Celsius
9. Add psidium guajava plant extract solution
10. Add 0.5 grams of bromophenol blue powder
11. Pour food mixture into Petri dishes until full
12. Seal Petri dishes with lids after cooling to room temperature
13. Store sealed Petri dishes in refrigerator at 4 degrees Celsius
14. Prepare carbon dioxide tanks, blowgun with needles, fly pad, paintbrush, and microscope
15. Select vials containing at least 10 Drosophila pupae
16. Invert vial and insert needle between cotton plug and vial sidewall
17. Anesthetize adult flies using carbon dioxide blowgun until unconscious on cotton plug
18. Open vial above glass bottle containing 70% ethanol and drop flies into ethanol
19. Seal vial with cotton plug
20. Incubate vial at 25°C with 60% humidity and 12-hour light/dark cycle
21. Prepare microscope for fly sorting
22. Sort flies into virgin females and males using microscope
23. Transfer sorted flies into two fresh tubes (one per sex)
24. Incubate tubes at 25°C for 6-8 days
25. Stack labeled Petri dishes on top of each other
26. Invert Petri dishes with dyed food onto blotting paper
27. Soak up excess liquid using blotting paper
28. Divide food in each dish into 12 equal segments using spatula
29. Transfer one food slice into empty Petri dish
30. Anesthetize flies using carbon dioxide until asleep on cotton plug
31. Transfer six healthy flies into each Petri dish
32. Place sealed Petri dishes in incubator
33. Secure top and bottom covers of each Petri dish with tape
34. Anesthetize flies using carbon dioxide
35. Transfer flies to container filled with 70% ethanol for disposal
36. Discard remaining food from Petri dishes
37. Launch Epson Scan 2 software
38. Assign file name for scan
39. Perform preview scan
40. Individually scan top and bottom covers of each Petri dish
41. Crop scanned images using open-source image editor to eliminate artifacts and food residue
42. Save cropped image as TIFF file

45. Add Petri dish plate: Click Plates, select Add Plate, choose dish for analysis

48. View annotated images: Select Graphics > View Annotated Images
49. Review deposit counts: Zoom into images

51. Zoom into images to review deposit counts

53. Adjust fly count by clicking number flies field

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Place clean paper (5 by 35 millimeters) on center of double-sided tape
2. Excise paper using clean blade
3. Place glass cover slip on top of double-sided tape
4. Rub cover slip using pipet tip to form microfluidic chamber
5. Place assembled flow cell between two microscope slides
6. Clip microscope slides together
7. Connect lure lock syringe containing 3 mL deionized water
8. Wash chamber with 3 mL deionized water
9. Wash chamber again with 3 mL lipid buffer via drop-by-drop connection
10. Inject 1 mL of 0.04x biotinylated lipid in lipid buffer into chamber
11. Deliver injection in two shots
12. Incubate for 5 minutes per shot
13. Inject 1 mL of 0.025 mg/mL streptavidin in BSA into chamber
14. Deliver streptavidin injection in two shots
15. Incubate streptavidin for 10 minutes per shot
16. Inject 1 mL of ~30 pM biotinylated lambda phage DNA in BSA buffer into chamber
17. Deliver DNA injection in two shots with 10-minute incubations per shot
18. Connect syringe containing 10 milliliters of imaging buffer to syringe pump
19. Remove bubbles in all tubing lines of microfluidic system
20. Couple prepared flow cell with microfluidic system via drop-to-drop connection to avoid bubble injection
21. Assemble flow cell and flow cell holders
22. Mount assembly on custom-built total internal reflection fluorescence microscope
23. Inject 200 microliters of YOYO-1 dye in imaging buffer through 100 microliters sample loop
24. Run imaging software
25. Turn on 488-nanometer laser to check DNA curtain formation
26. Inject pre-incubated protein sample after YOYO-1 dye removal
27. Turn off syringe pump when proteins reach DNA curtain
28. Switch off shutoff valve
29. Incubate for 15 minutes for histone loading by Abo1
30. Wash out unbound Abo1 and histone proteins for 5 minutes
31. Transiently turn off buffer flow to check histone protein loading

1. Hand wash sample plate with 100 milliliters of detergent solution

5. Place beaker into ultrasonic bath and sonicate plate for 15 minutes
6. Remove sample plate from beaker
7. Blow off water drops from sample plate using pressurized nitrogen
8. Deposit 0.2 microliters of methanol on sample plate to validate surface cleanliness
9. Premix 0.25 microliters of 2,5-Dihydroxybenzoic acid solution and 0.25 microliters of Sialyl-Lewis A or maltoheptaose solution in micro centrifuge tube
10. Vortex tube for three seconds
11. Centrifuge mixed solution at 2000 × g for two seconds
12. Pipette 0.1 microliter of pre-mixed solution onto sample plate
13. Pipette 0.2 microliters of methanol onto dried sample spot
14. Wear nitrile gloves
15. Remove sample plate from drying chamber
16. Examine sample under microscope
17. Open mass spectrometer control software
18. Insert sample plate into mass spectrometer
19. Select pre-optimized data acquisition method in control software
20. Register whole sample region for imaging in imaging software
21. Start data acquisition in batch mode using control software
22. Plot ion images using imaging software

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Shave all dorsal skin hair from a 50-80 day old mouse in telogen phase using electric clippers
2. Disinfect skin with 70% ethanol to remove hair residue
3. Place mouse onto dissecting pad
4. Lift skin near tail using forceps
5. Nick the skin with scissors
6. Cut tissue in posterior-anterior direction to harvest entire dorsal skin
7. Pin two adjacent edges of tissue onto dissecting mat with hair side down
8. Apply light pressure with curved forceps to prevent skin tearing
9. Gently scrape away fat with blunted scalpel until dermis is clear and neat
10. Place skin dermis side down in 100 millimeter sterile culture dish
11. Straighten tissue to eliminate folds
12. Wash skin with 10 mL PBS without calcium and magnesium
13. Straighten skin as needed during washing
14. Remove PBS from tissue
15. Incubate tissue in 10 mL of 0.25% trypsin
16. Ensure skin is unfolded and freely floating during trypsin incubation
17. Hold skin in place with curved forceps
18. Scrape hair from skin starting at tail following growth direction
19. Scrape small areas sequentially to minimize follicle clumping
20. Transfer hairless skin to new culture dish
21. Hydrate tissue with 10 milliliters of PBS without calcium and magnesium
22. Confirm complete removal of hair follicles
23. Break down follicles using scalpel and forceps to obtain single follicle suspension
24. Vigorously triturate hair follicle solution with 10 milliliter pipette for few minutes to break clumps
25. Transfer cells into 50 milliliter tubes on ice
26. Transfer cells into pre-labeled 50 milliliter tubes on ice
27. Aspirate PBS from skin
28. Use aspirated PBS to rinse cell culture dish containing hair follicle suspension
29. Pool wash with hair follicle cells
30. Filter cell suspension through 70 micron cell strainer into new 50 milliliter tube
31. Wash first 50 milliliter tube and strainer with 10 milliliters of staining buffer
32. Filter suspension through 40 micron strainer into new 50 milliliter tube
33. Wash second 50 milliliter tube with 5-10 milliliters of fresh staining buffer
34. Perform first centrifugation of cells
35. Perform second centrifugation of cells while washing with 5 milliliters of fresh staining buffer
36. Resuspend pellet in 800 microliters of fresh staining buffer
37. Transfer 25 to 50 microliters of cells to unstained and DAPI control tubes
38. Add staining buffer to control tubes
39. Adjust total volume to 300 microliters per control tube
40. Add primary antibodies and DAPI to appropriate sample tubes
41. Mix cells with gentle flicking
42. Incubate labeled cells for 30 minutes on ice in dark
43. Flick tubes every 10 minutes during incubation to maintain cell suspension
44. Wash cells with approximately 4 ml staining buffer per tube
45. Re-suspend cell pellets in 800 microliters staining buffer
46. Filter cells into FACS tubes with cell strainer caps

Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Weigh anesthetized 8-12 week old mouse to obtain baseline weight
2. Place mouse in prone position
3. Subcutaneously inject mouse with 250 microliters of pre-warmed sterile 0.9% sodium chloride on each flank
4. Use electric shaver to remove hair from dorsal area
5. Apply thin layer of depilatory cream to exposed skin
6. Remove hair and excess lotion with warm water-moistened gauze
7. Subcutaneously inject 0.6-1 mg/kg sustained-release buprenorphine at mid-dorsal area using 25-gauge needle
8. Wipe surgical site in circular manner with three alternating sterile Betadine and alcohol swabs
9. Place plastic cling-wrap drap over surgical site
10. Stretch skin taut caudally
11. Stretch the skin taut caudally
12. Use sterile six millimeter diameter skin biopsy punch to make initial incision through left dorsal epidermis
13. Make second incision through right dorsal epidermis
14. Use forceps to tent skin from center of left outlined wound area
15. Use scissors to excise epidermal and dermal layers of left wound
16. Repeat excision on right outlined wound area to create symmetrical excisional wounds
17. Wash both wounds with 50 microliters of sterile saline
18. Cover wounds and dorsum with transparent film dressing
19. Place mouse in clean cage on heating pad
20. Weigh re-anesthetized mouse
21. Inject 250 microliters of pre-warmed sterile 0.9% sodium chloride subcutaneously in both flanks
22. Load 100 microliters of luminescent P. aeruginosa strain suspension into 500 microliter tuberculin safety cap syringe with 27 gauge needle
23. Inject 40 microliters of bacteria suspension through transparent film dressing into each wound
24. Ensure bacterial suspension is well mixed and transparent dressing is intact
25. Puncture transparent dressing once with needle bezel side up
26. Return mouse to cage on heating pad with monitoring
27. Provide high calorie nutritional supplement paste sandwiched between food pellets
28. Plate remaining inoculum on LB agar plate
29. Count colonies to confirm administered bacteria number
30. Place anesthetized mouse in prone position within imaging chamber
31. Set exposure time, binning, f-stop, and field of view in imaging software
32. Create region of interest at wound site
33. Measure average detected flux in photons per second
34. Create region of interest over random area on imaging platform for background measurement
35. Subtract background photons per second
36. Excise wound beds using sterile scissors and forceps in bio safety cabinet
37. Place each wound bed in 1 milliliter of sterile PBS in 1.5 milliliter polypropylene tube
38. Mince wound tissue with scissors
39. Incubate tissue pieces on shaker at 4°C and 300 rpm for 2 hours
40. Vortex each tube for 10 seconds
41. Serially dilute bacterial effluent in PBS
42. Plate diluted bacterial effluent on LB agar to quantify bacterial burden
43. Place anesthetized mouse in prone position within imaging chamber
44. Set imaging software parameters (exposure time, binning, f-stop, field of view)
45. Create region of interest at wound site
46. Measure average detected flux in photons per second
47. Create region of interest over random area on imaging platform for background measurement
48. Subtract background photons per second from measurements

50. Place each excised wound bed in 1 ml sterile PBS in 1.5 ml polypropylene tube
51. Mince wound tissue with scissors

53. Vortex each tube for 10 seconds
54. Serially dilute bacterial effluent in PBS
55. Plate diluted bacterial effluent on LB agar

7. Place tip of Hamilton syringe next to spine

9. Insert needle tip into ligament adjacent to bone

13. Configure apparatus with one brightly lit chamber and opposite dark chamber
14. Monitor animal's light/dark occupancy times and transitions via computer during 10-minute test
15. Place rodents on zero maze apparatus
16. Assess time spent in closed/open maze areas using stopwatch
17. Spray 10-30% sucrose solution on dorsal coat

19. Acclimate mice individually to clear plastic cage with open top for one hour
20. Add two identical toy mini figures to opposite cage corners for five minutes

22. Replace one original figure with distinctly different novel object
23. After four hours, return mice to test cage and record time spent exploring objects
24. Cut paper towel tube lengthwise on one edge
25. Spread open tunnel on clean printer paper
26. Hold mice gently until calm

15. Punch 8mm discs from dried coated metal foil

17. Wrap cathodes and transfer to glove box

19. Weigh uncoated foil discs
20. Store electrolyte in Nalgene bottle wrapped in aluminum foil
21. Place stored electrolyte bottle inside Argon glove box
22. Clean lithium foil surface using nylon brush or stainless steel scalpel until shiny silvery surface appears
23. Punch 12.7 millimeter discs from lithium foil
24. Prepare 19 millimeter discs of Cellgard C480 membrane for separators
25. Flush exchanger five times with argon
26. Transfer working electrode, coin cell cases, springs, spacers, and separators onto glove box working surface
27. Add two drops of electrolyte onto cell cup
28. Position working electrode in cell cup
29. Add three drops of electrolyte onto assembly
30. Place two separators with two drops of electrolyte between them
31. Add two drops of electrolyte onto separators
32. Position lithium counter electrode on assembly
33. Place two stainless steel spacers on lithium disc
34. Place spring on spacers
35. Close assembly with cell cap
36. Crimp cell three to four times
37. Handle finished cell with plastic tweezers and wipe off excess electrolyte immediately after assembly
38. Connect coin cell to battery tester in open circuit voltage mode
39. Maintain open circuit voltage mode for one hour
40. Define voltage window for cell testing based on active material in working electrode
41. Calculate weight of electrode material
42. Compute weight of active material in electrode
43. Determine theoretical capacity for electrode disc
44. Initiate charge-discharge cycles at required C-rate

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Put on personal protective equipment
2. Take proper precautions while making RTX stock solution
3. Dilute one 600 microliter aliquot of RTX in saline for experiment
4. Divide remaining RTX solution into 12 microliter aliquots for storage
5. Administer single dose of RTX solution intraperitoneally to test mice using micro-injection syringe
6. Inject equal volume of vehicle intraperitoneally to control group mice
7. House injected mice in home cages
8. Provide ad libitum access to food and water
9. Set metal hot plate to 52 degrees Celsius
10. Place animal into transparent plexiglass cage on hot plate
11. Start timer when animal's hind paws touch hot cage surface
12. Monitor for shaking, licking hind paws, or jumping behavior
13. Remove animal upon response behavior and record latency to nearest 0.1 second
14. Terminate trial after 25 seconds if no behavioral response occurs
15. Conduct three trials per animal with 30-minute inter-trial rest intervals
16. Place animal into transparent round cage with metal mesh floor
17. Allow animal to acclimate to cage environment
18. Apply von Frey hair filaments to plantar region of hind paw using up-down method
19. Apply middle force filament to hind paw for 5-8 seconds
20. Wait 2 minutes after stimulation, then apply next filament force based on animal's response
21. Use next lower caliber filament if hind paw withdrew
22. Use next larger filament if hind paw did not withdraw
23. Repeat stimulation process until mouse is poked six times
24. Calculate mechanical threshold using established formula
25. Perform three trials on each hind paw per testing day
26. Calculate mean mechanical threshold per animal from six thresholds
27. Euthanize subject using 5 percent isoflurane
28. Perform intracardiac perfusion
29. Remove first foot pads from two hind paws
30. Post-fix foot pads in 4 percent PFA for six hours
31. Transfer foot pads to 0.1 Molar phosphate buffer at 4 degrees Celsius
32. Soak foot pads in 30% sucrose in PBS overnight
33. Section foot pads into 30 micron slices using cryostat (vertical to plantar surface)
34. Label foot pad sections sequentially
35. Store sections in anti-freeze solution at -20 degrees Celsius
36. Select every third section for sampling
37. Transfer selected sections to coated glass slide for air drying
38. Cover each sample side with cover plate
39. Quench foot pad sections with 1% hydrogen peroxide in methanol for 30 minutes
40. Wash tissues three times with room temperature 0.5 Molar Tris buffer
41. Incubate tissues in block solution for one hour
42. Incubate sections with anti-sera against pan-axonal marker overnight at 4°C
43. Wash sections with Tris buffer three times
44. Incubate sections in biotinylated anti-rabbit IgG at room temperature for 1 hour
45. Wash sections with Tris buffer three times to remove unbound antibodies
46. Incubate tissues with avidin biotin complex at room temperature for 45 minutes
47. Apply DAB solution for 45 seconds to stain reaction product
48. Wash foot pad sections with distilled water
49. Air dry foot pad sections before mounting
50. Dissect fourth and fifth lumbar DRG tissues
51. Post-fix dissected DRG tissues in 4% PFA for 2 hours at room temperature
52. Cryoprotect DRG tissues in 30% sucrose/PBS solution overnight
53. Cut cryoprotected DRG tissues into 8-micron sections
54. Place tissue sections on slides
55. Label slides containing tissue sections
56. Store labeled sections at -80°C
57. Prepare primary antisera mixture containing ATF-3 injury marker and peripherin neuronal marker
58. Incubate sections with primary antisera mixture overnight
59. Wash sections after primary antibody incubation
60. Incubate sections with appropriate secondary antisera
61. Perform series of washes after secondary antibody incubation
62. Mount sections for quantification

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Wrap desired amount of polylactic acid fibers (200 micron diameter) around lower three quarters of customized spindle
2. Reduce fiber overlap to maximize surface area exposure in sealable bottle
3. Mix 400 milliliters of deionized water with 40 milliliters of dysbaric
4. Close bottle
5. Shake bottle until homogeneous solution is obtained
6. Place 1000 milliliter beaker in water bath at 37 degrees Celsius
7. Pour 400 milliliters of tri fluoro ethanol into beaker
8. Add water disper solution to beaker
9. Stir mixture until uniform
10. Add one gram of malachite green dye to mixture
11. Stir mixture until dye dissolves
12. Attach spindle to digital mixer
13. Adjust spindle height
14. Position spindle 0.5 inches from container bottom
15. Set mixer speed to 400 RPM
16. Initiate slow mixing
17. Add 1.3 grams of tin oxalate catalyst to mixture
18. Adjust pH using sodium hydroxide
19. Calibrate pH to 6.8-7.2 range
20. Secure lid to beaker
21. Increase spindle rotation to 500 RPM
22. Maintain 500 RPM rotation for 24 hours
23. Manually break up agglomeration of tin oxalate during first two hours
24. Preheat oven to 35 degrees Celsius
25. Remove spindle from mixer
26. Place spindle in preheated oven
27. Leave spindle to dry overnight (≥8 hours)
28. Remove dried spindle from oven
29. Unwrap fibers from spindle
30. Remove excess catalyst from fibers
31. Obtain laser-cut brass patterning plates with desired microvascular pattern
32. Affix patterning plates on clip holders
33. Cut 10-inch length of catalyzed fiber per micro channel
34. Use plate cut to fiber diameter to remove remaining catalyst from fibers
35. Taper fiber edges using hot glue gun tip by slowly extruding fiber tips
36. Thread fibers through matching holes in brass patterning plate pairs
37. Screw plates onto molding box
38. Verify fibers are not twisted during plate attachment
39. String fiber tips through tuning pegs of custom tensioning board
40. Apply tension to PLA fibers until taut while avoiding over-tensioning
41. Remove excess particulates from fiber pattern using compressed air
42. Mix poly dimethyl siloxane base with curing agent in 10:1 volume ratio
43. Place mixture in desiccator jar
44. Degas mixture under vacuum for 10 minutes
45. Pour PDMS mixture into molding box (avoiding direct pour over fibers)
46. Remove bubbles in molding box and between fibers using 26 gauge needle
47. Cure assembly at 85°C for 30 minutes

1. Wrap desired amount of polylactic acid fibers (200 micron diameter) around lower three quarters of customized spindle

4. Close bottle
5. Shake bottle until homogeneous solution is obtained
6. Place 1000 milliliter beaker in water bath at 37 degrees Celsius
7. Pour 400 milliliters of tri fluoro ethanol into beaker
8. Add water disper solution to beaker
9. Stir mixture until uniform
10. Add one gram of malachite green dye to mixture
11. Stir mixture until dye dissolves
12. Attach spindle to digital mixer
13. Adjust spindle height
14. Position spindle 0.5 inches from container bottom
15. Set mixer speed to 400 RPM
16. Initiate slow mixing
17. Add 1.3 grams of tin oxalate catalyst to mixture
18. Adjust pH using sodium hydroxide
19. Calibrate pH to 6.8-7.2 range
20. Secure lid to beaker
21. Increase spindle rotation to 500 RPM
22. Maintain 500 RPM rotation for 24 hours
23. Manually break up agglomeration of tin oxalate during first two hours
24. Preheat oven to 35 degrees Celsius
25. Remove spindle from mixer
26. Place spindle in preheated oven
27. Leave spindle to dry overnight (≥8 hours)
28. Remove dried spindle from oven
29. Unwrap fibers from spindle
30. Remove excess catalyst from fibers
31. Obtain laser-cut brass patterning plates with desired microvascular pattern
32. Affix patterning plates on clip holders
33. Cut 10-inch length of catalyzed fiber per micro channel
34. Use plate cut to fiber diameter to remove remaining catalyst from fibers
35. Taper fiber edges using hot glue gun tip by slowly extruding fiber tips
36. Thread fibers through matching holes in brass patterning plate pairs
37. Screw plates onto molding box
38. Verify fibers are not twisted during plate attachment
39. String fiber tips through tuning pegs of custom tensioning board
40. Apply tension to PLA fibers until taut while avoiding over-tensioning
41. Remove excess particulates from fiber pattern using compressed air
42. Mix poly dimethyl siloxane base with curing agent in 10:1 volume ratio
43. Place mixture in desiccator jar
44. Degas mixture under vacuum for 10 minutes
45. Pour PDMS mixture into molding box (avoiding direct pour over fibers)
46. Remove bubbles in molding box and between fibers using 26 gauge needle
47. Cure assembly at 85°C for 30 minutes
48. Unfasten brass plates from molding box
49. Remove cured first stage from molding box
50. Select hypodermic needle with gauge ≥2× fiber outer diameter
51. Puncture holes in RTV end cap using selected needle
52. Thread fiber through needle-punctured hole
53. Remove needle from end cap
54. Fasten end caps to larger molding box
55. Pour second stage of PDMS
56. Remove remaining gas bubbles from PDMS
57. Cure PDMS at 85 degrees Celsius for 30 minutes

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Collect bulk soil samples using ethanol-sterilized soil core collector at 23-30 cm depth from plant base
2. Transfer collected soil to plastic bag
3. Homogenize soil by gentle shaking of plastic bag
4. Transfer 600-milligram soil aliquot into two-milliliter tube
5. Place two-milliliter tube on dry ice for preservation until DNA extraction
6. Dig up plant using ethanol-sterilized shovel while preserving root mass
7. Gently shake excess soil from roots until approximately 2mm of soil adheres to root surface
8. For large plants, cut representative root subsection using sterile scissors
9. Place minimum 500mg root tissue into 50-milliliter conical vial
10. Add epiphyte removal buffer until roots are covered
11. Immediately place sample on dry ice
12. Thaw root sample on ice
13. Sonicate root samples
14. Transfer roots to chilled 50-milliliter tube using sterile forceps
15. Centrifuge tube containing buffer and rhizosphere fraction
16. Decant supernatant
17. Place rhizosphere fraction in dry ice
18. Add 20 milliliters of 4°C sterile water to root fraction
19. Transfer 250 milligrams of soil and rhizosphere sample into commercial DNA isolation kit collection tubes using sterile spatula
20. Store extracted DNA at -20°C after elution
21. Chill sterilized mortar and pestle with liquid nitrogen
22. Measure 600-700 mg of root tissue
23. Place measured root tissue into mortar
24. Add sufficient liquid nitrogen to cover roots in mortar
25. Grind roots into small pieces
26. Repeat liquid nitrogen addition and grinding process ≥2 times consistently
27. Grind until roots become fine powder
28. Transfer root powder using sterile spatula to pre-weighed 1.5-mL tubes on ice before thawing
29. Prepare sufficient PCR master mix for triplicate amplification of each DNA sample
30. Pour master mix into sterile 25-milliliter multichannel pipette reservoir
31. Distribute 66 microliters of master mix into each well of new 96-well PCR plate
32. Add 6 microliters of 5 nanograms/microliter DNA from normalized DNA plate to master mix plate
33. Add 1.5 microliters of 10 micromolar forward primer with column-specific barcodes to master plate
34. Add 1.5 microliters of 10 micromolar reverse primer with row-specific barcodes to master plate
35. Cover reaction plate with sealing film
36. Centrifuge plate briefly at 3,000 RCF
37. Gently mix plate contents using multichannel pipette
38. Divide mixture into three plates with 25 microliters each
39. Cover all plates with PCR sealing film
40. Amplify DNA in plates using thermocycler
41. Pool three replicate amplification plates into a single 96-well plate
42. Calculate volume for 100 nanograms of each sample and average volume using computer spreadsheet
43. Add calculated average volume of blank PCR product into 1.5-milliliter tube
44. Pool 100 nanograms of each successfully amplified sample into the same 1.5-milliliter tube
45. Measure concentration of pooled product using benchtop fluorometer
46. Elute 600 nanograms of DNA in molecular-grade water to final volume of 100 microliters in 1.5-milliliter tube
47. Prepare 600 microliters of fresh 70% ethanol
48. Shake tube of magnetic beads to resuspend settled beads
49. Add 100 microliters of bead solution to 600-nanogram DNA aliquot
50. Mix solution and beads by pipetting 10 times
51. Incubate mixture for five minutes at room temperature
52. Place tube on magnetic stand for two minutes or until solution clears
53. Keep tube in magnetic stand
54. Aspirate clear supernatant carefully without touching magnetic beads
55. Add 300 microliters of 70% ethanol to tube
56. Incubate beads at room temperature for 30 seconds
57. Aspirate out ethanol and discard
58. Remove tube from magnetic stand
59. Air-dry contents for five minutes
60. Add 30 microliters of molecular-grade water to dried beads
61. Mix sample by pipetting 10 times
62. Incubate at room temperature for two minutes
63. Place tube on magnetic stand for one minute to separate beads from solution
64. Transfer eluate to new tube
65. Measure DNA concentration using benchtop fluorometer
66. Dilute aliquot to 10 nanomolar in 30 microliters (or to sequencing facility specifications)

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Collect up to 15 larvae in a 2 ml tube using glass pipette and pipette pump
2. Wash larvae twice with 1 ml cold 1X PBS on ice
3. Aspirate PBS from tube using glass pipette and pipette pump
4. Add 1 ml cold 4% PFA in PBS to tube
5. Incubate at 4°C overnight with gentle rocking
6. Aspirate PFA from tube using glass pipette and pipette pump
7. Rinse three times with 1 ml cold PBS
8. Hold larva on its back using fine forceps
9. Grip skin on either side of larva's head gently with forceps
10. Grab skin overlying heart with very fine forceps in other hand
11. Pull skin down diagonally toward tail on one side of fish in dish
12. Repeat skin pulling procedure on opposite side of fish
13. Continue grabbing skin flaps and pulling down until all skin/melanophores near liver are removed
14. Hold 5-6 DPF larvae on their back using fine forceps
15. Gently prod yolk of 5-6 DPF larvae using very fine forceps to lift in one piece
16. Hold 3-4 DPF larvae on their back using fine forceps
17. Stroke yolk of 3-4 DPF larvae from ventral side using very fine forceps
18. Scrape yolk off 3-4 DPF larvae in pieces
19. Transfer dissected larvae to fresh cold PBS in 2ml tube using glass pipette and pipette pump
20. Pour few milliliters of 3% methylcellulose onto lid of clean plastic Petri dish
21. Transfer larvae to methylcellulose using glass pipette with pipette pump, minimizing PBS transfer
22. Orient fish laterally under dissecting microscope at low magnification using fine forceps (right side facing left)
23. Verify alignment with one eye directly above the other eye
24. Remove bent tail by forceful pinching with forceps to ensure flat positioning
25. Zoom microscope to high magnification and focus on liver specimen
26. Press Capture Image button to acquire and save liver image
27. Repeat imaging process for all fish specimens at identical magnification
28. Acquire image of micrometer at same magnification for calibration
29. Rename files randomly and create randomization file listing original and blinded file names
30. Open randomized files in order starting with file one
31. Choose Freehand Selections tool
32. Outline each liver using Freehand Selections tool
33. Press Control-M to measure outlined liver area
34. Identify livers with residual skin/yolk, out-of-focus, or non-intact for placeholder measurement
35. Insert placeholder measurement for inadequate livers
36. Save all measurements in text file
37. Open measurements file and randomization file in spreadsheet program
38. Insert new column in measurements file
39. Add original file names for blinded files using randomization file
40. Save file as Unblinded Measurements file
41. Sort data by original file name
42. Exclude liver measurements with inadequate pictures
43. Convert measurement values to square millimeters if necessary
44. Perform procedure using small and controlled movements to prevent liver damage
45. Wear gloves when handling PFA
46. Handle concentrated PFA solutions in chemical fume hood

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Place CryoTubes containing frozen cells in 37°C water bath for two minutes
2. Transfer entire thawed cell suspension to a fresh 50 milliliter conical tube
3. Wash each tube with 1 ml of 37°C prewarmed plating medium
4. Add washed medium to 50 ml conical tube containing thawed cells
5. Add 8 ml of warm plating medium to tube
6. Carefully mix the suspension
7. Count live cells using trypan blue exclusion method and hemocytometer
8. Adjust cell concentration to 50,000 cells/ml by adding warm plating medium
9. Distribute cell suspension into two 384-well cell culture plates
10. Add 25 microliters of cell suspension to each well
11. Incubate plates at 37°C in humidified atmosphere with 5% CO₂
12. Replace plating medium with 50 microliters of warm maintenance medium
13. Replace half of maintenance medium every two to three days
14. Replace maintenance medium entirely on days seven, 14, and 21
15. Turn on plate reader and set heating system to 37 degrees Celsius
16. Warm 40 milliliters of maintenance medium supplemented with 1% penicillin streptomycin to room temperature
17. Distribute test compounds and controls into two 384-well plates by adding 1.5 microliters of stock solution per well
18. Centrifuge compound plates at 100 G for one minute
19. Add 37 microliters of maintenance medium to each well of compound plates
20. Centrifuge plates at 100 G for one minute
21. Warm 100 milliliters of maintenance medium supplemented with 1% volume-by-volume penicillin streptomycin in a 37 degree Celsius water bath
22. Add 10 milliliters of warmed medium to calcium-sensitive fluorescent dye and quencher mix to reconstitute as stock fluorescence medium
23. Start plate reader software
24. Configure software to record two-minute calcium wave segment with acquisition frequency of at least 10 hertz
25. Dilute three milliliters of stock fluorescence medium with 12 milliliters of warm maintenance medium with antibiotics to prepare fluorescence medium
26. Replace 20 microliters of medium in each well with 30 microliters of fluorescence medium
27. Mix contents by pipetting up and down once
28. Place plate into plate reader
29. Equilibrate HIPSCCMs to reader temperature for 60 minutes
30. Start plate reader software
31. Record two-minute segment of calcium waves with ≥10 Hz acquisition frequency to establish baseline beating rate
32. Use plate reader robot to pipette 5 microliters of test and reference compounds well-to-well from compound plate to cell plate
33. Start data acquisition in plate reader software to record 2-minute segments of calcium waves at 5, 15, 30, 45, and 60 minutes post-addition

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Cut hole in bottom of one-ounce cup
2. Glue screen over hole for air exchange
3. Aspirate insect colony into 15 milliliter conical tube
4. Chill insects on ice briefly
5. Identify female insects by presence of ovipositor on ventral abdomen
6. Transfer identified females into prepared cup
7. Seal cup with 5x5 cm paraffin wax film after collecting 15 females
8. Apply 400 microliters of 10% sucrose solution to top of film
9. Place second 5x5 cm paraffin wax film over sucrose solution
10. Place adult chamber on egg collection dish with plastic paraffin wax film contacting oviposition medium
11. Wrap entire egg-laying chamber with plastic wrap while avoiding air holes
12. Place chamber at 25°C with 70% humidity and 14-hour light/10-hour dark cycle
13. Apply 1x15 mm double-sided tape strip onto 22x30 mm cover slip
14. Place cover slip onto oviposition medium of egg-laying chamber
15. Transfer individual semi-transparent embryos from agar surface to double-sided tape using fine brush under dissecting microscope
16. Arrange approximately 25 banana-shaped embryos laterally on tape with larger ends adhered
17. Place cover slip onto 100 × 15 ml Petri dish containing 1% agar
18. Position setup under dissecting microscope inside humidified hood
19. Place tip of quartz injection needle in water drop
20. Initiate injection cycle for pressure calibration
21. Insert needle into larger end of embryo approaching from left side of cover slip
22. Deliver injection solution into egg
23. Withdraw needle from embryo
24. Transfer cover slip to new 1% agar dish surface
25. Place dish into humidity chamber
26. Transfer surviving embryos using fine brush to 35x10mm Petri dish containing water-moistened filter paper
27. Seal Petri dish with plastic paraffin wax film
28. Place sealed Petri dish in 25°C incubator
29. Transfer emergent first instar nymphs using fine brush to Petri dish containing leaf clippings
30. Seal nymph dish with plastic paraffin wax film
31. Transfer two-day-old nymphs to rearing cage with corn plants using fine brush
32. Separately cage insects with visible phenotype for target trait recovery
33. Rear insects under controlled temperature/humidity with regular transfers to fresh corn plants
34. Screen progeny regularly for expected phenotypes
35. Place phenotype-positive individuals into separate cages to establish homozygous lines

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Fix pancreas sample in freshly prepared 4% paraformaldehyde
2. Incubate sample at 4°C for 48 hours
3. Chill flask by placing it in ice-filled bucket
4. Place bucket on magnetic stir plate
5. Position flask flat in bucket
6. Add magnetic stir bar to flask
7. Pour 147.8 milliliters of ice-cold distilled deionized water into flask
8. Add 20 milliliters of 0.1 molar PBS
9. Add 20 milliliters of ice-cold 40% acrylamide solution
10. Add 12.2 milliliters of 16% paraformaldehyde solution
11. Add 250 milligrams of VA-044 initiator
12. Mix entire hydrogel solution for at least 10 minutes
13. Place 15 milliliter conical tube in ice next to flask containing hydrogel solution
14. Pipette 14 milliliters of monomer solution into conical tube
15. Add one piece of fixed and sectioned tissue sample to tube
16. Cap conical tube
17. Incubate sample in monomer solution for 3 days at 4°C protected from light
18. Place sample on ice
19. Connect tubing to nitrogen tank through stopcock
20. Pierce cap of conical tube containing sample with 18 gauge hypodermic needle
21. Insert needle into tube until submerged under surface of liquid monomer solution
22. Puncture opposite side of cap with 18-gauge hypodermic needle
23. Ensure needle remains unsubmerged to act as vent
24. Connect nitrogen tank tubing to submerged hypodermic needle beneath hydrogel
25. Slowly turn on nitrogen until steady bubbling through liquid
26. Remove both needles after deoxygenation
27. Cover cap with paraffin film to prevent gas exchange
28. Incubate sample at 37°C for three hours to polymerize hydrogel
29. Pour away remaining hydrogel
30. Wash sample in 3-5 exchanges of 0.01 M PBS for 15 minutes per wash
31. Transfer sample to 50 ml conical tube containing 40 ml clearing buffer
32. Incubate sample in clearing buffer at 37°C with buffer replacement every other day
33. Verify clearing by holding sample to light and confirming light transmission with tan coloring in exocrine regions
34. Exchange clearing buffer with 40 milliliters of 0.01 molar PBS
35. Place samples on shaker at 60 RPM for one day at room temperature
36. Perform 4-5 buffer changes
37. Continue final wash overnight
38. Prepare PACT staining buffer in 2 milliliter flat bottom tube
39. Add 2% normal serum to 1 milliliter base PACT buffer
40. Add approximately five times standard amount of primary antibody to staining buffer
41. Remove sample from wash buffer using spatula
42. Dab excess buffer onto paper towel
43. Place sample into tube containing primary antibody solution
44. Incubate sample in solution for 2-4 days at room temperature on shaker at 60 RPM
45. Remove primary antibody solution
46. Add 0.01 molar PBS
47. Wash samples on shaker at 60 RPM
48. Replace PBS buffer 4-5 times during washing
49. Leave final wash on shaker overnight
50. Prepare secondary antibody at 1:200 dilution in PACT staining buffer with 2% serum
51. Remove sample from wash buffer using a spatula
52. Dab excess buffer off sample onto paper towel
53. Place sample in tube containing secondary antibody solution
54. Incubate sample at room temperature on shaker at 60 RPM for two days with light protection
55. Remove antibody solution following incubation
56. Replace solution with 0.01 molar PBS
57. Wash samples thoroughly on shaker at 60 RPM with 4-5 buffer changes, leaving final wash overnight
58. Weigh 11 grams of non-ionic density gradient medium
59. Transfer weighed medium to 50 milliliter conical tube
60. Add 5 milliliters of 0.02 molar phosphate buffer to tube
61. Use spatula to release air from powdered medium
62. Adjust volume to 10 milliliters using additional 0.02 molar phosphate buffer
63. Mix solution with spatula
64. Scrape excess solution from spatula into tube
65. Incubate buffer at 37 degrees Celsius until fully dissolved
66. Periodically invert and gently mix solution during incubation
67. Transfer samples into refractive index match solution buffer
68. Incubate samples on bench protected from light for 2-4 days
69. Place refractive index match solution into eight-well coverslip bottom chamber slide
70. Add sample to well
71. Cap slide

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Euthanize mouse with carbon dioxide
2. Collect colon and flush with PBS
3. Open colon longitudinally using scissors
4. Place colon under stereo microscope
5. Remove tumor-containing regions using scissors
6. Collect and wash tumor tissue with PBS
7. Transfer intestinal fragments to EDTA chelation buffer
8. Incubate fragments in EDTA chelation buffer on ice for 60 minutes
9. Aspirate chelation buffer containing detached normal epithelial cells
10. Wash remaining tumor fragments with 5 mL cold chelation buffer
11. Aspirate chelation buffer after washing
12. Wash tumor fragments with 5 mL cold 1X PBS
13. Add digestion buffer to tissue fragments
14. Incubate mixture at 37°C for 2 hours
15. Allow tumor fragments to settle under normal gravity for 1 minute
16. Collect supernatant in 15 milliliter centrifuge tube
17. Centrifuge supernatant at 200×g for 3 minutes to pellet cells
18. Wash pellet with 5 milliliters of PBS
19. Centrifuge again after washing
20. Resuspend tumor pellet with 500 microliters of PBS
21. Count isolated single tumor cells using a hemocytometer
22. Centrifuge tumor cells at 200 times G for three minutes
23. Resuspend tumor cells in five milligrams per milliliter matrigel on ice
24. Plate 15,000 cells in 50 microliter volume of matrigel per well in 24-well plate
25. Allow matrigel-cell mixture to polymerize for 15 minutes at 37 degrees Celsius
26. Add 500 microliters of basal culture medium containing 50 nanograms per milliliter human EGF to each well
27. Change basal culture medium containing EGF
28. Mechanically massage organoids
29. Replace culture medium with fresh basal medium
30. Use P1000 pipette with cut tip to mechanically disrupt organoids and matrigel
31. Transfer organoid suspension into 15 milliliter falcon tube
32. Use fire-polished pasteur pipette to further disrupt organoids
33. Wash dissociated organoids with 5 milliliters of basal culture medium
34. Centrifuge at 200 × g for 2 minutes
35. Discard supernatant
36. Resuspend pellet with matrigel and plate
37. Dissociate organoids
38. Wash organoids
39. Centrifuge organoids
40. Discard supernatant
41. Resuspend pellet
42. Resuspend pellet in DMEM containing 20% fetal bovine serum and 10% dimethyl sulfoxide
43. Transfer cell suspension into 1.5 milliliter cryo tubes
44. Transfer cryo tubes to Nalgene Mr. Frosty freezing container
45. Store container in -80°C freezer to achieve cooling rate of -1°C per minute
46. Transfer cells to liquid nitrogen after overnight incubation
47. Collect cells for RNA extraction
48. Isolate RNA using PicoPure RNA isolation kit
49. Collect cell pellet for protein extraction
50. Resuspend cell pellet in 100 microliters RIPA buffer
51. Aspirate cell culture medium for immunohistochemistry
52. Transfer tumor organoids to cryo mold using cut P1000 pipette
53. Place cryo mold on dry ice
54. Add tissue freezing medium to cryo mold
55. Cut 7-micron frozen sections
56. Stain sections according to published protocols

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Create R mix by dissolving 0.88 grams of polyethyleneimine in 25.00 grams of water using a planetary mixer
2. Create F mix by dissolving 0.88 grams of polyethyleneimine in 16.83 grams of water using a planetary mixer
3. Dissolve 12.60 grams of resorcinol powder into R mix until solution turns clear
4. Add 17.03 grams of formaldehyde solution to F mix and ensure complete mixing
5. Add 5.25 grams of boron carbide powder incrementally into R mix and F mix separately
6. Add 6.50 grams of acetic acid to R mix and F mix
7. Ensure complete mixing of both R mix and F mix
8. Print molds using fused deposition modeling 3D printer with ABS filaments
9. Combine R mix and F mix to obtain final suspension
10. Mix final suspension using stirring plate at 200-300 RPM
11. Apply vacuum to final suspension for 10 minutes using vacuum jar to remove air bubbles (without boiling water)
12. Pour de-aired suspension into 3D printed mold
13. Seal glass container to prevent moisture loss
14. Place sealed container with mold into 60-80°C oven
15. Cure cast for at least eight hours
16. Remove sealed container with mold from oven
17. Allow container to cool to room temperature
18. Transfer mold to new container
19. Add acetone until mold is fully submerged
20. Extract free green body from acetone bath after ABS dissolution
21. Place green body in oven at 80 degrees Celsius for drying
22. Place dried green bodies in two-inch quartz tubes lined with graphite foil
23. Load quartz tubes into furnace
24. Establish gas flow at 250 SCCM with 4 wt% H₂ and 96 wt% Ar to create reducing atmosphere
25. Heat green bodies at 5°C/min to 1050°C and hold for 3 hours
26. Verify uniform dark coloration indicating carbon formation from pyrolysis
27. Place carbonized parts in graphite furnace
28. Backfill furnace with flowing helium gas under vacuum
29. Heat furnace to 2,290 degrees Celsius
30. Hold temperature at 2,290°C for one hour
31. Remove parts from furnace

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Sterilize cover slips with 70% ethanol for 2 minutes
2. Air dry sterilized cover slips
3. Place each cover slip into 10 cm culture plate
4. Add 300 microliters warm gelatin solution to center of cover slip (avoiding edges)
5. Aspirate gelatin solution after 5 minutes incubation
6. Harvest confluent human umbilical vein endothelial cells using 0.25% trypsin-EDTA
7. Count cells and pellet by centrifugation
8. Aspirate supernatant media
9. Resuspend cell pellets in basic nuclear effector solution
10. Add 0.2-2 micrograms of GFP beta actin vector plasmid
11. Transfer 100 microliters of cell suspension to nuclear effector cuvette
12. Tap covered cuvette to eliminate bubbles between bottom and sample
13. Place cuvette into nuclear effector device
14. Run program A034 on device
15. Add 500 microliters of warmed EGM2-MV media to cuvette using transfer pipette
16. Transfer mixture back to microcentrifuge tube
17. Incubate mixture for 15 minutes
18. Gently mix transfected VE suspension once
19. Seed 300 microliters of suspension onto gelatin-coated cover slip without touching edges
20. Incubate for one to four hours
21. Inspect transfected cells to confirm attachment to cover slip
22. Add 10 milliliters of E GM two MV media to plate
23. Continue incubation for GFP actin expression
24. Add albumin physiological salt solution (PSS) to gravity flow system
25. Connect gravity flow system to inline heater
26. Adjust flow regulator to achieve 40 milliliters per hour flow rate
27. Close stop cock using cotton tipped applicator to stop flow
28. Apply vacuum grease to outer edge of underside of diamond bath
29. Remove transfected culture from incubator
30. Gently lift cover slip using forceps
31. Soak up excess EGM-2MV media from bottom slide using Kim wipe
32. Position cover slip with cells facing upward
33. Place diamond bath over cover slip to form chamber
34. Place chamber into stage heater
35. Tighten clamps over chamber
36. Pipette approximately 1 milliliter of EGM-2MV media from plate into chamber
37. Attach vacuum hose to holder on left side of chamber
38. Connect A-PSS line from inline heater to channel on right side of chamber
39. Turn on vacuum
40. Open A-PSS line to initiate bath perfusion over transfected cells
41. Switch on inline heater at 37 degrees Celsius
42. Place thermistor probe for heated stage into bath at edge
43. Switch on stage heater at 37 degrees Celsius
44. Wipe underside of cover slip using Kim wipe soaked with 70% ethanol
45. Wipe underside of cover slip using dry Kim wipe
46. Equilibrate transfected HU cells for 30 minutes
47. Lock microscope course adjustment
48. Focus microscope on selected culture cells
49. Set camera exposure time between 0.5-2 seconds based on GFP-actin signal intensity
50. Set interval and duration times for time-lapse experiment
51. Capture single test image with room lights off to evaluate settings
52. Initiate time-lapse image acquisition series
53. Draw single-pixel line perpendicular to cell edge
54. Extract region from each time-lapse image to generate kymograph montage
