question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Mix and degas 1.1 grams of PDMS prepolymer mixture in speed mixer
2. Run speed mixer at 2350 RPM under vacuum for three minutes
3. Place doctor blade on 100% isopropanol-cleaned PET foil
4. Adjust backing layer thickness using micropositioning screws
5. Load PDMS into 10 milliliter syringe
6. Fill doctor blade reservoir with polymer
7. Initiate doctor blade movement at 2 millimeters per second
8. Transfer coated PET film to 95°C oven
9. Cure film in 95°C oven for one hour
10. Remove thin strips from long sides of backing layer using blade
11. Apply second layer of PDMS using doctor blade technique on PET foil
12. Place foil into oven
13. Cut films on PET foil into 4 cm² pieces using razor blade
14. Secure film pieces onto glass slide using UV glue
15. Illuminate pieces with UV light for 3 minutes
16. Mount first polymeric sample onto sample holder
17. Adjust tilt angle using goniometer
18. Adjust tilt angle using goniometer until substrate contacts polymeric film with parallel alignment
19. Move aligned substrate to polymeric film surface until achieving 13 ± 5 kPa pre-load stress
20. Start custom LabView software package
21. Set measurement parameters for adhesion testing of 3 samples at 6 locations per film
22. Cut polymeric sample into 0.25 square centimeter pieces using a razor blade
23. Attach sample pieces to edge of glass slide
24. Place glass slide in vertical orientation under upright microscope
25. Measure thickness of film cross section
26. Cut approximately 5x5 mm pieces from PET supportive layer using scalpel
27. Transfer cut pieces onto individual 12 mm glass cover slips using tweezers
28. Transfer cover slips into reaction chamber of plasma device
29. Close plasma device lid
30. Evacuate chamber until 1.6×10⁻² mbar pressure is reached
31. Treat films with plasma for 3 minutes
32. Ventilate reaction chamber
33. Transfer samples into individual wells of 24-well plate
34. Wash 70-80% confluent L929 cell culture with calcium/magnesium-free DPBS for 30 seconds in laminar flow cabinet
35. Incubate cells with 2 ml proteolytic/collagenolytic solution at 37°C and 5% CO₂ for 5 minutes
36. Add 8 ml serum-supplemented medium after cell detachment
37. Transfer cell suspension to 15 ml conical tube
38. Seed 60,000 cells/ml per well onto polymeric samples in 24-well plate
39. Place plate in cell culture incubator for 3 days

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Transfer all needed materials into glove box inside HEPA-filter equipped fume cupboard
2. Weigh desired quantity of nanomaterials into bottle and add water to achieve 1 g/L nanoparticle concentration
3. Seal bottle with lid
4. Ultrasonicate at 43 kHz and 60 watts for 30 minutes to disperse CNTs and GNPs
5. Pour nanoparticles suspension into reactor flask containing magnetic stir-bar
6. Insert ozone bubbler and connect it to ozone generator
7. Place reactor flask into refrigerated bath
8. Stir solution to prevent CNTs from settling
9. Turn on cooling water recirculation unit to cool ozone reactor and ultrasonic cell
10. Turn on oxygen supply to ozone generator
11. Adjust oxygen flow rate to 0.5 liters per minute
12. Turn on ozone generator and allow it to run for 30-60 minutes
13. Turn on peristaltic pump
14. Adjust peristaltic pump to 0.67 Hz
15. Turn on ultrasonic horn
16. Adjust ultrasonic horn power to 60 watts
17. Observe sonication process for at least 30 minutes to ensure stable operation
18. Turn off ozone generator, sonicator, and pump after processing time
19. Stir carbon nanomaterial dispersion for one hour to decompose ozone and solution
20. Sonicate nanomaterial suspension if electrophoresis is not performed within 24 hours of ultrasonicated ozonolysis processing
21. Abrade electrodes using P1000 aluminum oxide sandpaper
22. Clean abraded electrodes in ultrasonic bath for 10 minutes in ultra pure water
23. Dry electrodes in clean oven for 10 minutes at 100 degrees celsius
24. Transfer electrodes to desiccator to cool
25. Weigh cooled electrodes using four-figure analytical balance for anotic deposition
26. Transfer 35 milliliters of carbon nanomaterial dispersion to a 50 milliliter beaker
27. Secure electrode fixture to retort stand
28. Slowly lower electrodes to bottom of beaker
29. Attach positive terminal of DC power supply to central anode
30. Attach negative terminal to one outer cathode
31. Connect two outer cathodes using lid with alligator clips
32. With power supply off, adjust current to maximum setting
33. Adjust voltage to required value for study
34. Prepare stopwatch
35. Turn on power supply for required coating duration
36. Switch off the power supply
37. Slowly raise electrode fixture out of dispersion while ensuring film is not disturbed
38. Disconnect terminals from electrodes
39. Rotate electrode fixture to horizontal orientation
40. Allow film on anode to dry evenly
41. Disassemble fixture
42. Dry anode in oven at 100°C for one hour
43. Place dried anode in desiccator to cool
44. Record anode weight to four decimal places using analytical scale
45. Photograph coded anode

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Measure approximately 5 ml of glass beads in a 50 ml conical tube
2. Add 25 ml of 2M sodium hydroxide to the tube
3. Mix gently for 2 hours using shaker or rotor
4. Centrifuge tube briefly if beads are in suspension
5. Decant sodium hydroxide while retaining beads
6. Wash beads thoroughly with cell culture grade water until pH neutral, decanting water after each wash
7. Verify neutral pH using test strip on effluent
8. Wash beads thoroughly with 100% ethanol 2-3 times
9. Decant ethanol from container
10. Dry beads and sprinkle to form thin layer in sterile container
11. Place open container in biosafety cabinet for overnight air-drying
12. Gently tap or shake container to check for sandy texture without clumping/flaking
13. Add beads to bead holding chamber of apparatus
14. Cover entire opening of bead holding chamber with polypropylene mesh (105 micrometer openings)
15. Clamp mesh between male and female ends of metal reusable imaging chamber
16. Seal apparatus tightly with waxy film
17. UV sterilize apparatus for 15 minutes
18. Store apparatus in dry container desiccated by silica gel or equivalent desiccant medium
19. Seal storage container
20. Aspirate medium from cells and edges of chamber
21. Tilt chamber at 45-degree angle
22. Aspirate remaining media drop from center micro well
23. Pipette bead loading solution onto glass micro well
24. Disperse monolayer of glass beads onto cells using bead loading apparatus
25. Perform tapping technique: pinch chamber, lift ~2 inches, bring down firmly
26. Add medium back into chamber by pipetting slowly onto plastic side
27. Aspirate floating beads without disturbing cells
28. Incubate cells for 4.5 hours in incubator
29. Wash cells three times with medium to remove residual beads and loading components
30. Image cells immediately or as required by experiment using manuscript instructions
31. Add monolayer of beads to cells
32. Incubate for 30-minute recovery period post bead loading

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Sterilize scalpel with 70% ethanol-moistened paper towels
2. Place cover slip in 35 millimeter cell culture dish using scalpel
3. Add one milliliter of culture medium to dish with cover slip
4. Hold cover slip center with sterile forceps
5. Aspirate culture medium completely from dish
6. Prepare three milliliter cell suspension in culture medium in 15 milliliter tube
7. Add cell suspension to culture dish
8. Incubate sample overnight at 37°C and 5% CO₂
9. Verify cell attachment and viability using inverted phase-contrast microscope
10. Irradiate dish with 6 Gy X-ray radiation
11. Incubate cells for 72 hours at 37°C and 5% CO₂
12. Take cells from incubator
13. Aspirate culture medium from culture dish
14. Cut 5 mm from end of 1,000 microliter micropipette tip using scissors
15. Add 1 milliliter fixation solution to culture dish along walls to minimize cell damage
16. Transfer culture dish into square culture dish and shake gently to distribute fixation solution evenly over cover slip
17. Incubate dish for 10 minutes at room temperature
18. Aspirate fixation solution from dish
19. Add 2 milliliters of PBS along dish walls
20. Aspirate PBS from dish
21. Add 5 microliters of DAPI staining reagent onto glass slide
22. Remove cover slip from dish using scalpel
23. Drain excess PBS by touching edge of cover slip with paper towel
24. Mount cover slip upside down on DAPI staining reagent drop on glass slide
25. Examine stained cells under fluorescence microscope with DAPI filter

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Place anesthetized mouse in supine position on sterile-draped surgery table
2. Apply ocular lubricant to protect eyes from drying
3. Shave abdominal hair for operation and leg hair for postoperative perfusion measurements using pen trimmer
4. Clear shaved hair off surgical field
5. Fixate upper and lower extremities with rubber bands and tacks
6. Check anesthesia depth via toe pinch reflex and titrate anesthesia as necessary
7. Perform respiratory pattern evaluation every 3-5 minutes to calibrate anesthesia level
8. Perform midline laparotomy from lower sternal margin to pubis symphysis
9. Dissect pubis fat pad to widen operative field
10. Open celiotomy to access peritoneal contents
11. Cover bowels with saline-soaked non-woven sponge
12. Dissect perivascular fascia and adipose tissue from 1 cm proximal to aortic bifurcation to left iliac bifurcation using straight Dumont forceps and double-ended sharp cotton swabs
13. Pass tip of angled forceps under left common iliac vascular bundle
14. Gently spread forceps multiple times to mobilize vessels from retroperitoneal musculature
15. Place two 4-0 silk sutures around isolated left common iliac arteriovenous bundle as ligatures
16. Create single knot with each 4-0 silk tie
17. Apply knots sequentially (proximally then distally)
18. Rotate left iliac arteriovenous bundle clockwise using 4-0 silk suture strings as handles
19. Fine-tune position to temporarily locate vein anterior to artery
20. Make 1mm longitudinal venotomy using straight Vannas spring scissors
21. Flush residual blood from venous lumen with 0.9% saline
22. Place 10-0 nylon suture through posterior wall of vein
23. Grab suture ends and apply gentle tension to separate anterior and posterior vessel walls
24. Make elliptical incision (1.0 × 0.3 mm) using curved Vannas spring scissors
25. Remove adherent walls of iliac artery and vein
26. Flush exposed arterial lumen with 0.9% saline and heparinized saline
27. Repair initial anterior wall venotomy with 2-3 10-0 nylon sutures in interrupted fashion
28. Restore vascular bundle to original anatomic orientation
29. Place saline-soaked absorbable gelatin sponge adjacent to repaired venotomy
30. Loosen 4-0 single knot cross clamp ligatures sequentially from distal to proximal
31. Monitor venotomy site for excessive bleeding
32. Gently rub vascular bundle with double-ended hard sharp-pointed cotton swabs
33. Visualize pulsatile bright red oxygenated blood entering iliac vein and mixing with dark venous blood to confirm fistula success
34. Inject heparinized saline into inferior vena cava for systemic anticoagulation
35. Reinspect surgical site for hemostasis after heparinized saline injection
36. Close midline fascia with absorbable 5-0 PGA sutures in running fashion if no bleeding concerns
37. Close skin incision with absorbable 5-0 PGA sutures in running fashion

1. Plant Nicotiana benthamiana seeds in wet soil
2. Plant seeds at high density
3. Place plants in controlled environmental chamber at 20-25°C with 16 hours light daily (75 μmol photons/m²/s)
4. Transfer seedlings to larger pots after hypocotyl diameter reaches 0.5 cm
5. Continue growth in chamber under same conditions
6. Maintain plants until four-to-eight leaf stage (approximately four weeks)
7. Add one microgram of pPZP-RCS2-based plasmid to 100 microliter culture of Agrobacterium tumefaciens competent cells
8. Incubate cells on ice for 30 minutes
9. Flash-freeze cells in liquid nitrogen for one minute
10. Warm cells at 28°C for 15 minutes
11. Add 1 milliliter of LB medium to competent cell mixture
12. Incubate cells at 28°C for 2 hours
13. Centrifuge cells at 3,000 × g for 1 minute
14. Resuspend cell pellet in 0.2 milliliters of LB medium
15. Plate cells on antibiotic-selective LB agar plates
16. Incubate plates at 28°C for 48 hours until colonies are visible
17. Pick and plate individual colonies onto fresh LB agar plates
18. Grow plated cells at 28 degrees Celsius for 48 hours
19. Take small amount of bacteria from each plate
20. Perform Colony PCR to confirm presence of specific binary constructs
21. Add confirmed Agrobacterium colony to 5 mL LB medium supplemented with appropriate antibiotics
22. Grow cells overnight at 28 degrees Celsius
23. Centrifuge cells at 3,000 × g
24. Resuspend pellet to OD600 of 0.5 in agroinfiltration buffer
25. Incubate suspension for two hours at room temperature
26. Load inoculum into 1 mL plastic syringe
27. Hold mature N. Benthamiana leaf with gloved finger on adaxial face
28. Press syringe nozzle against lower epidermis of leaf
29. Slowly inject Agrobacterium in infiltration medium
30. Maintain infiltrated plant until observation
31. Cut infiltrated leaves into fragments between veins using blade
32. Place leaf fragments on object slide with abaxial surface facing up
33. Place drop of water on leaf slice
34. Cover sample with cover glass
35. Immobilize cover glass with tape on each side
36. Transfer slide to confocal microscope stage
37. Use 10X objective lens to identify cells with best signal
38. Use 63X objective lens to record detailed cell images

44. Conduct 62-second habituation period with guillotine doors closed

46. Allow mice to freely explore all three chambers for 1200 seconds

13. Moisten four layers of 20-25 μm pore size filtration material with grinding medium
14. Filter homogenate through all four layers of filtration material using plastic funnel into 500 mL conical flask

16. Combine entire homogenate in filtration material for maximum filtration
17. Grind remaining tissue on filtration material with 150 mL grinding medium and filter into 500 mL conical flask
18. Centrifuge homogenate at 2,500 g at 4°C in fixed angle rotor for 5 minutes
19. Pour supernatant into clean centrifuge tubes

21. Leave 3 mL of supernatant in tube
22. Resuspend pellet in remaining supernatant using fine paintbrush
23. Add 10 mL of single strength wash buffer to each tube

26. Repeat centrifugation steps

33. Aspirate upper 5 cm of solution above mitochondrial band
34. Distribute mitochondrial solution into two clean tubes
35. Fill tubes with single strength wash buffer
36. Cover tube mouth with plastic paraffin film
37. Invert tube several times to distribute colloidal density gradient and mitochondria
38. Centrifuge at 31,000 g for 15 minutes at 4°C with slow brake
39. Aspirate supernatant and adjust tube volume to 50 mL with single strength wash buffer

42. Aspirate supernatant and resuspend mitochondrial pellet in 500 µL single strength wash buffer using pipette with modified 200 µL tip
43. Add 970 µl of air-saturated respiration medium to reaction chamber with plunger open
44. Add 150 µg of total mitochondrial protein to reaction chamber using modified pipette
45. Add 500 µM Adenosine Triphosphate to reaction chamber
46. Continuously stir mitochondrial suspension using magnetic stir bar
47. Close plunger
48. Start recording oxygen consumption
49. Wait for stable linear oxygen consumption trace (1-2 minutes)
50. Add 5 mM succinate
51. Record oxygen consumption for approximately 2 minutes
52. Add 1 mM Adenosine Diphosphate (ADP)
53. Record oxygen consumption for 2 minutes
54. Add 1 mM Nicotinamide Adenine Dinucleotide Hydrogen (NADH)
55. Record oxygen consumption for 4-8 minutes
56. Add cytochrome C pathway inhibitors
57. Record oxygen consumption via alternative oxidase for 2 minutes
58. Add 5 mM dithiothreitol and 10 mM pyruvate

61. Record oxygen consumption for 2 minutes

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Transfer eggs laid by adult moths to two liter boxes
2. Add wax moth substrate to boxes containing eggs
3. Incubate boxes at 30 degrees Celsius in the dark
4. Transfer 25 grams of larva-containing substrate to fresh wax moth substrate after 1-2 weeks when larvae become visible
5. Synchronize larvae by size into groups of 30-40 larvae after two weeks
6. Transfer synchronized larvae groups to new two liter containers with wax moth substrate
7. Select larvae for experiment by weight (180-200mg) after two weeks, choosing only pale, fast-moving specimens
8. Load insulin syringe with blunt-ended needle containing 1 × 10⁷ bacteria per 10 microliters of DPBS per larva
9. Load syringe into microsyringe pump
10. Wait for larva to open mouth parts
11. Insert syringe carefully between larval mandibles
12. Deliver bacterial suspension
13. Incubate fed larvae in dark at 37°C for 1-24 hours
14. Clean safety hood with ethanol
15. Spray reagent to prevent RNase contamination
16. Snap-freeze living larva in liquid nitrogen
17. Homogenize individual frozen larva using mortar and pestle with small volume of liquid nitrogen
18. Transfer powdered homogenate to disposable weigh boat
19. Wait for liquid nitrogen to evaporate
20. Mix powdered homogenates with 1 ml TRIzol in 2 ml tube
21. Incubate mixture at room temperature for 1 hour
22. Pellet homogenate by centrifugation
23. Transfer supernatant to new tube
24. Mix supernatant with 200 μl BCP (1-Bromo-3-chloropropane)
25. Vortex mixture and incubate for 5 minutes at room temperature
26. Incubate mixture on ice for 10 minutes
27. Centrifuge BCP-added reaction mixture
28. Transfer upper transparent aqueous layer to new 2 ml tube
29. Add 500 microliters isopropanol to precipitate RNA
30. Mix by inverting tube for 5 minutes
31. Collect precipitated RNA via centrifugation
32. Wash RNA pellet with 500 microliters of 75% ethanol
33. Air-dry RNA pellet for 5-10 minutes at room temperature
34. Add 100 microliters of ribonuclease inhibitor in nuclease-free water to dried RNA pellet
35. Vortex tube until RNA pellet is completely dissolved
36. Measure RNA quality and quantity by spectrophotometry (260/280≈2, 260/230=2-2.2)
37. Prepare 5 micrograms isolated RNA for DNase digestion and gene expression analysis

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Add 20 microliters of freshly prepared POPC solution and 4 microliters of freshly prepared biotin-PEG-DSPE solution to a glass vial
2. Evaporate organic solvent by air flow until lipid film forms
3. Place lipid film in desiccator for one hour to completely evaporate organic solvent
4. Add 200 microliters of mineral oil to the vial
5. Cover vial opening with plastic paraffin film
6. Sonicate vial contents in ultrasonic bath at 120 watts for at least one hour
7. Create a lipid film
8. Add 200 microliters of 200 millimolar glucose in LB medium to the lipid film
9. Sonicate the film at 120 watts for at least one hour
10. Extrude resulting giant vesicles into small vesicles using a mini extruder and polycarbonate membrane with 100-nanometer pore size
11. Inoculate E. coli from LB plate into LB medium
12. Incubate culture overnight at 37°C
13. Transfer 20 microliters of culture supernatant to 1.98 milliliters of fresh LB medium
14. Incubate sub-culture for two hours
15. Measure optical density at 600 nm (OD600) using spectrophotometer
16. Mix pre-culture solution with sucrose solution and fresh LB medium according to table one
17. Add 50 microliters of freshly prepared outer aqueous solution to a 1.5 milliliter lidded plastic tube
18. Layer 150 microliters of lipid-containing oil solution over the outer aqueous solution
19. Incubate solution for 10 to 15 minutes at room temperature
20. Verify oil-aqueous solution interface is flat
21. Add 2 microliters of freshly prepared inner aqueous solution of giant vesicles to 50 microliters of sonicated lipid-containing oil solution in 0.6 milliliter lidded plastic tube
22. Tap tube to emulsify components
23. Use pipette to add 50 microliters of water and oil droplet solution to oil-aqueous solution interface
24. Sediment bacterial cell-containing giant vesicles by centrifugation
25. Aspirate top oil layer
26. Collect giant vesicles
27. Drill a seven-millimeter hole with hollow punch on 10x10x1 mm double-faced seal
28. Paste double-faced seal onto 30x40 mm cover glass
29. Add 30 microliters of small vesicle solution to chamber hole
30. Incubate vesicles at room temperature for 30 minutes
31. Gently wash chamber hole twice with 20 microliters LB medium supplemented with 200 mM glucose
32. Introduce 10 microliters of neutravidin in outer aqueous giant vesicle solution to the hole
33. Incubate for 15 minutes at room temperature
34. Gently wash the hole two times with 20 microliters of LB medium supplemented with 200 mM glucose
35. Add entire volume of giant vesicle solution to the hole in the chamber
36. Seal the hole with 18 × 18 millimeter cover glass
37. Select 40X objective on inverted microscope
38. Place chamber on microscope heating stage system
39. Incubate bacterial cell-containing giant vesicles in chamber under static conditions at 37°C for six hours
40. Capture and record images of bacterial cell growth every 30 minutes using scientific complementary metal oxide semiconductor camera
41. Aspirate oil carefully before giant vesicle correction

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Position anesthetized mouse in stereotactic platform with incisors hooked in bite bar
2. Tighten nose clamp while maintaining mouse head on level plane
3. Adjust ear bar height to reach caudal portion of ear canal for head immobilization
4. Disinfect head with alternating wipes of topical antiseptic and 70% ethanol
5. Make 0.75 centimeter horizontal incision in mid scalp using sterile scalpel
6. Drill two symmetrical holes using 0.45 millimeter burr: positioned above right/left cortical hemispheres, 2 mm from sagittal suture and lambdoid suture at middle of parietal bone
7. Attach 10 microliter syringe to stereotactic platform
8. Load 10 microliters of freshly prepared chemical inhibitor into syringe
9. Advance needle perpendicularly into first burr hole
10. Zero coordinates on stereotactic digital display after needle traverses skull
11. Advance needle tip to 2.5 millimeters depth
12. Withdraw needle 0.5 millimeters to 2 millimeters depth
13. Slowly inject entire 10 microliter volume over one minute
14. Leave needle in brain for one minute after injection completion
15. Repeat injection procedure in second burr hole using vehicle only
16. Close skin over incision
17. Transfer mouse from stereotactic apparatus to 37°C heating pad
18. Monitor mouse until full recovery
19. Secure mouse on dissecting pad
20. Make lateral incision through integument and abdominal wall beneath ribcage using scissors and forceps
21. Separate liver from diaphragm
22. Cut diaphragm along entire ribcage length to expose pleural cavity
23. Make incision to posterior end of left ventricle using scissors
24. Perfuse right heart chamber with 15 ml PBS over 2 minutes
25. Perfuse heart with 10 ml of 4% paraformaldehyde in PBS over 2 minutes
26. Make midline incision in scalp using scissors
27. Place scissors tip into foramen magnum to create lateral skull incision toward eye
28. Make similar lateral incision on opposite skull side keeping scissors superficial
29. Cut skull region between eyes and above nose
30. Use forceps to gently peel cranial bones from brain hemispheres
31. Use spatula to elevate brain
32. Dissect cranial nerve fibers fixing brain to skull
33. Place brain into plastic dish
34. Excise cerebellum and olfactory bulbs
35. Fix brain in 15 mL tube with 4% paraformaldehyde in PBS at 4°C overnight
36. Rinse brain tissue three times for 5 minutes each at 4°C in fresh PBS
37. Remove front of each hemisphere with scissors and forceps
38. Place samples into labeled cryo-molds front side down
39. Submerge each tissue in optimal cutting temperature medium
40. Snap freeze samples in liquid nitrogen in 10 mm plastic Petri dish for -80°C storage
41. Immerse 5-6 micrometer brain tissue sections in antigen retrieval solution
42. Incubate slides in antigen retrieval solution for 5 minutes on 100°C heat block
43. Wash slides with four 5-minute washes using fresh PBS at room temperature
44. Immerse slides in blocking solution
45. Wash slides three times for 5 minutes each in fresh wash buffer at room temperature
46. Stain tissue samples with appropriate primary antibodies
47. Incubate with primary antibodies at 4°C overnight
48. Image slides on fluorescence microscope
49. Adjust microscope contrast and brightness for GFP-positive cell quantification

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Set up LabWare in fume hood
2. Connect round bottom flask to condenser
3. Immerse flask in silicone oil bath on hot plate
4. Combine reagents in metal flask (iron acetylate, oleic acid, oleylamine, 1,2-hexadecanediol, phenyl ether)
5. Stir mixture vigorously
6. Heat mixture to 250-260°C
7. Maintain temperature at 250-260°C for one hour under reflux
8. Cool reaction to room temperature
9. Add gold acetate, oleic acid, oleylamine, 1,2-hexadecanediol, and phenyl ether to new round bottom flask
10. Add 5 mL magnetic nanoparticle suspension to flask
11. Heat reaction mixture to 180°C under reflux conditions
12. Maintain reaction at 180°C under reflux for 1 hour
13. Cool solution to room temperature
14. Initiate precipitation of hybrid primary nanoparticles
15. Add 50 milliliters of ethanol to nanoparticle suspension
16. Transfer suspension to centrifugation tube
17. Centrifuge suspension
18. Resuspend pellet in 25 milliliters of hexane using sonication
19. Add 25 milliliters of ethanol to resuspended mixture
20. Centrifuge mixture again
21. Repeat washing process (resuspension in hexane/sonication + ethanol addition + centrifugation) three more times
22. Dry pellet of primary hybrid nanoparticles in vacuum desiccator overnight until completely dry
23. Resuspend dry primary nanoparticles in 1 milliliter of hexane
24. Sonicate suspension until no visible precipitate is present
25. Add nanoparticle suspension dropwise to 10 milliliters of sodium esal sulfate solution in 20 milliliter glass-capped vial
26. Cap vial and shake by hand
27. Sonicate two-phase solution for two hours
28. Position vial near center of sonication bath
29. Heat mixture in 80°C water bath for 10 minutes
30. Let mixture cool to room temperature
31. Centrifuge suspension at 100 G for 30 minutes
32. Centrifuge suspension at 100 G for 30 minutes
33. Save supernatant
34. Resuspend pellet in 0.1 millimolar sodium citrate using 10 minutes of sonication
35. Centrifuge saved supernatant at 400 G for 30 minutes
36. Save supernatant
37. Resuspend pellet in 0.1 millimolar sodium citrate using 10 minutes of sonication
38. Centrifuge saved supernatant at 1500 G for 30 minutes
39. Centrifuge saved supernatant at 1500 G for 30 minutes
40. Resuspend pellet for 90 nm nano clusters
41. Transfer 300 microliters of each suspension to individual wells in 96-well plate
42. Read UV-Vis-NIR absorption spectra of samples in 96-well plate
43. Transfer 10 microliters of suspension to carbon-coated copper TEM grids
44. Prepare TEM grids for imaging
45. Transfer 100 microliters of antibodies to a 10K molecular weight cutoff centrifuge filter
46. Add 3.9 milliliters of 4 millimolar HEPES pH 7.2 to the filter
47. Centrifuge at 3,250 G at 8°C for 20 minutes
48. Add 10 microliters of 100 millimolar sodium periodate to oxidize antibodies
49. Cover vial with foil
50. Incubate on room-temperature orbital shaker for 30 minutes
51. Add 0.5 milliliters to quench oxidation reaction
52. Quench oxidation reaction by adding 0.5 mL of PBS
53. Add 2 microliters of linker solution to oxidized antibodies
54. Shake reaction for 1 hour at room temperature
55. Filter reaction through 10K MWCO filter at 3,250g for 20 minutes at 8°C
56. Transfer antibody to new vial
57. Transfer antibody to new vial
58. Adjust volume to 100 microliters using 1X PBS
59. Collect 100 microliters of nano cluster suspension (OD ~1.0)
60. Add 1 microliter of modified antibodies to nano cluster suspension
61. Incubate mixture at room temperature for 2 hours with mild shaking
62. Add 10 microliters of reagent after incubation
63. Add 10 microliters of 1 millimolar 5 kilodalton thiol-PEG
64. Incubate reaction for 15 minutes at room temperature with shaking
65. Pellet antibody-conjugated nano clusters at 830 G for 3 minutes
66. Resuspend pellet in 100 microliters of 2% weight/volume 5 kilodalton PEG in PBS at pH 7.2
67. Measure absorbance of suspension compared to bare nano clusters
68. Increase thiol PEG concentration to 5 mM
69. Increase incubation time to 30 minutes
70. Decrease centrifugal speed incrementally by 200 G until nanoparticle aggregation ceases

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Seed adipose-derived stromal cells (ASC) at approximately 80% confluency in conventional tissue culture plates
2. Seed ASC at approximately 80% confluency in PNIPAAm coated tissue culture plates of corresponding size
3. Maintain ASC in complete culture medium at 37°C and 5% CO₂
4. Change culture medium every two days
5. Wrap rim of plunger disk with lab tape at least two times to prevent leakage
6. Spray down 15 ml conical tube rack containing empty uncapped tubes with 70% ethanol in biosafety cabinet
7. Spray each tape-wrapped plunger with 70% ethanol and place into empty conical tube
8. Spray approximately 6.3 gram metal washers and place on rack with hooked forceps
9. Close cabinet sash and turn on UV light for overnight drying/sterilization
10. Add 0.75 grams of gelatin powder to 10 milliliters of HBSS
11. Balance solution pH with 100 microliters of sodium hydroxide
12. Place gelatin solution in 75-degree Celsius water bath
13. Shake tube vigorously every five minutes until powder dissolves
14. Strain homogeneous gelatin solution through syringe filter
15. Apply filtered gelatin solution to plungers
16. Allow gelatin to solidify in plunger mold
17. Unwrap tape from solidified gelatin mold
18. Remove gelatin from outer edge of plunger using hooked forceps
19. Ensure remaining central gelatin is completely level
20. Gently apply gelatin-removed plungers to PNIPAAm coated ASC plates
21. Use metal washers to weigh down plungers
22. Transfer plates to ice-water bath
23. Clean bottom of PNIPAAm coated plates to remove non-sterile water
24. Add cold cell culture medium to one 1.5 milliliter microcentrifuge tube per culture to be seeded
25. Wash large segments of adipose tissue three times in sterile PBS
26. Remove as much PBS as possible between washes
27. Coarsely mince tissue using forceps and sterile razor
28. Remove vasculature and fascia during mincing
29. Continue mincing until solution achieves thick liquid consistency with minimal visible tissue segments
30. Transfer minced tissue to prepared 1.5 ml tubes using modified P1000 micro-pipette tip
31. Briefly mix minced tissue with medium within tubes
32. Replace medium in each ASC culture with one volume of white adipose tissue solution
33. Transfer gelatin plungers to white adipose tissue mixture on base ASC plates
34. Examine monolayers in PNIPAAm coated plates under microscope to confirm cellular detachment
35. Move plates to 37-40°C heat block surface in biosafety cabinet
36. Add 2-3 milliliters of warmed culture medium to incubate cells and facilitate gelatin melting
37. Gently remove plungers from plate surface
38. Cover cultures with appropriate plate lids
39. Place plates in cell culture incubator
40. Aspirate and replace cell culture medium with phenol red-free M199 medium supplemented with insulin, dexamethasone, and antibiotics

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Harvest subconfluent Panc02-H7 cells from T225 flask using 4 ml of 0.25% Trypsin
2. Incubate cells with trypsin for 5 minutes at 37°C
3. Collect cells in 15-ml centrifuge tube
4. Centrifuge at 900 × g for 5 minutes at 4°C
5. Wash cells by re-suspending pellet in 1 ml PBS via pipetting up/down ≥2 times
6. Transfer suspension to 1.5-ml microcentrifuge tube on ice
7. Centrifuge at 900 × g for 5 minutes at 4°C
8. Discard supernatant without disturbing pellet
9. Maintain pelleted cells on ice until injection
10. Remove fur along femur on dorsal side of 8-week-old C57BL/6J mice using surgical clipper or chemical hair removal agent
11. Disinfect surgical stage
12. Anesthetize mice using 2% isoflurane in induction chamber
13. Apply vet ointment to eyes to prevent dryness
14. Inject weight-appropriate amount of analgesic subcutaneously
15. Transfer mouse to nosecone in surgical area
16. Provide heat support throughout surgical procedure
17. Place anesthetized mouse on ventral side
18. Secure each limb with hypoallergenic tape to create mild tension
19. Clean injection site with Betadine swab
20. Clean injection site with 70% alcohol swab
21. Repeat Betadine and alcohol cleaning cycle two more times while ensuring no loose hair remains
22. Apply Betadine solution to surgical area
23. Don sterile gloves
24. Place sterile drape
25. Apply toe pinch using dedicated sterile tool
26. Make 1-cm incision with sterile small scissors 2 mm below and parallel to femur
27. Retract skin laterally with forceps to expose underlying muscles
28. Separate gluteus maximus and biceps femoris along fascial plane to expose sciatic nerve
29. Free sciatic nerve from surrounding muscles using blunt dissection
30. Draw 3 microliters of cancer cells from pellet into sterile 10-microliter syringe
31. Place small metal spatula underneath nerve at injection point for support
32. Position needle parallel to nerve under dissecting microscope visualization
33. Insert needle into nerve while avoiding puncture through back of nerve
34. Slowly inject cells into nerve over 5 seconds
35. Leave needle in place for 3 seconds to minimize backflow
36. Remove needle gently
37. Return the nerve to its original position
38. Cover the nerve with overlying muscles
39. Treat the mice with proper analgesia
40. Close the skin with 5-0 nylon sutures
41. Place the mouse alone in a clean cage for observation
42. Provide heat support during recovery until the mouse regains sufficient consciousness to maintain sternal recumbency
43. Place euthanized mouse on ventral side
44. Stabilize distal limbs using pins
45. Remove skin on dorsal side of injected limb and torso
46. Use blunt dissection to expose sciatic nerve deep to muscles
47. Insert closed scissors into narrow area where sciatic nerve is located
48. Open scissors while holding mouse
49. Dissect sciatic nerve distally to femur end and proximally to spinal cord
50. Cut distal end of sciatic nerve
51. Lift nerve while freeing it from adjacent tissue
52. Cut proximal end of nerve near spinal column exit
53. Measure gross invasion length using Vernier caliper
54. Embed dissected nerve in O.C.T. compound
55. Position nerves longitudinally and flat on mold bottom
56. Mark proximal (P) and distal (D) sides on cassette
57. Place embedded nerves on dry ice
58. Section samples at 5-micron thickness using cryostat microtome
59. Place sections on glass slides (preferably two sections per slide)
60. Indicate proximal side of nerve on slides

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Clean 50 ml tungsten carbide milling jar and 10×10 mm tungsten carbide balls, then dry in 100°C oven for 1 hour
2. Wipe clean inside of cooled milling jar to remove residue
3. Purge glove box antechamber with argon 3 times (10 minutes per purge)
4. Transfer milling jar, balls, and clean spatula into argon-filled glove box
5. Weigh vanadium and boron powders in 1:2 molar ratio into milling jar
6. Add tungsten carbide balls to milling jar and seal jar
7. Remove sealed milling jar from glove box and place in planetary ball mill
8. Set ball mill to 600 RPM and mill for 4 hours
9. Allow milling jar to cool to room temperature in ball mill before removal
10. Purge glove box anti-chamber with argon three times for 10 minutes each
11. Transfer milling jar, round bottom flask, paraffin film, spatula, and magnetic stir bar into glove box
12. Scrape walls of milling jar with spatula to recover bulk starting mass into whey boat
13. Weigh collected nano vanadium diboride
14. Weigh collected nano vanadium diboride
15. Transfer weighed vanadium diboride to round bottom flask
16. Weigh 3.5% by weight zirconium chloride relative to vanadium diboride
17. Add zirconium chloride powder to flask
18. Place magnetic stir bar in flask and seal with paraffin film
19. Remove sealed flask from glove box
20. Transfer 10 milliliters of diethyl ether into flask using 10mL syringe
21. Cover syringe hole with additional paraffin film
22. Place flask on stir plate and mix for one hour at medium setting
23. Evaporate remaining diethyl ether using rotary evaporator until nano vanadium diboride appears dry
24. Obtain freshly prepared 8M KOH/NaOH electrolyte (4M KOH + 4M NaOH)
25. Use diagonal cutting pliers to create a cut in the lip of zinc air coin cell casing
26. Crimp the outside edges of the lip outward
27. Repeat crimping process completely around the cell twice
28. Use razor blade to slowly push up on edges of the cap
29. Gently force open the cell while avoiding damage or cracks
30. Use razor blade to gently remove zinc anode material from cap and separator parts
31. Use cotton swab to carefully wipe remaining zinc residue from cap bottom and separator
32. Clean cap and outside of cell with isopropyl alcohol
33. Weigh 1.2 milligrams of zirconium-coated vanadium diboride per electrode
34. Transfer weighed zirconium-coated vanadium diboride to mortar and pestle
35. Add IC carbon black equal to 30% of total anodic material mass to mortar and pestle
36. Grind mixture in mortar and pestle for 30 minutes
37. Verify absence of large visible material clusters after grinding
38. Transfer approximately 1.7 milligrams of 70:30 powder mixture to each clean electrode cap using a spatula
39. Add a single drop of isopropyl alcohol to each cap
40. Swirl powder with small pointed tip until no clumps remain and suspension is evenly distributed across cap surface
41. Allow electrodes to dry for 30 minutes
42. Inspect each cap to ensure anode material is evenly spread and not cracked
43. Add 27 microliters of electrolyte mixture to each bottom
44. Use cotton swab to dab once in bottoms to remove excess electrolyte
45. Turn over each bottom and place on cap to contact anode material with electrolyte
46. Apply pressure and seal assembly using fast-drying epoxy
47. Place cells on discharge rack and rest for 10 minutes for equilibration
48. Measure open circuit potential
49. Measure cell discharge at constant load of 3000 ohms using battery tester
50. Measure discharge of cell at constant load of 3000 ohms using battery tester

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Weigh and mince hind limb skeletal muscle from 20-week-old C57BL/6 male mice in ice-cold PBS
2. Place minced skeletal muscle tissue in 50 ml conical tube containing 10 ml PBS on ice
3. Centrifuge sample at 300 × g at 4°C for 5 minutes
4. Remove PBS supernatant by aspiration
5. Estimate pellet volume using reference tubes with 1-4 ml water
6. Add 7 volumes ice-cold 1% formaldehyde in PBS to pellet
7. Homogenize samples on ice using probe tissue homogenizer
8. Cross-link sample by incubating at room temperature for 10 minutes
9. Quench cross-linking reaction by adding 1M glycine to 0.125M final concentration
10. Incubate quenched sample for 5 minutes at room temperature
11. Centrifuge sample at 3,000 × g for 5 minutes at 4°C
12. Remove supernatant via aspiration
13. Rinse pellet by resuspending in 10 ml ice-cold base buffer with freshly added protease inhibitors
14. Centrifuge the sample
15. Remove supernatant by aspiration
16. Resuspend pellet in 6 ml lysis buffer with freshly added protease inhibitors
17. Transfer sample to pre-chilled 15 ml Dounce homogenizer
18. Incubate sample for 10 minutes on ice
19. Homogenize sample on ice with 15 slow strokes using loose pestle
20. Homogenize sample with 15 strokes using tight pestle to release nuclei
21. Filter homogenate through cell strainer
22. Rinse with 4 ml lysis buffer
23. Centrifuge samples at 1,000 × g for 10 minutes at 4°C
24. Resuspend pellet in 5 ml ice-cold base buffer
25. Dounce homogenize 10 times with tight pestle to release nuclei
26. Filter suspension through cell strainer
27. Rinse tube with 2 ml base buffer
28. Filter suspension again
29. Repeat filtration with gradually reduced pore size cell strainers
30. Centrifuge at 1,000 × g at 4°C for 10 minutes
31. Remove supernatant
32. Resuspend pellet in 500 μl base buffer
33. Centrifuge again (implied same parameters)
34. Discard supernatant
35. Store pellet at -80°C
36. Thaw samples in 500 microliters of SDS lysis buffer
37. Resuspend samples with gentle pipetting
38. Transfer nuclei DNA suspension to glass vial on ice
39. Run sonication with focused bursts using settings from text protocol
40. Transfer sonicated chromatin to 1.5 milliliter tube
41. Centrifuge at 12,000 × g for 15 minutes
42. Transfer supernatant to new tube
43. Transfer 50 microliters of sonicated sample to new 1.5 milliliter tube
44. Perform reverse cross-linking overnight at 65 degrees Celsius
45. Aliquot thawed chromatin to 100-120 micrograms per tube
46. Dilute chromatin 1:10 with immunoprecipitation buffer
47. Add 40 microliters of 50% slurry of BSA pre-blocked protein HE agarose or IgY beads
48. Incubate sample on rotator for 3 hours at 4°C
49. Centrifuge sample at 1,000 × g for 10 minutes
50. Transfer supernatant carefully to new tubes
51. Add antibodies at concentration of 1 μg antibody per 25-100 μg chromatin DNA
52. Rotate gently overnight at 4°C and approximately 15 rpm
53. Add 10 microliters of BSA pre-blocked protein HE agarose or IgY beads and mix
54. Rotate bead suspension in cold room for two hours
55. Wash beads sequentially with RIPA buffer, high salt buffer, lithium chloride buffer, and Tris EDTA buffer
56. Centrifuge beads at 1,000 × g for 1 minute
57. Remove supernatant carefully
58. Resuspend beads in 50 microliters of elution buffer
59. Incubate resuspended beads at 65°C for 10 minutes
60. Centrifuge sample at 12,000 × g for 5 minutes
61. Remove supernatant
62. Add 50 microliters of elution buffer
63. Centrifuge sample at 12,000 × g for 5 minutes
64. Incubate eluted chromatin at 65°C overnight to reverse cross-links
65. Add 1 microliter of RNase A
66. Incubate for 30 minutes at 37°C
67. Incubate with 8 microliters of 10 mg/ml protease K for 30 minutes at 55°C
68. Harvest DNA using PCR cleanup kit with 50 microliters elution volume according to manufacturer's protocol

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Drill one 4 mm diameter hole 20 mm from each end of 1200 × 30 × 4.5 mm acrylic diffuser bar
2. Drill and tap threaded holes 20 mm from each end of aluminum u-bar section
3. Drill and tap threaded holes into base of aluminum u-bar to suit mounting hardware
4. Straighten one 1.25m length of 1.25mm bare copper wire by securing one end to vise
5. Secure opposite end of wire within grips of hand drill
6. Turn on hand drill at low speed
7. Repeat wire straightening process for second wire
8. Use fine tip permanent marker to mark photodiode positions along diffuser edge
9. Mark first photodiode position at 13.5cm from one diffuser end
10. Mark subsequent photodiode positions every 2cm toward far diffuser end
11. Center photodiode on diffuser bar with electrical tabs pointing sideways
12. Place wire under one tab to mark first copper wire position
13. Mark wire position at center and opposite end of diffuser bar
14. Secure first straightened copper wire to diffuser along marks using cyanoacrylate glue
15. Glue 50 photodiodes facedown at 20mm intervals ensuring center alignment and uniform orientation (large tab on wire, small tab opposite)
16. Place second copper wire under smaller tabs of photodiodes
17. Secure wire to diffuser with cyanoacrylate glue
18. Apply solder flux to both tabs of one photodiode, adjacent wires, and underlying wires
19. Solder each diode tab to underlying copper wires using fine-tipped soldering iron at 350-400°C
20. Shine light onto each photodiode to test solder connections
21. Check voltage signal for each diode across wires using multimeter
22. Solder 1.5 ohm low-temperature coefficient precision resistor in parallel across copper wires (optional)
23. Solder male end of waterproof DC connector to ends of copper wires
24. Seal connections using glue-lined heat shrink tubing
25. Apply bead of silicone sealant to surface of diffuser near edge to create continuous barrier around circuitry
26. Inspect silicone bead closely to ensure no air gaps remain between silicone and diffuser bar
27. Fill well with epoxy resin after sealant curing
28. Allow epoxy resin to harden overnight
29. Remove silicone sealant using razor blade
30. Bolt diffuser to pre-threaded aluminum u-bar using M4 bolts
31. Secure diffuser to aluminum along its whole length using masking tape
32. Fill void inside ceptometer with polyurethane foam filler
33. Allow filler to set overnight
34. Remove masking tape
35. Solder female end of direct current connector to two-conductor cable
36. Seal connections with glue-lined heat shrink
37. Connect PARbar and quantum sensor to data logger
38. Place both sensors outside in full sun on level plane
39. Record sensor outputs during period of widely varying solar radiation
40. Calculate PARbar calibration factor as slope of linear regression (quantum sensor PAR vs. raw voltage output)
41. Install one PARbar above canopy ensuring no shading by light-absorbing elements
42. Install another PARbar below all light-absorbing elements
43. Align both PARbars at 45° angle to planting rows
44. Confirm upper PARbar does not shade lower PARbar
45. Level both PARbars using spirit level
46. Connect PARbars to data logger
47. Convert differential voltage output to PAR using previously determined calibration factors

25. Dry patterned wafer under stream of nitrogen gas or compressed air

27. Place patterned wafer in pressurized open-ended tube furnace

30. Set gas flow rate to furnace tube volume per minute for remainder of sequence
31. Program temperature ramp to 300°C at 5°C per minute

33. Program temperature ramp to 900°C at 5°C per minute
34. Program hold at 900°C for 1 hour
35. Program cooling to 300°C at 10°C per minute

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Homogenize 100 grams of fresh radish using kitchen blender for 10 minutes
2. Transfer resulting slurry to 50 milliliter centrifuge tube
3. Centrifuge at 14,000 × g at 4°C for 20 minutes to remove insoluble material
4. Transfer supernatant to new 50 milliliter centrifuge tube
5. Add equal volume of 2M trichloroacetic acid solution
6. Centrifuge at 14,000 × g at 4°C for 30 minutes
7. Remove supernatant
8. Wash pellet with 20mL deionized water
9. Centrifuge at 14,000 × g at 4°C for 5 minutes
10. Resuspend pellet in 1mL deionized water
11. Transfer protein solution to fresh 1.5mL centrifuge tube
12. Mix 50 microliters of protein solution with 0.2 milliunits of recombinant PNGase H+ in ten millimolar acetic acid
13. Incubate mixture at 37 degrees Celsius for 12 hours
14. Centrifuge mixture at 14,000 × g for five minutes to remove residual protein and enzyme
15. Transfer supernatant to fresh 1.5 milliliter centrifuge tube
16. Wash solid phase extraction column with 3 mL water
17. Activate column with 3 mL of 80% acetonitrile containing trifluoroacetic acid
18. Equilibrate column with 3 mL deionized water
19. Transfer sample onto column
20. Discard flow-through
21. Wash column with 1.5 mL deionized water
22. Add 1.5 milliliters of aqueous 20% acetonitrile solution containing 0.1% TFA to elute N-glycans into a two milliliter tube
23. Remove solvent by centrifugal evaporation at room temperature
24. Evaporate sample until completely dry
25. Add 5 microliters of prepared 2-AB solution to dried samples
26. Vortex samples until completely dissolved
27. Incubate samples at 65 degrees Celsius for 2 hours
28. Cool samples to room temperature for 5 minutes
29. Add 5 microliters of deionized water and 40 microliters of acetonitrile to cooled samples
30. Centrifuge samples at 14,000 × g for 3 minutes
31. Transfer 48 microliters of supernatant to 300 microliter HPLC vial
32. Store samples at -20 degrees Celsius
33. Connect UPLC glycan column to online fluorescence detector
34. Set fluorescence detector excitation wavelength to 330 nm and emission wavelength to 420 nm
35. Set column temperature to 60 degrees Celsius
36. Inject 45 microliters of sample into UPLC system
37. Elute N-glycans by UPLC using 15-40 minute retention window
38. Collect each UPLC peak fraction into 2-milliliter centrifuge tubes
39. Dry samples using centrifugal evaporation
40. Inject 1 picomole of 2-AB labeled dextran standard for calibration, elution time assignment, and glucose unit standardization
41. Dissolve dried samples in 5 microliters of LCMS grade water
42. Mix 1 microliter of dissolved sample with 1 microliter of ATT solution
43. Pipet mixture into MALDI-TOF sample carrier
44. Load sample carrier into MALDI-TOF mass spectrometer for analysis

question:

1. Turn on Hitachi F-7000 Spectra Fluorimeter
2. Allow Xenon lamp to warm up for one hour
3. Create file containing standards and sample data
4. Save file in CSV format
5. Click method button to open analysis method window
6. Select photometry from measurement field pull-down menu
7. Click quantitation tab
8. Select wavelength in quantitation type field
9. Select first-order calibration type
10. Select one wavelength for number of wavelengths
11. Enter nanograms per milliliter for concentration unit

15. Enter 10,000 in upper concentration limit field
16. Click instrument tab
17. Select fluorescence in data mode field
18. Verify chopping speed field is inactive

20. Enter 480 nanometers in ex WL one field
21. Enter 520 nanometers in EM WL one field
22. Set ex slit to 5.0 nanometers
23. Set EM slit to 5.0 nanometers

25. Set PMT voltage to 700 volts
26. Set auto statistic calculation to 2 replicates

28. Set delay to zero
29. Open monitor tab

31. Set Y-axis minimum to zero
32. Select checkbox for open data processing after acquisition

36. Click okay button to close window

38. Select wells A1 to E1 for standards by clicking and dragging
39. Click the standard set button to designate selected wells
40. Select sample wells by clicking and dragging over plate positions
41. Click the sample set button to designate selected wells
42. Add 50 microliters of 200X PicoGreen reagent to 9.95 milliliters of TE buffer
43. Prepare serial dilutions of Lambda double-stranded DNA in TE buffer for concentrations of 1-100 ng/mL
44. Pipette 150 microliters of each DNA standard into designated wells of 96-well plate
45. Pipette 150 microliters of each test sample into designated wells of plate
46. Add 150 microliters of PicoGreen solution to each well using multi-pipette and mix solution with DNA samples
47. Incubate plate gently in dark area for 2-5 minutes for reaction development
48. Click MPR display screen button
49. Click exchange position button
50. Open microplate reader door
51. Place 96-well plate on holding platform with A1 well toward front
52. Close microplate reader door

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Culture cells on six 24-millimeter filters
2. Transfer filters to cold PBS on ice ensuring buffer covers entire apical surface
3. Transfer plate on ice to cold room and set on benchtop
4. Hold filters in place and invert plate to remove buffer
5. Add 2 ml PBS to apical and basolateral sides
6. After 2 minutes, suction wash from one side at a time and add fresh PBS to one side at a time
7. Aspirate last wash, add 1 ml PBS to basolateral side and 1.5 ml biotin buffer to apical side
8. Incubate in dark for 30 minutes
9. Store plate in cold room
10. Keep four filters in one plate
11. Add approximately two milliliters of PBS to apical and basolateral side of each filter
12. Take plate on ice to benchtop next to 37°C incubator
13. Transfer filters to PBS in plate within 37°C incubator
14. Cover entire filter surface with warm PBS
15. Incubate one filter per specific time point at 37°C
16. Completely fill four wells in plate on ice with cold PBS during incubation
17. Turn filter upside down to drain warm PBS after incubation
18. Transfer filter to plate on ice and add cold PBS to overflow apical side
19. Move samples to cold room benchtop
20. Suction off PBS from both sides of all filters
21. Add 1.5 milliliters of PBS (pH 8.6) to apical side of filter A
22. Add 1.5 milliliters of GSH buffer to apical side of remaining filters to reduce disulfide bonds in biotinylated apical membrane proteins
23. Incubate for 15 minutes (repeated six times)
24. Maintain 1 milliliter of PBS (pH 8.6) on basolateral side while avoiding buffer spillage
25. Rinse all filters twice with PBS
26. Place plate flat on benchtop
27. Add 500 microliters of lysis buffer to apical side of each filter
28. Incubate plate on shaker in cold room for 15 minutes
29. Harvest cells by scraping
30. Collect scraped cells in 1.5 milliliter Eppendorf tube
31. Centrifuge samples at 14,000 × g for 10 minutes at 4°C
32. Transfer supernatants of whole cell lysates
33. Remove wash buffer using 27-gauge needle attached to suction
34. Wash resin twice with 1 ml PBS
35. Wash resin once with 1 ml lysis buffer
36. Suction dry agarose
37. Add remaining whole cell lysates
38. Adjust volume to 1.2 ml with lysis buffer
39. Rotate mixture in dark for ≥2 hours or overnight in cold room
40. Load 40 microliters of whole cell lysate samples into wells of 7.5% denaturing gel
41. Load entire volume of biotinylated samples into separate wells of 7.5% denaturing gel
42. Run gel electrophoresis at 120 Volts in SDS-containing running buffer
43. Transfer proteins to PVDF membrane at 90-95 Volts for 1.5 hours in transfer buffer without SDS
44. Block PVDF membrane overnight
45. Block membrane overnight
46. Blot membranes with anti-CFTR mouse monoclonal primary antibody and goat anti-mouse HRP-conjugated secondary antibody
47. Visualize western blots by chemiluminescence
48. Reprobe membranes with either anti-ezrin or anti-actin antibody

3. Include blank tube for PBS-TBN vehicle only control for each antibody
4. Add 75 microliters of PBS-TBN to all antibody dilution tubes labeled 500 micrograms per milliliter and below, including vehicle control tubes
5. Pipette 150 microliters of antibody stock solution into tube labeled 1,000 micrograms per milliliter
6. Transfer 75 microliters from 1,000 micrograms per milliliter tube to subsequent lower dilution tube in series
7. Close newly completed dilution tube and briefly vortex

9. Add 25 microliters of biotinylated recombinant human PDL1 to every reaction well
10. Cover microtiter plate with foil or plastic adhesive seal

13. Remove 96-well microtiter reaction plate from shaker
14. Peel adhesive plate seal
15. Add 50 microliters of recombinant human PD1-coupled bead mixture to each well
16. Seal plate tightly
17. Incubate plate for one hour in dark at room temperature on orbital shaker (600 RPM)
18. Remove adhesive plate seal after incubation
19. Place microtiter plate on magnetic plate separator
20. Immobilize beads for 2 minutes using magnetic separation
21. Confirm secure connection between magnet and microtiter plate
22. Flip microtiter plate over
23. Discard supernatant carefully
24. Remove microtiter plate from magnetic plate separator
25. Add 150 microliters of PBS-TBN buffer to each well
26. Add 100 microliters of SAPE working solution to each reaction well
27. Resuspend beads in wells by pipetting
28. Seal reaction plate

30. Remove adhesive plate sealer after beads are immobilized and magnet-plate assembly is secured
31. Invert plate to discard supernatant
32. Remove microtiter plate from magnetic plate holder
33. Add 150 microliters of PBS-TBN to each well to resuspend beads
34. Place microtiter plate on magnetic plate separator for two minutes to immobilize beads
35. Invert plate and remove supernatant after confirming secure placement
36. Add 100 microliters of diluted Brilliant Violet 421 conjugated anti-human or anti-rabbit immunoglobulin G to respective wells
37. Seal plate
38. Incubate sealed plate for one hour in the dark at room temperature
39. Immobilize beads
40. Remove adhesive plate sealer
41. Confirm magnet and microtiter plate are secured
42. Dump supernatant
43. Add 150 microliters of PBS-TBN to each well to resuspend beads
44. Place microtiter plate on magnetic plate separator
45. Immobilize beads using magnetic separator for 2 minutes
46. Invert plate and dump supernatant
47. Invert plate to remove liquid
48. Dump supernatant from plate
49. Remove microtiter plate from magnetic plate separator
50. Resuspend beads in 100 microliters PBS-TBN per well using pipette
51. Set flow analysis system aspiration volume to 50 microliters
52. Set flow analysis system minimum bead count to 100 beads
53. Set flow analysis system timeout to 40 seconds
54. Set flow analysis system gating range to 7,000-17,000
55. Set flow analysis system operating mode to dual reporter
56. Read plate on dual reporter flow analysis system to determine median fluorescent intensity

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Autoclave 15 grams of agar in 900 to 1000 milliliters of water
2. Mix autoclaved agar with 166 grams of wheat germ diet in laboratory blender
3. Pour blended insect diet into dish
4. Allow diet to cool in dish
5. Transfer cooled diet to aluminum foil, wrap tightly, and store at 4°C
6. Place Manduca sexta eggs in glass filter holder with 90mm filter paper in vacuum flask apparatus
7. Pour 250 milliliters of 0.6% bleach solution onto eggs for 2-3 minutes while stirring occasionally
8. Turn on vacuum to drain bleach solution
9. Wash sterilized eggs 3-4 times with 250 ml sterile distilled water per wash
10. Place fresh 90 mm filter paper into Petri plate lid
11. Transfer eggs to filter paper for drying in flow hood until no longer adhesive
12. Place recently prepared insect diet on secured rubber stoppers in plastic containers
13. Transfer approximately 40 eggs into each container
14. Separate eggs from diet to prevent moisture-induced fungal contamination
15. Maintain eggs at 26°C with 16-hour light/8-hour dark photoperiod
16. Pick up each larva by the long black horn using forceps
17. Transfer approximately 25 larvae to new containers with diet at the bottom
18. Incubate larvae for two days under same conditions as eggs
19. Transfer larvae to individual containers with small pieces of food
20. Incubate larvae again
21. Replace food and clean feces from containers every other day
22. Centrifuge 500 microliters of bacterial strains at 17,000 G for 2 minutes at room temperature
23. Wash pellets in 1 milliliter of sterile PBS under same centrifugation conditions
24. Resuspend cells in 0.5 milliliter of sterile PBS
25. Measure optical density of suspensions
26. Prepare six serial tenfold dilutions of bacterial strain in sterile PBS using fresh pipette tips in 96-well microtiter plate
27. Spot 10 microliters of dilutions (10⁻² to 10⁻⁶) along top of Petri plate containing growth medium
28. Tilt plate to spread spots toward center
29. Place insect containers on ice for ~5 minutes after removing diet and feces
30. Rinse syringe needle three times in each of two tubes of 70-100% ethanol followed by one tube of sterile water
31. Quantitate diluted cell suspensions using hemocytometer under microscope
32. Draw 10 microliters from appropriate microtiter well
33. Swab insect injection surface with 95% ethanol
34. Inject 10 microliters of cell suspension into insect at <45° angle behind abdominal proleg
35. Plate 10⁻² to 10⁻⁶ dilutions on middle of agar plate
36. Let samples flow toward bottom of plate
37. Incubate plates at appropriate temperature
38. Count colonies to enumerate inoculum
39. Inject three to five insects with sterile PBS for negative control group
40. Incubate all insects at 26°C with 16-hour light/8-hour dark photoperiod
41. Monitor insect survival over time after injections
42. Inject insects with approximately 50 CFU of wild type or attenuated mutant strain of Xer Haus NLA grown to mid-log phase
43. Observe injected insects for approximately 72 hours
44. Record percentage of injected insects alive at each time point

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Cut a five millimeter section of tubing
2. Incise at midpoint and remove anterior half of tubing
3. Crush flat portion of tubing using hemostat
4. Melt end of angiocatheter using lighter to create lip
5. Cut injection port from 27 gauge angiocatheter
6. Connect catheter to 10 cm section of 27 gauge cannula tubing
7. Position anesthetized rat with nose in anesthesia nose cone and extremities immobilized
8. Attach vital signs monitor to left hind extremity
9. Perform toe pinch to confirm depth of anesthesia
10. Spray abdomen with 70% isopropyl alcohol
11. Allow abdomen to dry
12. Place sterile drape over animal
13. Make midline incision from xiphoid to pubis using sharp scissors
14. Enter peritoneum and incise muscle
15. Extend incision laterally to left and right to form cross at inferior liver border
16. Turn isoflurane anesthesia down to 2%
17. Retract xiphoid process using curved mosquito clamp
18. Retract ribs using rib retractors
19. Cut falciform, phrenic, and gastrohepatic ligaments using sharp scissors
20. Tie off phrenic vein with 7-0 suture near its origin
21. Eviscerate rat using sterile moistened cotton-tip applicator
22. Wrap bowel in gauze moistened with 0.9% normal saline
23. Dissect over inferior vena cava to remove excess tissue
24. Dissect behind IVC superior to bifurcation
25. Pass loop of 4-0 silk suture behind IVC for later use
26. Retract right kidney to expose right adrenal vein
27. Retract right liver lobe superiorly with gauze
28. Tie off right adrenal vein with 7-0 silk suture near IVC
29. Cauterize across right adrenal vein distal to suture tie
30. Dissect out the splenic vein
31. Tie off splenic vein with two 7-0 silk sutures
32. Cut across splenic vein between sutures
33. Dissect around gastroduodenal artery
34. Tie off gastroduodenal artery with 7-0 silk suture
35. Ligate gastroduodenal artery
36. Dissect around hepatic artery
37. Loosely place 7-0 silk suture tie around hepatic artery
38. Dissect out the bile duct
39. Check length of bile duct
40. Tie off bile duct at distal end
41. Place suture loop around bile duct proximally
42. Cut hole using small scissors (half diameter of duct)
43. Place 27-gauge catheter into bile duct
44. Advance catheter proximally in bile duct
45. Secure catheter using roman sandal tie suture
46. Inject 0.5 milliliters of heparin into IVC using 27-gauge needle
47. Clamp and tie off IVC using pre-placed 4-0 silk suture
48. Tie off hepatic artery using pre-placed 7-0 silk suture
49. Clamp portal vein using microsurgical clip
50. Cannulate portal vein using 22-gauge angiocatheter
51. Flush with 60 milliliters of cold 0.9% normal saline containing 100 units heparin until liver blanches
52. Expose the suprahepatic IVC
53. Cut across the suprahepatic IVC as high in the chest as possible
54. Perform hepatectomy by cutting around the diaphragm, hepatic artery, IVC, portal vein, and additional ligaments
55. Lift out the liver and place in ice-cold 0.9% normal saline
56. Place 16-gauge vascular cuff in the portal vein and connect liver to ex vivo normothermic liver perfusion circuit
57. Flow perfusate at 2 milliliters per minute
58. Monitor portal vein pressure for spikes indicating occlusion
59. Insert portal vein cannula into cuffed portal vein for perfusate return flow
60. Suture cannula using 7-0 silk suture
61. Increase perfusate flow by 1 milliliter per minute until physiological pressure of 10-16 cm H₂O is reached
62. Remove 1 milliliter samples from pre-port and post-port during perfusion
63. Divide each 1 milliliter sample into two 0.5 milliliter aliquots
64. Snap freeze one 0.5 milliliter aliquot in cryogenic tubes using liquid nitrogen
65. Perform arterial blood gas analysis using remaining 0.5 milliliter perfusate
66. Measure pH and buffer perfusate as needed to maintain pH 7.4
67. Disconnect liver from perfusion circuit after 4 hours
68. Divide liver into 0.5 gram segments
69. Transfer segments to cryogenic tubes and snap freeze in liquid nitrogen

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Cut small hole in 1 kg package of wet Isochrysis paste
2. Squeeze approximately 300 grams of paste onto 150 × 75 mm crystallizing dish
3. Air dry paste to form 20 mm thick layer
4. Scrape dried biomass into cellulose extraction thimble of known mass
5. Record weight of Isochrysis biomass
6. Transfer 50-60 grams of dry Isochrysis into Soxhlet extraction apparatus
7. Fill Soxhlet flask with 400 milliliters of hexanes
8. Turn on condenser water and heat source
9. Allow Soxhlet apparatus to cycle for 1-2 days
10. Cool apparatus to room temperature
11. Remove hexanes using rotary evaporator
12. Weigh hexane algal oil
13. Dissolve hexane algal oil in flask with 10 volumes of 2:1 methanol-dichloromethane mixture
14. Add stir bar and 2.67 volumes of water relative to oil volume
15. Add potassium hydroxide catalyst (mass = 0.5 × oil weight)
16. Attach flask to 500 mm reflux condenser
17. Heat mixture with stirring to 60°C for 3 hours in fume hood
18. Remove organic solvents using rotary evaporator after cooling
19. Pour remaining aqueous mixture into 1L separatory funnel
20. Pour remaining aqueous mixture into 1L separatory funnel
21. Add equal volume of hexanes to separatory funnel
22. Rinse original flask with hexanes
23. Transfer rinse hexanes to separatory funnel
24. Shake separatory funnel
25. Allow layers to separate in funnel
26. Drain lower aqueous layer into flask
27. Pour top organic layer into second flask
28. Repeat hexane extraction until organic layer turns faint yellow-green
29. Combine collected organic extracts
30. Condense combined extracts using rotary evaporator
31. Acidify aqueous phase to pH 2 using 6M hydrochloric acid
32. Extract free fatty acids from acidified aqueous phase using hexanes and separatory funnel
33. Remove hexanes from combined organic extracts using rotary evaporator to obtain purified free fatty acids
34. Dissolve fatty acids in six volumes of methanol chloroform
35. Pour solution into thick-walled high-pressure reaction flask with stir bar
36. Rinse container to transfer residual fatty acids to reaction flask
37. Add concentrated sulfuric acid under fume hood
38. Seal reaction flask
39. Heat mixture to 90°C while stirring for one hour
40. Cool reaction mixture to room temperature
41. Pour mixture into separatory funnel
42. Add two volumes of water to separatory funnel
43. Shake separatory funnel
44. Allow phases to separate in separatory funnel
45. Drain bottom layer into pre-weighed round bottom flask
46. Concentrate solution using rotary evaporator
47. Record mass of resulting biodiesel
48. Scrape neutral lipids from round bottom flask
49. Weigh scraped neutral lipids
50. Transfer weighed neutral lipids to purification flask
51. Dissolve neutral lipids in minimal amount of dichloromethane
52. Pipette dissolved solution onto chromatography column containing 100 grams silica gel
53. Apply light pressure to elute solution through silica with 150 milliliters dichloromethane
54. Add small layer of sand to top of column
55. Add small layer of sand to top of column to protect silica
56. Collect eluent in 250 milliliter round bottom flask
57. Remove dichloromethane using rotary evaporator
58. Remove dichloromethane using rotary evaporator
59. Add initial 100 milliliters of boiling hexanes to solids
60. Continue adding boiling hexanes until solution becomes homogeneous
61. Slowly cool solution to room temperature to promote crystallization
62. Collect crystallized alkenones using filtration apparatus
63. Rinse flask with small volume of ice-cold hexanes

16. Fill electrophoresis tank with cold alkaline electrophoresis buffer until slides are completely submerged
17. Apply voltage at 1 V/cm for 15-30 minutes
18. Submerge slide in cold deionized water in container
19. Wait for 2 minutes
20. Remove deionized water and add fresh deionized water
21. Repeat wash cycle (remove and add fresh deionized water) second time
22. Replace deionized water with cold 70% ethanol
23. Wait for 5 minutes
24. Gently remove slide without tilting
25. Let slide air dry
26. Add 100 microliters of diluted DNA dye to each spot
27. Incubate for 15 minutes at room temperature
28. Configure epifluorescence microscope with FITC filter
29. Capture images of 50-100 comets per sample
30. Discard culture medium from wells
31. Wash cells three times with PBS
32. Add 1 mL of 0.25% Trypsin per well
33. Incubate at 37°C for 5 minutes
34. Check cell detachment under microscope

36. Transfer cell suspension to 15-mL tube
37. Centrifuge at 200 × g for 5 minutes at 4°C
38. Centrifuge sample for 5 minutes at 4°C and 200 × g
39. Discard the medium
40. Add 1 milliliter of CMM
41. Resuspend the cells
42. Count cells and adjust concentration to 2 million cells in 1.6 milliliters of CMM
43. Seed 200 microliters of cell suspension per well in eight-well chamber slide
44. Tap slide gently to distribute cells evenly in wells
45. Discard culture medium from iPS-derived cardiomyocyte wells
46. Wash cells with PBS
47. Treat cells with 1μM doxorubicin at 37°C
48. Aspirate medium
49. Wash cells with PBS
50. Wash the cells three times with PBS
51. Add 200 microliters of freshly-prepared 4% paraformaldehyde to each well
52. Fix the cells for 20 minutes in the dark at room temperature
53. Add 200 microliters of blocking buffer per well
54. Block for 30 minutes in a humidified chamber at room temperature
55. Remove the blocking buffer
56. Remove blocking buffer
57. Add 200 microliters of primary antibody solution
58. Incubate for 45 minutes in dark humidified chamber at room temperature
59. Wash wells three times with PBS
60. Remove PBS
61. Add 200 microliters of secondary antibody mix
62. Wash wells three times with PBS
63. Wash with deionized water
64. Mount slides with antifade
65. Place cover glass
66. Store slides in dark at 4°C

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Set up drill press with fence and stop using short dowel to secure tubular seedling container
2. Drill two vertical holes approximately 1 cm apart in container side
3. Cut plastic between drilled holes to create elongated opening (2 cm wide × 5 cm long)
4. Repeat elongated opening cutting process on opposite container side
5. Cut 40 micrometer pore nylon mesh into 9.5 cm × 8.5 cm pieces (one per container)
6. Fasten nylon mesh externally onto containers using industrial hot glue to cover both openings with fabric overlap
7. Apply hot glue around container openings and along long edges of nylon mesh
8. Roll container onto nylon mesh
9. Add hot glue along overlapping mesh edges
10. Press glued mesh edge onto cardboard to seal
11. Attach top and bottom fabric ends to container with flexible tape to prevent loose edges
12. Cover small holes on conical container ends with tape to prevent root growth
13. Place glass marbles in bottom of containers for drainage
14. Assemble pots according to manuscript specifications
15. Use solid unmodified containers for control treatment
16. Add AM fungus inoculum by uniformly mixing 1.2 cm chopped root pieces with soil
17. Prepare soil mixture by blending desired soil with infertile silica sand or glass beads
18. Position filled containers in previously-constructed drilled foam within pots
19. Mix medium particle size sand with small particle size sand in cement mixer to create nutrient-poor silica sand mixture
20. Fill interstitial space between containers with sand mixture using funnel
21. Transplant nurse plants of desired species into each container to sustain AM fungi
22. Grow plants at 24°C in greenhouse for 2-3 months to establish common mycorrhizal networks
23. Sow experimental plant seeds into containers
24. Remove pretreatment nurse plants by clipping shoots after all containers show germinated seedlings
25. Leave designated containers unmoved for intact CMN treatment
26. Rotate designated containers weekly through one full rotation to sever hyphae
27. Heavily water all pots to reestablish substrate-container contact after rotation
28. Fertilize neighboring plants with 0.5% nitrogen-15 enriched potassium nitrate and ammonium chloride
29. Fertilize target individual with nitrogen-14 fertilizer of equal concentration
30. Re-randomize positions of pots monthly during experiment
31. Clip all aboveground tissue from plants
32. Place individual plants into envelopes labeled with treatment, pot, and position
33. Dry above-ground tissues at 60°C in forced air drying oven to constant weight
34. Measure dry weight of each plant tissue
35. Allow soil to dry for two to three days
36. Gently brush soil from root systems
37. Wash roots on 250 micron pore size sieve
38. Weigh whole air-dried root system
39. Clip root system randomly to obtain fragments
40. Place root fragments in 50% ethanol
41. Weigh remaining root system
42. Store remaining root system in labeled paper envelope
43. Dry stored roots at 60°C for biomass assessment

2. Configure treadmill with mouse-sized lanes and 5-degree inclination
3. Place absorbent pad below treadmill bell to catch feces
4. Allow mouse to acclimate to procedure room in transported home cage with cage mates for 1-2 hours

7. Weigh mouse

9. Place mouse in treadmill with motor turned off

11. Remove sedentary group mice from treadmill
12. Return mice to cage or conduct post-exercise procedures per experimental design

14. Gently tap or lift mouse hindquarters 1-3 times with tongue depressor to encourage running
15. Start laboratory timer once mouse sustains 10 m/min running speed
16. Increase treadmill speed gradually by 1 meter per minute approximately every 2 minutes when mouse is at top of belt
17. Reduce treadmill speed to previous setting
18. Use tongue depressor to gently tap or lift rear of animal to encourage running resumption
19. Adjust treadmill speed while observing mouse behavior to determine maximal sustained running speed
20. Remove and identify mice refusing to run from analysis
21. Identify exhausted mice and stop timer
22. Record total running time at all speeds
23. Return mouse to cage (if required by experimental design)
24. Conduct immediate post-exercise procedures (blood/tissue collection if required)
25. Wipe treadmill belt clean with germicidal towelette after testing each mouse
26. Test next mouse
27. Use fresh tongue depressor for each mouse

1. Warm chemically competent E. coli cells on ice
2. Add 25 microliters of competent cells per construct to chilled 1.5 milliliter microtube
3. Add 1 microliter of 100 ng/μL solution of each co-expression construct
4. Incubate mixtures on ice for 30 minutes with gentle mixing every 10 minutes by scraping tubes across microtube rack
5. Heat shock cell mixtures at 42°C for 1 minute
6. Place cells back on ice for at least 2 minutes
