question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Wash neonatal foreskin twice with 5 ml wash media in 5 ml conical tube
2. Transfer washed foreskin to sterile Petri dish
3. Scrape off adipose tissue and loose connective tissues from dermal layer using scalpel and forceps
4. Rewash foreskin with wash media
5. Place foreskin epidermis side up in six-well plate containing 2 ml Dispase enzyme diluted in wash media
6. Transfer plate to incubator for 4 hours
7. Transfer incubated foreskin to Petri dish
8. Separate epidermis from dermis layer using fine-tip forceps
9. Place epidermis into 15 ml conical tube containing 2 ml of 0.25% Trypsin-EDTA
10. Crush floating epidermis using 5 ml serological pipette
11. Incubate sample for 15 minutes at 37°C with periodic vortexing (5 seconds every 5 minutes)
12. Add 2 ml soybean trypsin inhibitor
13. Mix by pipetting to neutralize trypsin
14. Centrifuge cell suspension
15. Resuspend pellet in 12 milliliters of complete KSFM-SCM medium
16. Plate cells in 10 centimeter dish
17. Incubate dish overnight at 37 degrees Celsius and 5% carbon dioxide
18. Remove medium using aspirating pipette
19. Replace medium with 12 milliliters of complete KSFM-SCM
20. Repeat medium replacement procedure on days four and seven
21. Add 2.5 grams of agarose to 50 milliliters of PBS in a 100 milliliter glass bottle
22. Autoclave the bottle on liquid cycle
23. Allow agarose solution to cool to room temperature
24. Place cooled agarose bottle in a one liter beaker filled with 200 milliliters of deionized water
25. Melt agarose mixture in research-grade microwave for up to two minutes
26. Mix agarose every 60 seconds by gently tilting bottle side to side
27. Add 3 milliliters of melted 5% agarose to 12 milliliters of prewarmed KSFM-SCM at 42°C to achieve 1% final concentration
28. Dispense 200 microliters of 1% agarose mix into each well of 96-well plate using multi-channel pipetter
29. Aspirate media from spheroid-forming normal human keratinocytes
30. Wash cells with two milliliters of PBS
31. Aspirate PBS from cells
32. Add two milliliters of 0.25% Trypsin-EDTA to washed cells
33. Incubate cells for five minutes at 37 degrees Celsius
34. Add two milliliters of soybean trypsin inhibitor to plate
35. Wash cells from plate into 15 milliliter tube
36. Centrifuge at 250 times G for five minutes
37. Resuspend cell pellet in 1 mL PBS
38. Quantify cells using Trypan Blue staining
39. Aliquot 20,000 normal human keratinocytes in 100 μL KSFM-SCM
40. Seed cells in each well of prepared 96-well plate
41. Incubate plate overnight
42. Analyze seeded wells for epidermis sphere formation using inverted phase contrast microscope
43. Add 4 ml of prewarmed KSFM-SCM at 37°C to six-centimeter dish
44. Transfer single spheroid to plate using wide-bore 1 ml pipette tip
45. Incubate plate overnight
46. Analyze seeded spheroid for attaching/propagating cells using inverted phase contrast microscope
47. Remove media from plate
48. Add 2 ml of fresh KSFM-SCM media to plate
49. Quantify cell viability using Trypan Blue and automated cell counter
50. Aliquot 100 microliters containing 100,000 to 4 million keratinocytes into 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tubes
51. Place tubes on ice in dark environment by turning off laminar hood lights
52. Add 2 μL FITC-conjugated anti-integrin alpha six and 2 μL PE-conjugated anti-EGFR to tubes for 1:50 dilution
53. Prepare tube without antibodies for unstained control
54. Incubate tubes on ice in dark for 30 minutes
55. Perform flow cytometry analysis with appropriate lasers
56. Use negative and positive controls to establish gates
57. Sort epidermal stem cell fraction, proliferative progenitor fraction, and committed progenitor fraction
58. Transfer sorted cells from sorting tubes into 15 ml conical tubes containing 10x volume of PBS
59. Centrifuge tubes at 250 × g for 5 minutes
60. Remove supernatant from tubes
61. Resuspend pellet in 4 mL of KSFM-SCM
62. Transfer resuspended cells to 6 cm plate
63. Incubate plate overnight at 37°C in 5% CO₂ incubator with 95% humidity
64. Remove media from plates
65. Add 4 mL of prewarmed KSFM-SCM (37°C) to plates
66. Repeat media change with 4 mL prewarmed KSFM-SCM every 3 days until 70-80% confluency

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Cut skin from neck to nose using small scissors following sagittal suture (15-20 mm)
2. Insert scissor tips into foramen magnum parallel to skull
3. Cut skull from each side toward eyes
4. Cut between eyes with small scissors to detach skull and brain
5. Grab skull near olfactory bulbs with two forceps and carefully tear
6. Remove cerebellum and olfactory bulb using razor blade
7. Cut brain into two pieces
8. Place brain pieces in Petri dish containing 40 ml HBSS without calcium and magnesium
9. Prepare dissociation mixture according to specified table
10. Transfer 12 brain pieces into C tube (total weight: 1.2 grams per tube)
11. Place C tubes on dissociater with heating
12. Start optimized NTDK program on dissociater
13. Centrifuge samples for 20 seconds
14. Complete mechanical dissociation by pipetting three times
15. Transfer dissociated cells to four 15-mL tubes with attached strainers
16. Rinse strainers with 10 mL HBSS containing calcium and magnesium
17. Centrifuge sample for 10 minutes
18. Remove supernatant using 10-milliliter pipette
19. Add 10 milliliters of HBSS with calcium and magnesium
20. Re-suspend pellet
21. Centrifuge sample
22. Remove supernatant
23. Re-suspend pellet with 6 milliliters of sorting buffer
24. Centrifuge sample
25. Discard supernatant
26. Add 200 microliters of CD11b microbead solution
27. Incubate tubes at 4°C for 15-20 minutes
28. Re-suspend pellet with 6 ml sorting buffer
29. Centrifuge sample
30. Re-suspend pellet with 8 ml sorting buffer
31. Prepare 8 columns using Possel program on separator
32. Pass cells through column by adding 1 ml cell suspension aliquots
33. Elute CD11b-positive cells using 1 ml sorting buffer onto sterile elution plate
34. Pool cells in 50-ml tube
35. Centrifuge collected cells
36. Re-suspend pellet with 10 ml cold microglia medium
37. Count CD11b-positive cells
38. Re-suspend cells in cold microglia medium to achieve 650,000-700,000 cells per milliliter
39. Dispense cell suspension into cell culture plates
40. Incubate plates overnight at 37°C with 5% CO₂
41. Replace medium with preheated microglia medium
42. Incubate overnight
43. Calculate number of beads using table at 50 beads per cell ratio
44. Prepare beads mixture
45. Incubate tube in 37°C water bath for one hour
46. Vortex tube every 10 minutes during incubation
47. Add calculated volume of bead solution to each well
48. Incubate plate for three hours

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Grow Lactobacillus reuteri in malic enzyme induction medium without cystine at 37°C overnight without shaking
2. Centrifuge 1 milliliter of overnight culture in 1.5 milliliter microcentrifuge tube to harvest cells (target yield: 50-100 μg cellular protein)
3. Completely remove supernatant from cell pellet
4. Add 250 microliters GITC lysis buffer to pellet and resuspend by vortexing
5. Incubate at 95°C for 10 minutes to lyse cells
6. Store lysate at -80°C
7. Prepare BSA standards containing 0, 0.1, 0.2, and 0.4 mg/mL BSA in GITC lysis buffer
8. Aliquot 5 microliters of thawed, well-mixed cell lysates to wells in a clear 96-well plate
9. Aliquot 5 microliters of BSA standards to separate wells in the clear 96-well plate
10. Add 195 microliters of Bradford reagent to each well
11. Measure absorbance at 595 nanometers using a plate reader
12. Calculate protein amount in each well by comparison to BSA standard curve
13. Multiply calculated protein value by 0.05 to determine total protein per sample
14. Add 250 microliters of 95% ethanol to each GITC lysed sample
15. Vortex samples to mix
16. Pipette mixture to silica membrane spin column
17. Place spin column in 1.5 milliliter centrifuge tube
18. Centrifuge at 16,100 g for 30 seconds
19. Discard flow-through
20. Add 750 microliters of tris-hydrochloride, sodium chloride, EDTA, and ethanol mixture
21. Centrifuge at 16,100 g for 30 seconds
22. Discard flow-through
23. Centrifuge spin column at 16,100 g for two minutes
24. Add 150 microliters of 50 mM pH 8 tris-HCl
25. Incubate at room temperature for 5 minutes
26. Elute polyP by centrifuging at 8,000 g for 2 minutes

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Aliquot 200 microliters of 2% gelatin solution to each well of a 48-well culture plate
2. Incubate gelatin-coated plate at 37 degrees Celsius for at least 30 minutes
3. Aspirate gelatin solution from wells
4. Seed 5×10⁴ HEK293 cells per well in 200 microliters of cell culture medium containing 10% FBS
5. Incubate plate overnight at 37°C in humidified incubator with 5% CO₂
6. Replace spent culture medium in each well with 200 microliters of fresh culture medium containing 10% FBS
7. Prepare solution A by mixing NR1-GFP plasmid with culture medium
8. Prepare solution B by mixing transfection reagent with culture medium
9. Mix solution A and solution B
10. Incubate mixture (solution C) at room temperature for 20 to 30 minutes
11. Aliquot 40 microliters of solution C to each well containing HEK293 cells
12. Swirl plate gently
13. Incubate plate at 37 degrees Celsius in humidified incubator with 5% carbon dioxide overnight
14. Check NR1-GFP recombinant protein expression in host cells under fluorescent microscope at 40x to 200x magnification
15. Aspirate spent medium from wells of culture plate
16. Wash each well with 200 microliters of PBS (three times)
17. Add 200 microliters of 4% paraformaldehyde solution to each well
18. Incubate plate at room temperature for 15 minutes
19. Aspirate paraformaldehyde solution from wells
20. Wash each well with 200 microliters of PBS (three times)
21. Add 200 microliters of 10% skim milk in PBST solution to each well
22. Incubate plate at room temperature with gentle shaking for one hour
23. Aspirate 10% skim milk in PBST solution from wells
24. Wash wells with 200 microliters of PBST once
25. Incubate wells with 1:100 diluted plasma in PBST (primary antibody) with gentle shaking at room temperature for one hour
26. Aspirate diluted plasma from wells
27. Wash each well with 200 microliters of PBST three times
28. Add 200 microliters of goat anti-human IgG conjugated with Alexa Fluor 594 to each well
29. Incubate culture plate at room temperature with gentle shaking for one hour
30. Aspirate goat anti-human IgG conjugated with Alexa Fluor 594 solution
31. Wash each well with 200 microliters of PBST three times
32. Add 100 microliters of glycerol solution containing DAPI to each well
33. Observe and image cells under fluorescent microscope using correct filters for each dye and 10x eyepiece with 4x/10x/20x objectives

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Add 1 mg of fluorescently labeled compound to 100 microliters of deionized water
2. Dissolve 65 mg of PLGA in 750 microliters of dichloromethane in scintillation vial
3. Ultrasonicate mixture for 10-30 seconds at 160 watts to dissolve PLGA
4. Add entire first water phase to oil phase dropwise
5. Emulsify solution using handheld homogenizer at 20,000 rpm for 30 seconds
6. Add entire first water-oil phase dropwise into 15 ml of 1% PVA solution
7. Emulsify mixture as previously demonstrated
8. Transfer entire emulsion volume into 200 milliliter round-bottom flask
9. Remove solvent using rotary evaporator at 635 mmHg vacuum for 1 hour
10. Aliquot 1 milliliter of aqueous phase into each of 14 microcentrifuge tubes
11. Pellet microspheres by centrifugation
12. Remove 900 microliters of aqueous solution from each tube using micropipette without disturbing pellets
13. Wash microspheres with 1 milliliter de-ionized water
14. Remove 900 microliters of supernatant carefully
15. Seed 40 visually size-matched islets into one well of a 96-well tissue culture plate
16. Add 200 microliters of islet culture medium to the seeded well
17. Resuspend microspheres in pre-assay medium to achieve 10 nanograms per microliter compound concentration
18. Sonicate microspheres in ice water bath for 10 minutes at 160 watts with 10-second pulses at 4°C
19. Remove 100 microliters of islet culture medium from each well without dislodging islets
20. Add 100 microliters of microsphere-containing assay medium to each well
21. Carefully discard assay medium from all wells without disturbing islets
22. Gently wash islets with 200 microliters of PBS (two times)
23. Immerse islets in 100 microliters of trypsin EDTA solution
24. Incubate islets in trypsin EDTA for 3 minutes at room temperature
25. Perform gentle pipetting after 2 minutes of incubation
26. Transfer entire well volume into individual microcentrifuge tubes
27. Add 400 microliters of islet culture medium to each tube to stop dissociation
28. Centrifuge tubes to collect microsphere-treated islets
29. Remove supernatants gently using micropipette
30. Resuspend pellets in 200 microliters of fresh islet culture medium
31. Spin dissociated islets onto charged microscope slides using cytocentrifuge
32. Draw boundary around islets with hydrophobic marking pen
33. Fix cells with 75 microliters of 4% paraformaldehyde at room temperature for 10 minutes
34. Perform first wash with 75 microliters of PBS
35. Perform second wash with 75 microliters of PBS
36. Permeabilize cells with 75 microliters of 0.2% Triton X-100 in PBS for 10 minutes
37. Perform final wash with 75 microliters of PBS
38. Place slides cell side up in humid chamber
39. Remove PBS from slides
40. Apply 75 microliters normal donkey serum to block non-specific binding
41. Replace serum with 75 microliters primary antibody of interest
42. Incubate with primary antibody for 1 hour at room temperature
43. Aspirate primary antibody solution
44. Wash samples with 375 microliters PBS
45. Incubate islets with 75 microliters of immunofluorescent tagged secondary antibody for one hour at room temperature protected from light
46. Replace secondary antibody solution with 75 microliters of DAPI for three minutes
47. Rinse sample in deionized water for five minutes
48. Spot 100 microliters of fast-drying mounting solution containing anti-fade reagent onto glass cover slip
49. Invert microscope slide (sample side down) onto mounting solution

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Clean trachea and larynx from excess surrounding tissues (lateral muscles, excessive tongue) using dissecting scalpel, Adson forceps, and Metzenbaum scissors by cutting and dissecting
2. Remove hyoid bone and surrounding soft tissues by finding eminence of greater horn, following horn with blade, and passing through contralateral side
3. Cut trachea at distal side approximately 15 centimeters from larynx using dissecting knife
4. Reserve removed mediastinal organs for next procedural step
5. Take previously discarded mediastinal organs
6. Search for thymus in mediastinal organs
7. Detach thymus from surrounding tissue using dissecting forceps
8. Cut thymus into butterfly shape using dissecting knife to create 1.5 cm thick slice
9. Shape thymus slice with Metzenbaum scissors into butterfly form (two 3x2 cm lobes connected by isthmus)
10. Suture thyroid to tracheal wall
11. Place simulated thyroid between first and third tracheal rings
12. Take needle holder
13. Take surgical forceps
14. Grab two zero silk suture
15. Suture thyroid with two lateral horizontal mattress stitches in each lobe to lateral portion of trachea
16. Pass needle only in superficial part of trachea
17. Remove esophagus from larynx connection using scalpel or surgical scissors
18. Cut and open esophagus longitudinally with surgical scissors
19. Hold muscle and mucosa with tooth forceps during cutting
20. Suture esophagus to trachea
21. Place muscle layer on trachea and larynx with mucous membrane face up
22. Suture opened esophagus with six simple stitches
23. Place proximal suture on thyroid cartilage summit
24. Place distal suture on frontal partition of last tracheal ring
25. Mark a line on esophagus mucosa with white India ink to simulate linea alba using insulin syringe
26. Withdraw India ink into insulin syringe
27. Track line on simulated fascia by scratching mucosa with needle while dispensing ink drops
28. Remove excess ink gently using small swab
29. Cut foam square to dimensions 3 cm × 10 cm × 15 cm
30. Shape central furrow measuring 2.5 cm wide × 10 cm long
31. Fold foam square in half to create longitudinal hump
32. Cut 1 cm off entire length of hump using scissors
33. Unfold foam and trim furrow edges smoothly
34. Take wooden tablet matching foam dimensions
35. Place foam square on wooden tablet
36. Insert model into foam furrow
37. Staple tracheal end with wood stapler
38. Staple thyroid cartilage lateral muscles
39. Staple epiglottis
40. Take pig skin collected from butcher shop
41. Cut skin into 25x20 cm square shape to cover model
42. Cover model with skin using dissecting knife
43. Staple skin to wooden tablet with 10-15 clips using stapler
44. Trim excess skin on each side of tablet using knife
45. Use Kocher forceps to firmly hold skin for safer cutting

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Make fly behavior observation chamber (FBOC) using translucent plastic bottle cap (17x22 mm)
2. Alternative: Cut 17 mm section from thick end of 5 ml pipette tip to make FBOC
3. Pour 1% agar into FBOC to adjust depth
4. Pour agar to 12 mm depth when food needs placement in FBOC
5. Pour agar to 5 mm depth when no food needed in FBOC
6. Place cutoff pipette tip section in 35 mm or 60 mm Petri dish (for pipette-tip FBOC)
7. Pour 1% agar gel into Petri dish to ~5 mm thickness
8. Wait for agar to solidify and seal FBOC bottom
9. Pour agar gel into FBOC to desired thickness
10. Bore a 10 millimeter deep hole in center of ethylamine foam sheet matching FBOC diameter
11. Insert FBOC into bored hole
12. Pour prepared food into Petri dish to 8 mm height
13. Place solidified food on rectangular-drawn paper
14. Cut food according to paper rectangle
15. Transfer cut food piece onto plastic surface
16. Shape food into quadrangular pyramid or frustum using plastic guide
17. Cut plastic piece for handling
18. Use tweezers to position plastic and shaped food in FBOC center
19. Pour 1% agar gel into clean empty bottle
20. Transfer flies to conditioning bottle
21. Place bottle at 25°C for ≥36 hours
22. Chill and inactivate flies in crushed ice
23. Sort flies on ice pack
24. Transfer flies to FBOC
25. Place FBOC under stereo microscope for observation
26. Illuminate the FBOC
27. Mount mini LED video lights on flash hot shoe mount stands
28. Position LED lights on left and right sides of FBOC
29. Turn on LED lights
30. Set LED color temperature to 5,000-5,600 Kelvin
31. Set LED brightness to 100%
32. Turn on LED video lights
33. Adjust stereo zoom microscope until FBOC edge is clearly visible with naked eye
34. Move FBOC to center of field of view
35. Attach universal telescope digital camera adapter clamp to stereo microscope eyepiece
36. Attach compact digital camera to adapter by alternately turning camera mounting screw and camera fixing screw
37. Turn on digital camera
38. Adjust horizontal/vertical fine-tuning knobs until FBOC edge appears clearly in center of bright circular field on camera's LCD screen
39. Rotate mode dial to aperture priority auto-mode
40. Rotate mode dial to aperture priority auto-mode
41. Press focus mode on multi-selector
42. Choose macro close-up mode and press OK button
43. Move zoom switch from wide angle to telephoto end until central image fills LCD screen
44. Press movie-record button to start recording fly behavior
45. Turn focus knob of microscope
46. Turn microscope focus knob until flies in FBOC are clearly visible
47. Turn microscope zoom knob to achieve desired magnification for observation/recording

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Check depth of anesthesia by pinching rat's hind feet and verifying absence of reflexes
2. Apply veterinary ointment to eyes to prevent dryness
3. Spread hind legs and fix position using tape
4. Shave inguinal hair with hair trimmer
5. Disinfect area with alternating povidone-iodine and 80% alcohol scrubs repeated three times
6. Perform vertical incision under microscope
7. Repeat alternating scrubs three times
8. Perform vertical incision along linea inguinalis under microscope
9. Use two forceps to gently separate subcutaneous tissues and expose superficial epigastric vein up to its origin on femoral vein
10. Apply two clamps to stop blood flow through superficial epigastric vein
11. Harvest segment of superficial epigastric vein
12. Apply two clamps to superficial epigastric vein
13. Harvest segment of clamped vein
14. Lift isolated vein segment carefully
15. Transect vessel using micro-scissors
16. Place vein segment on sterile gauze
17. Insert 30-gauge needle into one end of vein
18. Flush vein with heparin solution
19. Transfer vein into 1% lidocaine solution
20. Store vein on ice to prevent spasms
21. Euthanize donor rat
22. Shave medial side of legs using hair trimmer
23. Disinfect skin with three alternating scrubs of povidone-iodine and 80% ethanol
24. Perform toe pinch to monitor anesthesia depth
25. Perform median femoral incision from knee to inguinal fold
26. Under microscope, separate femoral artery from surroundings using two forceps
27. Apply two micro clamps to stop blood flow
28. Place proximal micro clamp to stop blood flow
29. Place second micro clamp to stop blood flow
30. Cut out short segment of clamped femoral artery and discard
31. Shorten arterial stump to create 1-2mm gap larger than vein graft
32. Flush arterial stump with heparin using 30-gauge needle
33. Use forceps to pull adventitia slightly over end of vessel stump
34. Remove piece of adventitia
35. Place harvested vein segment between arterial stumps
36. Adjust gap size between stumps for proper fit
37. Perform proximal anastomosis using 10-0 prolene suture
38. Perform proximal anastomosis using 10-0 prolene suture
39. Suture each lateral side of proximal anastomosis
40. Add three sutures to ventral side of proximal anastomosis
41. Place three stitches on dorsal side of proximal anastomosis
42. Connect distal vessels using same anastomosis technique
43. Suture each lateral side of distal anastomosis
44. Add three sutures to ventral side of distal anastomosis
45. Place sutures on dorsal side of distal anastomosis
46. Load two one-milliliter syringes
47. Load two 1-milliliter syringes with fibrin glue components
48. Lift graft with forceps
49. Apply 100 microliters of fibrin glue component one under graft
50. Apply 100 microliters of fibrin glue component two under graft
51. Place graft back in position
52. Apply 100 microliters of each fibrin glue component over graft to cover graft and anastomosis
53. Open clamps starting with distal clamp
54. Confirm successful surgery by checking for visible pulse in transplanted vein and distal artery of graft
55. Remove excessive glue that could impede closure
56. Close skin layers using 5-0 prolene sutures
57. Close skin layers using 5-0 prolene sutures
58. Inject 4-5 mg/kg carprofen subcutaneously
59. Monitor animal until it maintains sternal recumbency
60. Transfer rat to individual home cage
61. Administer metamizole in drinking water for three days
62. Perform daily recovery checks

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Disinfect operating surface with 70% ethanol
2. Sterilize all surgical instruments using hot bead sterilizer
3. Adjust homeothermic blanket system temperature to maintain 37±1°C
4. Prepare cotton gauze and applicators for bleeding management
5. Record weight and identifying characteristics of healthy animals scheduled for surgery
6. Administer dose of buprenorphine to animals
7. Anesthetize the rat
8. Shave fur from neck and chest areas
9. Disinfect shaved areas with Betadine solution
10. Apply 70% ethanol to disinfected areas
11. Repeat disinfection cycle (Betadine + ethanol) three times
12. Place animal on homeothermic blanket system
13. Position animal lying on its right side
14. Bevel edges of polyethylene endotracheal tube
15. Insert endotracheal tube using surgical stereo microscope while manipulating tongue and visualizing trachea
16. Connect endotracheal tube to ventilator set at 80 breaths/minute and 1.2 mL/100g body weight tidal volume
17. Place sterile drape over rat with surgical area exposed
18. Open chest at left fourth intercostal space while minimizing tissue cutting to reduce bleeding risk
19. Position chest retractor within fourth intercostal space and spread ribs to expose left ventricle (LV) while avoiding lung damage
20. Open pericardium and leave in place
21. Identify proximal left anterior descending (LAD) artery using surgical stereo microscope
22. Ligate LAD with slip knot to induce ischemia while avoiding heart exteriorization
23. Confirm LAD ligation by observing myocardial tissue blanching distal to suture and anterior wall dysfunction
24. Begin cardiac reperfusion after 30-minute ischemic period
25. Release ligature around LAD using microsurgical scissors to initiate cardiac reperfusion
26. Verify reperfusion by observing return of red color to myocardial tissue and recovery of anterior wall motion
27. Remove chest retractor
28. Suture ribs together using interrupted pattern
29. Create negative chest pressure by drawing air from chest cavity
30. Close skin with continuous suture pattern
31. Transfer rat to recovery cage with heating pad
32. Monitor body temperature (rectal thermometer), respiratory rate (visual inspection), heart rate (palpation), and pain signs until ambulatory
33. Administer sterile saline
34. Administer buprenorphine if signs of dehydration observed
35. Place re-anesthetized rat into surgical area
36. Visualize left ventricle
37. Ligate LAD or retie slipknot
38. Inject 1 mL of 5% Evans blue and saline into right ventricular chamber
39. Allow Evans blue solution to perfuse for 2 minutes
40. Inject 1 mL saturated potassium chloride solution into right ventricular chamber to stop heart
41. Rapidly excise heart
42. Embed heart in warmed 2% agar phosphate-buffered saline
43. Wait for agar to solidify at room temperature
44. Cut heart into 1-2 mm sections
45. Stain viable myocardium with 2% triphenyltetrazolium chloride (TTC) for 20 minutes
46. Capture image of each heart slice

Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Load 1 mL syringe with 27-gauge needle with 0.1 mL LPS in PBS per mouse
2. Manually restrain 6-8 week old male C57BL/6J mouse
3. Deliver LPS intraperitoneally
4. Return mouse to cage
5. Make 5 mm incision in center of animal's neck
6. Use blunt forceps to gently separate muscles and expose trachea
7. Insert sterile 27-gauge needle attached to 10-mL syringe into trachea and inject 1 mL sterile PBS
8. Replace 10-mL syringe with 3-mL syringe equipped with new 27-gauge needle
9. Aspirate bronchoalveolar lavage fluid (BOLF) and transfer to labeled 15-mL conical tube on ice
10. Centrifuge BALF samples
11. Transfer supernatants to new 15 milliliter tubes
12. Centrifuge samples again
13. Transfer supernatants to ultra-centrifuge tubes
14. Transfer supernatants to new conical tubes
15. Load samples into 0.2 micron syringe filters
16. Add filtered samples to new ultra-centrifuge tubes
17. Discard supernatants
18. Re-suspend pellets in 100 microliters of PBS per tube
19. Transfer exosome samples to individual 1.5 milliliter tubes
20. Add 0.4 microliters of appropriate exosome dye to a tube containing 100 microliters of suspension solution per sample
21. Add one thawed exosome sample to each dye-containing tube
22. Thoroughly mix exosomes with dye
23. Incubate samples at room temperature for 5 minutes protected from light
24. Place one exosome spin column per sample into a 1.5 milliliter tube
25. Transfer exosome solutions to the center of each spin column
26. Centrifuge samples
27. Discard spin columns
28. Store purified samples at -20°C
29. Seed 1×10⁴ mouse lung epithelial 12 cells in 0.5 mL medium per well of eight-well chamber slide
30. Culture seeded cells for 16 hours at 37°C in 5% CO₂
31. Add 10 μL thawed fluorescent labeled exosomes to each well
32. Incubate cell cultures for 1 hour
33. Wash cells with PBS
34. Fix co-cultures with 1% paraformaldehyde for 10 minutes at room temperature
35. Remove medium from slide
36. Mount slide with DAPI-supplemented medium
37. Image samples by fluorescence microscopy at 40× magnification
38. Seed 2 × 10⁵ mouse lung epithelial 12 cells in 2 ml medium per well in 12-well culture plate
39. Incubate seeded plate at 37°C and 5% CO₂ for 2 hours
40. Add 20 μg exosomes to three wells of epithelial cells per experimental group
41. Return culture plate to incubator
42. Remove medium after 24-hour incubation
43. Wash cells with PBS
44. Add 600 μl lysis buffer to each well with gentle shaking
45. Transfer entire well contents to individual 1.5 ml tubes

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Prepare 10% bleach solution
2. Place beaker with 10% bleach solution and sterile 10 microliter syringe in workspace
3. Place microcentrifuge tube containing AAV aliquot in bench vice
4. Load at least 4 microliters of AAV into 10 microliter syringe
5. Ensure no air bubbles in syringe containing 4 microliters of AAV solution
6. Dispose empty virus tube in beaker containing 10% bleach
7. Add additional 10% bleach to waste container
8. Allow waste to sit for 30 minutes
9. Dispose decontaminated liquid waste according to institutional guidelines
10. Place absorbent bench paper under stereotaxic apparatus and on adjacent virus handling area
11. Induce anesthesia using 3% isoflurane with oxygen at 1 liter per minute
12. Shave fur from between eyes to behind ears
13. Place rat back in induction chamber for 3 minutes
14. Secure rat in stereotaxic apparatus
15. Cover eyes with ophthalm ointment
16. Clean incision site with Betadine
17. Make 2.4 centimeter midline incision over skull starting 2 mm caudal to eyes
18. Retract skin to expose skull
19. Clear membranous tissue from skull until bgma and lambda are visible
20. Ensure skull is flat
21. Position drill at bgma, zero coordinates, then move to desired medial/lateral/anterior-posterior coordinates
22. Drill hole using 0.9, 0.7, or 0.5 millimeter drill bit
23. Set infusion pump to deliver virus at 0.2 microliters per minute
24. Load syringe into infusion arm of stereotaxic device
25. Lower syringe to desired coordinate
26. Activate pump to deliver desired amount of virus
27. Leave syringe in place for 10 minutes to prevent backflow
28. Raise syringe at 5 millimeters per minute
29. Move to next coordinate to repeat infusion procedure
30. Administer postoperative analgesia according to approved protocol
31. Place rat in clean cage with bedding, lid, and proper signage
32. Monitor rat until fully recovered from anesthesia
33. Return rat to housing room
34. Prepare fresh 1 mg/ml solution of clozapine-N-oxide according to protocol instructions
35. Inject CNO solution intraperitoneally at 1 mg/kg dosage
36. Initiate computer code for behavioral session
37. Place rats in testing chambers
38. Begin first preconditioning session (64 minutes duration)
39. Present 12 intermixed auditory-visual stimulus trials
40. Present 10-second tone stimulus (6 trials)
41. Immediately present 5-second flashing light stimulus after tone
42. Present 10-second white noise stimulus alone (6 trials)
43. Present visual stimulus (flashing light) for 5 seconds
44. Deliver 2 food pellets (45 mg each) immediately after stimulus
45. Implement inter-trial intervals averaging 7 minutes
46. Perform 78-minute test session with 12 intermixed trials of auditory stimuli alone
47. Present tone stimulus (preconditioned stimulus) alone for 10 seconds on 6 trials
48. Present white noise stimulus (unpaired stimulus) alone for 10 seconds on 6 trials
49. Remove rats from chambers
50. Return rats to animal colony
51. Analyze behavioral data from conditioning and test phases according to protocol
52. Perform immunohistochemistry against tagged reporter or reporter protein
53. Examine stained sections by microscopy
54. Ascertain location and expression of designer receptor and/or reporter

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Shave thorax, abdomen, and back of euthanized mouse with hair clipper
2. Disinfect shaved area with 70% ethanol
3. Pin mouse to polystyrene tray with extended limbs, head, and tail
4. Position pinned mouse under microsurgery microscope
5. Make cutaneous incision from submandibular region to lower abdomen using Mayo straight pattern scissors
6. Dissect subcutaneously to expose thoracic wall and peritoneum
7. Cut pectoralis major using microsurgical scissors
8. Cut pectoralis major and pectoralis minor muscles along sixth intercostal space on both sides of thoracic wall using microsurgical scissors
9. Cut sternum along previous incisions using straight pattern scissors
10. Complete sternotomy by cutting sternum along its long axis
11. Elevate and pin both sides of thoracic wall to expose cardiopulmonary complex
12. Excise thymus using round tipped micro forceps
13. Excise thymus using round tipped micro forceps
14. Excise surrounding adipose tissue by delicately pulling off attachments
15. Cut esophagus with micro scissors
16. Separate brachiocephalic veins with sharp micro forceps
17. Separate left common carotid and left subclavian arteries from underlying tissue
18. Place stitches above emergence of brachiocephalic, left common carotid, and left subclavian arteries using microneedle holder, sharp micro forceps, and 9-0 suture
19. Cauterize brachiocephalic veins
20. Open entrance using micro scissors
21. Section ligament with micro scissors to create entrance
22. Introduce 27-gauge catheter into trachea
23. Advance catheter delicately to tracheal bronchi without disruption
24. Secure tracheal catheter with three stitches using six-O suture around trachea
25. Section mouse at 12th thoracic vertebra, including descending aorta anterior to spine
26. Retrogradely catheterize aorta until reaching aortic arc
27. Secure aortic catheter with four stitches around aorta starting 5 mm below catheter tip
28. Connect mouse to pump system using silicone tubing
29. Connect mouse to pump system using silicone tubing and lower connectors
30. Perfuse with deionized water at 200 microliters per minute
31. Change perfusion agent to 0.5% DOC diluted in deionized water
32. Change perfusion agent to 0.1% SDS diluted in deionized water
33. Perfuse with deionized water for 24 hours to wash away SDS and DOC
34. Resect decellularized heart and lungs by sectioning attachments to thorax
35. Store tissue in sterile cryo tube with deionized water, 1% penicillin streptomycin, and 0.3 micromolar sodium azide at four degrees Celsius
36. Block sample by incubating in cryo tube with 6% donkey serum and 3% BSA overnight
37. Incubate sample with primary antibody in 3% donkey serum in PBS for 24 hours
38. Wash sample five times in PBST (1 hour per wash)
39. Incubate sample with fluorescently conjugated secondary antibody in 3% donkey serum in PBS for 24 hours
40. Repeat PBST washes
41. Add ionized water and store sample at 4°C away from direct light
42. Place sample in glass bottom dish
43. Humidify sample with two droplets of storing solution
44. Set microscope objective
45. Inspect sample using fluorescence light
46. Switch to computer control and turn on lasers
47. Adjust laser intensity, pinhole aperture, detectors wavelength, gain, resolution, and zoom
48. Set number and step size for Z-stack
49. Begin acquisition
50. Excise one lung lobe from euthanized mouse
51. Place excised lung lobe in 10×10×5 mm cryo mold
52. Cover lung lobe with approximately 500 microliters of OCT compound
53. Freeze sample on dry ice
54. Maintain sample at freezing temperature
55. Excise one decellularized lung lobe from processed mouse
56. Place decellularized lobe in cryo mold with largest surface area down
57. Cover decellularized lobe with OCT compound
58. Freeze sample on dry ice
59. Maintain sample at freezing temperature until required
60. Section frozen tissue blocks into 5 micrometer sections using cryostat at -20°C
61. Place tissue sections on adhesive glass slides
62. Transfer slides to room temperature until air-dried
63. Briefly immerse slides in PBS
64. Immerse slides in 4% paraformaldehyde in PBS for 15 minutes
65. Wash slides once in PBS
66. Wash slides twice in distilled water (5 minutes per wash)
67. Immerse slides in Myer's hematoxylin solution for 10 minutes
68. Wash slides under running distilled water in Copeland jar for 10 minutes
69. Immerse slides in eosin solution for 7 minutes
70. Dip slides in xylene several times
71. Apply few drops of DPX mounting medium to slides
72. Place glass cover slip on slides
73. Leave slides to dry overnight under chemical hood
74. Scan dried slides in slide scanner

12. Fit flask with three-neck vacuum adapter connected to vacuum line and argon balloon

31. Add 0.25 millimoles of dicyclohexyl isobutylamine to vial
32. Add 1.0 millimole of potassium carbonate to vial

38. Backfill vial with argon (3 cycles)

51. Make cut extending to distal edge of posterior skull surface
52. Make identical cut on contralateral side
53. Use rongeurs to clear skull around cerebellum
54. Slide scissors tip along inner skull surface from dorsal distal posterior corner to distal frontal ledge
55. Lift blade tip during cutting to avoid brain damage
56. Repeat cutting procedure on opposite side
57. Peel dorsal surface of skull away from brain using rongeurs
58. Trim sides of skull using rongeurs
59. Sever olfactory bulbs and ventral nervous connections using spatula
60. Cut dura connecting brain to skull using iris scissors
61. Place brain in vial containing fixative (≥10x brain volume)
62. Store vial at 4°C for 24 hours
63. Wash brain with PBS 3 times with gentle swirling
64. Store brain in PBS or Hanks solution with sodium azide at 4°C

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Place mouse head in ice-cold oxygenated sucrose solution
2. Remove skin of skull using fine scissors
3. Cut skull along midline and near temporal lobes
4. Peel cut skull towards sides of head to expose brain
5. Remove brain from skull using spatula
6. Place extracted brain on ice-cold surgical stage covered with wet filter paper
7. Remove cerebellum using ice-chilled blade
8. Separate brain hemispheres by cutting along the midline
9. Place separated hemispheres into beaker containing ice-cold sucrose solution bubbled with 95% O₂/5% CO₂
10. Transfer one brain hemisphere to ice-cold stage covered with filter paper
11. Place paper towels or filter papers around brain to absorb excess solution
12. Use two fire-polished glass pipettes to separate cortex from central brain
13. Apply 2-3 drops of ice-cold sucrose ACSF on tissue
14. Remove excess solution from tissue
15. Cut cortical connections at both ends of hippocampus
16. Dissect hippocampus from brain using fire-polished glass tools
17. Position hippocampus with ous side facing up and sulcus facing down
18. Apply 2-3 drops of ice-cold sucrose ACSF on tissue
19. Remove excess solution around tissue
20. Use fire-polished glass tool to turn hippocampus over
21. Expose sulcus using ice-cold blade
22. Trim septal and temporal ends of hippocampus with ice-cold blade
23. Insert custom-made glass needle into sulcus
24. Cut fiber track from DG to CA3 using glass needle
25. Use metal wire loop to pull DG (dentate gyrus) over
26. Unfold hippocampus using metal wire loop
27. Apply 2-3 drops of ice-cold sucrose A CSF onto tissue
28. Remove excess solution around tissue
29. Trim edges of unfolded hippocampus with ice-cold blade
30. Transfer unfolded hippocampus preparation to recovery chamber filled with normal ACSF bubbled with 95% oxygen and 5% carbon dioxide at room temperature
31. Maintain preparation in recovery chamber for one hour
32. Transfer the unfolded hippocampus to the recording chamber using a custom made glass pipette dropper
33. Position the unfolded hippocampus under microscope with alveus side facing down, CA3 area pointing away, and CA1 field pointing towards researcher
34. Aspirate solution from recording chamber edge using vacuum pipette until chamber is dried and tissue lies on array
35. Place custom tissue anchor on top of tissue to secure unfolded hippocampus onto array
36. Refill recording chamber with a few drops of solution
37. Gradually open inlet and outlet valves
38. Adjust flow rate to two drops per second in IV trip chamber
39. Incubate tissue in recording chamber with normal ACSF for one minute
40. Switch solutions in recording chamber
41. Apply 4-AP dissolved ACSF and adjust flow rate properly
42. Incubate tissue in 4-AP dissolved ACSF for 5-10 minutes
43. Control tissue anchor using micro manipulator
44. Gradually lift tissue anchor from recording chamber
45. Shut down inlet and outlet to stop flow
46. Use small paintbrush to lift corner of tissue
47. If tissue not floating, employ vacuum tube to carefully dry chamber with tissue on array
48. Carefully open inlet to gradually refill chamber
49. Shut down inlet to stop flow after refilling
50. Use small paintbrush to lift corner of tissue when chamber is full
51. If tissue is floating, use vacuum tube to suck tissue away
52. Open flow at inlet and vacuum at outlet

question:
Based on the full experimental procedure，determine the step numbers shown in the video.
1. Clean 10 by 10 millimeter silicon substrate in isopropanol
2. Rinse substrate with deionized water
3. Blow dry substrate with nitrogen
4. Place substrate into homemade stainless steel cylindrical reaction cell (7000 mm³ volume)
5. Place silicon-based micro-machined platforms into reaction cell
6. Align platforms with shadow mask to confine material growth
7. Close reaction cell lid and ensure proper seal to prevent leakage
8. Turn on temperature control system (heaters, thermocouple, PID controller)
9. Set temperature to 400°C and stabilize for 30 minutes
10. Add 50 milligrams of zinc chloride
11. Add 5 milliliters of ethanol to a 100 milliliter glass vial
12. Secure vial to vacuum trap using glass joint clip
13. Position downpipe end 60 millimeters above vial bottom without solution submersion
14. Clamp vial to universal support
15. Adjust support height to align vial bottom with ultrasonic atomizer focal point
16. Connect inlet and exhaust of vacuum trap to nitrogen pipe in reaction cell
17. Verify reaction cell temperature has reached steady state
18. Adjust nitrogen flow to 200 cm³/min using mass flow controller
19. Allow nitrogen to flow through system
20. Turn on aerosol generator and maintain constant aerosol flow
21. Turn off aerosol generator
22. Turn off temperature control system to cool reaction cell
23. Stop nitrogen flow
24. Open reaction cell
25. Remove sample with grayish matte surface and columnar zinc oxide structures
26. Perform aerosol assisted chemical vapor deposition of zinc chloride on bare silicon wafers
